**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · issue updated: `2026-04-10T02:08:15Z` > [!NOTE] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary A backward-compatible enhancement to the `issue.created` activity event payload to include core issue fields. This enables plugin event handlers to act on creation without an extra round-trip API call — consistent with plugin extensibility goals and the event-driven plugin architecture.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Plugin event bus | Richer `issue.created` payload to reduce follow-up API calls for plugin consumers | `PluginEventBus` routes domain events to subscribed plugins; event-driven pattern is first-class | ✅ Aligned | | Plugin extensibility | Enable plugins to act on issue creation with complete context | "Thin core, rich edges" — plugins need enough event data to do useful work without coupling to internals | ✅ Aligned | | Backward compatibility | Additive payload change — existing consumers unaffected | Manifest-driven SDK contract; additive changes are safe for plugins already consuming this event | ✅ Aligned | | Governance / audit trail | `assigneeUserId`, `assigneeAgentId` in payload aids plugins implementing board notification/triage | Governance enforced at server layer; activity log records every mutation | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`plugins/runtime/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — PluginEventBus routes domain events to subscribed plugins - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Capability-gated access; event-driven plugin architecture - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Auditable everything; activity log drives plugin event delivery
### Recommendation Straightforward, low-risk enhancement. The proposed field set (`status`, `priority`, `assigneeAgentId`, `assigneeUserId`, `projectId`, `goalId`, `parentId`, `description` truncated at 500 chars) is reasonable and avoids unbounded payload growth. 3259.body:
**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `medium` · issue updated: `2026-04-10T02:08:24Z` > [!NOTE] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary The CEO onboarding `AGENTS.md` is missing an explicit section on board escalation mechanics. This causes a real governance failure mode: agents produce comments on their own tasks instead of creating board-assigned subtasks, which means board users miss critical blockers. The fix is squarely within Paperclip's governance-first design.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Board escalation mechanism | Teach CEO to create subtasks with `assigneeUserId` pointing at the board user | Tasks are the communication channel — all agent communication flows through tasks and comments; delegation = creating a task and assigning it | ✅ Aligned | | Governance-first design | Ensure board blockers surface immediately rather than silently stalling | "Surface problems, don't hide them" is a core product principle; board must always be reachable | ✅ Aligned | | Approval gates and oversight | Board-dependent blockers (approvals, secrets, business decisions) must reach the human operator | Board has unrestricted access and must be notified of blockers; approval workflow is synchronous | ✅ Aligned | | CEO strategy scaffolding | Onboarding assets should encode correct CEO behavior from the start | V1 approval gate: CEO strategy approval must precede execution; board governance is non-negotiable | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Tasks are the communication channel; delegation = creating an assigned task - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers; approval gates; "surface problems, don't hide them" - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Board-level abstraction wins; governance-first
### Recommendation This is a correctness fix for the onboarding experience, not a feature request. The gap directly undermines board governance — the safety foundation the entire product is built on. The proposed section (create a board-assigned subtask with `assigneeUserId`, use `Board:` title prefix, set `blockedByIssueIds`) matches how the task system is designed to work. Ship it. --- Reviewed issue updated: 2026-04-10T02:08:24Z · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

Implemented the CEO board escalation path in onboarding.

What changed:
- added an explicit "Escalating to the board" section to `server/src/onboarding-assets/ceo/AGENTS.md`
- teaches the CEO to create a child issue with `parentId`
- instructs using `assigneeUserId` for the board user
- requires a `Board:` title prefix
- calls out `blockedByIssueIds` so the blocker is tracked correctly
- added a regression check in `agent-skills-routes.test.ts` to keep the CEO bundle honest

Validation:
- `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/agent-skills-routes.test.ts` ✅

3260.body: Related: #2774 (local plugin upgrades reuse cached manifest modules). That issue describes the caching bug we hit; this issue proposes the CLI-level `plugin upgrade` command that would solve the broader workflow problem.

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `medium` · issue updated: `2026-04-10T02:09:04Z` > [!NOTE] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary A missing CLI command for plugin lifecycle management. The proposed `plugin upgrade` path correctly targets the server-side plugin lifecycle state machine and preserves `plugin_config` — consistent with how the tree describes graceful lifecycle transitions and database-backed plugin state.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Plugin lifecycle | Add `upgrade` command that re-validates manifest, cycles the worker, and preserves config | Runtime defines `installed -> ready -> disabled/error -> uninstalled` with validated transitions; "Graceful lifecycle state machine" is a first-class runtime concern | ✅ Aligned | | Plugin config preservation | Preserve `plugin_config` row across upgrades unless schema is incompatible | Plugin state, entities, job runs persisted in PostgreSQL with `pluginId` scoping; lifecycle manager coordinates worker stop/start | ✅ Aligned | | CLI tooling | New `paperclipai plugin upgrade` subcommand | CLI is a monorepo workspace package; plugin management already has `install`/`uninstall` commands | ✅ Aligned | | Developer experience | Reduces friction for local plugin iteration | Plugin SDK includes `PluginDevWatcher` for hot-reload; upgrade path is the production-safe complement | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`plugins/runtime/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — PluginLifecycleManager, graceful shutdown with deadline, database-backed state - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Graceful lifecycle state machine; manifest-driven contract
### Recommendation Well-specified request aligned with the plugin runtime's existing lifecycle model. The proposed five-step upgrade sequence (validate manifest → stop worker → update DB → preserve config → restart) maps directly onto existing runtime services. Worth prioritizing as it reduces the cost of iterating on plugins significantly during development. Ready for a PR. --- Reviewed issue updated: 2026-04-10T02:09:04Z · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3261.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a bug where the inbox badge counted all `mineIssues` (up to 100) rather than only those with `isUnreadForMe: true`, causing "Mark all as read" to leave a stale non-zero count. The fix is a one-line filter in `computeInboxBadgeData` backed by a new targeted test. The PR description does not follow the required PR template from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`. It is missing the **Thinking Path**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections — all of which are required by CONTRIBUTING.md for every PR. Since this is a UI/badge change, before/after screenshots are also expected per the template and CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Code change is safe to merge — minimal, correct, and well-tested; only the PR description needs to be updated to match the required template. The one-line fix is correct and directly addresses the stated bug. The new test explicitly covers the read/unread filtering case, and all 21 existing tests continue to pass. No logic, data-integrity, or security concerns were found in the changed code. The only gap is the missing PR template sections (Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used, Checklist, screenshots), which are process requirements and do not affect code correctness. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/lib/inbox.ts | Added `.filter((issue) => issue.isUnreadForMe)` to `visibleMineIssues` in `computeInboxBadgeData` — targeted, correct fix for the badge overcounting bug | | ui/src/lib/inbox.test.ts | Added "excludes read issues from the badge mineIssues count" test that directly validates the fix with a mixed read/unread set | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix: inbox badge counts all issues inste..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/ba179a6e370d23c13c6bd4652024b61da9ce6652) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=27949521)

3262.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a comprehensive Company Files knowledge base feature: a `knowledge_entries` table, 10 REST endpoints, a three-tab UI at `/:companyPrefix/files`, and manifest injection into the agent heartbeat so all six adapters receive relevant file/document context on demand. The PR description **does not follow the required PR template** from `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` — it is missing the mandatory **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Risks**, and **Checklist** sections. Since this is a visual change (new sidebar nav item, new page), **before/after screenshots** are also required. Please fill out the full template before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the empty-name validation bug and filling out the required PR template sections. One P1 logic bug (empty name accepted on PATCH rename) needs to be fixed before merge. The PR template is also missing required sections (Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks, screenshots). The remaining findings are P2 quality suggestions. server/src/routes/knowledge.ts (empty-name validation on PATCH) and server/src/services/heartbeat.ts (unbounded manifest injection).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/knowledge.ts | New 10-endpoint router for the knowledge feature; missing `name` validation on the PATCH rename route allows empty/whitespace names to be persisted. | | server/src/services/knowledge.ts | Core CRUD service with recursive CTE-based department resolution and BFS delete cascade; unsafe `tx as unknown as Db` cast and no storage cleanup on delete are minor concerns. | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Injects knowledge manifest into agent context on every heartbeat; no size cap means large knowledge bases will bloat every agent prompt. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0055_knowledge_entries.sql | New `knowledge_entries` table with correct FK references, composite indexes, and partial unique indexes on document/asset IDs. | | ui/src/pages/CompanyFiles.tsx | New 874-line page with three-tab layout, folder tree, markdown editor with 1.5 s autosave, and file upload dialog; logic is clean with no obvious bugs. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts | Adds `renderPaperclipKnowledgePrompt` utility that builds a markdown manifest from the knowledge entries array; safe with array/null guards. |

Comments Outside Diff (2)

 1. `server/src/services/knowledge.ts`, line 976-978 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/66c2574fb8b15cfbec9bb8dfeaf8ed134c8537a6/server/src/services/knowledge.ts#L976-L978)) **Unsafe transaction cast may silently break** `tx as unknown as Db` casts a Drizzle transaction object to the full `Db` type. Currently only `execute` is called on it, which transactions do support, but if `Db` gains new methods (or if the call site is refactored), this will silently pass type-checking while failing at runtime. Passing `tx` through a narrower interface (or overloading `getDescendantIds` to accept either) would be safer. Longer-term, consider extracting a minimal `DbLike` interface (`{ execute: … }`) that both `Db` and transaction satisfy.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/knowledge.ts Line: 976-978 Comment: **Unsafe transaction cast may silently break** `tx as unknown as Db` casts a Drizzle transaction object to the full `Db` type. Currently only `execute` is called on it, which transactions do support, but if `Db` gains new methods (or if the call site is refactored), this will silently pass type-checking while failing at runtime. Passing `tx` through a narrower interface (or overloading `getDescendantIds` to accept either) would be safer. Longer-term, consider extracting a minimal `DbLike` interface (`{ execute: … }`) that both `Db` and transaction satisfy. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
2. `server/src/services/knowledge.ts`, line 1003-1008 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/66c2574fb8b15cfbec9bb8dfeaf8ed134c8537a6/server/src/services/knowledge.ts#L1003-L1008)) **Storage objects orphaned on delete** Asset DB rows are removed inside the transaction, but the underlying storage objects (S3/GCS blobs etc.) are left behind permanently. Without a reconciliation job, deleted files will accumulate indefinitely in storage. Consider enqueueing a background cleanup task or at minimum documenting a `TODO` referencing a follow-up issue so this doesn't get lost.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/knowledge.ts Line: 1003-1008 Comment: **Storage objects orphaned on delete** Asset DB rows are removed inside the transaction, but the underlying storage objects (S3/GCS blobs etc.) are left behind permanently. Without a reconciliation job, deleted files will accumulate indefinitely in storage. Consider enqueueing a background cleanup task or at minimum documenting a `TODO` referencing a follow-up issue so this doesn't get lost. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/knowledge.ts Line: 268-274 Comment: **Empty `name` accepted on rename** The PATCH handler passes `name` straight to the service with no validation. If a client sends `{ "name": "" }` or `{ "name": " " }`, the entry will be persisted with a blank/whitespace name, which would break every downstream display and the agent manifest. ```suggestion const { name, parentId, description, sortOrder } = req.body; if (name !== undefined && (typeof name !== "string" || name.trim().length === 0)) { throw unprocessable("name must be a non-empty string"); } const result = await svc.updateEntry(companyId, entryId, { name: name !== undefined ? name.trim() : undefined, ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/knowledge.ts Line: 976-978 Comment: **Unsafe transaction cast may silently break** `tx as unknown as Db` casts a Drizzle transaction object to the full `Db` type. Currently only `execute` is called on it, which transactions do support, but if `Db` gains new methods (or if the call site is refactored), this will silently pass type-checking while failing at runtime. Passing `tx` through a narrower interface (or overloading `getDescendantIds` to accept either) would be safer. ```suggestion const allIds = await getDescendantIds(tx as Db, companyId, entryId); ``` Longer-term, consider extracting a minimal `DbLike` interface (`{ execute: … }`) that both `Db` and transaction satisfy. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/knowledge.ts Line: 1003-1008 Comment: **Storage objects orphaned on delete** Asset DB rows are removed inside the transaction, but the underlying storage objects (S3/GCS blobs etc.) are left behind permanently. Without a reconciliation job, deleted files will accumulate indefinitely in storage. Consider enqueueing a background cleanup task or at minimum documenting a `TODO` referencing a follow-up issue so this doesn't get lost. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 2721-2733 Comment: **Unbounded manifest bloats every heartbeat** `resolveAgentVisibleEntries` runs 3–4 DB queries on every single heartbeat and its full result is injected into the agent context without any size cap. A company with hundreds of company-wide documents will add significant tokens to every heartbeat prompt for every agent, regardless of whether they are relevant. Consider adding a `limit` to the DB queries or slicing the manifest to the N most-recently-updated entries. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3262.body:
**🌱 gardener:verdict:** \`CONFLICT\` · severity: \`high\` · commit: \`a4690b3\` > [!WARNING] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary This PR adds a knowledge base as a first-class core feature (DB table, 10 REST endpoints, heartbeat injection across all 6 adapters, UI page), which directly contradicts three explicit tree decisions: knowledge bases belong in plugins not the core control plane, Paperclip is explicitly "not a knowledge base", and knowledge base was deferred from V1 scope.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Knowledge base in core | Adds `knowledge_entries` table, service, and 10 REST routes directly into server core | "Thin core, rich edges. Knowledge bases… go into plugins — not the core control plane." (`product/NODE.md`) | ⚠️ Conflict | | Paperclip identity | Positions knowledge base as a first-class Paperclip feature | "Paperclip is explicitly **not**: …a knowledge base…" (`product/NODE.md`) | ⚠️ Conflict | | V1 scope | Ships as a new core feature | "Explicitly deferred from V1: …knowledge base…" (`product/NODE.md`) | ⚠️ Conflict | | Adapter integration | Injects knowledge manifest into all 6 adapter heartbeat prompts | Adapter abstraction is aligned; the question is whether the data source should be core vs plugin | ❓ Partial | | Company-scoped isolation | All queries are scoped to `company_id`; auth follows existing `assertCompanyAccess` pattern | Company-scoped isolation (`engineering/NODE.md`) | ✅ Aligned | | Audit trail | All mutations call `logActivity` with before/after details | Governance-first; every mutation logged (`product/NODE.md`) | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Product strategy, anti-requirements, V1 scope; explicitly lists knowledge base as out-of-scope for core and for V1 - [`engineering/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/NODE.md) — Backend architecture; service-oriented pattern; company-scoped isolation
### Recommendation This work is solid in implementation quality — the scoping, audit trail, and adapter integration are all well-executed. The conflict is with *where* it lives, not *whether* it should exist. Two paths forward: 1. **Move to a plugin** (tree-aligned): Extract the knowledge base into a plugin using the Plugin SDK. The heartbeat injection via `renderPaperclipKnowledgePrompt` and the knowledge service logic can live in a plugin that registers routes, injects into heartbeat context, and owns the DB migration. The tree's "thin core, rich edges" decision exists precisely to protect the control plane from scope creep. 2. **Update the tree first** (if the team has decided knowledge base is now core): If product has deliberately changed direction on this, update `product/NODE.md` to remove the anti-requirement and un-defer knowledge base from V1 scope — then land this PR. That PR should be discussed and merged before this code lands. Either way, this PR should not merge until the conflict is resolved. The tree is the decision record; the code should follow from it. --- Reviewed commit: a4690b3 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

## Response to gardener context review

We investigated the plugin path thoroughly. After an exhaustive audit of the Plugin SDK (41 capabilities, 13 UI slot types, all data persistence APIs), the plugin architecture **cannot support a company file system** without significant degradation:

| Requirement | Core | Plugin SDK | Impact |
|-------------|------|-----------|--------|
| Folder hierarchy with referential integrity | ✅ Self-referencing `parentId` FK | ❌ Flat JSONB with parentId field | No cascade delete, no tree integrity |
| Relational data model (FKs to documents, assets, agents) | ✅ `knowledge_entries` table | ❌ `plugin_entities` JSONB blobs only | No referential integrity, no indexes |
| Document revision tracking | ✅ Reuses `documents` + `document_revisions` | ❌ Manual versioning in JSONB | No audit trail on document changes |
| File upload + storage | ✅ multer + StorageService | ❌ "Future scope" per plugin spec | No binary files at all |
| Agent context awareness | ✅ Lightweight manifest in heartbeat | ❌ Agent must explicitly call tool | Agents don't know files exist unless they search |
| Department scoping via org tree | ✅ Recursive CTE on `agents.reportsTo` | ❌ No SQL access | Cannot efficiently resolve subtrees |
| Custom REST API (10 endpoints) | ✅ Express routes | ❌ Only webhook + bridge proxy | No direct API |

### The case for core

The gardener classifies this as "a knowledge base." It isn't. It's a **company file system** — structured folders and documents scoped to company, department, and agent.

Every real organization has files. Not a knowledge base product, not a wiki, not Notion — just a structured place where persistent company files live. This is as fundamental as having an org chart or a budget. A company without a file system is incomplete infrastructure.

- **Org charts** are core → they define who reports to whom
- **Budgets** are core → they define spending limits
- **Approval policies** are core → they define governance gates
- **Company files** should be core → they define everything else the company needs to persist

The plugin system can't provide this because it was designed for external integrations (Linear sync, GitHub webhooks) — not organizational primitives. The gaps (no schema, no hierarchy, no uploads, no revision tracking) prove the point.

### Tree PR

Per the gardener's guidance, we've opened a PR to update the context tree:

**[serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree#1](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/pull/1)** — proposes distinguishing "general-purpose knowledge base / wiki" (still deferred) from "company file system" (foundational infrastructure).

Happy to hear the team's perspective.

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a route-doubling bug on company switch: `toCompanyRelativePath()` previously stripped the company prefix only when the second path segment was in `BOARD_ROUTE_ROOTS`. Plugin page routes (e.g. `youtube-trends`) don't appear in that set, so the prefix was preserved in localStorage and then re-prepended during navigation. The fix changes the logic to strip the prefix whenever the first segment is **not** a known global or board route root — a more robust allowlist-to-denylist flip. Regression tests are included for plugin routes, board routes, and global routes. The PR description does not follow the required PR template (`PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`). The following sections are missing: **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Risks**, and the **Checklist**. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, all sections are required before merge — please fill them in.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to follow the required template — no code-level blockers found. The logic change in `toCompanyRelativePath` is correct and well-tested; all remaining findings are P2 (missing PR template sections), which do not affect runtime behavior. No files require special attention on the code side; the only gap is the incomplete PR description.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/lib/company-routes.ts | Core fix: `toCompanyRelativePath` now strips the company prefix for any path whose first segment is not a known global or board route root — correctly handling plugin page routes that were previously missed. | | ui/src/lib/company-routes.test.ts | Adds regression tests for plugin page routes, board routes, and global routes against `toCompanyRelativePath`; coverage is appropriate for the bug fixed. | | ui/src/lib/inbox.test.ts | No changes to inbox logic visible; test file appears to have been included in the PR's broader merge commit but contains no issues. | | ui/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx | Dashboard page changes (company kill-switch UI, plugin slot outlet) appear unrelated to the route-fix but look correct on review. | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["Fix plugin page routes doubling company ..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/0c4871637040231613f98f5b5ddb7d06951b3eca) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=27952338)

3265.body:
<!-- gardener:state · reviewed=ccd77762d2085a69347ee9f25709246c6b6934e5 · verdict=NEEDS_REVIEW · severity=medium · tree_sha=33dc6fd046c372a3bbb88c0de72121c4c8f3df50 -->
<!-- gardener:last_consumed_rereview=none -->

> [!WARNING]
> **gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `ccd7776`

---

## Summary

This PR is titled as a bug fix for plugin page route prefix-doubling, and that fix is correct and aligned. However, the PR bundles four substantial and largely unrelated changes under a misleading title: a new BastionClaw adapter (filesystem IPC + SQLite polling), a company-level kill switch (pause/resume), a heartbeat model override feature, and the actual bug fix. The BastionClaw adapter introduces a new architectural pattern not recorded in the context tree, and there is a SQL injection risk in the adapter's polling loop.

---

## Context Match

| Area | Tree | PR | Match |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plugin page route fix (`company-routes.ts`) | Plugin extensibility is core; routing correctness required | Correctly strips company prefix for any non-global segment | ✅ Aligned |
| Company pause/resume (kill switch) | Governance: "Board can pause/resume any Agent immediately"; company lifecycle has `paused` status | Board-only `POST /:companyId/pause` + `POST /:companyId/resume`, cancels active runs | ✅ Aligned |
| Heartbeat skips paused companies | Company lifecycle: "Paused companies stop all heartbeats" | `tickTimers` now queries paused companies and skips their agents | ✅ Aligned |
| BastionClaw adapter pattern | Tree lists gateway adapters as WebSocket-based (`openclaw-gateway`); "gateway adapters connect to a remote agent over WebSocket" | BastionClaw uses **filesystem IPC + SQLite polling** — not WebSocket | ⚠️ New Territory |
| Heartbeat model override | No tree node records adapter-level model override at heartbeat invocation | `heartbeat.model` added to heartbeat policy, injected into `runtimeConfig` on timer runs only | ⚠️ New Territory |

---

## Issues

### 1. SQL injection risk in BastionClaw adapter (medium severity)

In `packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/server/execute.ts`, the task poll query interpolates `taskId` directly into an SQL string with only single-quote escaping:

```ts
`SELECT status, result, error, duration_ms FROM task_run_logs WHERE task_id = '${taskId.replace(/'/g, "''")}' ORDER BY run_at DESC LIMIT 1`
```

`taskId` is constructed as `paperclip-${ctx.runId}` where `ctx.runId` comes from the server. While the server controls `runId` today, this pattern bypasses parameterized queries and creates a brittle trust assumption. The `sqlite3` CLI used here supports parameterized queries via `-cmd`. This should use a parameterized form or at minimum assert that `taskId` contains only safe characters before interpolation.

### 2. PR bundles four unrelated changes under a misleading title (low-medium)

The title "Fix plugin page routes doubling company prefix on company switch" covers only ~10 lines of the 3,800-line diff. The rest introduces: a new adapter, a company kill switch feature, a heartbeat model override, GitHub issue templates, and a SECURITY.md. Bundling makes review harder and commit history less useful. These should be separate PRs.

### 3. BastionClaw adapter pattern is not in the tree (new territory)

The adapters tree node defines two patterns: **local** (spawns a child process) and **gateway** (WebSocket). BastionClaw uses a third pattern: **filesystem IPC + SQLite polling**. The adapter writes a JSON task file to a shared directory and polls a SQLite DB for completion. This is a novel integration pattern with meaningful tradeoffs (local-only, polling latency, SQLite lock sensitivity) that should be captured in the tree before the adapter ships, so future adapters don't re-litigate this design.

### 4. `models: []` in BastionClaw index.ts

`packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/index.ts` exports `models: []`. The tree notes that adapters report model info via `detectModel`. If BastionClaw can detect the model from the Claude instance it dispatches to, this should be wired up rather than left empty.

---

## Tree Nodes Referenced

- `adapters/NODE.md` — local vs gateway pattern, `ServerAdapterModule` interface
- `adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md` — gateway adapter precedent
- `product/governance/NODE.md` — board powers, pause/resume, budget hard-stops
- `product/company-model/NODE.md` — company lifecycle (`active`/`paused`/`archived`)
- `product/NODE.md` — control plane principles

---

## Recommendation

The routing fix and the governance/heartbeat changes are aligned and look correct. The **BastionClaw adapter** is new territory worth flagging:

1. **Fix the SQL injection** before merge — use parameterized queries or character-validation on `taskId`.
2. **Confirm the IPC+SQLite pattern is intentional** and draft a `adapters/bastionclaw/NODE.md` tree node capturing the design rationale and constraints (local-only, SQLite polling, requires BastionClaw to be co-located).
3. **Consider splitting the PR** — the bug fix and the BastionClaw adapter are independent and the bundling makes each harder to review.

Reviews against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree).</sub>

Greptile Summary

This PR makes the `gemini_local` adapter stitch in `HEARTBEAT.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `TOOLS.md` alongside `AGENTS.md` when those companion files are present in the same bundle root, and refactors the `isLocal` UI guard into a shared `supportsInstructionsBundles` helper that incidentally fixes `gemini_local` being absent from the bundle-panel check. Both the runtime behavior and the UI gap are well-targeted fixes with a clear integration test.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all remaining findings are P2 style suggestions that do not affect correctness. The core logic is correct: companion files are loaded sequentially from the same directory, sections are joined cleanly, and the integration test validates end-to-end. The two P2 notes (catch-all error swallow and a slightly misleading log note) are improvement suggestions rather than defects that can cause wrong runtime behavior. packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts — minor error-handling and log-message polish worth considering before merge.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts | Adds companion file loading (HEARTBEAT.md, SOUL.md, TOOLS.md) when entry is AGENTS.md; minor: catch-all error swallow in helper and slightly misleading log note for non-AGENTS.md entry files. | | server/src/__tests__/gemini-local-execute.test.ts | New integration test verifies all four companion files appear in the prompt; uses a temp-dir fake Gemini binary and proper cleanup — looks solid. | | ui/src/lib/instructions-bundles.ts | Extracts adapter-type allow-list into a shared helper, correctly adding gemini_local which was missing from the UI's isLocal check. | | ui/src/lib/instructions-bundles.test.ts | Basic unit tests for the new helper; covers the happy path and two rejection cases. | | ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx | Replaces inline adapter-type union with supportsInstructionsBundles, fixing the missing gemini_local case in the UI bundle panel. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 81-87 Comment: **Catch-all swallows non-ENOENT errors silently** The bare `catch` absorbs every failure — including `EACCES` (permission denied) or `EISDIR`. If a companion file exists but is unreadable, the agent silently receives no companion content and there's no log entry to diagnose it. Narrowing to `ENOENT` (file-not-found) and re-throwing (or at least warning) on other codes would preserve the intended "skip if absent" semantics while surfacing real I/O problems. ```suggestion async function readInstructionFileIfPresent(filePath: string): Promise { try { return await fs.readFile(filePath, "utf8"); } catch (err) { if ((err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === "ENOENT") return null; throw err; } } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 311-313 Comment: **Misleading log note for non-AGENTS.md entry files** When `instructionsFilePath` points to something other than `AGENTS.md` (e.g. `SOUL.md`), companion loading is never attempted, yet the note still reads `"No companion instruction files were loaded."` This may puzzle someone reading logs for a non-AGENTS.md agent. A small guard makes the intent explicit: ```suggestion loadedInstructionFiles.length > 1 ? `Loaded companion instruction files: ${loadedInstructionFiles.slice(1).join(", ")}` : path.basename(instructionsFilePath) === "AGENTS.md" ? "No companion instruction files were found." : undefined, ``` (And filter `undefined` out of the `notes` array, or just omit the else branch entirely.) How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3267.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds two independent features: a `PAPERCLIP_AUTH_ALLOWED_EMAIL_DOMAINS` env var (and config-file equivalent) that gates new sign-ups to a comma-separated allowlist of domains via a better-auth `databaseHooks.user.create.before` hook, and a production-ready AWS ECS Terraform module (Graviton/arm64, EFS, ALB, Secrets Manager, optional Route53/ACM) plus a Dockerfile `HEALTHCHECK`. The auth logic, parsing, and test coverage are solid; all remaining findings are P2 style/infrastructure best-practices.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all findings are P2 style/infrastructure suggestions with no blocking correctness or security issues. The auth feature is correctly implemented with good test coverage and the gating logic is sound. The Terraform module is additive and won't affect existing deployments. All remaining comments (example file placeholder, ECR mutability, single NAT Gateway, silent rejection UX) are non-blocking best-practice suggestions. `.env.production.example` (hardcoded domain placeholder), `deploy/terraform/modules/paperclip/ecr.tf` (image tag mutability), `deploy/terraform/vpc.tf` (single NAT Gateway).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/auth/better-auth.ts | Adds `isEmailDomainAllowed` pure function and a `databaseHooks.user.create.before` hook that blocks sign-ups from non-allowlisted domains; logic is correct and well-guarded behind a non-empty allowlist check. | | server/src/config.ts | Adds `authAllowedEmailDomains` to the Config interface and `parseAllowedEmailDomains` helper; env var correctly takes precedence over file config, parsing handles whitespace, case, and empty entries cleanly. | | .env.production.example | New production reference env file; contains `imaginarium.vc` as a non-placeholder example value for `PAPERCLIP_AUTH_ALLOWED_EMAIL_DOMAINS` — should use a generic placeholder instead. | | deploy/terraform/modules/paperclip/ecs.tf | ECS cluster, task definition (EC2/bridge, arm64), and service with ALB integration; secrets injection, EFS mount, and lifecycle ignore rules are all configured correctly. | | deploy/terraform/modules/paperclip/ecr.tf | ECR repository with `scan_on_push = true`; uses `MUTABLE` image tags which allows accidental tag overwrites in production. | | deploy/terraform/vpc.tf | Full VPC with dual public/private subnets, dual-stack IPv6, and routing; only one NAT Gateway is provisioned (in `public_a`), making private-subnet egress a single point of failure across AZs. | | deploy/terraform/modules/paperclip/iam.tf | Correctly scoped IAM roles for ECS instance, task execution (ECR/CWLogs/Secrets Manager), and task (SSM exec + Secrets Manager); least-privilege design looks appropriate. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: deploy/terraform/modules/paperclip/ecr.tf Line: 3 Comment: **Mutable image tags in production ECR** `MUTABLE` allows a push to silently overwrite a tag that is already running in production (e.g. `latest` or a semver tag). `IMMUTABLE` is the standard production posture — it forces a new tag per push and prevents accidental rollbacks caused by reusing a tag. ```suggestion image_tag_mutability = "IMMUTABLE" ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: deploy/terraform/vpc.tf Line: 65-70 Comment: **Single NAT Gateway is a single point of failure** Only one NAT Gateway is provisioned (in `public_a`). If `${var.aws_region}a` experiences an AZ-level disruption, private instances in `private_b` lose all IPv4 egress (ECR pulls, Secrets Manager calls, etc.). Adding a second NAT Gateway in `public_b` and a per-AZ private route table entry eliminates this dependency. For a single-instance deployment this is a cost/reliability trade-off worth documenting in the README if left as-is. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/auth/better-auth.ts Line: 114-117 Comment: **Silent rejection gives no user-facing error** Returning `false` from a better-auth `before` hook aborts the operation, but the resulting HTTP response to the client is a generic error with no indication that the domain is blocked. 3268.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a 60-second per-agent cooldown in `startNextQueuedRunForAgent` to prevent rapid-fire cascading runs caused by the `issueCommentRetry` → run-completion → `startNextQueued` feedback loop (#1241). It also removes now-unused `executionStage` payload fields, the `shouldRequireIssueCommentForWake` guard, and dead wake-reason branches. Two P1 concerns need attention before merge: - The default for `heartbeat.enabled` was quietly flipped from `false` to `true` (line 2150), which will silently activate heartbeat for any agent that doesn't explicitly set the flag — this is not mentioned in the PR description. - The cooldown is checked unconditionally in `startNextQueuedRunForAgent`, including when called from `enqueueWakeup` for user-initiated `on_demand` runs; a manual \"wake\" button press will be delayed by up to the scheduler interval (≤30 s) if a run finished in the last 60 seconds. Additionally, the PR description does not follow the required PR template from CONTRIBUTING.md. Please add the mandatory sections — **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** — before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not safe to merge as-is: silent default-on for heartbeat and unconstrained cooldown on user-triggered runs are production regressions. Two P1 findings: (1) heartbeat.enabled default flipped to true without documentation, which could activate heartbeat for agents that were never meant to run it; (2) the cooldown blocks on-demand user-initiated wakeups when a recent run exists, degrading a user-facing feature. The PR description also omits required template sections per CONTRIBUTING.md. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — specifically lines 2150 (default change) and 2471–2485 (cooldown scope).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds a 60 s per-agent cooldown in `startNextQueuedRunForAgent` to fix cascade (#1241), but also silently flips `heartbeat.enabled` default from `false` to `true` (unmentioned in description), and the cooldown applies to all call sites including user-triggered on-demand wakeups. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts`, line 2150 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/37a8bdd32fa0b1b1401abb4b334009d6f9d398e3/server/src/services/heartbeat.ts#L2150)) **Silent default-on for `heartbeat.enabled`** The default for `heartbeat.enabled` was changed from `false` to `true`, but this isn't mentioned in the PR description. Any agent whose `runtimeConfig` omits `heartbeat.enabled` will now have its heartbeat silently activated, potentially triggering runs that were never expected for those agents. This is a behavioral change independent of the cooldown fix. If enabling heartbeat by default is intentional, it should be documented in the PR description with explicit reasoning and a risk assessment.

This is a behavioral change independent of the cooldown fix. If enabling heartbeat by default is intentional, it should be documented in the PR description with explicit reasoning and a risk assessment. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Any agent whose `runtimeConfig` omits `heartbeat.enabled` will now have its heartbeat silently activated, potentially triggering runs that were never expected for those agents. This is a behavioral change independent of the cooldown fix. ```suggestion enabled: asBoolean(heartbeat.enabled, false), ``` If enabling heartbeat by default is intentional, it should be documented in the PR description with explicit reasoning and a risk assessment. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 2483-2485 Comment: **Cooldown blocks user-initiated on-demand wakeups** `startNextQueuedRunForAgent` is called from `enqueueWakeup` immediately after inserting a new run (lines ~4006 and ~4114), including for `source: "on_demand"` (UI button) triggers. The cooldown is checked unconditionally, so if a run finished within the past 60 seconds, the newly enqueued on-demand run will remain stuck in `queued` until the next `resumeQueuedRuns()` scheduler tick (default 30 s, up to 30 s delay). A user pressing the wake button would see the run sit in `queued` with no feedback that the cooldown is suppressing it. The cooldown was designed to prevent automated cascades from `issueCommentRetry`, not to throttle user-initiated runs. Consider skipping the cooldown for on-demand invocation sources: ```typescript const latestQueued = queuedRuns[0]; const isOnDemand = latestQueued?.invocationSource === "on_demand"; if (!isOnDemand && lastFinished && Date.now() - new Date(lastFinished).getTime() < MIN_GAP_MS) { return []; // Too soon — leave queued runs for the next tick } ``` The test plan also leaves this scenario unchecked ("Verify `on_demand` wakeup still works immediately when no recent runs" — "when no recent runs" specifically avoids the cooldown-active case). How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 2471-2485 Comment: **Hardcoded cooldown ignores agent's own `intervalSec`** `MIN_GAP_MS` is a fixed 60 seconds for every agent regardless of how frequently the agent is supposed to run. An agent with `intervalSec: 30` would be consistently throttled below its configured rate, while an agent with `intervalSec: 3600` gets almost no benefit since the cooldown expires long before the next scheduled tick anyway. Consider deriving the gap from the agent's policy: ```typescript const policy = parseHeartbeatPolicy(agent); // already computed below const MIN_GAP_MS = Math.min(60_000, policy.intervalSec > 0 ? policy.intervalSec * 1000 : 60_000); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Thanks for the thorough review. Addressed all three points in the latest commit (df09acb):

**1. `heartbeat.enabled` default** — This was NOT changed by this PR. Confirmed by checking `git show master:server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` — the default was already `true` in upstream master before our changes.

**2. Cooldown blocks on-demand wakeups** — Fixed. The cooldown now peeks at the next queued run's `invocationSource`. If it's `on_demand` (UI button), the cooldown is bypassed entirely. Only automated/timer-sourced runs are throttled.

**3. Hardcoded 60s ignores `intervalSec`** — Fixed. `MIN_GAP_MS` now derives from the agent's `policy.intervalSec`, capped at 60s: `Math.min(60_000, policy.intervalSec > 0 ? policy.intervalSec * 1000 : 60_000)`. Agents with 30s intervals won't be throttled below their configured rate.

3269.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes Hermes agents being unable to authenticate with the Paperclip API in authenticated deployments by injecting `ctx.authToken` as `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` into the child process environment and adding an `{{authHeader}}` template variable to all curl commands in the prompt, applied via a `pnpm patchedDependencies` patch. Two minor issues in the patch are worth addressing: the `userEnv` merge in `execute()` runs *after* setting `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY`, so any `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` present in an agent's `config.env` will silently overwrite the injected JWT; and `buildPrompt` falls back to `process.env.PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` (the *parent* process environment) when `ctx.authToken` is absent, which could embed an ambient key unexpectedly. Additionally, the PR description is missing its Thinking Path details, What Changed bullets, Verification steps, Risks, and Model Used — all required by CONTRIBUTING.md.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge after addressing the PR description gaps and considering the two P2 patch logic concerns. All findings are P2: the userEnv ordering and parent-env fallback are edge cases that don't affect the primary fix path, and the incomplete PR description is a process issue rather than a code defect. The core logic is correct, tests are solid, and the change is narrow in scope. patches/hermes-paperclip-adapter@0.2.0.patch — the userEnv override ordering and process.env fallback in buildPrompt.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | patches/hermes-paperclip-adapter@0.2.0.patch | Core fix: injects ctx.authToken as PAPERCLIP_API_KEY for the child process and adds {{authHeader}} template variable to all curl commands in the prompt; has a minor ordering issue where userEnv.PAPERCLIP_API_KEY can silently overwrite the injected JWT, and buildPrompt falls back to the parent process's PAPERCLIP_API_KEY env var unexpectedly. | | server/src/__tests__/hermes-local-execute.test.ts | Well-structured integration tests covering the three key scenarios: token injection, absence of token, and prompt template rendering; fake hermes binary pattern is clean and the finally-blocks handle cleanup correctly. | | package.json | Adds hermes-paperclip-adapter@0.2.0 to pnpm patchedDependencies so the patch is applied on install; straightforward one-liner change. | | pnpm-lock.yaml | Lockfile updated to record the patch hash for hermes-paperclip-adapter@0.2.0; auto-generated, no issues. |

Path: patches/hermes-paperclip-adapter@0.2.0.patch Line: 56-59 Comment: **`process.env.PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` fallback reads parent-process env** `buildPrompt` falls back to `process.env.PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` when `ctx.authToken` is falsy. The `env` object built in `execute()` is passed to the **child** process, not `process.env` of the current Node.js process. If an ambient `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` happens to be set in the parent environment (e.g., from a different deployment context), it will silently be embedded as a Bearer token in the rendered prompt even when no auth was intentionally requested. Consider removing the fallback or making the intent explicit with a comment. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: patches/hermes-paperclip-adapter@0.2.0.patch Line: 78-83 Comment: **`userEnv` assignment can silently override the injected auth token** `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` is set from `ctx.authToken`, but the immediately-following `Object.assign(env, userEnv)` will overwrite it if the agent's `config.env` contains its own `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY`. > This issue is a support question with no design proposal, feature request, or architectural change — there is nothing for the gardener to align or flag against the context tree.

**What the tree says about Claude auth:**
The `claude-local` adapter does not manage API keys or subscription state directly. Auth is fully delegated to the Claude CLI's own credential store (see `adapters/claude-local`). Whether a given Claude credential is subscription-based or API-credit-based is determined by the CLI — Paperclip passes through whatever auth the CLI holds.

**Context match:**

| Topic | Tree node | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Claude adapter auth model | `adapters/claude-local` | Delegation to CLI — no Paperclip-level opinion |
| Billing/quota reporting | `adapters/claude-local` | Adapter reports usage from Claude stream-json output |
| API key ownership | `adapters/claude-local` | Out of scope for Paperclip |

**Recommendation:** This is a support question best answered by a maintainer or in the project's documentation/FAQ. If the answer reveals a documentation gap (e.g., subscription vs. API-credit auth modes are undocumented), consider opening a docs issue.

No tree update needed — auth delegation to the Claude CLI is already captured in `adapters/claude-local`.

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `69913807` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. 3277.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds auto-reassignment to the issue creator when an issue transitions to `in_review`, so delegated work is automatically handed back to the delegating agent/user without requiring a manual step. - The `applyStatusSideEffects` side effect runs *after* the `assertAssignableAgent`/`assertAssignableUser` guards (lines 1624–1629), so the auto-assigned creator is never validated against termination or active membership rules — a terminated creator agent or a departed user can be silently written as the new assignee. - The dual-assignee guard at line 1618 is evaluated from pre-side-effect values and will not catch conflicts introduced by future side effects.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the missing assignability validation for auto-reassigned creators. Two P1 logic issues exist: the auto-reassign path bypasses assertAssignableAgent/User (allowing terminated agents or non-member users to be assigned), and the dual-assignee guard predates the side effects. The core feature logic and test coverage are solid, but the missing guard is a real present defect on the changed path. server/src/services/issues.ts — specifically the ordering of assignability validation relative to applyStatusSideEffects at line 1642.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds auto-reassign logic to applyStatusSideEffects on in_review transition; bypasses assertAssignableAgent/User checks used for all other assignment paths, and runs before the dual-assignee guard. | | server/src/__tests__/issues-service.test.ts | Adds 3 integration tests covering agent-creator reassign, user-creator reassign, and explicit-assignee override; tests are well-structured and cover the primary cases. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issues.ts Line: 67-77 Comment: **Auto-reassign bypasses assignability validation** `assertAssignableAgent` / `assertAssignableUser` are called at lines 1624–1629, but only for assignees provided in `issueData` (the raw request body). Because `applyStatusSideEffects` runs afterwards and writes `patch.assigneeAgentId`/`patch.assigneeUserId` directly, the creator is never validated. If the creator agent has since been `terminated` or the creator user has lost their active company membership, the auto-reassign silently writes an invalid assignee — exactly the condition those guards are meant to block. The fix is to call the guards after the side effects are applied in the `update` function right after `applyStatusSideEffects`: ```typescript applyStatusSideEffects(issueData.status, patch, existing); // Validate auto-assigned creator if (patch.assigneeAgentId && patch.assigneeAgentId !== issueData.assigneeAgentId) { await assertAssignableAgent(existing.companyId, patch.assigneeAgentId); } if (patch.assigneeUserId && patch.assigneeUserId !== issueData.assigneeUserId) { await assertAssignableUser(existing.companyId, patch.assigneeUserId); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issues.ts Line: 67-77 Comment: **Dual-assignee guard runs before auto-reassign** `nextAssigneeAgentId` and `nextAssigneeUserId` are computed at lines 1613–1616 (from `issueData` + `existing`) and validated at line 1618 *before* `applyStatusSideEffects` runs. After the side effect fires, `patch.assigneeAgentId` can be set to `createdByAgentId` while `patch.assigneeUserId` is set to `null` (or vice-versa), so the final write is safe — but the "one assignee" guard never sees the effective post-side-effect values. If a future side effect is added that sets both fields, the existing guard will not catch it. Consider moving the dual-assignee and required-assignee checks to after `applyStatusSideEffects`, reading from `patch` rather than from `issueData`/`existing`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `228d1c6` > [!CAUTION] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's > context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary The auto-reassign behaviour sits in tension with two load-bearing product principles: "Surface problems, don't hide them" and the explicit decision that automatic recovery is not a Paperclip responsibility. Whether the delegation handoff pattern is a special case worth carving out, or a slippery slope toward hidden orchestration logic, needs a deliberate call from the maintainers.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | No automatic recovery | Auto-reassign to creator when `in_review` is set — implicit side effect on a status transition | Task system: "When an agent crashes mid-task, Paperclip does not auto-reassign or auto-release the task… Recovery is manual/explicit." | ❓ Partial | | Surface problems, don't hide them | A status change silently mutates the assignee field without any actor explicitly requesting it | Product decision 6: "No silent auto-recovery, no automatic reassignment. Good auditing and visibility let the right entity decide what to do." | ⚠️ Conflict | | Tasks are the communication channel | Delegation handoff via `in_review` is a real, common pattern agents rely on | Task system: all agent communication flows through tasks; completing a sub-task and handing back is expected workflow | ✅ Aligned | | Auditable everything | No audit-log entry for the auto-reassignment is visible in the diff | Governance: every mutation writes to `activity_log`; nothing should happen silently | ❓ Partial | | Single-assignee model | Reassign to creator clears the previous assignee — no multi-assignee conflict | Task system: single-assignee is deliberate | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "No Automatic Recovery"; all work must be explicit - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Product decision 6: surface problems, don't hide them - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — "Auditable everything"; every mutation writes to activity_log - [`product/agent-model/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Cross-team coordination via tasks; delegation pattern context
### Recommendation Two questions for the maintainers before merging: 1. **Is the `in_review` handoff exempt from the "no automatic reassignment" principle?** The tree's rationale for that principle is about crash recovery hiding failures. A deliberate `in_review` transition is different in intent — but the implementation is still invisible to the task's audit trail. If this is a carve-out, document the distinction in the tree (task-system node) so future contributors don't erode it further. 2. **Is the auto-reassignment logged in the activity_log?** The diff shows the side-effect fires inside `applyStatusSideEffects` before the DB write, but it's not clear whether the resulting assignee change generates an `activity_log` entry. The governance principle is that every mutation is auditable. A silent assignee change — even a useful one — is a gap. If both questions are answered yes, the verdict would flip to `ALIGNED`. Consider updating the task-system tree node to explicitly acknowledge the `in_review` handoff pattern as a sanctioned workflow shortcut. --- Reviewed commit: 228d1c6 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3278.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR extends `goals` with an `acceptance_criteria` JSONB column and a `target_date` date column, adds a computed progress endpoint, and surfaces both in a new UI tab and progress bar. The migration is non-rewriting, all new fields are optional, and the validation layer is thorough (max-50 cap, unique id/order refinements, round-trip ISO date check). Previous review concerns around uncontrolled criterion inputs and cancelled-issue counting are both addressed. One remaining P2: the whole-array PATCH pattern in `GoalAcceptanceCriteria` reads from the React Query cache, so two rapid edits (e.g., blur on one row then immediately click required on another) can silently lose the first change. Additionally, the PR contains visible UI changes but no screenshots are included in the description.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 quality/UX suggestions that don't block correctness. Migration is additive and instant, validators are solid, the cancelled-issue and uncontrolled-input P1s from prior rounds are fixed, and the new getProgress query is properly company-scoped. The stale-snapshot race in GoalAcceptanceCriteria requires very fast consecutive edits to trigger and results in a UX annoyance rather than data corruption at rest. ui/src/components/GoalAcceptanceCriteria.tsx — stale criteria snapshot on rapid mutations

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/shared/src/validators/goal.ts | Well-structured validators: max 50 criteria, unique id/order refinements, and round-trip ISO date validation. updateGoalSchema inherits new fields via .partial(). | | server/src/services/goals.ts | getProgress query correctly scopes by companyId and excludes cancelled issues from both numerator and denominator; Number() coercion handles pg string returns. | | server/src/routes/goals.ts | GET /goals/:id now computes and attaches progress; PATCH returns plain Goal and the UI invalidates/refetches for progress — correct pattern. | | ui/src/components/GoalAcceptanceCriteria.tsx | CriterionRow local-draft pattern fixes the previous uncontrolled-input bug; however whole-array PATCH against the React Query cache means two quick edits can silently lose the first. | | ui/src/pages/GoalDetail.tsx | Progress bar and criteria tab wired correctly; defaultValue="children" preserved; goal.progress null-guard is redundant but harmless. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0055_goal_acceptance_criteria.sql | Adds acceptance_criteria jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]' and target_date date; non-rewriting on Postgres 11+ as described. | | packages/db/src/schema/goals.ts | Adds GoalAcceptanceCriterion type and two new Drizzle columns; correct use of sql template for jsonb default. | | server/src/__tests__/goals-acceptance-criteria.test.ts | 17 tests cover validator edge cases and getProgress service logic; DB stub correctly simulates Drizzle chain and verifies correct zero-handling. | | packages/shared/src/types/goal.ts | GoalAcceptanceCriterion, GoalProgress, and GoalWithProgress types added cleanly. | | packages/plugins/sdk/src/testing.ts | Test fixture updated with acceptanceCriteria: [] and targetDate: null defaults. | | ui/src/lib/onboarding-launch.test.ts | Existing goal fixtures updated to include new required fields; no logic changes. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/GoalAcceptanceCriteria.tsx Line: 97-103 Comment: **Stale criteria snapshot on concurrent edits** Each handler (`commitText`, `toggleRequired`, `remove`) derives `next` from `sorted`, which is computed from the `criteria` prop — the React Query cache value. If two mutations are in-flight simultaneously (e.g., the user blurs a text field and immediately clicks the required checkbox on a different criterion before the first PATCH round-trip completes), the second mutation will be built from the pre-first-edit snapshot and the first edit will be silently overwritten. A lightweight fix is to hold the canonical array in `useRef` so in-flight mutations always read the latest version: ```tsx const pendingRef = useRef(criteria); useEffect(() => { pendingRef.current = criteria; }, [criteria]); function commitText(id: string, text: string) { const next = pendingRef.current.map((c) => (c.id === id ? { ...c, text } : c)); pendingRef.current = next; onUpdate(next); } ``` Applying the same pattern to `toggleRequired` and `remove` keeps all handlers composable without adding optimistic-update machinery. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/pages/GoalDetail.tsx Line: 181 Comment: **Screenshots requested for visual changes** This PR introduces a progress bar and a new Acceptance Criteria tab — both visible UI changes. Could you add screenshots to the PR description? Per the contribution guidelines, visual changes should include screenshots so reviewers can verify the look and feel without running the code locally. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `medium` > [!NOTE] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary Confirmed bug that aligns with two load-bearing tree decisions. The fix as proposed is correct: the wakeup is currently misplaced in the HTTP route handler instead of the service layer, which breaks every non-HTTP caller.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Service-level wakeup | Move `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup()` into the service so all callers (HTTP + plugin host + CLI) trigger it | "Routes are thin HTTP handlers — parse request, call service, return response. Services contain all business logic." | ✅ Aligned | | Plugin parity with HTTP | Plugin `ctx.issues.create()` must have the same side effects as the REST API | "Plugin system is first-class" — plugins extend Paperclip without forking | ✅ Aligned | | Silent failure | Agent stays idle after assignment, no error surfaced | "Surface problems, don't hide them" — silent idle is the anti-pattern | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`engineering/backend/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Route/Service split: "thin HTTP handlers... Services contain all business logic" - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Capability-gated plugin access; plugin host services must honor the same invariants as core API callers - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Task assignment is the primary agent signaling mechanism
### Recommendation Rather than duplicating the wakeup call in `plugin-host-services.ts` (the proposed fix), consider moving `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup()` into `issueService.create()` and `issueService.update()` themselves. This matches the tree's explicit guidance that "services are reusable (e.g., the CLI can call service logic directly for heartbeat runs)" — any future caller (CLI, cron jobs, internal scripts) will automatically get correct behavior instead of needing to remember to call the wakeup. If there is a reason the wakeup should NOT fire for certain callers, a `{ skipWakeup: true }` option on the service is cleaner than duplicating the call everywhere it's needed. Related items #1280 and #1063 suggest a broader pattern: several heartbeat/wakeup signals live outside the services that mutate the state they respond to. Worth considering whether the whole class of "mutation → wakeup" couplings should move into the service layer as part of this fix. --- Reviewed snapshot: issue@2026-04-10T07:48 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree).
