Greptile Summary

This PR adds two deployment-related features on top of #3296: (1) root-process safety for `claude_local` — when `uid=0`, `--dangerously-skip-permissions` is suppressed and a default `--allowed-tools` list is injected so headless runs still work; and (2) an optional gateway token field for the OpenClaw adapter create flow, written to `adapterConfig.headers['x-openclaw-token']`. The PR description does not follow the mandatory PR template from `CONTRIBUTING.md`. It is missing: a **Thinking Path** (blockquote format), a **What Changed** section, a **Verification** section, a **Risks** section, a **Model Used** section (only \"Made with Cursor\" is provided — the template requires provider, exact model ID/version, and capability details), and the **Checklist**. Additionally, `config-fields.tsx` introduces a visible UI change (new token field) but no before/after screenshots are provided, which are required by CONTRIBUTING.md for UI changes.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Code changes are functionally sound but the PR description is missing all required template sections and no UI screenshots are provided — both are hard requirements per CONTRIBUTING.md before merge. All remaining P2 findings are style/quality suggestions. The score is held at 4 solely because the PR does not satisfy the mandatory PR template (missing Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) and omits screenshots for a UI change — these are explicit merge-blockers per CONTRIBUTING.md, not code correctness issues. No files need code-level attention; the blocker is the PR description and missing UI screenshots.

Security Review

 - **Plain-text token storage** (`ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx`, `packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts`): `x-openclaw-token` is written directly into `adapterConfig.headers` as plain JSON in the agent record. It is not passed through `normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` and therefore bypasses the secrets vault. The value is visible in any API response or DB backup that returns the agent config. The PR documents this trade-off explicitly, but operators should be aware. - **Broad `--allowed-tools` list injected for root runs** (`packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts`): When running as `uid=0`, Paperclip injects a pre-approved tool list (`Bash(*)`, `Write`, `WebFetch`, etc.) so headless Claude runs can proceed. This is a deliberately broad surface by design; operators are warned at startup and can tighten it via `extraArgs`.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts | New module: cleanly encapsulates root-uid detection, `dangerouslySkipPermissions` resolution, and `--allowed-tools` injection logic; well-tested via imports in execute and test paths. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts | Adds optional `openclawGatewayToken` → `adapterConfig.headers['x-openclaw-token']`; token written as plain-text JSON into agent record (acknowledged in PR, no vault normalization). | | ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx | Adds `SecretField` for gateway token on both create and edit paths; correctly migrates `x-openclaw-auth` → `x-openclaw-token`; UI change with no screenshot provided. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts | Integrates `process-defaults` functions correctly; `--allowed-tools` injection and `--dangerously-skip-permissions` skipping on root are wired up in both `buildClaudeArgs` and the probe path. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/test.ts | Probe path correctly uses `resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions` and `shouldInjectRootHeadlessAllowedTools`, aligned with execute.ts behavior. | | server/src/__tests__/claude-local-parse-rate-limit.test.ts | New unit tests for `isClaudeRateLimitedOutput` covering rate-limit event detection, error result detection, and normal success — good coverage. | | server/src/__tests__/claude-local-adapter-environment.test.ts | Adds integration test verifying exit-1 + rate-limit stream JSON is classified as `claude_hello_probe_rate_limited` warn, not a probe error — good regression coverage. | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-comment-wake-batching.test.ts | Removes assertion on `secondPayload.paperclip.wake` structure, replacing with `toBeUndefined()` + `extraSystemPrompt` checks — appears intentional protocol change from #3296. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts | Adds optional `openclawGatewayToken` to `CreateConfigValues`; backward-compatible as optional field. | | server/src/index.ts | Adds root-uid startup `logger.warn` summarizing Postgres and Claude allowlist behavior; warning is clear and actionable. | | docs/deploy/deployment-modes.md | New 'Process user: root vs non-root' section documents the trade-offs clearly with a comparison table. | | ui/src/components/agent-config-defaults.ts | Adds `openclawGatewayToken: ""` to `defaultCreateValues` — correct default, keeps the field optional without breaking existing create flows. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx Line: 138-145 Comment: **Gateway token stored as plain-text JSON** The hint correctly tells operators that `x-openclaw-token` is persisted as plain JSON in the agent record and not passed through `normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` into the secrets vault. This means the token is readable via any API endpoint or DB query that returns `adapterConfig`, and would appear in database backups in the clear. The PR acknowledges this trade-off, which is appreciated. Consider adding a one-line note in the on-edit `SecretField` (line 199) as well, since operators editing an existing agent face the same storage behaviour and currently see no hint there. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts Line: 61-67 Comment: **`isPaperclipRunningAsRoot()` called twice per invocation path** `shouldInjectRootHeadlessAllowedTools` calls `isPaperclipRunningAsRoot()` internally, and its caller (`execute.ts` / `test.ts`) has already derived `dangerouslySkipPermissions` via `resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions`, which also calls `isPaperclipRunningAsRoot()`. Each call invokes `process.getuid()` separately. Since the uid is constant for the process lifetime you could compute it once at module load time (or accept the current approach as a non-issue since it's a trivial syscall). Minor, but worth noting if this path is hot. ```suggestion // Near top of process-defaults.ts: const _isRoot: boolean = typeof process.getuid === "function" && process.getuid() === 0; export function isPaperclipRunningAsRoot(): boolean { return _isRoot; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/index.ts Line: 24 Comment: **Doc comment line is very long** The updated `dangerouslySkipPermissions` doc comment is now a single run-on sentence spanning ~300 characters. Consider wrapping it at ~100 characters for readability in editors without soft-wrap. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

<!-- gardener:state · reviewed=b3c1082123f19ececdad66bb579208a3731aca08 · verdict=ALIGNED · severity=low · tree_sha=33dc6fd046c372a3bbb88c0de72121c4c8f3df50 -->
<!-- gardener:last_consumed_rereview=none -->

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `b3c10821`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Clean adapter feature. Mirrors the existing Claude login recovery flow exactly, which is the right precedent — Paperclip control plane exposes a UI action, the adapter owns the CLI invocation using its managed `CODEX_HOME` strategy, and the `codex_auth_required` error code lets the UI react when auth is missing mid-run. No cross-adapter abstraction leaks, no governance concerns, and existing API-key support is untouched.

Tree alignment is straightforward:
- **Adapter abstraction**: authentication lifecycle lives in the adapter, not in core orchestration
- **Surface problems, don't hide them**: the new `codex_auth_required` error code is a structured signal, not a silent failure
- **Unified API**: the agent-detail UI action is a parallel of the existing Claude login button — one UI surface, adapter-specific wiring behind it

One small note: the PR description mentions `pnpm build` still fails on Windows because the repo build script uses `cp -r`. That's unrelated to this PR and worth a separate issue so it doesn't block Windows contributors.

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Minimal, well-scoped fix. Claude adapter's instruction wrapper now explicitly treats the `instructionsFileDir` as the authoritative base for sibling files like `./HEARTBEAT.md`, `./SOUL.md`, `./TOOLS.md` — closes an onboarding-time failure where fresh CEO agents tried to read siblings from the parent agent directory instead of the managed bundle directory. This is exactly the kind of adapter-owned path semantics the tree expects to live inside the adapter package, and the regression test asserts the stronger directive during the resume-to-fresh fallback path. No cross-domain impact, no bundle persistence change. Related to prior issue #3191 which flagged a similar "sibling file resolution" class of bug in the managed AGENTS.md bundle — worth the reviewer confirming both paths now resolve consistently.

3300.body: **🌱 gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `high` · commit: `4ce3604e` > [!WARNING] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. This review flags a scoping issue that should be addressed before any part of this PR lands. ### Summary The stated fix is legit — re-reading `index.html` per request instead of caching at startup closes a real black-page bug on SPA direct navigation after a UI rebuild. But **this PR is not a small fix**. It is a 74-commit, 147-file, 12,834-addition bundle that spans months of fork-internal work: adapter failure taxonomy, heartbeat/memory gates, Telegram integration, self-hosted OpenTelemetry stack, remote_trigger adapter, agent bundles (`cowork/agents/*`), new plugin examples, workflows, and more. The stated fix is ~0.1% of the actual diff. This is almost certainly a mistaken branch target. The branch name is `fix/anga-766-spa-routing-stale-index-html` but it contains commits from dozens of unrelated ANGA-* tickets. Either the author meant to open a branch with only the ANGA-766 commits, or the fork branch was never rebased onto a clean base for the upstream PR. ### Concerns 1. **Impossible to review as one PR.** 74 commits across adapter, heartbeat, plugins, observability, telegram, agent bundles, and workflows is far beyond what any reviewer can sanely evaluate in one sitting. Even if every change is individually correct, they should be reviewed independently. 2. **Potential fork-internal content leak.** The diff includes `cowork/agents/{brand-artist,career-monitor,cto,dev-agent-platform,dev-agent-products,learning-agent-2,operations-lead,visibility-agent,youtube-ingest}/` instruction bundles, Taskfile additions, `doc/AGENT-GIT-WORKFLOW.md`, `doc/PUSH_CONVENTION.md`, and a plan doc at `docs/plans/2026-04-01-claude-rate-limit-fallback-investigation.md`. Some of that looks like org-internal material (agent personas, internal workflow docs) that the author may not intend to open-source. Worth double-checking before any of this lands. 3. **Possibly conflicting changes with in-flight PRs.** This branch touches several files that are currently under review in other PRs: - `packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts` and `parse.ts` — also touched by PRs #3296, #3297, #3299 - `packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts` — also touched by #3296, #3297 - `packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/execute.ts` — also touched by #3298 - `packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts` — conflicts with ongoing gemini work Landing this as-is would create hard-to-resolve merge conflicts for multiple open PRs. 4. **Scope laundering risk.** A PR whose title claims a 9-line fix but contains 12k lines of unrelated changes defeats the "small, reviewable PRs" model and the governance principle that every change should be independently auditable. This is the exact inverse of the scoping discipline we just saw on PR #3294 → PR #3296/#3297 (which split one bundled fix into two focused PRs). ### Recommendation **Before any part of this lands**: 1. **Close this PR and open a small, focused one for the ANGA-766 fix** (re-read `index.html` per request + the matching test). That fix is legitimate and should land on its own — estimated size: 1 file, ~10 lines, plus a regression test. 2. **If the author wants to contribute the other changes upstream**, open separate PRs per logical unit: - Adapter failure taxonomy + fallback chain (ANGA-184/205) — one PR - Self-hosted OpenTelemetry observability stack — one PR (large, but architecturally distinct) - `remote_trigger` adapter type — one PR - Memory pre-flight gate (ANGA-165) — one PR, and coordinate with PR #3250 / #3289 (two active memory PRs) - Telegram integration — one PR (note: this may conflict with "Thin core, rich edges" tree guidance — telegram is likely plugin territory) 3. **Audit the diff for fork-internal content before re-opening.** Agent bundles under `cowork/agents/` and internal workflow docs may not be intended for upstream. ### Scope laundering note for gardener runbook Gardener should probably flag any PR whose diff size is dramatically larger than what the title suggests, regardless of alignment, because scope laundering is itself a governance concern. This PR is the clearest example I've seen — stated as a 9-line SPA routing fix, delivered as 12,834 lines across 74 commits. --- Reviewed commit: 4ce3604e · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

Addressed review feedback: I force-pushed a cleaned branch so this PR now contains only the ANGA-766 SPA navigation fix in server/src/app.ts. Scope creep has been removed; PR is now reviewable as a focused bugfix.

3301.body:
**🌱 gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `medium` · issue snapshot: `2026-04-10T12:58Z` > [!NOTE] > **Context Review** — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. ### Summary Clear, well-diagnosed bug. Aligns with an existing class of "tool errors treated as terminal failures" bugs in the opencode-local adapter, and the tree's "surface problems, don't hide them" principle gives a clean framing for the fix. **This is the same root cause as #3193** — worth linking so a single fix addresses both symptoms.
Context match | Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-------------|---------------|-----| | Tool-error vs run-error classification | Keep guardrail/tool errors in the agent's thought chain, not bubble as terminal failure | "Surface problems, don't hide them" — silent termination on recoverable errors is the anti-pattern | ✅ Aligned | | Adapter-owned error shaping | Fix belongs in opencode-local JSONL parser / lifecycle code | Adapters parse agent output into the shared `TranscriptEntry` types | ✅ Aligned | | Control plane boundary | Paperclip surfaces exit code; adapter decides what's terminal | "Adapters do NOT own task scheduling" but DO own "parsing agent output" | ✅ Aligned | | Self-correction loop | Agent learns from adapter error and retries within the same run | Autonomous tasks must be able to recover from recoverable errors | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) — adapter lifecycle and output parsing contract - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter abstraction, three-layer package structure, session management - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — task communication and recovery semantics
### Duplicate root cause Issue #3193 ("opencode_local treats tool_use errors as terminal adapter failures") reports the same class of bug from a different angle: tool-level errors in the OpenCode JSONL stream are being folded into the same terminal-error signal as run-level failures. This issue (#3301) is the user-facing symptom of that same misclassification, specifically for the "Read before Write" guardrail. A single fix to the JSONL parser in `packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/parse.ts` (or equivalent) should address both. Worth linking the two issues and treating them as one work item. ### Recommendation — solution ranking The reporter proposes three solutions. Ranking them by tree alignment and blast radius: 1. **Fix the JSONL parser first** (implied by #3193 but not yet done). This is the root-cause fix: tool errors stay in the transcript, only fatal adapter errors bubble up as run-level failures. Scoped to the adapter package, no cross-domain changes. Do this regardless of the other options. 2. **OpenCode server API transition** (reporter's option 1) is architecturally attractive but much larger scope — it would enable persistent sessions and finer-grained error recovery, but deserves a dedicated design discussion and tree node under `adapters/opencode-local/` describing the lifecycle shift. Not a prerequisite for fixing this bug. 3. **Retry-loop logic in Paperclip** (reporter's option 2) should be avoided — it crosses the adapter boundary. Paperclip's control plane shouldn't know about "adapter_failed" strings or special-case opencode error patterns; that's the adapter's job to classify correctly. 4. **`--session`/`--continue` flags** (reporter's option 3) is a useful supplement to option 1, but only after the parser is fixed. If the parser still classifies tool errors as terminal, CLI-level session persistence doesn't help. ### Related tree observations This is now the fifth or sixth instance of the "something silently fails instead of being recoverable" pattern I've flagged across recent PRs and issues in this gardener run (#3282, #3215, #3207, #3284, #3290, and now #3301/#3193). Worth considering whether `adapters/NODE.md` should grow a short section on error classification conventions — specifically "tool errors vs adapter errors vs run errors" — so future adapter authors have a clear rule to follow. The current split looks like each adapter invents its own boundary. --- Reviewed snapshot: issue@2026-04-10T12:58 · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd0 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

It would be best to link to the opencode server rather than use the CLI, as that is where the development is headed.
I presume it would also reduce server resources, as each agent would hook into the existing server rather than create its own.

However, this was causing me real pain.

## Temporary solution
opencode already has a built-in bypass: set `OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true` before starting the CLI.
Example:
`OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true opencode`
Or export it in your shell:
`export OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true`

What it does:
- Disables the "read before overwrite" check
- Also disables the "file changed since last read" protection

Where it lives:
- packages/opencode/src/file/time.ts:90
- packages/opencode/src/flag/flag.ts:73

## How To in Paperclip
In each agent, you can set OpenCode Environment Variables.

Greptile Summary

Fixes the URL doubling bug on company switch for plugin page routes by correcting `toCompanyRelativePath` to check the **first** path segment against `GLOBAL_ROUTE_ROOTS` and `BOARD_ROUTE_ROOTS` (rather than the second segment), so any first segment that isn't a known global or board route root is treated as a company prefix and stripped. Regression tests for plugin page routes, board routes, and global routes are included. The PR description is missing required sections from the [PR template](CONTRIBUTING.md): a **Thinking Path**, a **Risks** section, and a **Model Used** section. Please add these to the description per the contribution guidelines.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the logic fix is correct, well-tested, and scoped to two files with no side effects on other utilities. The change is a targeted two-line fix with clear reasoning, matching test coverage, and no regressions in existing tests. All remaining findings are P2 or lower. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/lib/company-routes.ts | Core logic fix: toCompanyRelativePath now checks the first segment against GLOBAL_ROUTE_ROOTS and BOARD_ROUTE_ROOTS (not the second segment) to correctly strip company prefixes from plugin page routes | | ui/src/lib/company-routes.test.ts | Regression tests added for plugin page routes, board routes, and global routes in toCompanyRelativePath; well-structured and covers the key cases | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["Fix plugin page routes doubling company ..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/6363ab3b523eeedcef01a33a3cede8912a067bd4) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=27994958)

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `6363ab3b`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Direct fix for issue #3264 by the same reporter (`@harperaa`) who filed it — nice closing of the loop. The change matches the proposed fix exactly: instead of enumerating board routes and stripping only those, the new logic strips the company prefix whenever the first segment is not a global or board route. That's the right inversion — it treats plugin page routes as first-class citizens of the routing layer without requiring every new plugin to register itself in a central enumeration, which aligns with the tree's "thin core, rich edges" stance toward plugins.

The regression tests are well-structured: three separate test cases covering plugin page routes (the bug), board routes (to prove the fix doesn't regress existing behavior), and global routes (to prove the fix doesn't over-strip). The docstring explicitly links back to issue #3264, which makes the test self-documenting for future readers.

Two-line code change, 24-line test file. Perfect scope. Once this lands, issue #3264 can be closed.

**Pocholo-agents routing decision — 2026-04-13**

Valid intake for asertu Agents. Routing to Devi-agents for a UI fix.

**Scope (per the issue's proposed fix):**
- Extract the existing clipboard fallback pattern from `CopyText` and `CommentThread.tsx` into a single shared utility at `ui/src/lib/clipboard.ts` (`copyTextToClipboard(text: string): Promise<void>`)
- Migrate the 14 direct `navigator.clipboard.writeText` call sites listed in the issue to use the shared utility
- Utility logic: `navigator.clipboard.writeText` when in a secure context; `document.execCommand('copy')` via transient textarea fallback otherwise

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. All 14 call sites in the issue's grep result work correctly when accessing asertu Agents over plain HTTP from a non-localhost hostname (LAN, Tailscale, VPN)
2. Existing `CopyText` and `CopyMarkdownButton` behavior unchanged
3. Zero `navigator.clipboard.writeText` direct calls remaining in `ui/src/` outside the new utility

**Note:** PR #3531 exists upstream — Devi-agents should review it for reference or cherry-pick if applicable.

**Priority:** Medium — affects all self-hosted users on HTTP.
**Routing:** Devi-agents, UI-only fix.

3532.body:
**Pocholo-agents routing decision — 2026-04-13**

Valid intake for asertu Agents. Routing to Devi-agents for a server feature.

**Scope (per the issue's own suggested fix):**
- In the issue PATCH route (`server/src/routes/issues.ts` or equivalent), after a status transition to `in_review`, enqueue a wakeup for `createdByAgentId` if set and different from the current assignee, using `wakeReason: "execution_review_requested"`
- Additionally, if the issue has a `parentId`, query the parent issue's current `assigneeAgentId` and enqueue a wakeup for them as well

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. When agent A delegates an issue to agent B and B sets it to `in_review`, agent A receives a heartbeat wakeup with `wakeReason: "execution_review_requested"`
2. When a child issue moves to `in_review`, the parent issue's current assignee is also woken
3. No double-wakeup when the creator and current assignee are the same agent
4. No regression in the existing `backlog → active` and `assigneeChanged` wakeup paths

**Priority:** Medium — multi-agent delegation chains stall silently at handoff without this.
**Routing:** Devi-agents, server-only change.

## Workaround (self-hosted, while waiting for upstream fix)

Until wakeup-on-in_review is implemented server-side, you can work around this by **combining the status change with an explicit reassign** in a single PATCH. The assignee change already triggers `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup`, so the reviewer is woken immediately:

\`\`\`bash
BODY=$(printf '{"status":"in_review","assigneeAgentId":"%s"}' "$REVIEWER_UUID")
curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $PAPERCLIP_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-Paperclip-Run-Id: $PAPERCLIP_RUN_ID" \
  "$PAPERCLIP_API_URL/api/issues/$PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID" \
  --data-raw "$BODY"
\`\`\`

We updated all agent `HEARTBEAT.md` instruction files to enforce this pattern, with explicit reviewer UUIDs per role (Backend/Frontend → QA, QA → CTO, CTO/CMO/UX → CEO). This eliminates the silent stall without any server change.

3540.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR replaces five hardcoded adapter-type lists scattered across server routes and UI components with declarative capability flags on `ServerAdapterModule`, exposed through `GET /api/adapters`. The implementation is clean: all registered built-in adapters gain explicit flags, the server helpers fall back to the legacy lists for adapters that don't set flags, and the UI hook sources capabilities from the API instead of denylist checks.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge — all remaining findings are P2 style/maintenance suggestions with no correctness impact. - The capability flag logic is correct, legacy fallbacks prevent any regression for unupdated adapters, and the previous P1 concern (missing instructionsFilePath default) is resolved in this diff. The only outstanding note is KNOWN_DEFAULTS in the UI hook reintroducing a hardcoded adapter list for the pre-load state. - ui/src/adapters/use-adapter-capabilities.ts — KNOWN_DEFAULTS creates a new maintenance surface worth tracking

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/adapters/registry.ts | All registered built-in adapters (claude_local, codex_local, cursor, gemini_local, opencode_local, pi_local, hermes_local, openclaw_gateway) receive explicit flags; process and http adapters are imported from separate files and correctly default to false via the ?? false fallback in buildAdapterCapabilities. | | server/src/routes/adapters.ts | Adds buildAdapterCapabilities helper and AdapterCapabilities interface; capabilities object correctly derives supportsSkills from listSkills/syncSkills presence and defaults flags to false. | | server/src/routes/agents.ts | resolveInstructionsPathKey and adapterSupportsInstructionsBundle correctly layer flag checks over legacy fallback maps; the instructionsFilePath default for supportsInstructionsBundle:true adapters without an explicit key is correctly implemented. | | ui/src/adapters/use-adapter-capabilities.ts | KNOWN_DEFAULTS reintroduces a hardcoded adapter list for the pre-load state, creating a maintenance surface that the PR is otherwise eliminating. All values appear correct for current built-ins. | | ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx | Hardcoded isLocal allowlist replaced with capabilities.supportsInstructionsBundle from the API hook; correctly scoped to the PromptsTab component. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/adapters/use-adapter-capabilities.ts Line: 17-26 Comment: **New hardcoded list partially reintroduces what the PR removes** `KNOWN_DEFAULTS` is a client-side copy of per-adapter capability data that already lives in `registry.ts`. Every time a new built-in adapter is added or an existing one's capabilities change, this object must be updated separately. A new adapter like `droid_local` (which lives in the server legacy list) is absent here, so any agent with that type would flash `ALL_FALSE` on first render before the API response arrives. The loading-flash problem could instead be solved by passing `placeholderData` or `initialData` from a server-rendered context, or by accepting a brief flash for the uncommon cold-load case — both avoid the duplicated maintenance surface. As-is, this is the only hardcoded adapter list remaining after the PR. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3543.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a 23-test auth validation matrix for `assertBoard`, `assertCompanyAccess`, and `getActorInfo`, plus agent governance files, PM2 config, and a lockfile cleanup commit. The test logic itself is accurate against the current `authz.ts` implementation, but there are two P1 issues that need resolving before merge: - The local `Actor` type in the test file introduces `\"board_key\"` as a valid `source` value, but `express.d.ts` does not include it. The test at lines 185–194 therefore exercises an impossible runtime input; either `express.d.ts` is incomplete or the test case should use `\"session\"`. - `ecosystem.config.cjs` and `.claude/commands/pm2-3100.md` / `pm2-all.md` hardcode `/Users/dev3/Desktop/paperclipai/` paths that will fail immediately for any other developer. Additionally, the PR test-plan checkbox \"No pnpm-lock.yaml changes ✓\" is factually incorrect — `pnpm-lock.yaml` is among the 14 changed files (via the `d23169ed` lockfile-cleanup commit). Per CONTRIBUTING.md, the PR description should also include a **thinking path** — covering why the approach was chosen, trade-offs considered, and any risks.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge as-is: one test case exercises an impossible source value due to Actor type drift, and machine-specific paths in ecosystem.config.cjs will break other developers. Two P1 findings: the "board_key" source value divergence between the local Actor type and express.d.ts makes a test case fictional rather than auditable, and the hardcoded /Users/dev3/ paths in ecosystem.config.cjs and two command files will fail for any other user. The test logic for the remaining 22 cases is correct and well-structured. server/src/__tests__/auth-validation-matrix.test.ts (board_key source divergence), ecosystem.config.cjs and .claude/commands/pm2-3100.md / pm2-all.md (hardcoded developer paths)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/__tests__/auth-validation-matrix.test.ts | 23-test auth validation matrix; the local Actor type silently adds "board_key" to the source union that is absent from express.d.ts, making one test case exercise a source value that cannot exist at runtime. | | ecosystem.config.cjs | PM2 ecosystem config with cwd and log paths hardcoded to /Users/dev3/, breaking startup for any other developer. | | .claude/commands/pm2-3100.md | Slash command cd path hardcoded to /Users/dev3/Desktop/paperclipai; same issue in pm2-all.md. | | .claude/scout-claude.md | Agent identity file for Scout QA lead; no issues. | | .claude/rex-claude.md | Agent identity file for Rex engineering lead; no issues. | | CLAUDE.md | Project-level Claude agent identity for Scout; no code issues. | | pnpm-lock.yaml | Lockfile updated (chore: clean lockfile for governance snapshot); PR test plan incorrectly claims no lockfile changes. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/auth-validation-matrix.test.ts Line: 21 Comment: **Local `Actor` type diverges from `express.d.ts` — `"board_key"` source value does not exist** The test's local `Actor` type adds `"board_key"` to the `source` union, but `server/src/types/express.d.ts` defines `source` as `"local_implicit" | "session" | "agent_key" | "agent_jwt" | "none"` — `"board_key"` is absent. The test at lines 185–194 constructs a request with `source: "board_key"`, which is an input that can never exist at runtime. Either `express.d.ts` is missing this value (and production auth code has an unmodelled path), or the test is exercising an impossible state. ```suggestion source?: "local_implicit" | "session" | "agent_key" | "agent_jwt" | "none"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ecosystem.config.cjs Line: 5-12 Comment: **Hardcoded developer-specific paths break PM2 startup for any other user** `cwd`, `error_file`, and `out_file` are all anchored to `/Users/dev3/`, which is the committing developer's machine. Running `pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs` on any other machine will fail with "no such file or directory" for the `cwd`. The `CLAUDE.md` already uses the tilde convention (`~/Desktop/paperclipai/`) — the ecosystem config should do the same via `process.env.HOME` or a `__dirname`-relative path. ```suggestion cwd: `${process.env.HOME}/Desktop/paperclipai/server`, error_file: `${process.env.HOME}/.paperclip/instances/default/logs/pm2-error.log`, out_file: `${process.env.HOME}/.paperclip/instances/default/logs/pm2-out.log`, ``` How can I resolve this? Any other user invoking these commands will get a "no such file or directory" error. Replace with `$HOME` so the path expands correctly for whoever runs the command. ```suggestion cd $HOME/Desktop/paperclipai && pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --only paperclip-3100 && pm2 logs paperclip-3100 ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/auth-validation-matrix.test.ts Line: 185-194 Comment: **Test uses `"board_key"` source that doesn't exist in the real type** This test case constructs a request with `source: "board_key"` — a value present in the local `Actor` type but absent from `express.d.ts`. The test will pass because `authz.ts` only checks `source !== "local_implicit"`, but it is exercising an impossible runtime state. If `"board_key"` is a real auth path, it needs to be added to `express.d.ts`; otherwise this test should use `"session"` (the existing authenticated board source). How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3544.body:
Greptile Summary

Adds a review-stage checkout rebind path so an assigned current review-participant can re-checkout an `in_review` issue from a new run and atomically rebind `executionRunId` without forcing the issue back to `in_progress`. Non-participants still receive the existing `409 Conflict`. The `test-embedded-postgres` temp-dir fix and the two new regression tests are clean additions.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; the only open finding is a narrow concurrent-retry race that is a hardening improvement, not a blocking bug. All remaining findings are P2. The core allow/block split logic is correct, the fallthrough to a 409 already protects against the race in the common case, and the regression tests cover both scenarios. server/src/services/issues.ts — the reviewCheckout UPDATE WHERE clause

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds review-stage checkout rebind path; core logic is sound but the UPDATE lacks an optimistic lock on the old executionRunId, creating a narrow concurrent-retry race. | | server/src/__tests__/issues-service.test.ts | Adds two regression tests covering the allow (assigned reviewer) and block (non-participant) checkout cases; setup/teardown mirrors existing test suites. | | packages/db/src/test-embedded-postgres.ts | Introduces a tempRoot wrapper around dataDir so the embedded-postgres instance has exclusive ownership of its directory; cleanup targets the right path in all code paths. | | docs/api/issues.md | Documents the new in_review rebind semantic; change is accurate and matches implemented behavior. | | doc/SPEC-implementation.md | Spec updated to describe the review-stage preservation invariant alongside existing checkout contract items. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issues.ts Line: 1827-1842 Comment: **Missing optimistic lock on `executionRunId` in review rebind UPDATE** The `reviewCheckout` UPDATE's WHERE clause only checks `status = "in_review"` and `assigneeAgentId = agentId`, without pinning the old `executionRunId`. If the same reviewer agent makes two concurrent checkout calls (e.g., retry after a timeout), both pass `isAssignedPendingExecutionParticipantCheckout`, and the second UPDATE blindly overwrites the first's bound `executionRunId`. Both callers receive a "success" response holding different run IDs, leaving an inconsistent ownership state. Adding an optimistic guard matches the pattern already used by the regular checkout's `executionLockCondition`. ```suggestion const reviewCheckout = await db .update(issues) .set({ executionRunId: checkoutRunId, executionLockedAt: now, updatedAt: now, }) .where( and( eq(issues.id, id), eq(issues.status, "in_review"), eq(issues.assigneeAgentId, agentId), current.executionRunId ? eq(issues.executionRunId, current.executionRunId) : isNull(issues.executionRunId), ), ) .returning() .then((rows) => rows[0] ?? null); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3547.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR improves the New Issue Dialog validation UX by adding an inline title warning, a shake animation on the submit button, an auto-dismissing amber banner, and a contextual tooltip — all good ideas. However, there is a logic bug that undermines the core UX goal: the `hasAttempted` state is set in `handleButtonClick` but is never included in the condition that renders the inline \"Issue title is required\" hint, so the red error text is visible immediately when the dialog first opens (before any user interaction). - **P1**: `hasAttempted && !title.trim()` should gate the inline hint in `NewIssueDialog.tsx` (line 1128) — the flag exists but is unused, making the error always visible on empty title. - The PR description is missing the **Risks** and **Model Used** sections required by the PR template, and no before/after screenshots are included for this visual change (both required by CONTRIBUTING.md).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until the `hasAttempted` guard is added to the inline error hint — without it the 'required' text shows on dialog open, breaking the intended UX improvement. One P1 logic bug: the `hasAttempted` flag is declared and set but never read in the JSX condition, so the inline error is always visible on an empty title rather than only after a submit attempt. Remaining findings are P2. Score is 4 rather than 5 because of the P1 issue. ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx — the inline warning condition on line 1128 needs `hasAttempted &&` prepended.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx | Adds inline validation UX (shake, warning banner, tooltip); `hasAttempted` flag is declared but never used to gate the inline error hint, so it shows immediately on dialog open — defeating the intended UX improvement. | | ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.test.tsx | Adds mocks for DialogTitle and Tooltip to keep existing tests green; no new tests cover the validation UX behavior introduced in this PR. | | ui/src/index.css | Adds `@keyframes shake` and `@keyframes fadeIn` at the bottom of the file; straightforward and correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx Line: 1128-1132 Comment: **`hasAttempted` flag is declared but never used to gate the inline warning** The "Issue title is required" hint renders immediately when the dialog opens because `hasAttempted` (which is set to `true` only inside `handleButtonClick`) is never included in the condition. On first render the title is empty, so the error text is always visible before the user has typed anything — the exact anti-pattern the PR is trying to fix. ```suggestion {hasAttempted && !title.trim() && (
Issue title is required
)} ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx Line: 1724-1741 Comment: **Shake/warning fires during pending state when title is empty** If `createIssue.isPending` is `true` and the title field is empty, clicking the button still triggers the shake animation and warning banner (since `handleButtonClick` only checks `!title.trim()`, not `isPending`). Adding a guard keeps the validation feedback from conflicting with the loading state. ```suggestion

3548.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds three additive optional fields required by the gemini-local adapter: `thinkingTokens` to `UsageSummary`, `retryDelaySec` to `AdapterExecutionResult`, and `"estimated_cost"` to both `AdapterBillingType` and `BILLING_TYPES` (with a matching UI display name). - The `normalizeLedgerBillingType` function in `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` maps raw adapter billing types to `BillingType` via an explicit `switch`; it has no case for `"estimated_cost"`, so any adapter reporting that value will have it silently stored as `"unknown"` in the ledger. This function needs a new `case \"estimated_cost\": return \"estimated_cost\"` branch.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge only after updating normalizeLedgerBillingType in heartbeat.ts to handle "estimated_cost". One P1 defect: estimated_cost is defined in the type system and constants but the heartbeat normalization function silently converts it to "unknown", making the new billing type functionally inert on the ledger side. The other two fields (thinkingTokens, retryDelaySec) are clean additive changes. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — normalizeLedgerBillingType needs a case for "estimated_cost"

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts | Adds thinkingTokens to UsageSummary, estimated_cost to AdapterBillingType, and retryDelaySec to AdapterExecutionResult — all additive, but estimated_cost is not handled in heartbeat.ts normalization | | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Adds "estimated_cost" to BILLING_TYPES constant and derived BillingType — correct additive change | | ui/src/lib/utils.ts | Adds display name mapping for estimated_cost in billingTypeDisplayName — exhaustive Record stays complete |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts Line: 42 Comment: **`estimated_cost` not handled in billing normalization** `normalizeLedgerBillingType` in `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` (lines 411–429) has an explicit `switch` for each known billing type and falls through to `return "unknown"` for anything else. Since `"estimated_cost"` is not listed as a case, any adapter that reports `billingType: "estimated_cost"` will have it silently recorded as `"unknown"` in the ledger. The new constant is effectively dead unless the normalization function is updated to pass it through. ```ts // heartbeat.ts – add this case to normalizeLedgerBillingType case "estimated_cost": return "estimated_cost"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3549.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a startup catch-up mechanism for the automatic database backup scheduler: on server start, if no `paperclip-*.sql` / `paperclip-*.sql.gz` backup exists or the newest backup is older than the configured interval, a one-off backup runs before the regular interval takes over. The implementation correctly sets `backupInFlight = true` synchronously (before the first `await`) so the in-flight guard works even when the first scheduled tick fires during a slow startup backup.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — no blocking issues found. All findings are P2 or lower. The startup-decision logic, overlap guard, retention lookup, and error handling are all correct and consistent with the existing codebase. Tests cover stale, fresh, missing, and overlap cases with accurate timing assertions. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/database-backup-scheduler.ts | New scheduler module — startup decision logic, overlap guard, and interval setup are all correct; synchronous I/O (readdirSync/statSync) only runs once at startup. | | server/src/__tests__/database-backup-scheduler.test.ts | Good coverage of stale, fresh, missing, and overlap cases; mock timing and backupInFlight guard assertions are accurate. | | server/src/index.ts | Existing backup block cleanly replaced with startDatabaseBackupScheduler call; behavior preserved, return value discarded intentionally (no unhandled-rejection risk since runAutomaticBackup always resolves). | | doc/DEVELOPING.md | Accurate one-paragraph description of the new startup catch-up behaviour added in the right section. | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["DIG-510 document backup catch-up"](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/319b8952222c9ec5d0f45bd6f105ab33fc4cdf57) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=28161871)

3551.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR refactors the Slack sync plugin so that each issue's main channel message contains only the title (pinned as an anchor), with full details and subsequent updates posted as thread replies. It also registers the package in the root vitest workspace and adds a new test suite that covers the main event flows. The PR description is missing a **thinking path** and the "what/why/benefits/risks" breakdown required by [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md). Please add those before merging. Additionally, since this changes the visible format of Slack messages, before/after screenshots of the new message layout would be helpful per the contributing guide.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated; all remaining code findings are minor style/quality suggestions that do not block correctness. All inline findings are P2 (missing devDependency declaration, tsconfig include gap, fragile composite key). The logic changes are well-tested by the new spec and the implementation is straightforward. No data-loss or reliability risks were identified. packages/plugins/slack-sync/package.json and tsconfig.json have minor housekeeping gaps (missing vitest dep, tests/ not type-checked).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/plugins/slack-sync/src/worker.ts | Main logic change: posts title-only anchor + pin on issue.created, moves full details to thread; issue.updated only updates main message on title change; minor fragility with colon-delimited composite state key. | | packages/plugins/slack-sync/tests/worker.spec.ts | New test file with good coverage of the new threading behaviour; vitest is not declared as a devDependency so resolution relies on root-level hoisting. | | packages/plugins/slack-sync/package.json | Missing vitest devDependency despite new test file; otherwise correct. | | packages/plugins/slack-sync/tsconfig.json | `include` only covers `src/`, so `tests/worker.spec.ts` is not type-checked by tsc. | | vitest.config.ts | Adds `packages/plugins/slack-sync` to the vitest workspace projects list; looks correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/slack-sync/tsconfig.json Line: 8 Comment: **`tests/` excluded from type-checking** `"include": ["src"]` means `tests/worker.spec.ts` is never checked by `tsc`. Type errors in the test file will be silently skipped when running `pnpm typecheck`. Consider extending the include list so test imports are validated alongside source code. ```suggestion "include": ["src", "tests"] ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/slack-sync/package.json Line: 22-25 Comment: **`vitest` missing from devDependencies** `tests/worker.spec.ts` imports from `vitest`, but `vitest` is not listed as a `devDependency` for this package. In strict pnpm workspaces (`hoist=false` or `public-hoist-pattern` restrictions) the tests will fail to resolve `vitest` without an explicit declaration. Adding it makes the package self-contained and avoids subtle breakage if the root-level hoisting policy changes. ```suggestion "devDependencies": { "@types/node": "^24.6.0", "typescript": "^5.7.3", "vitest": "workspace:*" } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. 3553.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a \"done\" notification that posts a `✅ {identifier} {title} 已完成` reply to the originating Slack thread when an issue transitions to `done`, reading `sourceChannel` and `sourceThreadTs` from `issue.metadata` via type assertion. - **P1 — spurious re-notification**: The `statusChangedToDone` guard in `worker.ts` (line 439) evaluates `_previous.status !== \"done\"`. When `_previous` is a non-null object that simply doesn't carry a `status` key (e.g., only a title changed), the expression resolves to `undefined !== \"done\"` → `true`, causing a duplicate done-notification to fire for any subsequent update to an already-done issue. - The PR description is missing the \"thinking path\" required by CONTRIBUTING.md (a brief narrative tracing from the project goal down to this specific change, plus what was done, why it matters, how to verify, and any risks).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge as-is — the P1 guard bug can cause duplicate done-notifications on any subsequent update to an already-done issue. One P1 logic bug: the `statusChangedToDone` condition fires when `_previous` is present but lacks a `status` key, leading to repeated Slack notifications. The fix is a one-liner. The P2 missing try/catch is a hardening improvement but not blocking. packages/plugins/slack-sync/src/worker.ts — lines 439–441 (guard condition) and 448–459 (missing error handling)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/plugins/slack-sync/src/worker.ts | Adds done-thread notification logic; contains a P1 guard bug where absent `_previous.status` causes spurious re-notifications, and lacks error handling around the new `postMessage` call. | | packages/plugins/slack-sync/tests/worker.spec.ts | Adds three new tests for the done-notification path; missing a test case for `_previous` without a `status` key, which would have caught the P1 bug. | | vitest.config.ts | Adds `packages/plugins/slack-sync` to the vitest workspace projects list. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/slack-sync/src/worker.ts Line: 439-441 Comment: **Spurious done-notification when `_previous` omits `status`** If the event payload carries a `_previous` object that doesn't include a `status` key (e.g., only a title change: `{ title: "Old" }`), then `(payload._previous as ...).status` is `undefined`, and `undefined !== "done"` is `true`. Combined with an issue already in `"done"` state, `statusChangedToDone` evaluates to `true` and the `✅` notification fires again. The third test ("already done") covers the case where `_previous` explicitly includes `status: "done"`, but it doesn't cover the far more common case where `_previous` only tracks the actually-changed fields. ```suggestion const prevStatus = (payload._previous as Record)?.status; const statusChangedToDone = issue.status === "done" && typeof prevStatus === "string" && prevStatus !== "done"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/slack-sync/src/worker.ts Line: 448-459 Comment: **No error handling around source-thread notification** If `postMessage` to the source Slack channel fails (channel archived, bot removed, token revoked), the exception propagates out of the `issue.updated` handler entirely. The existing code wraps every potentially-failable Slack call in `try/catch` (e.g., `syncContextPin`, `pinMessage`). A failure here would prevent the normal thread-update message (posted earlier in the same handler) from being logged as successful, and the event may be retried and fire the notification a second time once the preceding logic is fixed. ```suggestion try { await client.postMessage( sourceChannel, `✅ ${(issue as unknown as { identifier: string }).identifier} ${issue.title} 已完成`, { threadTs: sourceThreadTs }, ); ctx!.logger.info("issue.updated → done notification sent to source thread", { issueId, sourceChannel, sourceThreadTs, }); } catch (e) { ctx!.logger.warn("issue.updated: done notification to source thread failed", { issueId, sourceChannel, sourceThreadTs, error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), }); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR updates the hardcoded SHA256 checksum for the GitHub CLI APT keyring in the Dockerfile, following GitHub's official PGP signing key rotation on April 8, 2026 ([changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-04-08-new-pgp-signing-key-for-github-cli-linux-packages/)). The one-line code change is correct and addresses the broken Docker builds reported in issues #3219 and #3358. However, the PR description does not follow the required PR template from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` as specified in `CONTRIBUTING.md`. The following required sections are missing: - **Thinking Path** — a blockquote-style reasoning trace from the project level down to this specific change - **Risks** — even \"Low risk\" is required by the template - **Model Used** — must specify provider, exact model ID/version, and relevant capability details (the footer \"Generated with Claude Code\" is not sufficient) - **Checklist** — the verification checklist from the template Please fill out the full PR template before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 4/5

The code change is correct and safe to merge, but the PR is missing required template sections per CONTRIBUTING.md. The one-line SHA256 hash update is accurate and fixes a real build breakage caused by GitHub's official keyring rotation. However, the PR description omits required fields from the PR template (Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used with version details, Checklist) that CONTRIBUTING.md marks as mandatory for all PRs. Score is 4/5 until those sections are filled in. No files require special attention — the Dockerfile change itself is straightforward and correct.

3557.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes UTF-8 encoding across three I/O layers on Windows: forces `client_encoding=UTF8` on all PostgreSQL sessions, adds a global Express middleware to set `res.charset`, and injects `PYTHONUTF8`/`PYTHONIOENCODING` into win32 agent child processes. The DB and agent-env changes are clean and correct; the only minor gap is that two HTML fallback routes (`static` and `vite-dev`) call `res.end()` directly and therefore bypass the new charset middleware, leaving their `Content-Type` headers without `; charset=utf-8`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all remaining findings are minor style suggestions that don't affect correctness or data integrity. The DB and agent-env changes are straightforward and correct. The only issues are P2 style suggestions about two HTML routes that call `.end()` and miss the charset declaration — browsers handle this gracefully via `` tags and the UTF-8 default, so there is no functional regression. server/src/app.ts — two HTML routes bypass the new charset middleware.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/db/src/client.ts | Adds `client_encoding: "UTF8"` to both `createUtilitySql` and `createDb` postgres connection options — correct, low-risk change that forces UTF-8 negotiation regardless of the Windows system code page. | | server/src/app.ts | Adds a global `res.charset = "utf-8"` middleware, but the two HTML fallback routes use `res.end()` directly rather than `res.send()`, so `res.charset` is not consulted and those responses still lack `; charset=utf-8` in their Content-Type header. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts | Injects `PYTHONUTF8=1` and `PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8` into agent child-process environments on win32 — targeted, correct, and guarded by the platform check. |

Comments Outside Diff (2)

 1. `server/src/app.ts`, line 268-270 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/390354c75727c839c879040260f4b1a186fec402/server/src/app.ts#L268-L270)) **`res.charset` bypassed by `.end()` in HTML routes** `res.charset` is only consulted by Express's `res.send()` / `res.json()` methods when they build the `Content-Type` header. Calling `.end()` directly (here and in the `vite-dev` path at line 300) bypasses that mechanism, so the response is sent as `Content-Type: text/html` without `; charset=utf-8` — partially defeating the purpose of the new middleware. Switching to embedding the charset in the header directly would make these two routes consistent with the rest of the API:

3562.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a short snippet to `doc/DOCKER.md` explaining how to run the onboard step after starting Paperclip via Docker Compose, since the default setup has no TTY. There is one P1 issue with the `npm run` command, and the PR description is missing required sections per `CONTRIBUTING.md` (Thinking Path, What Changed, Verification, Risks, and Model Used all contain unfilled placeholders — please complete them before requesting merge).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge as-is — the documented command won't run the onboard subcommand, and the PR description template is largely unfilled. There is one P1 finding: the missing `--` separator means `npm run paperclipai onboard` silently does nothing useful. The PR description is also missing all required template sections per CONTRIBUTING.md. doc/DOCKER.md — verify the corrected npm run command and consider aligning with the `npx paperclipai onboard` style used elsewhere in the file.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | doc/DOCKER.md | Adds a two-step `docker exec` + `npm run paperclipai onboard` snippet for first-time Docker Compose setup, but the `npm run` command is missing the `--` separator needed to pass `onboard` to the script. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: doc/DOCKER.md Line: 66 Comment: **Missing `--` to pass argument to npm script** `npm run paperclipai onboard` does not pass `onboard` to the script — npm requires `--` to forward extra arguments. Without it, the command runs `node cli/node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs cli/src/index.ts` with no subcommand, which won't trigger the onboard flow. Note that the "Onboard Smoke Test" section also uses `npx paperclipai onboard --yes` for consistency. ```suggestion npm run paperclipai -- onboard ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3563.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a production-observed bug where a routine dispatch fails with a unique constraint violation (`issues_open_routine_execution_uq`) when a prior execution issue was left open after its heartbeat run finished. It introduces `findStrandedOpenExecutionIssue` as a pre-check fallback (for non-`always_enqueue` policies) and also widens the constraint catch handler to coalesce into the stranded issue rather than propagating the error.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is narrowly scoped, correctly guarded by `concurrencyPolicy`, and backed by a passing regression test. No P0 or P1 findings. The new `findStrandedOpenExecutionIssue` query mirrors the filter conditions of `findLiveExecutionIssue`, the `??` null-coalescing is only invoked for non-`always_enqueue` policies in the pre-check (matching the existing coalescing guard), the constraint catch handler is already guarded by the `always_enqueue` throw above it, and return types are compatible across both call sites. The regression test faithfully reproduces the production scenario. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/routines.ts | Adds `findStrandedOpenExecutionIssue` and threads it into the pre-check and constraint catch handler; logic and types are consistent with the existing `findLiveExecutionIssue` pattern. | | server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts | Adds a well-structured regression test that seeds a finished heartbeat run, confirms `activeIssue` is null before the fix's path, and verifies the dispatch coalesces instead of failing. | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(routines): coalesce into stranded ex..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/29107dfc0051c86fa7f6c722579fbebef239c41d) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=28180185)

3568.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR cancels an agent's active heartbeat run when an external actor transitions an issue to `done` or `cancelled`, preventing orphaned Claude processes. The implementation correctly mirrors the existing interrupt pattern and the `resolveActiveIssueRun` helper is reused cleanly. - The PR description is missing the required template sections from CONTRIBUTING.md: **Thinking Path** (blockquoted reasoning chain), **Risks**, **Model Used**, and the **Checklist**. Please fill out the full PR template before merging. - The actor guard `actor.actorType !== \"agent\"` is slightly over-broad — see the inline comment on line 1836 for a more precise fix.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the actor-check edge case and filling out the required PR template sections. The core bug fix is correct and the implementation follows existing patterns. One P2 logic edge case exists (cross-agent run orphan) and the PR template is incomplete per CONTRIBUTING.md — both should be addressed before merge. server/src/routes/issues.ts — actor type guard on line 1836

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds run cancellation on terminal issue status transitions for non-agent actors; actor-type guard is slightly over-broad for multi-agent scenarios. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 1836 Comment: **Actor check is overly broad for multi-agent scenarios** `actor.actorType !== "agent"` skips cancellation whenever *any* agent makes the update, not just the agent assigned to the issue. If Agent B externally marks Agent A's issue as `done` or `cancelled`, Agent A's run will be left orphaned — the same leak this PR is fixing for board users. The condition should only skip cancellation when the *acting* agent is the same as the *assigned* agent: ```suggestion if (becameTerminal && !(actor.actorType === "agent" && actor.actorId === existing.assigneeAgentId)) { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3569.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes the lossy cron round-trip regression (e.g., `0 9,13,17 * * *` collapsing to `0 10 * * *`) by rewriting `parseCronToPreset` to route any expression it can't losslessly represent to the `custom` preset, and redesigns the triggers tab around a card list + modal workflow. The core correctness fix and the new test coverage are solid.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — no data loss, API, or schema impact; all remaining findings are P2 display-layer nits. The core correctness fix (lossy cron round-trip) is properly implemented and covered by regression tests. Both open findings are P2: one is an incomplete human-readable description for an edge-case cron shape that never reaches the editor (no data affected), and the other is a 12h/24h format inconsistency on the empty-cron fallback path. Neither blocks merge. ui/src/components/ScheduleEditor.tsx — minor display issues in describeSchedule

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/ScheduleEditor.tsx | Major rewrite: adds rich EditorPreset/EditorState system, fixes lossy cron parsing, and extends describeSchedule — one minor display gap in the hourly-step + non-standard dow path. | | ui/src/components/ScheduleEditor.test.ts | New test file covering regression crons, round-trip preservation, describeSchedule output, and parseCronToPreset; no issues found. | | ui/src/components/TriggerDialog.tsx | New dialog component for add/edit trigger; correctly gates webhook kind creation, resets draft on open, and builds distinct create vs PATCH payloads. | | ui/src/components/TriggerListCard.tsx | New card component rendering trigger summary, next-run/last-fired metadata, enable switch, and action buttons; straightforward and clean. | | ui/src/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx | Reusable confirmation dialog with busy/destructive state; clean and complete. | | ui/src/pages/RoutineDetail.tsx | Replaces inline trigger form with modal-driven flow (TriggerDialog + ConfirmDialog), moves tabs into PropertiesPanel, and wires all trigger mutations correctly. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/ScheduleEditor.tsx Line: 461-465 Comment: **Incomplete description for `*/N hours` with non-standard day-of-week** The `dayPart` here only appends ", weekdays" for `dow === "1-5"` but silently drops any other day restriction. For example, `0 */4 * * 0,6` (every 4 hours on weekends) would display as **"Every 4 hours at :00"** — omitting the weekend-only constraint entirely. Since `parseCronToEditorState` correctly routes such crons to the `custom` preset, no data is lost, but the card summary in `TriggerListCard` would be misleading to the operator. ```suggestion if (minutes.length === 1 && hourStep && dom === "*") { let dayPart = ""; if (dow === "1-5") dayPart = ", weekdays"; else if (dow === "0,6" || dow === "6,0") dayPart = ", weekends"; else if (dow !== "*") { const selectedDays = daysOfWeek.map((d) => DAY_NAMES_SHORT[d]).join(", "); dayPart = `, ${selectedDays} only`; } return `Every ${hourStep[1]} hours at :${pad(minutes[0])}${dayPart}`; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/ScheduleEditor.tsx Line: 427 Comment: **Inconsistent time format in empty-cron fallback** This path returns `"Every day at 10:00 AM"` (12h format), while every other description in the same function uses 24h format (e.g., `"Every day at 09:00"`). The mismatch is visible in the trigger card summary when a trigger has an empty cron expression. ```suggestion if (!cron || !cron.trim()) return "Every day at 10:00"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3569.body:
Addressed the remaining Greptile summary items in `19c67a8d`.

- `ScheduleEditor`: the hour-window controls now prevent inverted ranges by clamping the end hour and disabling invalid end-hour choices.
- `TriggerDialog`: `replayWindowSec` is now a numeric input and is sanitized before submit so malformed values do not reach the API payload.
- `RoutineDetail`: the properties-panel tabs are now memoized and the panel effect depends on that memoized node, so the stale-closure risk from the suppressed `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` warning is gone.

Verification run:
- `pnpm exec vitest run ui/src/components/ScheduleEditor.test.ts`
- `pnpm typecheck`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test:run` hit one unrelated flaky timeout in `server/src/__tests__/pi-local-execute.test.ts`; isolated rerun passed immediately.
@greptileai addressed the open findings in `19c67a8d` and resolved the active review threads. Please re-review when ready.

3571.body:
## Fix applied

Updated all agent `TOOLS.md` and `HEARTBEAT.md` instruction files (8 files total) to use the correct route:

\`\`\`bash
# Correct
POST $PAPERCLIP_API_URL/api/issues/$ISSUE_ID/comments

# Wrong (was documented, returns 404)
POST $PAPERCLIP_API_URL/api/companies/$PAPERCLIP_COMPANY_ID/issues/$ISSUE_ID/comments
\`\`\`

Also worth noting: agents using `bash -lc` to run shell commands should avoid capturing stderr with `2>&1` on comment POSTs — any noise on stderr (e.g. from `.profile`) can cause them to misinterpret a successful response as an error.

3572.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds `isClaudeGhostResumeResult` to detect resumed sessions that silently succeed with zero output tokens (a known quirk of Ollama/local model backends) and attempts to clear the stale session so the next heartbeat starts fresh. - **P1 — Session is never actually cleared:** `toAdapterResult(initial, { fallbackSessionId: null, clearSessionOnMissingSession: true })` computes `clearSession` as `Boolean(clearSessionOnMissingSession && !resolvedSessionId)`. Because the ghost-resume response still contains a valid `session_id`, `resolvedSessionId` is non-null, `clearSession` stays `false`, and the old session ID is propagated to the next heartbeat — causing an infinite ghost-resume loop. The fix should either retry with a fresh session (like `isClaudeUnknownSessionError` does) or add an explicit `clearSession: true` override to `toAdapterResult`'s opts.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge as-is — the session-clearing logic does not fire when the ghost-resume response includes a valid session ID, leaving the fix ineffective. A P1 logic bug means the primary goal of the PR (clearing the stale session) is never achieved: clearSession stays false because the ghost-resume result still carries a non-null session ID. Additionally, the PR description does not follow the required CONTRIBUTING.md template (missing Thinking Path, What Changed, Verification, Risks, Model Used, and Checklist sections). The detection function in parse.ts is correct and well-structured, so the fix is close — it just needs the toAdapterResult call corrected. packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts — the ghost-resume branch needs the session-clearing mechanism fixed.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts | Adds ghost-resume detection and session-clearing logic, but the toAdapterResult call does not actually set clearSession=true when the ghost run returns a valid session ID — the session is never cleared, producing an infinite ghost-resume loop. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts | New isClaudeGhostResumeResult helper correctly checks subtype, output_tokens, and result text. Minor: the resumedSession parameter is always true at the call site. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 634-641 Comment: **Session not actually cleared on ghost resume** `toAdapterResult` computes `clearSession` as `clearSessionForMaxTurns || Boolean(opts.clearSessionOnMissingSession && !resolvedSessionId)`. In a ghost resume, the model returns exit code 0 with a valid `session_id` in its output, so `resolvedSessionId` is non-null — meaning `!resolvedSessionId` is `false`, and `clearSession` stays `false`. The caller will therefore preserve the ghost session ID and pass it into the next heartbeat, which will hit the same ghost resume again. This produces an infinite loop of ghost resumes instead of breaking out. The `clearSessionOnMissingSession: true` flag only fires when the session ID is absent from the result, which is not the case here. The simplest fix is to retry with a fresh session (matching the `isClaudeUnknownSessionError` pattern) so the returned result has no old session ID to preserve: ``` // instead of returning initial, retry fresh and clear the stale session const retry = await runAttempt(null); return toAdapterResult(retry, { fallbackSessionId: null, clearSessionOnMissingSession: true }); ``` Alternatively, extend `toAdapterResult`'s `opts` to accept an explicit `clearSession: true` override. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts Line: 191-204 Comment: **`resumedSession` parameter is always `true` at the call site** The only call site is `isClaudeGhostResumeResult(initial.parsed, true)`, with `resumedSession` hard-coded to `true`. The outer `sessionId &&` guard in `execute.ts` already ensures this code only runs in a resumed-session context, making the parameter redundant. If the function is exported for reuse elsewhere, callers could mistakenly pass `false` and suppress the guard. Consider removing the parameter and moving the guard into the function body, or at minimum adding a note in the JSDoc. ```suggestion export function isClaudeGhostResumeResult( parsed: Record | null, ): boolean { if (!parsed) return false; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 629-641 Comment: **Missing tests for the ghost resume detection** There are no unit tests for `isClaudeGhostResumeResult` or integration coverage of the ghost-resume branch in `execute.ts`. Given that this fix targets a tricky edge case (silent success with zero tokens), a few test cases covering the detection logic — and distinguishing it from legitimate zero-output scenarios — would make the fix safer to rely on and easier to verify in CI. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3573.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a three-state theme toggle (`dark → light → system`) with OS `prefers-color-scheme` support. The `ThemeContext` logic is sound, but the toggle button icons for the `light` and `system` preference states are swapped: `light` shows `Moon` (implying dark next) while clicking actually goes to `system`, and `system` shows `Laptop` while clicking actually goes to `dark`. - The PR description is missing the required **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Verification/Risks** sections from the PR template, and **before/after screenshots** for this UI change — please fill out the full PR template per CONTRIBUTING.md.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the swapped icon mappings for the light and system states. Two P1 logic bugs exist: the icons shown in the toggle button for `light` and `system` preferences are swapped, causing the visual indicator to contradict what clicking will actually do. The ThemeContext logic itself is correct. Once the one-line icon fix is applied in both mobile and desktop branches, the PR is ready. ui/src/components/Layout.tsx — icon/state mismatch at lines 362 and 435.

Important Files Changed
