3584.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a central `[REDACTED]` credential-redaction guardrail to run logs by extending `log-redaction.ts` with regex patterns for env-style assignments, JSON/header properties, `Authorization: Bearer` values, and JWT-like strings, then applies it at every heartbeat write path (log chunks, excerpts, errors, result JSON, read-responses) and at the local NDJSON write boundary in `run-log-store.ts`. It also bundles a stale-execution-lock sweep, a new PID-based instance server lock, the `plugin-board-notify` email plugin, new `engineer` and `pm` onboarding bundles, and a security fix in `MarkdownBody.tsx`. - **`srv.service` should not be committed**: it hardcodes `User=exedev` and absolute `/home/exedev/...` paths; the project's own `pnpm check:tokens` tool flags this file and it belongs in `.gitignore` or as a `.template` file.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after removing or templating srv.service; all other changes are well-tested and low-risk. One P1 issue: srv.service with hardcoded deployment-specific user/paths is committed and flagged by the project's own token checker — it should be removed or converted to a template before merging. The core credential-redaction logic is correct and well-tested. Remaining findings are P2 (JWT regex edge case, redundant double-redaction in the store). srv.service (should not be in the repo); server/src/log-redaction.ts (JWT regex word-boundary edge case)

Security Review

 - **`srv.service`** — commits machine-specific `User=exedev` and absolute home-directory paths into source control; exposes deployment environment details and is flagged by the project's own token-scan tool. - **JWT regex edge case** — `JWT_LIKE_VALUE_RE` uses `\\b` but allows `-` in its character class; JWT segments ending with `-` won't be matched, creating occasional false negatives (low practical impact since most real JWTs end in alphanumeric characters). - All other credential-redaction patterns reviewed: `Authorization: Bearer`, env-style assignments, and JSON property patterns are correctly implemented and tested.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/log-redaction.ts | Adds redactRunLogCredentialsText/redactRunLogCredentialsValue with regex patterns for env assignments, JSON properties, Bearer tokens, and JWT-like values; JWT regex has a word-boundary edge case with segments ending in -. | | server/src/services/run-log-store.ts | Adds credential redaction in the append method before writing NDJSON to disk; creates redundant double-redaction when called from heartbeat.ts which pre-sanitizes chunks. | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Wraps all run-log write paths (log chunks, excerpts, errors, result JSON, read responses) with credential redaction; also adds stale execution-lock sweep on startup and improves releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote error logging. | | srv.service | New file with hardcoded machine-specific deployment config (User=exedev, absolute home paths); flagged by pnpm check:tokens and should not be committed to the shared repo. | | packages/plugins/plugin-board-notify/src/worker.ts | New email-notification plugin using Resend; secrets resolved via refs, HTML escaped correctly; DEFAULT_CONFIG hardcodes developer-specific email addresses that operators will need to reconfigure. | | server/src/services/instance-server-lock.ts | New PID-based instance server lock preventing duplicate server starts; stale locks are detected and reclaimed; clean implementation with well-tested happy and error paths. | | ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.tsx | Adds target=_blank and upgrades rel to noopener noreferrer on both mention-chip and general markdown links — a straightforward security improvement. | | server/src/__tests__/log-redaction.test.ts | Adds regression tests for env-style assignments, Bearer tokens, JWTs, quoted secrets, multiline env dumps, and false-positive guard for TOKENIZER_MODEL. | | server/src/__tests__/run-log-store.test.ts | New test verifies credential-shaped values are redacted in persisted NDJSON chunks before they can be read back from the local log store. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: srv.service Line: 3-16 Comment: **Hardcoded deployment config should not be committed to the repo** This file contains machine-specific credentials and paths (`User=exedev`, `WorkingDirectory=/home/exedev/paperclip`, `Environment=HOME=/home/exedev`, etc.) that should not live in source control. The PR description itself notes that `pnpm check:tokens` explicitly flagged this file for local username/home-path entries. Committing it leaks operational details about the deployment environment and will break anyone who checks out the repo expecting to use the file as-is. If a service unit is needed for reference, the file should either be `.gitignore`d or provided as a template (e.g., `srv.service.template`) with placeholder values stripped. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/run-log-store.ts Line: 116 Comment: **Redundant double-redaction of log chunks** When `onLog` in `heartbeat.ts` calls `store.append`, it already passes an explicitly sanitized `sanitizedChunk` (the result of `redactRunLogCredentialsText(redactCurrentUserText(chunk, ...))`). The store then calls `redactRunLogCredentialsText` a second time on the same, already-redacted value — a no-op in the normal code path. This is harmless today (a second pass on already-redacted text is idempotent), but it splits ownership ambiguously: if a future caller removes the heartbeat-side redaction assuming "the store handles it," excerpts and live events become unprotected; conversely, if the store's redaction is removed assuming "the caller handles it," the on-disk NDJSON is unprotected. Consider documenting clearly which layer is the authoritative redaction boundary (or add a comment noting this is intentional belt-and-suspenders) so the invariant is explicit. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/log-redaction.ts Line: 29-30 Comment: **JWT regex `\b` boundary fails on segments ending with `-`** The character class `[A-Za-z0-9_-]` includes `-`, which is **not** a `\w` character. `\b` asserts a boundary between a `\w` char and a `\W` char — so if the last character of a matched segment is `-`, the trailing `\b` will only match if the following character is a word character, which is rarely the case (space, newline, comma typically follows a JWT). Any JWT whose final segment ends in `-` will be silently skipped. Replacing the leading/trailing `\b` with negative lookahead/lookbehind over the full character class avoids the mismatch: ```typescript const JWT_LIKE_VALUE_RE = /(? 3584.body:
CTO review: changes requested before merge.

The core run-log redaction direction looks aligned with DIG-534, but this PR is not currently mergeable as a scoped security guardrail:

- Remove `srv.service` from this PR, or replace it with a safe template that uses placeholders only. The current file is machine-specific deployment config and is already causing `pnpm check:tokens` to fail.
- Narrow the PR to the DIG-534 redaction guardrail and focused tests. The current 25-file diff includes unrelated plugin, onboarding, server lock, stale lock sweep, Markdown link, and dev-runner changes. Move those to separate issues/PRs unless one is strictly required for the redaction fix and documented as such.
- Fix the JWT-like redaction boundary issue called out by review: avoid `\b` around a character class that includes `-`, and add a fake-value regression test for a token-shaped string whose final segment ends in `-`.
- Make the persistence boundary invariant explicit. If heartbeat and run-log-store redaction are intentionally belt-and-suspenders, add a brief comment or test naming that invariant so future maintainers do not remove one side and leave either DB excerpts/live events or on-disk NDJSON unprotected.
- Re-run the focused tests, typecheck/build, and `pnpm check:tokens` after the scope cleanup.

Do not include real credentials or real env dumps in any follow-up test, commit, PR body, or issue comment.

Note: I attempted a formal GitHub review and connector issue comment first, but the GitHub integration returned 403 for those write endpoints, so I am posting via the authenticated GitHub CLI fallback.

Addressed the CTO review feedback for DIG-534: force-pushed the branch onto upstream master so the PR is narrowed to the five redaction files, removed the unrelated srv.service/fork-only diff from PR scope, fixed the JWT-like boundary to avoid word-boundary handling around hyphen characters, added the fake trailing-hyphen token regression, documented the heartbeat primary boundary vs run-log-store defense-in-depth redaction, and reran the requested checks. Focused redaction tests, pnpm -r typecheck, pnpm build, and pnpm check:tokens all pass.

CTO review: changes requested before merge.

The scoped diff and CI are now clean, and the earlier unrelated-file/JWT-boundary issues are addressed. I found one remaining security blocker:

- server/src/services/heartbeat.ts: the normal adapter-failure path computes persistedError and uses it for heartbeat_runs, wakeup status, and task-session lastError, but still calls updateRuntimeState(agent, finalizedRun, adapterResult, ...). updateRuntimeState writes agent_runtime_state.lastError from result.errorMessage ?? null, so an adapter error containing PAPERCLIP_API_KEY=..., Authorization: Bearer ..., or JWT-shaped text can still be persisted raw outside the run-log/run-status path.
- server/src/services/heartbeat.ts: the outer setup-failure catch currently derives message directly from outerErr.message and then writes that raw message to setRunStatus, setWakeupStatus, and appendRunEvent. That is another run-status/timeline persistence path that bypasses the new credential redactor.

Please sanitize both paths before resubmitting. Suggested direction: make the outer catch use the same redactRunLogCredentialsText(redactCurrentUserText(...)) pipeline, and make updateRuntimeState receive/store a sanitized last-error value rather than raw adapterResult.errorMessage while preserving the raw adapter result fields needed for usage/billing/model accounting.

Please add regression coverage that proves a failure string containing a fake credential is redacted in agent_runtime_state.lastError, and cover the setup-failure persistence path if the existing heartbeat test harness can exercise it without excessive complexity.

Addressed the remaining security blocker from CTO review: agent_runtime_state.lastError now stores the sanitized persisted failure message instead of raw adapterResult.errorMessage, while usage, billing, provider, and model accounting still use the raw adapter result. The outer setup-failure catch now redacts before writing run status, wakeup status, and timeline events, and the catch logger payload is redacted too. Added heartbeat-redaction regression coverage for runtime-state lastError and setup-failure persistence. Local validation passed: focused redaction tests including heartbeat-redaction, pnpm -r typecheck, pnpm build, and pnpm check:tokens.

CTO review: approved for merge.

Reviewed the latest PR #3584 update after the failure-persistence blocker. The PR is now scoped to the six DIG-534 files, all required checks are green, and the remaining redaction paths are covered:

- `agent_runtime_state.lastError` now stores the sanitized persisted failure message instead of raw `adapterResult.errorMessage`.
- The outer setup-failure catch redacts before writing run status, wakeup status, and run-event timeline records.
- Logger error payloads are sanitized for the same failure text.
- `heartbeat-redaction.test.ts` covers runtime-state `lastError`, run status parity, usage/cost preservation, and setup-failure run/wakeup/timeline persistence.

I will proceed with a normal non-admin merge if branch policy allows it.

3585.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds GCP OIDC service-to-service authentication to the `openclaw_gateway` adapter via a new `useGcpIdentityToken` config flag, fetching a short-lived ID token through `google-auth-library`'s Application Default Credentials and injecting it as the `Authorization: Bearer` header on the WebSocket upgrade. - **Silent bypass, no warning**: when `useGcpIdentityToken: true` but an `authorization` header is already present (from `authToken` or an explicit `headers` entry), the GCP token fetch is silently skipped with no log warning — a user who misconfigures both will not know GCP auth was bypassed, making production auth failures hard to diagnose.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after adding a warning log for the silent GCP-auth-bypass case; all other changes are additive and well-guarded. One P1 reliability issue: when `useGcpIdentityToken: true` is silently skipped because an auth header is already set, the user has no visibility that their intended GCP auth is inactive. This is a diagnosability gap that can cause hard-to-debug production auth failures and warrants a fix before merging. packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts — specifically the `useGcpIdentityToken` conditional block around lines 1071–1093

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Adds `fetchGcpIdToken` helper and wires `useGcpIdentityToken`/`audience` config options; silent skip (no warning) when GCP auth is bypassed by an existing auth header is a reliability concern | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/index.ts | Documents the two new config fields (`useGcpIdentityToken`, `audience`) in `agentConfigurationDoc`; documentation is accurate and clear | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/package.json | Adds `google-auth-library: ^9.0.0` as a runtime dependency; version range is appropriate | | docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-07-rust-paperclip-design.md | Rust Paperclip design spec; appears unrelated to this PR's GCP OIDC changes and may be an accidental carry-over from a prior branch commit |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts Line: 1071-1093 Comment: **Silent skip with no warning when `useGcpIdentityToken: true`** When `useGcpIdentityToken: true` but an `authorization` header is already present (set by `authToken`, an explicit `headers.authorization`, or an `x-openclaw-auth` header), the GCP token fetch is silently skipped. The user configured GCP OIDC auth expecting it to be active, but falls back to the existing credential without any log entry — making this failure mode hard to diagnose in production. A warning should be emitted in this branch: ```typescript if (useGcpIdentityToken) { if (headerMapHasIgnoreCase(headers, "authorization")) { await ctx.onLog( "stdout", "[openclaw-gateway] warning: useGcpIdentityToken is true but an authorization header is already set; skipping GCP ID token fetch\n", ); } else { // existing fetch logic... } } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts Line: 8 Comment: **Unconditional top-level import of `google-auth-library`** `GoogleAuth` is imported at module load time regardless of whether `useGcpIdentityToken` is enabled (its default is `false`). This means every adapter invocation pays the module initialization cost and creates a hard runtime dependency on `google-auth-library` even for deployments that never use GCP auth. A dynamic import inside `fetchGcpIdToken` would keep the dependency lazy: ```typescript async function fetchGcpIdToken(audience: string): Promise { const { GoogleAuth } = await import("google-auth-library"); const auth = new GoogleAuth(); // ... } ``` Then remove the top-level import on line 8. ## Greptile feedback addressed

Pushed fixes in commit 69510213:

**1. Silent bypass warning (P1)**
Added a `stdout` warning log when `useGcpIdentityToken: true` but an authorization header is already present — the fetch is now explicitly logged as skipped so production auth failures are diagnosable.

**2. Lazy dynamic import**
Removed the top-level `import { GoogleAuth } from "google-auth-library"` and switched to `await import("google-auth-library")` inside `fetchGcpIdToken`. The module now only loads when GCP auth is actually used.

**3. Unrelated spec file**
Removed `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-07-rust-paperclip-design.md` — it was carried over from a prior branch commit and is unrelated to this feature.

All 7 existing tests still pass.

Greptile Summary

Fixes two display bugs in the Anthropic subscription quota panel: `toPercent()` now correctly handles the current API's 0-100 percentage range (using the same `< 1` heuristic already in the Codex adapter), and `formatExtraUsageLabel()` divides `used_credits`/`monthly_limit` by 100 to convert cents to dollars. The acknowledged edge case — `utilization` values between 0 and 1 (sub-1% usage) will be treated as legacy fractions and over-reported — is consistent with the Codex adapter and is an acceptable trade-off given how rare <1% utilization is in practice. The previously noted gap in `extra_usage.utilization` test coverage has been addressed.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — two narrowly scoped bug fixes, no logic regressions, and all previously noted test gaps are now covered. Both changes are correct, well-documented with a full thinking path, and tested end-to-end. No P0 or P1 findings remain; all remaining considerations (sub-1% edge case, JPY-style currencies) are pre-existing design decisions explicitly acknowledged in the PR description. No files require special attention.

3591.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a supported `local_encrypted` master-key rekey workflow: helper utilities in `local-encrypted-provider.ts`, a `rekeyLocalEncryptedMasterKey` service method with transactional DB updates and per-company activity log entries, and `generate-local-master-key` / `rekey-local-master-key` CLI commands backed by embedded-Postgres tests and a full operator runbook. The implementation is sound and the cryptographic primitives (AES-256-GCM, per-version IV/tag) are used correctly. A few quality improvements are noted inline — most significant is the activation-failure UX gap and the TOCTOU window documented but not enforced at code level.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all findings are P2 style/hardening suggestions with no blocking correctness issues The rekey logic is correct: rows are fetched, decrypted, re-encrypted, and written in a single transaction with a matching activity log. The test suite covers dry-run, successful apply, history preservation, and wrong-key rollback. All remaining comments are P2 and do not affect correctness. server/src/services/secrets.ts (TOCTOU gap) and cli/src/commands/secrets.ts (activation failure UX)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/secrets.ts | Adds `rekeyLocalEncryptedMasterKey` to secretService — loads rows outside the transaction, re-encrypts in memory, then writes in a single DB transaction; activity log entries per company are correct | | server/src/secrets/local-encrypted-provider.ts | Refactors private helpers into exported utilities (generate, format, decode, read, encrypt, decrypt, rekey); AES-256-GCM and key validation are correct | | server/src/secrets/index.ts | New public exports surface for CLI use; `decryptLocalEncryptedValueWithKey` is re-exported but not consumed by the CLI, unnecessarily widening the API | | cli/src/commands/secrets.ts | New CLI commands for key generation and rekey; atomic temp-file rename for activation, correct option guards, and JSON output mode | | server/src/__tests__/secrets-rekey.test.ts | Embedded-Postgres tests cover dry-run, successful rekey, history preservation, activity log, and wrong-key rollback; imports use `.ts` extension inconsistently with `.js` used for helpers in the same file | | packages/db/src/index.ts | Exports `resolveMigrationConnection` and `MigrationConnection` to allow CLI access to migration DB connection | | server/package.json | Adds `./secrets` export entry for both dev (`.ts`) and published (`.d.ts`/`.js`) paths; correct dual format | | docs/deploy/secrets.md | Adds full operator runbook for the rekey workflow, including failure-handling steps and rollback guidance |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/secrets.ts Line: 370-398 Comment: **TOCTOU gap between row load and transaction** `loadLocalEncryptedVersionRows` runs outside the transaction. Any `companySecretVersions` row inserted or updated (via `create`/`rotate`) after this query but before `db.transaction()` commits will be missed: those rows stay encrypted under the old key, but after the operator swaps the key file the server will be unable to decrypt them. The runbook documents "stop Paperclip before applying," but there is no code-level guard (e.g. advisory lock, serializable isolation, or an in-transaction re-select) to enforce this. Consider loading and updating rows within a single serializable transaction to make the operation self-enforcing. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: cli/src/commands/secrets.ts Line: 244-271 Comment: **Silent ambiguity when activation fails after DB commit** If `activateNewKeyFile` throws (e.g. permission error, or `COPYFILE_EXCL` collision on retry), the exception bubbles out of the `try` block and the CLI prints the raw OS error in red, then exits 1. At this point the database transaction has already committed and all material is encrypted under the new key — but an operator reading only the red error has no way to know this without consulting the runbook. A minimal improvement would be to wrap the activation call and emit a distinct, actionable message on failure: ```ts try { activation = activateNewKeyFile(oldMasterKey.filePath, newKeyFilePath, newMasterKey); } catch (activationError) { console.error( pc.yellow( "Database rekey committed successfully, but key-file activation failed. " + "The database is now encrypted under the new key. " + "Install the --new-key-file contents as the active master key before restarting Paperclip.", ), ); throw activationError; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/secrets/index.ts Line: 4-5 Comment: **`decryptLocalEncryptedValueWithKey` not needed in public surface** `decryptLocalEncryptedValueWithKey` decrypts raw plaintext values and is used only internally by `rekeyLocalEncryptedMaterial`. The CLI does not import it. Exporting it from the `./secrets` entry point unnecessarily widens the API surface for any future consumer of `@paperclipai/server/secrets`. Consider removing it (and `encryptLocalEncryptedValueWithKey` / `rekeyLocalEncryptedMaterial` if not needed externally) from this re-export barrel. ```suggestion export { asLocalEncryptedMaterial, decodeLocalEncryptedMasterKey, formatLocalEncryptedMasterKey, generateLocalEncryptedMasterKey, readLocalEncryptedMasterKeyFile, type LocalEncryptedMaterial, } from "./local-encrypted-provider.js"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/secrets-rekey.test.ts Line: 15-16 Comment: **Inconsistent import file extensions** These two imports use `.ts` extensions while the helper import on line 13 uses `.js`. In an ESM TypeScript project Vitest resolves `.ts` at test time, but the convention elsewhere in the test suite (and in the production codebase) is to use `.js` specifiers. ```suggestion import { formatLocalEncryptedMasterKey } from "../secrets/local-encrypted-provider.js"; import { secretService } from "../services/secrets.js"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3594.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a mobile web handoff flow that lets the iOS ClipIOS app deep-link a logged-in user into the web onboarding wizard and redirect back to the app when complete, plus a new `GET /companies/:id/readiness` endpoint that reports whether a company has agents and secrets configured. The implementation is clean: tokens are SHA-256-hashed before storage, are single-use (optimistic `isNull(usedAt)` locking), have a 10-minute TTL, and the only accepted `returnUrl` is the hardcoded `clipios://onboarding-complete` allowlist entry. The double-validation (server-side on creation, client-side in `OnboardingWizard`) is a nice defence-in-depth touch. **The PR description is missing several sections required by CONTRIBUTING.md and the PR template:** specifically the **Thinking Path**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and the **Checklist**. Please fill these in before merging — the CONTRIBUTING guide treats the full template as mandatory for all PRs.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Code is safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 style/cleanup suggestions. No logic or security bugs found. The token flow is correctly secured (hashing, TTL, single-use locking, returnUrl allowlist). The only open items are the incomplete PR description, duplicated helper function, and missing expired-token cleanup — all P2. No files require special attention; the `requestBaseUrl` duplication between `routes/mobile-web-handoff.ts` and `routes/companies.ts` is the most actionable cleanup item.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/auth/mobile-web-handoff-plugin.ts | New better-auth plugin that validates a handoff token, creates a session, and redirects; properly handles all failure states and uses the `never` return type for control-flow narrowing. | | server/src/services/mobile-web-handoffs.ts | Token service with SHA-256 hashing, 10-min TTL, and optimistic locking (`isNull(usedAt)`) to prevent replay attacks; no expired-record cleanup mechanism. | | server/src/routes/mobile-web-handoff.ts | New POST endpoint creates handoff tokens; `returnUrl` is allowlisted to `clipios://onboarding-complete` only; contains a duplicate `requestBaseUrl` helper also defined in `companies.ts`. | | server/src/routes/companies.ts | Adds `GET /:companyId/readiness` endpoint checking agents + secrets; introduces a private `requestBaseUrl` helper that duplicates the one added in `mobile-web-handoff.ts`. | | ui/src/components/OnboardingWizard.tsx | After onboarding completes, redirects back to `clipios://onboarding-complete` when present in query params; properly validates the URL against the hardcoded allowed value on the client side. | | server/src/__tests__/mobile-web-handoff-route.test.ts | Good route-level test coverage: no-company, company-scoped, returnUrl passthrough, unsupported returnUrl rejection, 404, 403, and the redirect flow. | | server/src/__tests__/company-readiness-route.test.ts | Thorough tests for readiness endpoint covering ready/not-ready states, individual missing reasons, prefix fallback, 404, and auth enforcement. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0056_mobile_web_handoffs.sql | Clean migration creating `mobile_web_handoffs` with correct FK constraints (cascade on user, set null on company). |

3595.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `scheduledFor` date field to issues with auto-transition logic: a future-dated `todo` issue is demoted to `backlog`, and a server cron every 5 minutes promotes `backlog` issues back to `todo` when their date arrives. The migration, schema, types, validators, routes, and UI are all well-structured — but there is a P1 regression in the service layer. - **P1 — ReferenceError on all issue updates:** `applyStatusSideEffects` (module-level, line 60) references `existing?.status` on line 64, but `existing` is a `const` local to the `update` closure at line 1581 — it is completely out of scope. Every call to the update path will throw `ReferenceError: existing is not defined` at runtime. The function needs an `existingStatus?: string` parameter, and the call site at line 1640 must pass `existing.status`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until the `existing` ReferenceError in `applyStatusSideEffects` is resolved — it will break all issue updates in production. One P1 bug: `existing?.status` referenced in a module-level function where `existing` is not in scope, causing a ReferenceError on every issue update. Everything else (migration, schema, validators, route parsing, cron wiring) looks correct. The P2 items (N+1 updates, hardcoded `color-scheme`) are quality improvements that don't block merge once the P1 is fixed. `server/src/services/issues.ts` line 64 must be fixed before merging.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds `applyScheduledForAutoBacklog` and `tickScheduledIssues`, but `applyStatusSideEffects` references the undeclared variable `existing` (a ReferenceError at runtime), and `tickScheduledIssues` uses an N+1 update loop instead of a single bulk UPDATE. | | ui/src/components/IssueProperties.tsx | Adds a native date picker for `scheduledFor`; hardcoded `[color-scheme:dark]` class will render the native picker in dark theme even when the app is in light mode. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0056_scheduled_for.sql | Additive-only migration: adds nullable `scheduled_for` timestamp column and composite index `(status, scheduled_for)` — safe for zero-downtime deploy. | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Correctly extracts `scheduledFor` from the request body, converts the raw string to a `Date`, and passes it to the service layer for both create and update paths. | | packages/shared/src/validators/issue.ts | Adds `scheduledFor` as an optional nullable string with a `Date.parse` refine guard; straightforward and correct. | | server/src/index.ts | Wires up the 5-minute cron via `setInterval` to call `tickScheduledIssues`; correctly guarded by the existing server-mode check. | | packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts | Adds `scheduledFor` timezone-aware timestamp column and composite index — matches the migration exactly. | | packages/shared/src/types/issue.ts | Adds `scheduledFor: Date | null` field to the `Issue` interface, consistent with the DB column and other timestamp fields. |

Calling `existing?.status` here will throw `ReferenceError: existing is not defined` at runtime for any issue update, not just ones involving `scheduledFor`. The fix is to thread the existing status through `applyStatusSideEffects` as a new parameter, and pass `existing.status` at the call site (line 1640): ```typescript function applyStatusSideEffects( status: string | undefined, patch: Partial, existingStatus?: string, // ← add this ): Partial { applyScheduledForAutoBacklog(patch, existingStatus); // ← use it here ... } ``` And at line 1640: ```typescript applyStatusSideEffects(issueData.status, patch, existing.status); ``` How can I resolve this? A single bulk statement is simpler, more efficient, and avoids the TOCTOU window where an issue's status could change between the `SELECT` and its individual `UPDATE`. ## Reviewer feedback addressed

Pushed `71098121` with fixes for all flagged items:

**P1 — `existing` ReferenceError (Greptile + @xkonjin)**

**P2 — N+1 in `tickScheduledIssues` (Greptile + @xkonjin)**
- Replaced the select-then-loop with a single `UPDATE ... WHERE ... RETURNING`. Eliminates the TOCTOU window and is O(1) queries regardless of row count.

**P2 — Hardcoded `[color-scheme:dark]` (Greptile)**
- Changed to `[color-scheme:light] dark:[color-scheme:dark]` so the native date picker respects the app's theme.

**P2 — `db as any` cast (xkonjin)**
- Imported `Db` type and typed the `let db` declaration, removing the `as any`.

**Tests (xkonjin)**
- Added 6 integration tests for `scheduledFor` auto-backlog and tick logic:
  - Create with future `scheduledFor` → demoted to backlog
  - Create with past `scheduledFor` → stays todo
  - Update with future `scheduledFor` → demoted to backlog
  - `tickScheduledIssues` transitions due issues
  - `tickScheduledIssues` skips future issues
  - `tickScheduledIssues` skips hidden issues
- Added missing `scheduledFor: null` to all UI test fixtures (8 files).

3597.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new `plugin-agentcash` Paperclip plugin that gives agents access to paid external APIs via x402 micropayments and SIWX authentication. It registers 4 tools (`fetch`, `discover-endpoints`, `check-schema`, `get-balance`), shells out to the `agentcash` CLI binary for all operations, and bridges USDC spend into Paperclip's `cost-events` table. - **P1 — `checkWallet` uses the wrong CLI subcommand (`balance`) while the actual balance tool uses `accounts`.** If `balance` isn't a valid subcommand, the plugin always starts in degraded mode and logs a misleading warning, even when the wallet is fully operational. - The PR description does not follow the mandatory PR template (missing Thinking Path, Model Used, What Changed, Risks, and Checklist sections).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the checkWallet CLI subcommand mismatch and completing the PR template. One P1 issue — the checkWallet / balance vs accounts subcommand divergence — will cause the plugin to permanently report degraded health on startup. The remaining findings are P2 (NaN guard, duplicate URL parsing, inaccurate health message, missing PR template sections). The core functionality is architecturally sound. packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/agentcash-adapter.ts (line 74 — checkWallet subcommand), packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/worker.ts (cost-event NaN guard, health message accuracy)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/agentcash-adapter.ts | CLI adapter for agentcash binary; `checkWallet` uses `"balance"` subcommand while `agentcashGetBalance` uses `"accounts"` — mismatch causes permanent degraded health status if `balance` is not a valid command. | | packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/worker.ts | Tool registration and cost-event bridge; NaN can bypass the zero-cost guard, URL is parsed twice in reportCostEvent, and the health message inaccurately claims lazy re-initialization happens. | | packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/manifest.ts | Plugin manifest with 4 tool definitions; schema and metadata look correct. | | packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/constants.ts | Simple constants file for plugin ID, version, and tool names; no issues. | | packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/index.ts | Barrel re-export of manifest and worker; no issues. | | packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/package.json | New plugin package with correct workspace deps and build scripts; no issues. | | packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/tsconfig.json | TypeScript config extending base; correct outDir/rootDir setup. | | pnpm-lock.yaml | Lockfile updated to add agentcash@0.13.8 and its transitive deps. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/agentcash-adapter.ts Line: 74 Comment: **CLI subcommand mismatch — plugin always starts degraded** `checkWallet` probes with `exec(["balance"])`, but the actual balance feature calls `exec(["accounts"])` (line 101). If `balance` is not a valid `agentcash` subcommand (which the divergence here suggests), `checkWallet` always throws, `walletChecked` stays `false`, and every subsequent call to `isWalletLoaded()` returns `false`. The plugin then logs a misleading "wallet not available" warning on every startup, `onHealth` permanently returns `"degraded"`, and the health message claims tools "will retry on first tool call" — but they never call `checkWallet()` again. Use the same subcommand that actually succeeds (e.g. `"accounts"`) or a lightweight probe like `--version`: ```suggestion await exec(["accounts"]); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/worker.ts Line: 33-35 Comment: **`NaN` bypasses the zero-cost guard and reaches the API** `parseFloat(payment.price)` returns `NaN` if the CLI ever emits an empty string for `price` (note that `"" ?? "0"` evaluates to `""` — nullish coalescing only catches `null`/`undefined`). `Math.round(NaN * 100)` is `NaN`, and `NaN === 0` is `false`, so the early-return guard is skipped and `costCents: NaN` is sent to the cost-events endpoint, which may store or reject it silently. ```suggestion const usdcAmount = parseFloat(payment.price); const costCents = Math.round(usdcAmount * 100); if (!costCents || costCents === 0) return; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/worker.ts Line: 37-49 Comment: **URL parsed twice unnecessarily** `endpoint` is passed through `new URL()` in two separate try/catch blocks for `hostname` and `pathname`. If the URL is invalid the second parse also throws but produces a different fallback (`endpoint` as the pathname). Parse once and destructure: ```suggestion let hostname = "unknown"; let pathname = endpoint; try { const parsed = new URL(endpoint); hostname = parsed.hostname; pathname = parsed.pathname; } catch { // leave defaults } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/worker.ts Line: 264-267 Comment: **Health message is inaccurate — tools never retry wallet init** `onHealth` says `"Wallet not loaded — will retry on first tool call"` but none of the tool handlers call `checkWallet()` — they go straight to the CLI. If the CLI succeeds, `walletChecked` stays `false` and the health status stays `"degraded"` indefinitely. Either call `checkWallet()` inside each tool handler before the CLI call (so a successful run flips the flag), or change the health message to something like `"Wallet not confirmed at startup — tools will operate and surface errors if wallet is missing"`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash/src/agentcash-adapter.ts Line: 1 Comment: **PR description is missing required template sections** The PR description doesn't follow the mandatory template at `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` as required by CONTRIBUTING.md. The following sections are absent: - **Thinking Path** — top-down narrative from "Paperclip orchestrates AI agents…" down to why this plugin exists - **What Changed** — concrete bullet list per logical change - **Verification** — reproducible steps a reviewer can follow (the current "Test plan" approximates this but isn't in the required format) - **Risks** — missing entirely; a shared wallet spending real USDC on behalf of agents warrants an explicit risk callout - **Model Used** — required field; specifying the exact model ID, version, and capability details - **Checklist** — the template checkbox list Please fill out the template in full before requesting merge. **Context Used:** Contribution guidelines ([source](https://app.greptile.com/review/custom-context?memory=a595932a-f6ed-448b-899b-5ccac43f9148)) How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3597.body:
## CI Note

This PR adds a new workspace package (`packages/plugins/plugin-agentcash`), which creates a catch-22 in CI:

1. **Lockfile check** rejects the PR if `pnpm-lock.yaml` is modified ("CI owns lockfile updates")
2. **Install step** fails with `--frozen-lockfile` because the lockfile doesn't include the new package's dependencies

This affects any PR that adds a new workspace package. The lockfile needs to be regenerated after merge, or the lockfile check needs to be bypassed for PRs that add new packages to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` scope.

Happy to rebase with the lockfile included if that's the preferred path — just let me know.

3599.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds fail-safe dispatch handling for scheduled and webhook routines: when an automated run fails to dispatch, it creates a visible blocked issue assigned to the routine assignee and links it to the run record. It also adds two regression tests (stale issue-counter correction and failure-blocker linkage) and documents the behavior in the API docs. - **Missing PR template sections**: The description omits the required **Thinking Path**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections mandated by CONTRIBUTING.md and the PR template. Please fill these in before merging. - The `createAutomatedFailureIssue` helper (called inside the `db.transaction()` catch block) uses the outer `db` connection rather than `txDb`, so the blocker issue INSERT is committed outside the enclosing transaction. If `finalizeRun` or `updateRoutineTouchedState` subsequently fail and the transaction rolls back, the blocker issue persists with a stale run ID in its description.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until the transactional atomicity bug in `createAutomatedFailureIssue` is fixed and the PR description is completed per the template. One P1 finding: the blocker issue is written to the DB outside the enclosing transaction, which can produce an orphaned visible issue if the transaction rolls back. Additionally, the PR description is missing required template sections (Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used, Checklist). server/src/services/routines.ts — specifically the `createAutomatedFailureIssue` function and its call site inside the transaction catch block.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/routines.ts | Adds failure-blocker issue creation for schedule/webhook dispatch errors, but the helper uses the outer `db` connection instead of `txDb`, breaking transactional atomicity. | | server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts | Adds two new regression tests: stale issue-counter correction on scheduled dispatch, and dispatch-failure blocker linkage. Tests are well-scoped and verify the right DB state. | | docs/api/routines.md | Adds a paragraph documenting fail-safe run behavior; accurate and clear. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/routines.ts Line: 698-708 Comment: **Blocker issue created outside the enclosing transaction** `issueSvc.create` is called with the outer `db` connection, not `txDb`, so the INSERT is committed immediately and independently of the surrounding `db.transaction()`. If either of the subsequent `finalizeRun(…, txDb)` or `updateRoutineTouchedState(…, txDb)` calls throw (e.g. a constraint violation on `routine_runs`), the transaction rolls back and `createdRun` is gone — but the blocker issue already exists in the DB, referencing a non-existent run ID in its description. The fix is to thread `txDb` through to `createAutomatedFailureIssue` and pass it to `issueSvc.create`: ```typescript async function createAutomatedFailureIssue( input: { ... }, dbConn: Db, ) { if (input.source !== "schedule" && input.source !== "webhook") return null; return issueSvc.create(input.routine.companyId, { ... }, dbConn); } ``` and at the call site inside the catch block: ```typescript failureIssue = await createAutomatedFailureIssue({...}, txDb); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3599.body:
Staff review: changes requested before QA.

- `server/src/services/routines.ts`: the failure blocker is created through the outer `issueSvc.create()` while the dispatch run is still inside `db.transaction()`. If later run finalization/touched-state update fails, the transaction rolls back but the visible blocked issue can remain orphaned with only description text tying it to the run. Please make blocker creation atomic with the run update, or add a compensating cleanup path and regression coverage.
- `server/src/services/routines.ts`: automated webhook failures are documented as fail-safe, but variable/assignee validation happens before the `routineRuns` row and failure-blocker catch. Either bring webhook validation failures into the fail-safe path or narrow the docs and add webhook queue-failure coverage so the claim is precise.
- PR hygiene: the PR body does not use `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`; it is missing Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used, and Checklist.

Local verification from Staff passed: `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts`, `pnpm -r typecheck`, `pnpm build`, and full `env -u DATABASE_URL ... pnpm test:run` (229 files, 1256 passed, 1 skipped).

Note: GitHub would not allow this token to submit a formal request-changes review because it is treated as the PR author, so I am leaving the blocker as a PR comment and routing the Paperclip issue back to Builder.

Staff review: changes requested before QA.

- `releases/v2026.410.0.md` is deleted in this branch. That file is unrelated to the routine dispatch fail-safe work and contains the current security release notes, including the GHSA advisory and upgrade guidance. Restore it so this PR stays scoped to routines.
- Focused verification passed on my side: `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json`.

The routine fail-safe changes look materially improved from the previous review; I am holding this at Staff only because of the unrelated release-note deletion.

Staff review: approved for QA.

- The previous routine transaction/webhook blockers are addressed: automated schedule/webhook validation now lands inside the routine-run lifecycle, failure runs link to visible blocked issues, and externally-created dispatch/blocker issues are cleaned up if the enclosing transaction aborts.
- The release note file is byte-identical to current origin/master; it appears in the three-dot diff only because the branch point is older, not because this PR changes release content.
- Local verification passed: pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts; pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json; pnpm run typecheck; pnpm build; git diff --check FETCH_HEAD...HEAD.
- Full env -u DATABASE_URL ... pnpm test:run hit one unrelated suite-order failure in issue-update-comment-wakeup-routes.test.ts; that exact file passed immediately in isolation. GitHub checks are green.

Next gate is QA validation for the screenshot-backed routine/run behavior requested by [VEN-314](/VEN/issues/VEN-314).

Making routine dispatch failures visible aligns with Product Decision #6: "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery." Reviewed commit: 90e906cf · Tree snapshot: 4a91f3cc · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

## Staff review: changes requested

This is close, but I do not think it is safe for QA yet.

Blocking issues:

- `server/src/services/routines.ts:925` queues the heartbeat wakeup before the run is finalized at `server/src/services/routines.ts:934`. If `finalizeRun()` or `updateRoutineTouchedState()` fails after the wakeup succeeds, the catch path deletes the execution issue at `server/src/services/routines.ts:949`, and the outer cleanup only deletes issue rows at `server/src/services/routines.ts:995`. The queued `heartbeat_runs` / `agent_wakeup_requests` rows can remain pointed at an issue that was deleted and no longer has a linked routine run. The rollback test at `server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts:773` only covers the easier case where wakeup throws before wakeup artifacts commit, so this guardrail is still unproven.

- `tickScheduledTriggers()` advances `routineTriggers.nextRunAt` at `server/src/services/routines.ts:1626` before dispatching at `server/src/services/routines.ts:1644`. If dispatch aborts after that claim, the scheduled occurrence is consumed with no run and no visible blocker. The existing rollback test asserts no run/issue remains, but does not assert trigger state. That still conflicts with the goal that automated visual routines cannot disappear silently.

Please either make those side effects atomic, or add compensating cleanup/retry behavior for both the wakeup artifacts and schedule claim, with regression coverage that forces the post-wakeup finalization failure path.

Verification I ran on a merge-result worktree against current `origin/master`:

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` completed, with existing plugin bin warnings for unbuilt plugin SDK artifacts.
- `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts` passed: 22 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/master` passed.

I did not run broader gates after finding the blocking review issue.

## Staff Review

Not ready for QA yet. The latest patch still does not cover the production wakeup lifecycle for the post-wakeup finalization failure case.

Blocker:
- `server/src/services/routines.ts` cleanup only selects `heartbeat_runs` with `status = 'queued'` and only deletes wakeup requests with `status in ('queued', 'coalesced')`.
- The real `heartbeat.wakeup()` path inserts the queued run, then immediately calls `startNextQueuedRunForAgent()` before returning. That can claim the run as `running` and mark the wakeup request `claimed` before `dispatchRoutineRun()` reaches `finalizeRun()`.
- If `finalizeRun()` or touched-state update fails after wakeup returns, `cleanupIssueWakeupArtifacts()` can delete the routine execution issue while leaving a running heartbeat and claimed wakeup request pointed at the deleted issue. That is the same class of orphaned wakeup artifact the fix is meant to close.
- The new regression test uses a fake wakeup that leaves the run/request queued, so it does not exercise the production path where `wakeup()` starts the run before returning.

What I would expect before QA:
- Make the cleanup/failure path safe when the wakeup has already been claimed/running, or change dispatch ordering so the run cannot be claimed until routine run finalization is durable.
- Add regression coverage that uses the real heartbeat wakeup behavior or faithfully simulates `queued -> running` and `queued -> claimed` before post-wakeup finalization fails.

Verification I ran:
- `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts` passed: 24 tests.
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/master...HEAD` passed.

## Staff Review

Not ready for QA yet. The latest patch improves the steady queued and steady running cases, but there is still a race in the cleanup path.

Blocking issue:

- `cleanupIssueWakeupArtifacts()` snapshots live runs, classifies queued runs, then later deletes the queued run ids without re-checking status. If a queued run is claimed between the select and the delete, the cleanup can delete a now-running `heartbeat_runs` row while leaving the wakeup request in `claimed` status. The follow-up wakeup delete only covers `queued`/`coalesced`, so the claimed `agent_wakeup_requests` row can survive pointing at an issue that the caller then deletes.
- Relevant flow: `server/src/services/routines.ts:724` selects live runs, `server/src/services/routines.ts:739` classifies queued/running, `server/src/services/routines.ts:804` deletes queued run ids without a status predicate, and `server/src/services/routines.ts:816` only deletes queued/coalesced wakeup requests. The issue row can then be deleted at `server/src/services/routines.ts:1080` or `server/src/services/routines.ts:844`.
- The new tests cover stable queued and stable running artifacts, but not the queued-to-running race between cleanup snapshot and cleanup delete.

Expected before QA:

- Revalidate run status before deleting queued run ids, or handle all selected live runs through a cancellation path that remains safe if they are claimed mid-cleanup.
- Ensure claimed wakeup requests cannot survive for a transient routine issue that is deleted.
- Add regression coverage that forces a queued artifact to become running/claimed during cleanup before the issue deletion proceeds.

Verification I ran:

- `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts` passed: 25 tests.
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/master...HEAD` passed.

## Staff Review

Approved for QA.

- The previous queued-to-running cleanup race is addressed: routine cleanup now reconciles live heartbeat runs over bounded passes, deletes only still-queued runs, cancels running/claimed runs through `heartbeat.cancelRun()`, and leaves the transient issue in place if cleanup cannot prove wakeup artifacts are inactive.
- Scheduled trigger claim restoration is covered for dispatch aborts after the trigger has advanced.
- Regression coverage now includes stale issue counters, visible automated blockers, rollback cleanup, webhook validation failures, post-wakeup finalization failure, claimed/running wakeups, and the queued-to-running cleanup race.

Verification run locally on `c0dd7633`:
- `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts` passed: 26 tests.
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json` passed.
- `pnpm run typecheck` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed.
- `env -u DATABASE_URL PATH=<without /usr/local/bin> pnpm test:run` passed: 229 files, 1263 passed, 1 skipped.
- `git diff --check` passed.

GitHub checks are green at review time: `policy`, `verify`, `e2e`, and Snyk passed; PR merge state is `CLEAN`.

Next owner: QA Engineer for the screenshot-backed routine/run validation gate on [VEN-314](/VEN/issues/VEN-314).

3601.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR wires up mobile onboarding end-to-end: the CLI auth page now threads an `authMode` query param through the sign-in redirect so ClipIOS can land directly on the sign-up form, and newly created companies automatically bootstrap the creator with `owner` membership and `tasks:assign` permission. - The instance-admin guard on `POST /companies` was fully removed, so any authenticated user can now create companies — not just during onboarding. This is the most significant behavioural change and warrants confirmation that unlimited company creation per user is acceptable. - The PR description is missing the required **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, and full PR template sections per CONTRIBUTING.md. Please add those before merge. - The `Auth.tsx` page now renders differently based on the `?mode=` URL param (sign-up vs sign-in pre-selected), which is a visual change — please include before/after screenshots per CONTRIBUTING.md.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not ready to merge — the removal of the instance-admin gate on company creation is an intentional but potentially broad permission change that should be explicitly confirmed before shipping. One P1 finding (unrestricted company creation for all board users) and one P2 security-adjacent finding (broadened adapter test endpoint) reduce confidence below the merge threshold. The PR template is also incomplete per repo policy. server/src/routes/companies.ts (removed instance-admin guard) and server/src/routes/agents.ts (permission downgrade on test-environment)

Security Review

 - **Unrestricted company creation** (`server/src/routes/companies.ts`): The instance-admin guard was removed from `POST /companies`, allowing any authenticated board user to create companies without bound. A compromised or malicious account could mint unlimited companies. - **Broadened adapter test endpoint** (`server/src/routes/agents.ts`): `test-environment` now accepts requests from any company member rather than only those with `agents:create` permission. The endpoint resolves secrets and makes outbound network calls, so the expanded access surface is worth noting.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/companies.ts | Removed instance-admin guard from POST /, granting all authenticated board users the ability to create companies; also correctly grants the creator `tasks:assign` permission for onboarding. | | server/src/routes/agents.ts | Downgraded `test-environment` auth from `assertCanReadConfigurations` (requires `agents:create`) to `assertCompanyAccess` (any company member), broadening who can exercise adapter config resolution and external calls. | | ui/src/pages/Auth.tsx | Adds URL-driven `?mode=sign_up|sign_in` support; `resolveAuthMode` helper and `useEffect` sync are correct, but form fields are not reset when the mode changes. | | ui/src/pages/CliAuth.tsx | Correctly threads `authMode` through CLI auth redirect URL so the signup/sign-in mode is preserved across the CLI → web auth → CLI callback flow. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/companies.ts Line: 267-269 Comment: **Company creation open to all authenticated users** Removing the `local_implicit || isInstanceAdmin` guard means any authenticated board user — including newly self-registered accounts — can now create an unlimited number of companies. The onboarding intent is clear, but the check was previously the only gate preventing arbitrary company proliferation. Without a per-user or per-instance creation limit, a compromised or rogue account can mint companies freely. Consider scoping the new path to e.g. `req.actor.source === "self_registered_onboarding"` or capping creation to one company per new user if unrestricted creation is undesirable. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/agents.ts Line: 800-803 Comment: **`test-environment` permission downgraded to company membership** Previously, `assertCanReadConfigurations` (which delegates to `assertCanCreateAgentsForCompany`) required `agents:create` permission. The replacement `assertCompanyAccess` only checks that the caller is authenticated and belongs to the company. This allows every company member — not just those with agent-creation rights — to submit arbitrary adapter configs to `test-environment`, which resolves secrets and makes external network calls. If the intent is only to unblock the new company owner, an alternative is to also grant `agents:create` in `companies.ts` when bootstrapping the creator, rather than broadening the endpoint itself. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/pages/Auth.tsx Line: 40-43 Comment: **Form fields not cleared on mode switch** When `requestedMode` changes (e.g. user navigates back from sign-up to sign-in), `setMode` and `setError` are called but `name`, `email`, and `password` are not reset. A user who partially filled the sign-up form will see those values persist when the mode flips to sign-in, which can be confusing. Consider resetting the fields here as well: ```suggestion useEffect(() => { setMode(requestedMode); setError(null); setName(""); setEmail(""); setPassword(""); }, [requestedMode]); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3608.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR wraps `hermesLocalAdapter.execute` to inject the run-scoped agent JWT as `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` and the execution ID as `PAPERCLIP_RUN_ID` into the Hermes adapter environment, ensuring Hermes agents write under the correct identity instead of inheriting a server- or board-level key. Both concerns raised in the previous review round — documenting the intentional `as unknown as` cast and adding a test for the no-`authToken` early-return path — are now fully addressed.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge; all previous review concerns are resolved and no new issues were found. - All four new tests pass, the intentional cast is documented, the early-return for missing authToken preserves backward compatibility, and the design trade-off of skipping auth-guard injection for default-prompt agents is clearly explained in both code comments and the PR description. No P0 or P1 findings remain. - No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/adapters/registry.ts | Wraps hermesLocalAdapter.execute to inject ctx.authToken as PAPERCLIP_API_KEY and ctx.runId as PAPERCLIP_RUN_ID; adds supportsLocalAgentJwt: true; intentional as unknown as cast is now documented; early-return preserves backward compatibility. | | server/src/__tests__/adapter-registry.test.ts | Adds four new Hermes-specific tests covering JWT injection, no-authToken pass-through, explicit key preservation, and default-prompt preservation; all previously flagged test gaps are now addressed. | Reviews (3): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(hermes): inject run id into adapter ..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/f89449d30c41ac01a7ca18032d2e38e660cdce4b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=28280820)

No concerns. Fixing identity attribution in the adapter registry ensures the audit trail correctly tracks which agent performed each action. --- Reviewed commit: 7b144879 · Tree snapshot: · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

QA blocker from review:

`server/src/adapters/registry.ts:197-218` now always writes `adapterConfig.promptTemplate`. In `hermes-paperclip-adapter`, `promptTemplate` is the full wake/task template, not an additive extension. For Hermes agents without a custom prompt template, this change replaces the entire default heartbeat/task prompt with only the auth guard, so the agent loses its assigned issue/workflow instructions.

Please preserve the default Hermes template when no custom prompt exists, or inject the auth guidance through a path that does not overwrite the base prompt.

## Fix for QA review feedback

**Root cause of the regression:** The previous patch always set `promptTemplate` on the patched config, even when no custom template was configured. Hermes treats `promptTemplate` as a full replacement for its built-in default heartbeat/task prompt, so agents without a custom template lost all assigned issue/workflow instructions — only the auth guard text remained.

**Change made:**
- `hermesLocalAdapter.execute` now only sets `promptTemplate` on the patched config when a custom template already exists on the agent. Auth guard is prepended to the custom template as before.
- When no custom template is configured, `promptTemplate` is left unset — Hermes uses its built-in default.
- The critical `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` injection (the actual identity fix) is unchanged.

**Test changes (`adapter-registry.test.ts`):**
- Updated "preserves explicit Paperclip API key" test: corrected the incorrect assertion that expected `promptTemplate` on agents without a custom template. Now asserts `promptTemplate` is `undefined` in that case.
- Added new explicit regression test: "does not set promptTemplate when no custom template is configured, preserving Hermes default".
- All 7 tests pass. Typecheck clean.

Updated the PR description to match the required template sections and checklist. 3612.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `PAPERCLIP_PLUGIN_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IPS=true` environment variable to bypass the private-IP SSRF guard in the plugin fetch path, enabling self-hosted deployments whose plugin endpoints (e.g. Honcho) resolve to RFC-1918 addresses. The flag defaults to `false`, so public deployments are unaffected. - **Security (P1):** The bypass disables _all_ private-IP checks, including the link-local range (169.254.0.0/16). On a cloud VM (AWS/GCP/Azure) this exposes the instance metadata service and any IAM credentials attached to it. Loopback and link-local should stay blocked unconditionally; only RFC-1918 and IPv6 ULA need relaxing for the homelab use-case.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not safe to merge as-is — the bypass also disables cloud metadata (IMDS) protection on cloud-hosted deployments. The P1 security finding (link-local / IMDS exposure) affects the core security invariant of the change and needs to be resolved before merge. The fix is straightforward but important. server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts — the PLUGIN_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IPS bypass needs to preserve loopback and link-local blocking.

Security Review

 - **IMDS / cloud metadata exposure** (`server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts`): `PLUGIN_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IPS=true` disables the entire private-IP filter, including the link-local range (169.254.0.0/16). On any cloud-hosted deployment (AWS, GCP, Azure) this allows plugins to reach the instance metadata service (`169.254.169.254`) and retrieve IAM role credentials, user-data, or other secrets. The stated use-case only requires RFC-1918 access; loopback and link-local should remain blocked unconditionally.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts | Adds PLUGIN_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IPS opt-in flag to bypass the private-IP SSRF guard; the bypass is broader than RFC-1918 and also disables loopback and link-local (cloud IMDS) protection. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts`, line 58-62 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/77c3b3937f224e557d1601d17fe85bf55d32726e/server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts#L58-L62)) **Link-local (169.254.0.0/16) bypass exposes cloud IMDS** When `PLUGIN_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IPS=true` is set on a cloud-hosted deployment (AWS EC2, GCP, Azure), the link-local range (169.254.0.0/16) is no longer blocked. A plugin targeting `http://169.254.169.254/` can reach the instance metadata service and retrieve IAM credentials or other secrets. The PR's stated use-case only needs RFC-1918 ranges; loopback (`127.x.x.x`) and link-local (`169.254.x.x`) don't serve private LAN endpoints. Consider keeping loopback and link-local always blocked and only relaxing RFC-1918 + IPv6 ULA. The simplest approach is extracting an `isAlwaysBlockedIP` helper for loopback + link-local and applying it unconditionally alongside the existing guard.

3615.body:
## Root Cause Identified: MDXEditor silently fails on angle brackets `<>` in markdown

After deeper investigation, this is **not** a race condition, cache, or batch-creation bug. The API, DB, disk state, and `adapterConfig` are all correct. The issue is in the **frontend markdown editor**.

### What actually happens

1. `GET /api/agents/{id}/instructions-bundle` returns the correct bundle — confirmed via both direct HTTP and direct service call.
2. `GET /api/agents/{id}/instructions-bundle/file?path=AGENTS.md` returns the full file content (9k+ chars).
3. The content is passed to `<MarkdownEditor>` (MDXEditor/Lexical) via the `markdown`/`value` prop — verified by inspecting the React fiber props of the mounted editor:
   ```
   markdown: "You are **COL-EURLEX**, the collection owner..."
   markdownLen: 9877
   ```
4. The Lexical parser encounters content it can't parse and **renders an empty document with no error**, no warning, no fallback. The contenteditable `<div>` has `innerText.length === 0` despite receiving 9877 chars of input.

### Minimal reproducer

Create any agent with this `AGENTS.md`:
```markdown
# Test

foo <> bar
```
→ GUI Instructions tab renders empty.

### Failure modes observed

| Markdown content | Rendered |
|---|---|
| `foo <> bar` | empty (0 chars) |
| `<https://example.com>` (autolink) | editor unmounts entirely |
| `<tag>` (HTML-like) | editor unmounts entirely |
| `foo -> bar` | works fine |
| `foo ↔ bar` | works fine |

All three angle-bracket patterns break silently — no console errors, no error boundary triggered, just empty render.

### Why only the 8 batch-created agents

The \"2-second batch creation window\" was a **red herring**. The real reason all 8 are broken is that they were generated from the same template, which contains this heading:

```markdown
### 6. GitHub <> Paperclip Linking
```

The `<>` (meant typographically as \"to/from\") breaks the Lexical parser. CEO, CTO, LIB, reviewer-* etc. were authored by hand without `<>` in their instructions, so they render fine. Individual agents created before/after the batch that don't use this template are also fine.

### Immediate workaround

Replace `<>` in instruction files with `↔`, `/`, `and`, or escape as `\<\>`. I fixed all 8 affected files with:
```bash
sed -i 's|GitHub <> Paperclip|GitHub ↔ Paperclip|g' AGENTS.md
```
All 8 instruction tabs became visible immediately after reload.

### Suggested upstream fix(es)

The bug is really in **MDXEditor/Lexical** (silent failure on unsupported inline syntax), but Paperclip can mitigate:

1. **Error boundary + textarea fallback**: Wrap `<MarkdownEditor>` in a React `ErrorBoundary`. Catches the unmount-entirely case. Does not catch the silent-empty case.
2. **Post-mount content-length sanity check**: After the editor mounts, compare `editor.innerText.length` vs. `props.markdown.length`. If rendered length is < 10% of input length, show a warning and fall back to a plain `<textarea>`.
3. **Pre-sanitize markdown**: Escape/encode angle brackets before passing to the editor — e.g. replace non-URL `<...>` with `<...>` when persisting or when rendering.
4. **Report upstream** to MDXEditor: autolink and inline HTML tag parsers should degrade gracefully, not render empty.

Happy to submit a PR with approach (2) — it catches both failure modes and doesn't require guessing which markdown patterns are problematic. Let me know if you'd prefer a different direction.

### Investigation method

For future debugging of similar \"the API works but the GUI is empty\" issues, the key was:
- Use Chrome DevTools / MCP browser to walk the React fiber tree up from the `[contenteditable]` element
- Inspect `memoizedProps` of each ancestor to find where the `markdown`/`value` prop is set
- Compare received prop vs. rendered DOM length — mismatch = parser failure
- Bisect the content by halving and testing each half in a working agent

This method quickly isolated the failure to a single line in a 155-line file.

3635.body:
## Diagnosis Update — Confirmed Root Cause & Scope

After deeper analysis, here is a more precise diagnosis:

### Exact failure point

The build fails in two independent places, not just the UI step:

**1. `packages/shared/src/network-bind.ts` line 1:**
```ts
import type { BindMode, DeploymentExposure, DeploymentMode } from "./constants.js";
```
`BindMode` and `BIND_MODES` are **no longer exported from `constants.ts`** in canary.2+. The type simply doesn't exist there. This is an incomplete refactor — `BIND_MODES`/`BindMode` were likely moved elsewhere but the import in `network-bind.ts` was not updated.

**2. UI `fastMode` TS errors** (as already noted in the issue body)

Both failures are confirmed by inspecting the canary.3 source tree directly:
```
grep -rn "BindMode\|BIND_MODES" packages/shared/src/constants.ts
# → empty (no output)
```

**3. The server build would also fail independently** for the same `BindMode` reason, even if the UI step were skipped.

### canary.2 is also broken

Confirmed: `canary/v2026.413.0-canary.2` has the exact same issue. The commit that removed `BindMode` from `constants.ts` is present in both canary.2 and canary.3. There is no working intermediate version between canary.1 and a future canary.4+.

### Current state

- Running: `canary/v2026.413.0-canary.1` (rolled back)
- canary.2 and canary.3 both fail to build
- Auto-update script now correctly stays on canary.1 and will retry when a new tag is published

### Fix needed

Either:
- Re-export `BindMode`/`BIND_MODES` from `constants.ts` (or update the import in `network-bind.ts` to point to wherever they were moved)
- Align the `fastMode` field type in `CreateConfigValues` with the server-side adapter

This is blocking canary promotion from canary.1 for all self-hosted deployments with auto-update.

3640.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds `agent.tool.pre_execute` and `agent.tool.post_execute` events to the plugin event bus, emitted fire-and-forget around every tool invocation so plugins can observe tool calls without coupling to agent workflows. The implementation correctly constructs the `PluginEvent` envelope (all required top-level fields present), generates a shared `callId` for correlation, excludes raw parameters from the payload, and gracefully handles event bus failures without blocking execution. Previous review concerns about event shape, `callId`, and parameter exposure have all been addressed.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all prior P0/P1 concerns are resolved and the only remaining finding is a P2 semantic naming suggestion. All three previously flagged issues (wrong event shape, missing callId, parameter exposure) have been fixed. The sole remaining finding is a P2 ambiguity where `success: true` can coexist with `hasError: true` in the success path — a naming/design note, not a correctness defect. Fire-and-forget error isolation is solid, tests cover all six lifecycle paths, and the change is purely additive. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Adds `agent.tool.pre_execute` and `agent.tool.post_execute` to PLUGIN_EVENT_TYPES constant array; change is additive and non-breaking. | | server/src/app.ts | Threads existing `eventBus` instance into `createPluginToolDispatcher` options; straightforward plumbing, no logic change. | | server/src/services/plugin-tool-dispatcher.ts | Adds optional `eventBus` to dispatcher options and forwards it to `createPluginToolRegistry`; minimal change with no behavioral impact when `eventBus` is absent. | | server/src/services/plugin-tool-registry.ts | Core change: emits pre/post tool events with correct PluginEvent shape (eventId, eventType, occurredAt, companyId all present), shared callId, fire-and-forget via `.catch()`. Minor semantic ambiguity in success path where `success: true` and `hasError: true` can coexist when ToolResult.error is set. | | server/src/__tests__/plugin-tool-registry.test.ts | New test file with 6 unit tests covering: pre_execute ordering, post_execute success/error paths, event bus failure isolation, no-bus fallback, and shared callId; coverage is thorough for the event lifecycle. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/plugin-tool-registry.ts Line: 500-503 Comment: **`success: true` + `hasError: true` can coexist** When `workerManager.call()` resolves but the returned `ToolResult` has `error` set (e.g. a tool that returns a partial result alongside an error string), the event fires with `success: true, hasError: true` simultaneously. Subscribers that short-circuit on `success === true` (assuming no error) will silently ignore the error field. Consider either reflecting the tool's own error in `success` — `success: !result.error` — or renaming the field `resultHasError` to make the dual-axis semantics explicit. ```suggestion success: !result.error, hasContent: !!result.content, hasError: !!result.error, error: result.error ?? null, ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Thanks for the thorough review @xkonjin. Addressing each point:

1. **`actorId` undefined guard** — Fixed. `actorId` and `actorType` are now derived dynamically from `runContext`: falls back to `runId` or `"unknown"` when `agentId` is undefined, and `actorType` is `"system"` instead of hardcoded `"agent"` in that case. `agentId` in the payload is also null-safe.

2. **`withAdapterMutex` comment** — That's in PR #3490, not this PR. Will note for that branch.

3. **`--no-save` removal** — Also in PR #3490 scope. The change was intentional: `--no-save` was causing sibling plugin pruning on reinstall.

4. **`deleteBySource` TOCTOU** — That's in PRs #3237/#3351 scope. Acknowledged — the window is narrow and the consequence is a failed delete (not data loss), but a code comment would be honest.

5. **Missing test coverage for pre/post_execute events** — Fair point. Will add a unit test for the event emissions in a follow-up.

6. **Unrelated Dockerfile changes** — Agreed, those are fork-local and have been removed from this PR branch after rebase.

Branch has been rebased on current master with the actorId fix applied.

3648.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR removes the stale `sha256sum` check for the GitHub CLI keyring file in `Dockerfile`, fixing build failures caused by GitHub rotating their signing keys. The change is technically sound — HTTPS transport and apt's `signed-by=` mechanism already provide equivalent integrity guarantees, matching GitHub's own recommended install approach. However, the PR description does not use the required [PR template](/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md). Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, every PR must include the following sections: - **Thinking Path** — a blockquote tracing reasoning from project context down to this change - **Model Used** — the AI model (provider, exact model ID/version) or "None — human-authored" - **Risks** — explicit risk assessment (even "Low risk" is acceptable) - **Checklist** — the standard checklist from the template Please update the PR description to follow the template before merging.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to follow the required template. - The code change is a single-line removal that fixes a real build breakage with no functional or security regression. All remaining feedback is P2 (missing PR template sections), which does not affect merge safety. - No files require special attention — the Dockerfile change is minimal and correct.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | Dockerfile | Removes the stale SHA256 checksum pin for the GitHub CLI keyring GPG file; the one-line deletion is correct, targeted, and aligns with GitHub's own installation guidance — HTTPS + apt's signed-by= already guarantee integrity. | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix: remove stale pinned sha256 for GitH..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/89295fee83202675e6b17fd500df262d1647d7f4) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=28342493)
