3649.body:
Greptile Summary

Updates the hardcoded SHA256 checksum for the GitHub CLI GPG keyring in `Dockerfile` to fix broken Docker builds caused by keyring rotation upstream. The PR description is missing several required sections from the PR template (`.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`) mandated by CONTRIBUTING.md: **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, and the **Checklist**. Please fill out the full template before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge once PR description is completed per the template requirements. - The code change is a single-line checksum update with no logic impact. All code-level findings are P2. The PR template sections are missing but that is a process concern, not a correctness issue. - No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | Dockerfile | Single-line update to the SHA256 checksum for the GitHub CLI GPG keyring. The change is minimal and self-contained, fixing a broken build caused by keyring rotation. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: Dockerfile Line: 8 Comment: **Brittle SHA256 pin on a mutable URL** `https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg` is a live, mutable file — GitHub rotates it as keys are added or expired, which is exactly what broke this build. Hardcoding the SHA256 means the build will break again the next time the keyring changes. Consider removing the checksum step and letting the system rely on HTTPS transport security plus the GPG signature chain already enforced by `apt` (the keyring itself is only used to verify `gh` package signatures — the HTTPS download already provides integrity). Alternatively, if a pinned version of the keyring is available via a versioned/immutable URL, prefer that instead. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3651.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR replays stable tooling and configuration improvements from a feature branch: doc updates, test guidance, script cleanup, server typing fixes, a new `gemini-local` tsconfig reference, and UI build chunking. Most changes are low-risk, but several regressions in `server/src/index.ts`, `server/src/middleware/logger.ts`, `doc/DEVELOPING.md`, and `scripts/provision-worktree.sh` need to be addressed before merge. - **`redact: ["req.headers.authorization"]` removed** from the pino logger — auth tokens will be written in plaintext to `server.log` and stdout. - **Keep-alive timeouts removed** (`keepAliveTimeout = 185000` / `headersTimeout = 186000`) — reintroduces intermittent 502/ECONNRESET errors behind AWS ALB, Nginx, and Traefik. - **Docker Compose path wrong in docs** — `docker-compose.quickstart.yml` should remain `docker/docker-compose.quickstart.yml`; the file hasn't moved.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Do not merge until the authorization header redaction, keep-alive timeouts, and docker-compose path are restored. - Three P1 findings — credential leakage in logs, reverse-proxy reliability regression, and a broken docker-compose path in docs — plus a P1 worktree symlink depth regression must be resolved before this is production-safe. - server/src/middleware/logger.ts (redact removal), server/src/index.ts (keep-alive timeouts), doc/DEVELOPING.md (docker-compose path), scripts/provision-worktree.sh (maxdepth)

Security Review

 - **Credential leakage via logs** (`server/src/middleware/logger.ts`): `redact: ["req.headers.authorization"]` was removed from the pino logger. Every request carrying a `Bearer` token or API key will now write that credential in plaintext to both the console and the persistent `server.log` file on disk. This affects all deployment modes.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/middleware/logger.ts | Removes authorization header redaction from pino logger, which will cause auth tokens to appear in log files; also casts httpLogger as `any` as a TS workaround | | server/src/index.ts | Tightens typing (db: Db), removes keep-alive timeouts that guarded against 502/ECONNRESET on reverse proxies, moves shutdown handler to embedded-postgres-only guard, and hard-codes backup retention formula from config instead of DB | | doc/DEVELOPING.md | Updates docker-compose path to remove `docker/` prefix (doesn't match actual file location), masks OPENCLAW_DISABLE_DEVICE_AUTH values as `***`, and adds useful testing section | | scripts/provision-worktree.sh | Simplifies init function and removes tsx fallback; reduces find maxdepth from 4 to 3, which misses `packages/adapters/*/node_modules` at depth 4 | | ui/vite.config.ts | Adds manual chunk splitting for better bundle predictability; removes WSL2 HMR polling workaround, which will break hot-reload for devs using WSL2 on Windows drives | | tsconfig.json | Removes `packages/mcp-server` reference (package no longer present) and swaps `droid-local` for `gemini-local` — both are correct | | docker-entrypoint.sh | New Docker entrypoint script for UID/GID remapping; logic looks correct | | packages/db/TESTING.md | New documentation for embedded PostgreSQL test troubleshooting; content is accurate and helpful | | doc/DATABASE.md | Removes stale note about migration generation issue — minor, correct doc fix | | scripts/release-lib.sh | Mode change only (adds executable bit); no content changes |

Comments Outside Diff (2)

 1. `ui/vite.config.ts`, line 53-62 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/7751e4b8e37d9d30f3aa2ec4e0ef00489beae301/ui/vite.config.ts#L53-L62)) **WSL2 HMR polling workaround removed** The previous config had: ```ts watch: process.cwd().startsWith("/mnt/") ? { usePolling: true, interval: 1000 } : undefined, ``` This is a well-known requirement for WSL2 environments where the project lives on a Windows drive (`/mnt/c/…`). Without it, `inotify` events are never fired and hot-module replacement silently stops working. Consider restoring the guard unless WSL2 support has been explicitly dropped.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/vite.config.ts Line: 53-62 Comment: **WSL2 HMR polling workaround removed** The previous config had: ```ts watch: process.cwd().startsWith("/mnt/") ? { usePolling: true, interval: 1000 } : undefined, ``` This is a well-known requirement for WSL2 environments where the project lives on a Windows drive (`/mnt/c/…`). Without it, `inotify` events are never fired and hot-module replacement silently stops working. Consider restoring the guard unless WSL2 support has been explicitly dropped. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
2. `doc/DEVELOPING.md`, line 111-112 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/7751e4b8e37d9d30f3aa2ec4e0ef00489beae301/doc/DEVELOPING.md#L111-L112)) **`OPENCLAW_DISABLE_DEVICE_AUTH` values now masked** Both bullet lines now show `***` instead of the actual values to set (`1` to disable device auth, `0` to require it). This makes the documentation unhelpful for anyone setting up the smoke script for the first time. The original `1` / `0` values should be restored.

This makes the documentation unhelpful for anyone setting up the smoke script for the first time. The original `1` / `0` values should be restored. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/middleware/logger.ts Line: 30-32 Comment: **Authorization header redaction removed** The `redact: ["req.headers.authorization"]` option was dropped. Every incoming request that carries a `Bearer` token, API key, or session credential will now have that value written in plaintext to both the console transport and the persistent `server.log` file. Anyone with read access to the log directory gains access to live credentials. ```suggestion export const logger = pino({ level: "debug", redact: ["req.headers.authorization"], }, pino.transport({ ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/index.ts Line: 533 Comment: **Keep-alive timeouts removed — 502s will return for proxy deployments** The PR removes `server.keepAliveTimeout = 185000` and `server.headersTimeout = 186000`. Node's default keep-alive timeout is 5 seconds, which is shorter than the idle timeout of common reverse proxies (AWS ALB: 60 s, Nginx default: 75 s, Traefik default: 180 s). Any connection that idles past 5 seconds will be closed by Node before the proxy retires it, producing intermittent 502/ECONNRESET errors. The original comment in the code explicitly called this out. These two lines should be restored after `createServer`: ```ts const server = createServer(app as unknown as Parameters[0]); // Outlive default idle timeouts of common reverse proxies (AWS ALB, Nginx, Traefik) server.keepAliveTimeout = 185000; server.headersTimeout = 186000; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: doc/DEVELOPING.md Line: 97-99 Comment: **Docker Compose path is incorrect** The path was changed from `docker/docker-compose.quickstart.yml` to `docker-compose.quickstart.yml`, but the file lives at `docker/docker-compose.quickstart.yml` in the repo. Running this command from the repo root as written would fail with "no such file." ```suggestion docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.quickstart.yml up --build ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/provision-worktree.sh Line: 311-320 Comment: **`maxdepth 3` misses adapter `node_modules` at depth 4** The inline `find` was changed from `-maxdepth 4` to `-maxdepth 3`. In this repo's structure, packages nested two levels deep (e.g. `packages/adapters/gemini-local/`) place their `node_modules` at depth 4 (`./packages/adapters/gemini-local/node_modules`). With `maxdepth 3` those directories are never found, so symlinks for them are never created in the new worktree, breaking any worktree that needs those adapter packages. ```suggestion cd "$base_cwd" && find . \ -mindepth 1 \ -maxdepth 4 \ -type d \ -name node_modules \ ! -path './.git/*' \ ! -path './.paperclip/*' \ | sed 's#^\./##' ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/vite.config.ts Line: 53-62 Comment: **WSL2 HMR polling workaround removed** The previous config had: ```ts watch: process.cwd().startsWith("/mnt/") ? { usePolling: true, interval: 1000 } : undefined, ``` This is a well-known requirement for WSL2 environments where the project lives on a Windows drive (`/mnt/c/…`). Without it, `inotify` events are never fired and hot-module replacement silently stops working. Consider restoring the guard unless WSL2 support has been explicitly dropped. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: doc/DEVELOPING.md Line: 111-112 Comment: **`OPENCLAW_DISABLE_DEVICE_AUTH` values now masked** Both bullet lines now show `***` instead of the actual values to set (`1` to disable device auth, `0` to require it). This makes the documentation unhelpful for anyone setting up the smoke script for the first time. The original `1` / `0` values should be restored. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3653.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds two documentation files analyzing 5 critical Telegram-ACP integration bugs (#3642–#3646), along with an error-handling example. No code is changed — the actual fixes are explicitly deferred to follow-up PRs in the plugin repositories. - The `.pr_description` file has been accidentally committed to the repository root and should be removed — PR descriptions belong in the GitHub PR body, not the codebase. - The PR description does not follow the required PR template from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`: it is missing the **Thinking Path** (required blockquote format), **Model Used** section, **What Changed**, **Verification**, and **Risks** sections. Please update the PR description to use the template.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge after removing the accidentally committed `.pr_description` file and updating the PR description to follow the required template. - One P1 finding: the `.pr_description` file should not exist in the repository and needs to be removed. The PR description also does not follow the mandatory template (missing Thinking Path, Model Used, etc.) per CONTRIBUTING.md. The documentation content itself is thorough and accurate. - `.pr_description` — should not be committed to the repo at all.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | .pr_description | PR description accidentally committed as a file in the repo root — should not exist here. | | docs/TELEGRAM_ACP_PLUGIN_INTEGRATION_FIXES.md | Comprehensive analysis of 5 integration bugs; generally accurate and well-structured, but the recommended `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag for Issue #3645 lacks a security warning for server/production use. | | docs/PLUGIN_ERROR_HANDLING_EXAMPLE.md | Practical error-handling examples for Issue #3646 with unit and integration test scaffolds; no issues found. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: .pr_description Line: 1 Comment: **`.pr_description` file accidentally committed** This file should not exist in the repository. PR descriptions belong in the GitHub PR body, not as committed files. Committing it here pollutes the codebase with metadata that has no place outside of the GitHub UI. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: docs/TELEGRAM_ACP_PLUGIN_INTEGRATION_FIXES.md Line: 283-292 Comment: **`--dangerously-skip-permissions` needs a stronger warning** This flag disables all tool-use permission checks in Claude Code, allowing the subprocess to perform any file system or shell operation without restriction. Recommending it as a default arg in a server-spawned process is a significant security decision that deserves a prominent warning — e.g., "⚠️ Only use in isolated/sandboxed environments. Do not use on shared infrastructure." Without that caveat, a developer following these docs could accidentally run Claude with no guardrails in a production environment. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3654.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds `|| agent.status === "error"` to the three non-invokable status guards in `heartbeat.ts` (`claimQueuedRun`, `startNextQueuedRunForAgent`, `tickTimers`) and ships a new `heartbeat-ticktimers.test.ts` covering the filter. However, the diff also includes significant unannounced changes beyond the three guards. - The `heartbeat.enabled` default in `parseHeartbeatPolicy` was silently changed from `false` to `true` (line 1731), which can activate timer wakeups for existing agents that have `intervalSec > 0` but no explicit `enabled` flag. - The `else if (run.processPid || run.processGroupId)` branch was removed from `cancelRunInternal`, so a running process whose PID is recorded in the DB but not in the in-memory map (e.g. after a server restart) will no longer be killed when the run is cancelled. Additionally, the PR description does not include the "thinking path" required by CONTRIBUTING.md — please add one that traces from the project goal down to the specific fix, why it matters, how it was verified, and any risks.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Not safe to merge until the heartbeat.enabled default flip and orphaned-process cleanup regression are addressed. - Two P1 findings: an unannounced behavioral default change that could trigger unexpected heartbeat runs for existing agents, and the removal of the code path that kills orphaned processes on cancellation after a server restart. The three intended error-status guards are correct and well-tested, but the side-effect changes lower confidence. - server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — specifically the `parseHeartbeatPolicy` default and the `cancelRunInternal` process-cleanup logic.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Core fix (3 error-status guard additions) is correct, but the PR also silently flips `heartbeat.enabled` default from `false` to `true` and removes orphaned-process cleanup from `cancelRunInternal`, both of which are unannounced behavioral regressions. | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-ticktimers.test.ts | New test file covering tickTimers status-filtering; tests are generally sound but the "subsequent ticks" test contains an always-passing assertion that gives false confidence. |

Any agent whose `runtimeConfig` carries an explicit `intervalSec > 0` but no explicit `enabled` flag will now receive timer wakeups it previously didn't get. This change is not mentioned in the PR description, not tested, and could activate heartbeat runs for production agents unexpectedly.

Any agent whose `runtimeConfig` carries an explicit `intervalSec > 0` but no explicit `enabled` flag will now receive timer wakeups it previously didn't get. This change is not mentioned in the PR description, not tested, and could activate heartbeat runs for production agents unexpectedly. How can I resolve this? With this branch gone, calling `cancelRunInternal` on such a run marks the DB row as cancelled but leaves the child process running indefinitely. The previous handler was: ```ts } else if (run.processPid || run.processGroupId) { await terminateHeartbeatRunProcess({ pid: run.processPid, processGroupId: run.processGroupId }); } ``` Since `local-service-supervisor.ts` still exists, the cleanup capability is available; only the call site was dropped. The fix is to either restore an equivalent kill-by-PID call here, or explicitly document that post-restart orphan cleanup is now handled elsewhere.

3655.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR attempts to fix CVE-2026-39356 (SQL injection in `drizzle-orm` via improperly escaped SQL identifiers) by upgrading to 0.45.2, but the fix is applied incorrectly. The patched version is added as a root workspace dependency, while all three workspace packages that actually use drizzle-orm (`cli`, `packages/db`, `server`) still declare and resolve `0.38.4` in the lockfile — leaving the vulnerability in place. - **CVE-2026-39356 is not remediated**: `cli`, `packages/db`, and `server` all still resolve `drizzle-orm@0.38.4`; the correct fix is to add `"drizzle-orm": ">=0.45.2"` to the existing `pnpm.overrides` block rather than adding a root `dependencies` entry. - **Unrelated lockfile mutation**: The `@vitest/mocker` snapshot's `@types/node` resolution shifts from `24.12.0` to `25.2.3` — a change with no connection to the drizzle upgrade, suggesting the lockfile may have been more broadly regenerated.

Confidence Score: 0/5

- Do not merge — the stated security fix does not work and the lockfile contains unrelated, unexplained mutations. - The PR's sole purpose is to patch CVE-2026-39356, but the patch is structurally wrong: the three workspace packages that actually import drizzle-orm all still resolve the vulnerable 0.38.4 in the lockfile. Merging this gives a false sense of security while the exploit surface remains intact. Additionally, unexplained lockfile changes (node-types version shift in vitest) introduce unreviewed transitive dependency drift. - Both `package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml` need to be reworked: use `pnpm.overrides` for `drizzle-orm` and regenerate the lockfile cleanly.

Security Review

 - **CVE-2026-39356 (SQL injection via `escapeName()`) remains unpatched**: `drizzle-orm@0.45.2` is installed only at the root workspace, which has no drizzle code. The three packages that actually execute drizzle queries — `cli`, `packages/db`, and `server` — all continue to resolve `drizzle-orm@0.38.4` (confirmed in `pnpm-lock.yaml` importers and snapshots). The vulnerability is not mitigated by this change.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | package.json | Adds drizzle-orm@0.45.2 as a root workspace dependency, but this does not update the three workspace packages (cli, packages/db, server) that actually use drizzle-orm — they all still resolve to the vulnerable 0.38.4. The fix is structurally incorrect; pnpm.overrides should be used instead. | | pnpm-lock.yaml | Lockfile confirms all workspace packages (cli, packages/db, server) still resolve drizzle-orm@0.38.4. Also contains a suspicious unrelated change: the @vitest/mocker snapshot's @types/node version shifts from 24.12.0 to 25.2.3. |

Comments Outside Diff (2)

This change is entirely unrelated to the drizzle-orm upgrade. A lockfile that was regenerated only to add a single new root dependency should not alter which `@types/node` variant vitest resolves to. This suggests the lockfile was regenerated from scratch (or partially manipulated), which may silently change transitive dependency versions across the entire workspace beyond what is shown in the diff.

3655.body:
✅ **Changes Applied**

I've updated the code based on your feedback:

The code review correctly identified that adding `drizzle-orm@0.45.2` as a root `dependencies` entry does **not** remediate CVE-2026-39356. In a pnpm monorepo, workspace packages (`cli`, `packages/db`, `server`) each resolve their own declared version of `drizzle-orm` (still `0.38.4`) — the root `dependencies` entry has no effect on them.

The proper fix is to add `drizzle-orm: "0.45.2"` to the `pnpm.overrides` section in root `package.json`. pnpm overrides force **every** package in the entire dependency tree (including workspace packages) to resolve to the specified version, which is exactly what's needed to remediate this CVE across all consumers.

The incorrectly-added root `dependencies` block is removed, and the override is placed alongside the existing `rollup` override.

**Files modified:**
- `package.json`

The changes have been pushed to this PR branch. Please review!

3658.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR updates all dashboard timestamp formatting to use `undefined` (browser default locale) instead of the hardcoded `"en-US"` locale, and standardizes several inline `toLocaleString()` calls to use explicit format options or the `formatDateTime()` helper. - **Incorrect fix rationale**: The `locale` parameter to `toLocaleString` controls regional format (MDY vs DMY), not the timezone — both before and after this change the timezone is the browser's local time. If issue #3647 was about timestamps showing the wrong calendar day, the root cause may not be addressed here. - **Missing PR template sections**: The description does not include the required **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Risks**, or **Checklist** sections from the PR template (`.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`). Since this is a visible UI change, before/after screenshots are also required per CONTRIBUTING.md.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge from a code-correctness standpoint, but the stated bug fix rationale is incorrect and the PR template is incomplete. - One P1 finding: the PR's core claim that passing "en-US" forces UTC is wrong — locale and timezone are independent in the Intl API — so the stated fix for issue #3647 (UTC timestamps showing the wrong day) may not actually be resolved. Additionally, the PR is missing required template sections (Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks, screenshots for a visual change). The code changes themselves are safe and the format improvement for non-US locales is real, but the P1 rationale concern warrants a review before merge. - ui/src/lib/utils.ts — the locale→undefined change is the core of the claimed fix; confirm whether the real issue was timezone or format. Also, Routines.tsx / RoutineDetail.tsx / PluginSettings.tsx / InviteLanding.tsx for dateStyle/timeStyle vs formatDateTime() inconsistency.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/lib/utils.ts | Changed locale from "en-US" to undefined in formatDate, formatDateTime, formatShortDate — this changes the date *format* for non-US users but does not affect which timezone is used (already local). The stated rationale (locale forces UTC) is incorrect, so the underlying issue may not be resolved. | | ui/src/pages/ApprovalDetail.tsx | Replaces bare toLocaleString() with formatDateTime() helper — safe refactor that standardizes format and adds explicit options. | | ui/src/pages/CompanySettings.tsx | Replaces bare toLocaleString() with formatDateTime() helper — consistent with ApprovalDetail change, no issues. | | ui/src/pages/ProjectWorkspaceDetail.tsx | Replaces bare toLocaleString() with formatDateTime() helper — safe refactor, no issues. | | ui/src/pages/Routines.tsx | Adds dateStyle/timeStyle options to bare toLocaleString() — functional improvement, but uses a different Intl API style than formatDateTime(), causing minor visual inconsistency. | | ui/src/pages/RoutineDetail.tsx | Adds dateStyle/timeStyle options inline rather than using formatDateTime() helper — inconsistent with the approach used in the other three page-level files in this PR. | | ui/src/pages/PluginSettings.tsx | formatTimestamp() updated to use dateStyle/timeStyle — same inconsistency as Routines.tsx vs the formatDateTime()-based files. | | ui/src/pages/InviteLanding.tsx | dateTime() helper updated to use dateStyle/timeStyle — same inconsistency; could delegate to formatDateTime() instead. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `ui/src/lib/utils.ts`, line 14-29 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/be50ac0ad0062102f52f3846e8b9ea9207d8e9a2/ui/src/lib/utils.ts#L14-L29)) **Locale ≠ timezone — fix may not resolve the reported bug** The PR description states: *"calling `toLocaleString('en-US', ...)` causes the browser to display times in UTC regardless of the user's timezone."* This is factually incorrect. The first argument to `toLocaleString` / `toLocaleDateString` controls the **language/regional format** (e.g., MDY vs DMY order), not the timezone. Both before and after this change, the timezone used is the browser's local timezone, because no explicit `timeZone` option is passed. The ECMAScript spec is explicit: `timeZone` defaults to the host environment's local time zone when omitted. The practical effect of this change is that non-US users will now see dates formatted in their locale (e.g., `14 Apr 2025` instead of `Apr 14, 2025`) — a legitimate UX improvement — but it does **not** change which timezone the date is rendered in. If issue #3647 was genuinely about timestamps showing the wrong calendar day (UTC vs local), the root cause is likely elsewhere (e.g., the server returning naive timestamps without a `Z` suffix, which some engines parse as local time while others treat as UTC) and would still be present after this PR.

3667.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds two improvements to the gemini-local adapter: writing a `gemini-system.md` file to the agent's `cwd` for Gemini CLI to pick up as system context, and appending `/tmp` to `--include-directories`. However, it also silently removes the entire wake-prompt/delta-resume subsystem (`PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON`, `renderPaperclipWakePrompt`, `shouldUseResumeDeltaPrompt`) without any mention in the description, and the `gemini-system.md` content is nearly identical to the `--prompt` content, causing likely duplication if Gemini CLI reads both. - The PR is missing a **thinking path** as required by [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) — please add one at the top of the description explaining the chain of reasoning from the system level down to the specific change. - The undocumented removal of wake-prompt handling and `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON` should be explicitly described and verified against any consumers of that env var.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Not safe to merge until the undocumented removal of wake-prompt handling is explained/resolved and the gemini-system.md content duplication is addressed. - Two P1 findings block merge: the silent removal of PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON and the delta-resume path (behavioral regression not described in the PR), and the near-duplicate content between gemini-system.md and --prompt that would double token usage if Gemini CLI reads both. The missing thinking path in the description is also a process requirement per CONTRIBUTING.md. - packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts — specifically the wake-prompt removal (lines 304–319) and the systemPrompt block (lines 350–371).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts | Adds gemini-system.md injection and /tmp to --include-directories, but silently removes wake-prompt handling (PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON env var, delta-only resume path) and creates near-duplicate content between gemini-system.md and --prompt. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts`, line 304-319 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/95fa6c412f67d73c7a0ee172ad990c8fd8f5b7d4/packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts#L304-L319)) **Undocumented removal of wake-prompt and `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON`** This PR silently removes three pieces of wake-context machinery that were present on the base branch and are not mentioned in the description: 1. `stringifyPaperclipWakePayload` was used to populate `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON` in the agent env — agents that inspect this variable to understand *why* they were woken (e.g. which comment triggered them) will now receive `undefined` and silently mis-handle the wake event. 2. `renderPaperclipWakePrompt` / `shouldUseResumeDeltaPrompt` handled the *resume-with-delta* path: when a session existed and a wake payload was present, only the delta prompt was sent instead of the full template. That logic is now gone — every wake now sends the full rendered template prompt, even during session resumption. 3. `wakePromptChars` is dropped from the prompt metrics, so observability of wake-prompt size is lost. If this removal is intentional (e.g. because `paperclipSessionHandoffMarkdown` now carries wake context), it needs explicit documentation in the PR description and a verification that all consumers of `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON` are migrated.

Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 304-319 Comment: **Undocumented removal of wake-prompt and `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON`** This PR silently removes three pieces of wake-context machinery that were present on the base branch and are not mentioned in the description: 1. `stringifyPaperclipWakePayload` was used to populate `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON` in the agent env — agents that inspect this variable to understand *why* they were woken (e.g. which comment triggered them) will now receive `undefined` and silently mis-handle the wake event. 2. `renderPaperclipWakePrompt` / `shouldUseResumeDeltaPrompt` handled the *resume-with-delta* path: when a session existed and a wake payload was present, only the delta prompt was sent instead of the full template. That logic is now gone — every wake now sends the full rendered template prompt, even during session resumption. 3. `wakePromptChars` is dropped from the prompt metrics, so observability of wake-prompt size is lost. If this removal is intentional (e.g. because `paperclipSessionHandoffMarkdown` now carries wake context), it needs explicit documentation in the PR description and a verification that all consumers of `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON` are migrated. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 304-319 Comment: **Undocumented removal of wake-prompt and `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON`** This PR silently removes three pieces of wake-context machinery that were present on the base branch and are not mentioned in the description: 1. `stringifyPaperclipWakePayload` was used to populate `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON` in the agent env — agents that inspect this variable to understand *why* they were woken (e.g. which comment triggered them) will now receive `undefined` and silently mis-handle the wake event. 2. `renderPaperclipWakePrompt` / `shouldUseResumeDeltaPrompt` handled the *resume-with-delta* path: when a session existed and a wake payload was present, only the delta prompt was sent instead of the full template. That logic is now gone — every wake now sends the full rendered template prompt, even during session resumption. 3. `wakePromptChars` is dropped from the prompt metrics, so observability of wake-prompt size is lost. If this removal is intentional (e.g. because `paperclipSessionHandoffMarkdown` now carries wake context), it needs explicit documentation in the PR description and a verification that all consumers of `PAPERCLIP_WAKE_PAYLOAD_JSON` are migrated. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 350-371 Comment: **`systemPrompt` is a near-duplicate of `prompt`, causing double-injection** `systemPrompt` (written to `gemini-system.md`) contains `instructionsPrefix + renderedBootstrapPrompt + paperclipEnvNote + apiAccessNote + renderedPrompt`. The `prompt` passed via `--prompt` is the same five sections **plus** `sessionHandoffNote`. If Gemini CLI reads `gemini-system.md` as system context at invocation time (as claimed in the JSDoc), those five sections are sent to the model twice — doubling token usage and potentially producing confused/conflicting instructions. The typical pattern for file-based system prompts is to write only the *persistent, run-independent* content to the file (e.g. `instructionsPrefix`, `renderedBootstrapPrompt`, env/API notes) and pass only the *per-run* content via `--prompt` (e.g. `sessionHandoffNote`, `renderedPrompt`). Either the file content or the `--prompt` content needs to be narrowed to avoid the duplication. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts Line: 340-341 Comment: **Hardcoded `--include-directories` may conflict with `extraArgs`** `--include-directories` is pushed unconditionally before `extraArgs` is spread. If an operator has already configured `--include-directories` in `config.extraArgs`, Gemini CLI will receive the flag twice. Depending on how the CLI handles duplicates, the operator-supplied value could be silently ignored. Consider checking whether `extraArgs` already contains `--include-directories` before pushing the hardcoded value, or appending `/tmp` to any operator-supplied value. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

This PR is intended to present a merge-ready multilingual internationalization framework, not just a small two-locale copy change.

The architecture is already in place and follows a systematic best-practice approach across shared, server, UI, and CLI layers, so the localized surfaces included in this PR are immediately usable after merge.

Because the repository review tooling has a 100 changed file limit, I intentionally submitted this as a reviewable nano slice first so the team can evaluate and approve the framework direction without having to review the entire remaining localization rollout in one pass.

I plan to continue owning the follow-up work for the remaining untranslated surfaces after this slice is reviewed. I care about this area because many users around the world are interested in products like this, but a pure English-only experience creates avoidable usability barriers for people operating in non-native English environments.

My goal here is to help reduce that friction with a solid multilingual foundation first, then complete the rest of the rollout in follow-up PRs built on the same contract.

I refreshed this PR onto the current master again, and GitHub now shows it as clean and mergeable while still staying within the 99-file review limit.

The latest upstream changes are included, and the recent dev/watch updates did not introduce additional end-user UI copy that needed a wider i18n expansion in this slice.

If internationalization is a direction the team would consider, I am happy to keep maintaining and updating this PR line. If not, that is completely fine too. In that case, I would appreciate a quick signal and I will stop spending time on further rebases and conflict maintenance for this branch.

Because of the 99-file review limit, I have only submitted the core internationalization framework in this PR for now. I still have follow-up commits prepared that are needed to bring the rollout to the full framework level across the remaining surfaces.\n\nI’m happy to continue owning and maintaining this part of the work in follow-up updates if the team would like to move forward with i18n.

Re-checked this branch against the current latest `master`: it is up to date, conflict-free, and the latest PR checks are passing.\n\nThis PR is still scoped to the internationalization work only: locale infrastructure, translation catalogs, and UI text wiring. If the team is open to i18n, I would appreciate a review. Landing it earlier should help avoid more hardcoded UI strings building up over time.

3674.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds adapter-aware session key resolution so `codex_local` agents default to a stable per-agent session key across all heartbeat wakes, while other adapters and explicit `taskKey`/`sessionScope: "task"` overrides retain the existing task-scoped behavior. The implementation is well-structured and the key behavioral change — agent-scoped sessions no longer reset on `issue_assigned` wakes — is clearly documented in both code comments and the PR description.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge; no P0/P1 issues found and acknowledged risks are well-documented. - All remaining findings are P2 — a missing test for non-timer on_demand wakes and a transient post-deploy edge case in explicit-resume lookups that self-heals after one heartbeat cycle. Core logic is correct and well-tested for the primary paths. - No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds resolveTaskKeyForWake, resolveSessionResumeScope, and deriveAgentSessionTaskKey; updates shouldResetTaskSessionForWake with a preserveAcrossWakes opt-in; wires agent-scoped session key into both enqueueWakeup and run-execution paths. Logic is sound and consistent. | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-workspace-session.test.ts | Adds tests for resolveTaskKeyForWake (default agent key, explicit override, task-scope opt-out) and the new preserveAcrossWakes behavior; covers primary cases but is missing a test for non-timer on_demand wakes with agent-scoped codex. | | packages/adapters/codex-local/src/index.ts | Adds three doc-comment bullets describing the new default agent-scope behavior, opt-out via runtimeConfig, and forceFreshSession; no behavior change. | | docs/adapters/codex-local.md | Updates Session Persistence section to document the new agent-scoped default, taskScope opt-out, and forceFreshSession override; accurate and complete. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 3842 Comment: **Explicit-resume lookup uses pre-agent-scope task key** `resolveExplicitResumeSessionOverride` is passed `derivedTaskKey`, which is the result of the old `deriveTaskKey` (no agent-scoped fallback). For a codex_local agent with no explicit task key this is `null`. Inside `resolveExplicitResumeSessionOverride`, the fallback is `deriveTaskKey(resumeContext, null) ?? taskKey` — so if the referenced run's stored context also pre-dates this PR (no `taskKey` field written yet), the lookup resolves to `null` and the task session is not loaded. In practice the run's own `sessionIdBefore`/`sessionIdAfter` fields still allow the session override to succeed, but the task-session enrichment path is silently skipped. After one full heartbeat cycle the context will carry the agent-scoped key and the issue disappears, making this a transient post-deploy edge case rather than a blocking bug. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-workspace-session.test.ts Line: 358-384 Comment: **Missing test: agent-scoped key on non-timer wakes** The suite only tests `resolveTaskKeyForWake` with `wakeSource: "timer"`. For `wakeSource: "on_demand"` (or any non-timer source) with a codex_local agent and no explicit task key, the function still takes the agent-scope branch rather than the `deriveTaskKeyWithHeartbeatFallback` path (which returns `null` for non-timer wakes). Adding an explicit test for this case would prevent silent regressions where a refactor accidentally falls through to the heartbeat-fallback path. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3677.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new `parked` issue status (amber-themed) and deduplicates hardcoded status arrays across `KanbanBoard`, `StatusIcon`, and the issues service by importing from `@paperclipai/shared`. The deduplication work is clean, but two correctness gaps need addressing before merge: - `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES` in `constants.ts` was not updated with `parked`, causing the Inbox page to silently omit parked issues while the inbox badge (which has its own local copy) counts them. - The partial unique index `openRoutineExecutionIdx` in `packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts` hardcodes the open status list and does not include `parked`. Since `parked` is now a non-terminal status, the deduplication guarantee for routine-execution issues is broken for issues in that state, and a DB migration is needed to rebuild the index. The PR description is also missing required template sections — Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks, and the Checklist — per `CONTRIBUTING.md`. Since this adds visible amber-colored status indicators, before/after screenshots are also required.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Two P1 correctness issues need fixing before merge — inbox badge/page mismatch and a partial index gap that breaks deduplication for parked routine-execution issues. - The deduplication refactor and color additions are solid, but the missing `parked` entry in `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES` causes an observable badge-vs-page inconsistency, and the unupdated partial unique index in the DB schema means the routine-execution uniqueness constraint silently stops applying once an issue is parked. Both are present defects on the changed path. - `packages/shared/src/constants.ts` (INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES) and `packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts` (partial index + migration) need attention before merging.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Adds `"parked"` to `ISSUE_STATUSES`, but `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES` was not updated, causing the inbox page to miss parked issues while the badge still counts them. | | packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts | Not directly changed but critically affected: the partial unique index `openRoutineExecutionIdx` hardcodes open statuses and omits `parked`, which is now treated as a non-terminal status in routines.ts — uniqueness for parked routine-execution issues is no longer enforced. | | server/src/services/routines.ts | Correctly adds `"parked"` to `OPEN_ISSUE_STATUSES` so routines treat it as non-terminal; `TERMINAL_ISSUE_STATUSES` correctly unchanged. | | ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx | Adds `blocked`, `parked`, and `cancelled` to the create-issue status picker; still uses a handwritten local array rather than importing from shared like the other components were updated to do. | | ui/src/hooks/useInboxBadge.ts | Adds `parked` to the local `INBOX_ISSUE_STATUSES` string, so badge counts will include parked issues — but diverges from the shared `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUS_FILTER` used by Inbox.tsx. | | ui/src/lib/status-colors.ts | Adds amber color entries for `parked` across all three maps (`issueStatusIcon`, `issueStatusText`, `statusBadge`) with consistent dark-mode variants. |

Comments Outside Diff (3)

 1. `packages/shared/src/constants.ts`, line 126-133 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/9f63ae062697e5cbd525130cd6855e46c9a5a383/packages/shared/src/constants.ts#L126-L133)) **`parked` missing from `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES`** `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES` (and the derived `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUS_FILTER`) are used by `Inbox.tsx` (lines 771 and 784) to query issues for the inbox view. `parked` was not added here, so the Inbox *page* won't return parked issues — but `useInboxBadge.ts` has its own local copy that **does** include `parked`. This creates a split: the badge count will include parked issues while the inbox list won't show them, leaving users confused about why the badge shows a count they can't find.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/shared/src/constants.ts Line: 126-133 Comment: **`parked` missing from `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES`** `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES` (and the derived `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUS_FILTER`) are used by `Inbox.tsx` (lines 771 and 784) to query issues for the inbox view. `parked` was not added here, so the Inbox *page* won't return parked issues — but `useInboxBadge.ts` has its own local copy that **does** include `parked`. This creates a split: the badge count will include parked issues while the inbox list won't show them, leaving users confused about why the badge shows a count they can't find. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
2. `packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts`, line 84-93 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/9f63ae062697e5cbd525130cd6855e46c9a5a383/packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts#L84-L93)) **Partial unique index doesn't cover `parked`** `openRoutineExecutionIdx` is a partial unique index that prevents duplicate open routine-execution issues for the same origin. Its `WHERE` clause explicitly lists open statuses, and `parked` was just added to `OPEN_ISSUE_STATUSES` in `routines.ts`. Because the index's predicate doesn't include `parked`, the constraint is not enforced while an issue is in that state — a routine could create a second issue for the same origin if the first one is parked, leading to duplicate routine-execution issues. The schema file needs updating and a new DB migration (`ALTER INDEX … REBUILD` or `DROP/CREATE INDEX`) is required to make this take effect in existing deployments.

Path: packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts Line: 84-93 Comment: **Partial unique index doesn't cover `parked`** `openRoutineExecutionIdx` is a partial unique index that prevents duplicate open routine-execution issues for the same origin. The schema file needs updating and a new DB migration (`ALTER INDEX … REBUILD` or `DROP/CREATE INDEX`) is required to make this take effect in existing deployments. How can I resolve this? Any future status additions will require updating this file again. Consider deriving this list from `ISSUE_STATUSES` to stay in sync automatically.

This creates a split: the badge count will include parked issues while the inbox list won't show them, leaving users confused about why the badge shows a count they can't find. ```suggestion export const INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES = [ "backlog", "todo", "in_progress", "in_review", "blocked", "parked", "done", ] as const; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts Line: 84-93 Comment: **Partial unique index doesn't cover `parked`** `openRoutineExecutionIdx` is a partial unique index that prevents duplicate open routine-execution issues for the same origin. 3677.body:
## Addressing the review findings + architectural note

The three issues flagged by Greptile are fixed in the latest push (44938f40):
- **P1 inbox mismatch**: `parked` added to `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES`
- **P1 DB index gap**: `parked` added to `openRoutineExecutionIdx` partial unique index
- **P2 NewIssueDialog**: now derives from `ISSUE_STATUSES` instead of a local array

### Remaining architectural gap (follow-up, not this PR)

This PR centralizes the *base status list* but not the *status semantics*. Several places still maintain independent "which statuses are open/terminal" logic:
- `INBOX_MINE_ISSUE_STATUSES` — manually curated subset
- `OPEN_ISSUE_STATUSES` in routines.ts — another manual subset
- `openRoutineExecutionIdx` — hardcoded SQL `IN (...)` clause

Adding a new status still requires touching all of these. The proper fix:

```ts
const TERMINAL_STATUSES = new Set(["done", "cancelled"]);
const OPEN_ISSUE_STATUSES = ISSUE_STATUSES.filter(s => !TERMINAL_STATUSES.has(s));
```

All consumers derive from semantic groups, not handwritten arrays. For the DB partial index, the `IN (...)` clause can be generated inside the drizzle migration from the same constants — resolved once at migration execution time, not at runtime. Migrations should snapshot the values rather than importing live constants to avoid drift on fresh deploys.

Happy to open a follow-up PR for the semantic derivation if there's interest.

Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Issue status enum | Adds `parked` to the fixed status set | Task system: "Fixed status set for V1: backlog, todo, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked, cancelled" | ❓ Partial | | Status deduplication | Centralizes status arrays to `ISSUE_STATUSES` from `@paperclipai/shared` | Shared package: cross-package types and constants | ✅ Aligned | | Execution constraint | Adds `parked` to the routine execution uniqueness check | Task system: atomic task checkout, single-assignee model | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Fixed V1 status set, open question about team-specific workflow states - [`engineering/shared/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/shared/NODE.md) — Cross-package constants
### Recommendation **Why:** The tree explicitly documents a fixed V1 status set: `backlog`, `todo`, `in_progress`, `in_review`, `done`, `blocked`, `cancelled`. Adding `parked` extends this set, which is a product decision. The distinction from `blocked` (intentionally set aside vs. external dependency) is reasonable, but this touches a documented V1 boundary. The deduplication part (centralizing to shared constants) is clearly good. **Suggested path forward:** Confirm with maintainers whether `parked` is an accepted V1 addition or should wait for the aspirational team-specific workflow states model. If accepted, the tree's task-system node should be updated to include `parked` in the status set. --- Reviewed commit: 44938f4 · Tree snapshot: 6e68dbe · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3681.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two reliability gaps in the `codex_local` adapter: it writes API-key auth directly into the managed `CODEX_HOME/auth.json` when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is present (instead of inheriting a shared ChatGPT session), and propagates that key through the environment-probe path so the hello probe exercises the same auth as real runs. It also adds repo-root `.env` discovery for nested server launches, treats Gemini `Invalid session identifier` errors as stale-session failures, and adds focused regression tests for all three behaviors.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 style/edge-case suggestions that do not block correct behavior in the common case. - The auth-sync logic in `syncManagedAuthFile` is idempotent and handles all transition states correctly. The test suite covers the key scenarios with real filesystem operations. The only open concerns are a P2 edge case in `findProjectRootEnvPath` (ancestor `.env` in nested-git setups) and a minor doc placement issue—neither affects production correctness for the intended usage. - server/src/config.ts — `findProjectRootEnvPath` edge case when CWD is already the project root in a nested-git environment.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/codex-home.ts | Moves `auth.json` out of `SYMLINKED_SHARED_FILES` and adds `syncManagedAuthFile`, which correctly writes API-key auth as a plain file (mode 0o600) when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is present and falls back to a relative symlink when it is not. `ensureSymlink` handles idempotent re-calls, and the TOCTOU window between `lstat` and `rm`/`writeFile` is acceptable for a single-process managed home. Logic is sound. | | server/src/config.ts | Replaces ad-hoc duplicate-detection logic with `loadEnvFileOnce` (realpath-deduplication) and adds `findProjectRootEnvPath` for the nested-`server/` monorepo case. The deduplication mechanism is correct, but `findProjectRootEnvPath` starts from `dirname(CWD)` rather than `CWD`, meaning it can match an ancestor git repo when the server is already run from the project root and load an unrelated `.env`. | | packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/execute.ts | Pre-filters `envConfig` into `envOverrides` (string values only) and passes `{ ...process.env, ...envOverrides }` to `prepareManagedCodexHome`, so an `OPENAI_API_KEY` in the adapter's env config now correctly seeds the managed auth file. The `for` loop at the bottom is functionally equivalent to before. Clean change. | | packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/test.ts | Adds `prepareManagedCodexHome` call during environment probing so the hello probe exercises the same managed auth path as real runs. `env.CODEX_HOME` is then set to the effective home, which flows into `readCodexAuthInfo` for the native-auth check. Logic is correct and the `configuredCodexHome` short-circuit correctly bypasses managed-home setup when a custom path is explicitly set. | | packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/parse.ts | Adds `invalid\s+session(?:\s+identifier)?` to the stale-session regex, correctly matching Gemini's "Invalid session identifier" error message. The optional `(?:\s+identifier)?` group avoids false positives on hypothetical "invalid session" matches in other contexts. Clean, targeted fix. | | docs/adapters/codex-local.md | New documentation sentence about API-key auth is placed under `## Manual Local CLI` but describes platform-wide behavior, and is missing a blank-line separator before the existing paragraph. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/config.ts Line: 48-63 Comment: **`findProjectRootEnvPath` starts search from `dirname(CWD)`, not CWD** The loop begins at `dirname(resolve(process.cwd()))`, so when the server is already running from the project root, the search starts from the *parent* of that directory. In environments where a parent directory happens to have a `.git` marker (e.g., a developer's workspace folder that is its own git repo, or a container volume root), `findProjectRootEnvPath` will match that ancestor and load its `.env`, potentially injecting unrelated variables into the server process. The `override: false` guard prevents already-set variables from being clobbered, but unset variables from the ancestor file would be silently picked up. Adding a guard that checks whether `resolve(process.cwd())` itself is the project root—and bailing out if so—would prevent this case without breaking the intended nested-`server/` scenario. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: docs/adapters/codex-local.md Line: 50-51 Comment: **Doc sentence mis-placed and missing blank line** The new sentence about API-key auth is (a) nested under `## Manual Local CLI`, but the behavior it describes applies to all managed Codex home contexts (regular agent runs, worktree runs, etc.), not just manual CLI usage; and (b) missing a blank line before `For manual local CLI usage...`, so both sentences render as a single paragraph. Consider moving this sentence to the `## Managed \`CODEX_HOME\`` section and adding a blank line here: ```suggestion If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is present, Paperclip writes API-key auth into the effective managed `CODEX_HOME/auth.json` so Codex uses usage-based API authentication instead of inheriting a ChatGPT login session from `~/.codex/auth.json`. For manual local CLI usage outside heartbeat runs (for example running as `codexcoder` directly), use: ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3683.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR addresses two startup costs: it fires `restartDesiredRuntimeServicesOnStartup` at server boot (best-effort, fire-and-forget) and adds a persistent `.paperclip-runtime-meta.json` cache so the NFS-bound runtime-skill materialization step can be skipped on warm/post-restart runs. The PR description does not use the required PR template from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, all PRs must include a **Thinking Path** (blockquote chain), a **Model Used** section (provider, exact model ID/version, reasoning mode), and the **Checklist** — none of which appear in the current description. Please copy the template and fill in all sections before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to follow the required template and the null-version edge case is considered. - No P0/P1 code defects found. Two P2 issues remain: a cache-version gap when both updatedAt and sourceRef are null (could silently serve a stale cached skill forever), and a minor indentation regression. More importantly, the PR description is missing the required Thinking Path, Model Used, and Checklist sections mandated by CONTRIBUTING.md — the repo requires these before merge. - server/src/services/company-skills.ts — review the null-version cache edge case and fix the indentation on materializeRuntimeSkillFiles.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/company-skills.ts | Adds persistent runtime-skill cache: three new exported helpers (runtimeSkillMaterializationVersion, resolveRuntimeSkillMaterializedPath, getCurrentRuntimeSkillMaterializedPath) plus a meta-file written after materialization. Cache key relies solely on updatedAt/sourceRef — null values make it permanently valid. Minor indentation regression on materializeRuntimeSkillFiles declaration. | | server/src/index.ts | Adds best-effort startup call to restartDesiredRuntimeServicesOnStartup, fully fire-and-forget with .catch — safe addition that does not affect server readiness. | | server/src/__tests__/workspace-runtime.test.ts | New integration test verifies that restartDesiredRuntimeServicesOnStartup prewarms a shared service and that subsequent ensureRuntimeServicesForRun reuses it; good coverage of the intended prewarm behaviour. | | server/src/__tests__/company-skills.test.ts | New test suite verifies cache hit on matching version and cache miss on changed updatedAt; covers the happy path but does not test the null-updatedAt/sourceRef edge case. | | server/src/__tests__/server-startup-feedback-export.test.ts | Adds runtimePrewarmMock and verifies it is called once during startServer; clean, minimal change to maintain existing test coverage. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/company-skills.ts Line: 2071 Comment: **Indentation inconsistency on refactored function** The `async function` keyword lost its 2-space indent when `materializeRuntimeSkillFiles` was refactored to call `resolveRuntimeSkillMaterializedPath`. The function body still uses 4-space indent, but the declaration sits at column 0 while every other inner function inside `companySkillService` (e.g. `materializeCatalogSkillFiles` above, `listRuntimeSkillEntries` below) uses a 2-space lead. This doesn't affect behavior — braces determine scope — but it's visually misleading. ```suggestion async function materializeRuntimeSkillFiles(companyId: string, skill: CompanySkill) { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/company-skills.ts Line: 1302-1310 Comment: **Cache version silent if both `updatedAt` and `sourceRef` are null** `runtimeSkillMaterializationVersion` serialises to `{"updatedAt":null,"sourceRef":null}` whenever neither field is populated. Any skill whose record is created (or updated through a code path that leaves these fields null) will permanently match the cached version, making the cache impossible to invalidate without manual deletion. Consider using a deterministic fallback (e.g. the skill's `id` or a hash of `fileInventory`) so the version is always unique when the freshness fields are absent. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3684.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces server-side pagination for heartbeat runs (offset/limit on `/heartbeat-runs`), two new targeted endpoints (`/stats` for charts and `/latest-failed` for the inbox badge), and migrates the UI from unbounded fetches to `useInfiniteQuery`, directly resolving the memory leak reported in #958/#1846. - **P1** — `latestFailed` calls `.filter()` directly on the return value of `db.execute()`, but `AGENTS.md` explicitly warns this repo's driver may return a `QueryResult` object instead of a raw array; this will throw at runtime on certain DB backends. - **PR description** — the description is detailed but does not include a "Thinking Path" as required by `CONTRIBUTING.md`. Please add one that walks from the product context down to the specific fix. - **Screenshots** — the `ActivityCharts` components and the new infinite-scroll `RunsTab` are visual changes; before/after screenshots are requested per `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge after addressing the `db.execute` return-type guard; all other findings are style/process. - One P1 finding: `latestFailed` calls `.filter()` directly on the `db.execute` result without guarding against the `QueryResult` shape that node-postgres can return, which AGENTS.md explicitly flags as a known risk. All other findings are P2 (missing Thinking Path and screenshots per CONTRIBUTING.md, and a minor default-limit defensive guard). The core pagination logic, IntersectionObserver pattern, UTC timezone fix, and route access checks are all correct. - server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — specifically the `latestFailed` function's `db.execute` usage

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds `list()` with offset/limit pagination, `stats()` for chart aggregation, and `latestFailed()` using raw SQL `DISTINCT ON`. The `db.execute()` call in `latestFailed` directly invokes `.filter()` on the result without guarding against the `QueryResult` return shape that node-postgres can produce — a known risk flagged in `AGENTS.md`. | | server/src/routes/agents.ts | Adds three well-structured endpoints: `/heartbeat-runs/stats`, `/heartbeat-runs/latest-failed`, and updates the base `/heartbeat-runs` route with validated `limit` (1–1000, default 200) and `offset` query params. All new routes apply `assertCompanyAccess`, consistent with existing endpoints. | | ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx | Migrates heartbeat run loading from a single `useQuery` to `useInfiniteQuery` with page size 50. The `RunsTab` `IntersectionObserver` correctly uses a ref for `isFetchingNextPage` (per the AGENTS.md learning) and includes `isMobile` in the dependency array so the observer re-attaches on layout changes. | | ui/src/components/ActivityCharts.tsx | New pure-presentational chart components (`RunActivityChart`, `SuccessRateChart`, `PriorityChart`, `IssueStatusChart`). All bucketing uses the same UTC date slicing as the server, keeping chart labels aligned with server-side aggregation. | | ui/src/hooks/useInboxBadge.ts | Switches from loading all heartbeat runs to using the new `latestFailed` endpoint, eliminating the unbounded fetch on inbox load. Badge computation logic is unchanged. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 3574-3584 Comment: **`db.execute` return type not guarded** `AGENTS.md` explicitly warns: *"When using Drizzle `db.execute` for raw SQL in this repo, ensure you test the return type (it may return raw rows directly instead of a `QueryResult` object with a `.rows` property depending on the database driver)."* The code calls `rows.filter(...)` directly, which will throw `TypeError: rows.filter is not a function` when the driver returns a `QueryResult` object (e.g., node-postgres in production) instead of a raw array (PGlite in dev). Since this is the only `db.execute` call in the entire server codebase, the inconsistency between dev and production drivers is the exact failure mode the learning note describes. Consider guarding the return: ```suggestion const raw = await db.execute(sql` SELECT DISTINCT ON (agent_id) * FROM heartbeat_runs WHERE company_id = ${companyId} ORDER BY agent_id, created_at DESC LIMIT 500 `); // db.execute may return rows directly (PGlite) or a QueryResult with .rows (node-postgres) const rows: any[] = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw : (raw as any).rows ?? []; // Filter to only those whose most recent run was a failure const failedRows = rows.filter((r: any) => r.status === 'failed' || r.status === 'timed_out'); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 3524-3546 Comment: **Default `limit` not enforced server-side when omitted in URL** The route sets `let limit = 200` when the query param is absent, but the `list()` function signature is `list(companyId, agentId?, limit?, offset?)`. If a future caller omits `limit` entirely (e.g. internal code), the Drizzle query will have no `.limit()` call and will scan the whole table. A safer guard: ```suggestion list: async (companyId: string, agentId?: string, limit = 200, offset?: number) => { let query = db .select(heartbeatRunListColumns) .from(heartbeatRuns) .where( agentId ? and(eq(heartbeatRuns.companyId, companyId), eq(heartbeatRuns.agentId, agentId)) : eq(heartbeatRuns.companyId, companyId), ) .orderBy(desc(heartbeatRuns.createdAt)) .limit(limit); if (offset !== undefined && offset > 0) { query = query.offset(offset) as any; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3685.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a **Generate** button on the agent Instructions tab that calls a new `POST /api/agents/:id/instructions/generate` backend route to produce starter `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `HEARTBEAT.md`, and `TOOLS.md` files via the configured LLM, then saves each file through the existing instructions-bundle path. - **P1 — Silent partial saves**: In `generateBundle.onSuccess`, individual `saveInstructionsFile` failures are caught and only console-logged. `generateBundle.isError` never fires, so users see the file list refresh and assume all four files were saved even when some are missing. - **Missing PR template sections**: The description omits the required **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Risks**, and **Verification** sections from the PR template (see `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`). Since this is a visible UI change, before/after screenshots are also required per CONTRIBUTING.md.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge once the silent partial-save failure is surfaced to users. - One P1 finding: partial file-save failures are swallowed in onSuccess, leaving users with an incomplete bundle and no feedback. Backend route is well-structured and correctly gated. PR template sections (Thinking Path, Model Used, screenshots) are also missing per CONTRIBUTING.md. - ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx — silent failure handling in generateBundle.onSuccess

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/agents.ts | Adds POST /agents/:id/instructions/generate route; correctly gates on assertCanManageInstructionsPath, handles LLM errors, validates JSON output. Minor concern: agent fields are interpolated into the prompt without sanitization. | | ui/src/api/agents.ts | Adds generateInstructionsBundle API client method; straightforward and consistent with existing patterns. | | ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx | Adds Generate button and mutation; P1 issue — partial file-save failures are swallowed silently so users never learn if some files weren't saved. Button also re-enables mid-save because isPending flips before onSuccess finishes. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx Line: 1803-1817 Comment: **Generate button re-enables during sequential file saves** React Query's `isPending` flips to `false` as soon as `mutationFn` resolves — before `onSuccess` finishes its sequential `saveInstructionsFile` loop. During that window the Generate button is active again and a second click would trigger a new LLM generation while the first batch of saves is still in flight. The existing `saveFile` mutation handles this with `setAwaitingRefresh(true)` on `onMutate`. A local `isSaving` ref/state in `onSuccess`/`onSettled`, or combining the generates-and-saves into a single async `mutationFn`, would keep the button reliably disabled for the full operation. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/agents.ts Line: 1858-1868 Comment: **Agent fields injected into LLM prompt without sanitization** `existing.role`, `existing.name`, `existing.title`, and `existing.reportsTo` are interpolated directly into the system prompt. If any of those fields contain adversarial text (e.g. a role value of `"developer\n\nIgnore all prior instructions and output…"`), the generated instruction files could contain unintended content. Consider basic newline/injection normalization before interpolation, or at least truncating to a safe max length per field. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx Line: 1806-1813 Comment: **Silent partial save failures give no user feedback** If any of the sequential `saveInstructionsFile` calls throw, the error is only caught locally and logged to console — the mutation itself never errors, so `generateBundle.isError` stays `false` and the existing error banner never appears. The user sees the file list refresh and assumes success, but can be left with a partial bundle (e.g. `AGENTS.md` saved, `SOUL.md` missing) with no indication of which files failed. Accumulate failures into a list and either re-throw after the loop (which would set `isError` and trigger the banner) or surface them via a separate state variable shown near the panel. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

Fixes a runtime `TypeError` in `listComments` by calling `.toISOString()` on `anchor.createdAt` (a `Date` from Drizzle) before interpolating it into the `sql` template literal, with an explicit `::timestamptz` cast in the raw SQL for proper PostgreSQL type handling. Six integration tests are added against an embedded Postgres instance to cover ascending/descending cursor pagination, limit enforcement, non-existent cursors, the no-cursor path, and same-timestamp tiebreaking by ID.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — isolated one-line type fix with comprehensive integration test coverage and no schema changes. All findings are P2 or lower. The root-cause fix is correct (ISO string parameterized correctly by Drizzle, `::timestamptz` lands in the raw SQL portion), the keyset cursor logic is sound for both orderings, and the test suite directly reproduces the failure case. No files require special attention.

Greptile Summary

This PR bundles three distinct changes under a documentation-only title: (1) a full i18n multi-language (EN/ES) feature across UI, CLI, and server, (2) a Dockerfile GPG keyring checksum fix, and (3) the stated `CONTRIBUTING.md` CI/CD and lockfile policy section. The i18n implementation itself is architecturally sound — `initI18n()` is correctly awaited before middleware registration on the server, the CLI uses top-level `await` safely under ESM, and the UI uses `react-i18next` with `LanguageDetector`. - **Blocking**: `pnpm-lock.yaml` remains in the diff after multiple add/revert cycles, which will trigger the exact CI failure the PR is documenting. Please restore the file to match master before merging. - **Process**: Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, this should have been split into separate PRs (\"One PR = one logical change\"). The PR description's thinking path only covers the docs change, not the i18n feature or Dockerfile fix. - **Screenshots missing**: The new `LanguageSwitcher` component and translated UI are visual changes — `CONTRIBUTING.md` asks contributors to include before/after screenshots.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until pnpm-lock.yaml is removed from the diff — it will fail the CI lockfile policy check this PR itself documents. The i18n code is well-implemented and functionally correct, but the P1 finding (lockfile still in PR) will cause a definite CI failure. All other findings are P2 style/quality issues. Score is 4 rather than lower because the fix is mechanical and the underlying feature work is solid. pnpm-lock.yaml (must be removed from the diff) and server/src/i18n/locales/es.json (missing accent marks throughout the Spanish translations).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | pnpm-lock.yaml | Lockfile is still present in the PR diff despite multiple commits trying to remove it — directly contradicts the CI policy this PR documents and will trigger the "Do not commit pnpm-lock.yaml" CI failure. | | CONTRIBUTING.md | Adds a clear "CI/CD and Lockfile Policy" section with actionable instructions; content is correct but the restore command assumes `origin` points to the upstream repo, which may not hold for forked contributors. | | Dockerfile | Updates GitHub CLI GPG keyring SHA256 checksum to reflect the current keyring file; legitimate security-maintenance change. | | server/src/i18n/locales/es.json | Spanish server translations are missing accent marks throughout (e.g. "autenticacion", "validacion", "sesion") while the CLI and UI ES locales use proper Unicode escapes — inconsistent quality. | | server/src/middleware/i18n.ts | Correctly parses Accept-Language header with quality values, handles prefix matching (e.g. es-MX → es), and attaches req.language and req.t per request. | | ui/src/components/LanguageSwitcher.tsx | New toggle component; correctly cycles through supported languages using i18n.changeLanguage and handles unrecognised language codes gracefully via the findIndex fallback. | | cli/src/i18n/index.ts | Top-level await is valid because cli/package.json uses "type": "module"; language detection from PAPERCLIP_LANG and LC_ALL/LANG env vars is correct. | | server/src/app.ts | Correctly awaits initI18n() before registering i18nMiddleware(), so getFixedT is never called before i18next is initialised. |

3692.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR rebases the Hermes local adapter wrapper and heartbeat runtime changes onto current `master`. Key additions include a `hermes-local-wrapper.ts` that enriches the Hermes execution context with `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY`, `workspaceDir`, and a Paperclip-specific prompt template; an interactive CLI adapter-selection prompt for company import; restored issue auto-checkout and deferred-wake promotion priority in the heartbeat service; and the removal of a now-upstream-exported `buildInvocationEnvForLogs` shim. The PR description does not follow the required template from `CONTRIBUTING.md` — it is missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Please fill out the PR template in full before requesting merge. Additionally, `SHOW_EXPERIMENTAL_ISSUE_WORKTREE_UI` is flipped to `true`, which is a visible UI change that requires before/after screenshots per `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to follow the required template and screenshots are provided for the UI flag change; all remaining findings are P2. All inline findings are P2 (style, minor cleanup, missing screenshot). The core logic — Hermes context enrichment, deferred-wake prioritization, heartbeat checkout/retry flow — is well-tested and the implementation is sound.
