Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a new `@paperclipai/adapter-nanoclaw-gateway` package that dispatches tasks to NanoClaw's Docker-containerized Claude agents (Dozer, Scout, Myco, Sally) via a fire-and-forget HTTP POST to `/paperclip/wakeup`, and wires it into the server, CLI, and UI registries. - **P1 — agent selection silently dropped on create:** `buildNanoClawGatewayConfig` only writes `url` and `timeoutMs` to the adapter config; it never reads `v.model` (where `config-fields.tsx` stores the selected agent name). `resolveAgentId` in `execute.ts` therefore always falls back to `"dozer"` for every newly created agent. - **PR description:** Per CONTRIBUTING.md, bigger changes (Path 2) should include a "thinking path" — the *why*, the benefits, and any risks — as well as before/after screenshots for UI changes. The new `NanoClawGatewayConfigFields` component is a visual addition; please add a screenshot of the config form in the PR description.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until the agentName persistence bug is fixed — every agent created via the UI will silently route to "dozer". One P1 defect: the selected agent name is dropped during config creation, making multi-agent routing non-functional from the UI. The rest of the adapter (HTTP dispatch, health check, registry wiring, constants) is structurally sound. packages/adapters/nanoclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts and ui/src/adapters/nanoclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/nanoclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts | P1 bug: `v.model` (selected agent name) is never mapped to `ac.agentName`, so all new agents silently default to "dozer" | | packages/adapters/nanoclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Core fire-and-forget HTTP dispatch to NanoClaw's `/paperclip/wakeup`; timeout/abort handling looks correct | | ui/src/adapters/nanoclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx | Uses `values.model` to hold agent name on create path, but no text-input for custom names in create mode; correctly uses `agentName` on edit path | | packages/adapters/nanoclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts | Health-check against `/health` endpoint with 5s timeout; clean error handling | | packages/adapters/nanoclaw-gateway/src/index.ts | Exports type, label, empty models array, agent list, and configuration doc string; all correct | | server/src/adapters/registry.ts | Correctly registers nanoclaw_gateway adapter without sessionCodec (fire-and-forget pattern matches openclaw_gateway) |

3695.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a "Delete company" button to the Danger Zone section of Company Settings, using `companiesApi.remove()` with an inline confirmation dialog and redirect to `/` on success. There are two P1 defects in the new mutation's `onSuccess` handler. - **Missing cache invalidation**: `deleteMutation.onSuccess` calls `navigate("/")` without invalidating `queryKeys.companies.all` or `queryKeys.companies.stats`, so the deleted company continues to appear in the sidebar after deletion until a background refetch occurs. - **Stale confirmation dialog**: `showDeleteConfirm` is not reset when `selectedCompanyId` changes, so switching companies while the dialog is open leaves it visible — clicking "Delete" would then delete the newly selected company. Additionally, the PR description does not follow the required PR template (missing Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks sections) and includes a UI change without before/after screenshots, both of which are required per CONTRIBUTING.md.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not safe to merge — two P1 defects in the delete flow need to be fixed first. Two P1 issues exist: stale query cache after deletion causes the deleted company to reappear in the UI, and the confirmation dialog not being reset on company switch risks deleting the wrong company. Both need to be resolved before merging. ui/src/pages/CompanySettings.tsx — deleteMutation onSuccess handler and the selectedCompanyId useEffect.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/pages/CompanySettings.tsx | Adds deleteMutation and inline confirmation UI; missing cache invalidation in onSuccess and showDeleteConfirm not reset on company switch. | | ui/src/pages/CompanySettings.test.tsx | New test file covering delete button render, confirmation dialog, cancel, and successful deletion — follows existing patterns. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `ui/src/pages/CompanySettings.tsx`, line 199-204 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/7b6af394ccec4c39cdcb2cca601fe7ced6be3f3b/ui/src/pages/CompanySettings.tsx#L199-L204)) **`showDeleteConfirm` not reset on company switch** The `useEffect` that resets invite-related state when `selectedCompanyId` changes doesn't include `showDeleteConfirm`. If a user opens the delete confirmation for one company and then switches to a different company via the sidebar switcher, the confirmation dialog remains visible. Clicking "Delete" in that state would delete the newly selected company, not the one the dialog was opened for.

The equivalent mutation in `Companies.tsx` invalidates both the companies list and stats keys; without this, the deleted company remains visible in the sidebar/switcher after redirect until the cache expires. - **P1 — `showDeleteConfirm` not reset on company switch**: The `useEffect` that resets invite state on `selectedCompanyId` change does not reset `showDeleteConfirm`, so the confirmation dialog can persist across a company switch.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Two P1 issues should be fixed before merging: missing cache invalidation after delete and showDeleteConfirm not resetting on company switch. The implementation is well-structured and follows existing patterns, but has two real defects: (1) the deleteMutation does not invalidate the companies cache on success, leaving stale data visible in the sidebar/switcher, and (2) the confirmation dialog state is not reset when switching companies. Both are straightforward one-liner fixes. ui/src/pages/CompanySettings.tsx — deleteMutation onSuccess and the selectedCompanyId reset useEffect both need updating.

3699.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR wraps the `POST /issues/:id/checkout` handler's `svc.checkout()` call in a try-catch that intercepts the `issues_open_routine_execution_uq` unique-constraint violation (PG error `23505`) and returns a 409 Conflict instead of letting it propagate as a 500, following the same detection pattern already used in `services/plugin-registry.ts`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is narrow, all non-matching errors are rethrown, and only P2 style findings remain. The change is a single targeted catch block with correct fall-through for all other errors. Both remaining findings are P2 style suggestions (redundant type annotation, optional omission of constraint name from response). No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds try-catch around svc.checkout() to convert the issues_open_routine_execution_uq unique-constraint violation (PG error 23505) into a 409 Conflict response; all other errors are rethrown. Pattern matches plugin-registry.ts. Two minor P2 style points: redundant `| null` in the cast and internal constraint name in the response body. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 2042 Comment: **Unnecessary `| null` in type cast** The `| null` in the type assertion is redundant. JavaScript `catch` blocks receive whatever value was thrown — if the thrown value is `null`, the optional-chaining guards (`err?.code`, etc.) still handle it correctly without the explicit `| null` annotation. The companion pattern in `plugin-registry.ts` omits it: ```suggestion const err = error as { code?: string; constraint?: string; constraint_name?: string }; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 2045-2049 Comment: **Internal constraint name exposed in response body** The `constraint` field in the 409 response body echoes the raw PostgreSQL constraint name to the caller. If this endpoint is ever reachable by external actors (or if the agent SDK forwards the raw body to logs), it leaks internal schema details. The human-readable `error` string is already sufficient for agents to distinguish the conflict type. Consider omitting the `constraint` field, or making it opt-in via an internal header check, unless agents are documented to require it. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3700.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds three UX improvements: auto-transitioning linked issues to `in_progress` on agent run start, a green "Approve" button in the comment composer for `in_review` issues, and auto-assigning the first agent when a Kanban card is dragged to `todo`. - The PR description is missing required PR template sections: **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, **Risks**, and **What Changed** bullets. Before/after screenshots are also required by CONTRIBUTING.md for visual changes (new Approve button, Kanban auto-assign). Please fill out the full PR template per [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md). - The `in_review` status in the heartbeat auto-transition allowlist (`heartbeat.ts` line 2539) will silently revert a human-gated review status back to `in_progress` whenever any run fires for the issue — including runs triggered by a clarifying comment while reviewing. This undermines the intended semantics of `in_review` as a human action-required state.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the `in_review` auto-transition and adding the required PR template sections with screenshots. One P1 finding: including `in_review` in the heartbeat auto-transition allowlist silently undoes a user-set review gate. The other findings are P2 (arbitrary agent selection, missing PR template content). server/src/services/heartbeat.ts (the in_review allowlist entry)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds auto-transition of linked issue to `in_progress` on run start; the `in_review` allowlist entry silently undoes a human-controlled review state whenever any run fires. | | ui/src/components/CommentThread.tsx | Adds `onApprove`/`issueStatus` props and an "Approve" button that fires when status is `in_review`; implementation is clean with correct shared `submitting` state management. | | ui/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx | Adds auto-assign on drag-to-todo; silently picks `agents[0]` without availability filtering or defined ordering. | | ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx | Wires `onApprove` into `CommentThread` — posts "Approved" comment and navigates to the source breadcrumb; `sourceBreadcrumb` has a safe fallback default. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 2537-2543 Comment: **`in_review` in the auto-transition allowlist silently undoes human review** Including `in_review` in the allowlist means any new agent run for the issue — including runs triggered by a user posting a clarifying question — will silently revert the status back to `in_progress`. The `in_review` status is intentionally a human-gated state; a user who moves an issue to `in_review` and then adds a follow-up comment will see it flip back without their knowledge, breaking their expectation of review-gated flow. The PR's own "Approve" button doesn't change the status — it only posts `"Approved"` — so the agent's responding run is what fires this transition. That intended case works, but so does any unintended run triggered while `in_review`. Removing `in_review` from the allowlist and instead relying on the agent's own status-update logic (as it does for `blocked`) would be safer. ```suggestion if ( issueContext && (issueContext.status === "backlog" || issueContext.status === "todo") ) { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx Line: 248-250 Comment: **Auto-assign picks `agents[0]` with no defined ordering** `agents[0]` selects whichever agent happens to be first in the array returned by the query, with no guarantee of ordering by availability, recency, or any other relevant criterion. The PR description calls this the "first available agent", but `agents[0]` is not filtered for availability — it includes any agent, including potentially terminated ones. Either filter to active agents first, or add a comment explaining the intended selection heuristic. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR removes the top-level `paperclip` field from the outbound OpenClaw gateway request by deleting `buildStandardPaperclipPayload` and destructuring `text`/`paperclip` out of `payloadTemplate` before spreading into `agentParams`. The approach is correct — since OpenClaw rejects that field at the schema level, the structured context it carried was never reaching the agent anyway, and the wake text already conveys the necessary Paperclip runtime context.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is narrowly scoped to removing a field that OpenClaw was already rejecting, and the only findings are minor test hygiene issues. All findings are P2 style issues in the test file (describe block placement and unawaited close). The production code change is correct and well-targeted, and the regression test validates the exact contract being fixed. packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.test.ts — minor test structure improvements suggested.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Removes buildStandardPaperclipPayload and strips top-level `text` and `paperclip` from the outbound gateway request via destructuring; fix is minimal and correct. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.test.ts | Adds a WebSocket regression test that captures the outbound agent request; the integration test is misplaced inside describe("resolveSessionKey") and the server teardown in afterEach doesn't await close(). |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.test.ts Line: 115-172 Comment: **Integration test misplaced inside `resolveSessionKey` describe block** The `execute()` regression test is nested inside `describe("resolveSessionKey", ...)`, which makes test output misleading — test runners will report it as a session-key test rather than a gateway payload test. It should live in its own describe block. ```suggestion }); describe("execute", () => { it("does not send a top-level paperclip payload to the gateway agent request", async () => { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.test.ts Line: 10-17 Comment: **`gatewayServer.close()` not awaited in `afterEach`** `WebSocketServer.close()` is asynchronous but does not return a promise — it only signals completion via a callback. As written, the `afterEach` hook resolves before the server has finished closing, which can leave a socket in `CLOSE_WAIT` and cause flakey behaviour under fast test runs. ```suggestion afterEach(async () => { if (!gatewayServer) return; for (const client of gatewayServer.clients) { client.terminate(); } await new Promise((resolve) => gatewayServer!.close(() => resolve())); gatewayServer = null; }); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3712.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two bugs in the Slack plugin integration: `rawBody` not being captured for `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` requests (breaking HMAC verification for slash commands), and the event bus delivering N copies of the same event to a plugin worker that has registered N matching subscriptions. The PR description does not follow the required PR template from CONTRIBUTING.md. It is missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Please fill out the [PR template](.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) in full — the "Model Used" section in particular is required for all PRs.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Code changes are correct and well-reasoned, but the PR description is missing required template sections (Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks, Checklist) per CONTRIBUTING.md. Both fixes address real bugs with sound implementations. The dedup approach has a documented gap (events without eventId can still duplicate), and extended: true is mildly broader than needed, but neither blocks correctness. Score is 4 rather than 5 solely because the mandatory PR template sections are absent — once the description is updated the PR should be ready to merge. server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts — the eventId-less dedup gap is worth a conscious decision before merge.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/app.ts | Adds express.urlencoded() with a verify callback to capture rawBody for form-encoded requests; guard is technically redundant (JSON and urlencoded middlewares don't overlap on Content-Type) but harmless. | | server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts | Adds a recentEventIds dedup Map to collapse N notifyWorker calls per event down to 1; events without eventId bypass dedup entirely and can still produce N×M deliveries. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/plugin-host-services.ts Line: 580-590 Comment: **Events without `eventId` still receive N×M deliveries** Events that don't carry an `eventId` skip the dedup path entirely, so a plugin with N subscriptions matching the same eventless event will still cause N `notifyWorker` calls and N×M handler invocations. The PR description calls this intentional, but the same duplicate-notification symptom that motivated this fix can recur for any event source that doesn't set `eventId`. Consider logging a warning or documenting the expectation that events should always carry an `eventId` when plugins may register multiple overlapping subscriptions. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/app.ts Line: 129-135 Comment: **`extended: true` is broader than needed** `extended: true` uses the `qs` library, enabling nested object / array parsing for the form body. For Slack slash commands (and most webhook form payloads) the body is flat, so `extended: false` (the `querystring` parser) is sufficient and avoids the extra complexity. This only affects `req.body` structure — the `rawBody` capture is unaffected either way. ```suggestion app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: false, verify: (req, _res, buf) => { const r = req as unknown as { rawBody?: Buffer }; if (!r.rawBody) r.rawBody = buf; }, })); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | **Plugin Event Bus** | Deduplicates event delivery by `eventId` within a 10-second window | `PluginEventBus` routes domain events to subscribed plugins; tree doesn't prescribe dedup strategy | ✅ Implementation detail; fixes real duplicate-notification bug | | **Plugin Isolation** | In-memory cache for dedup; doesn't affect process isolation | "One process per plugin"; capability enforcement; data-backed state | ✅ Cache is transient; acceptable for collision prevention | | **Webhook Body Handling** | Adds `express.urlencoded()` with `verify` callback to capture `rawBody` | Backend middleware contract; no explicit tree guidance on form-encoded payloads | ✅ Enables Slack plugin HMAC verification; isolated to body parsing | | **eventId-less events** | Events without `eventId` skip dedup and can still duplicate | Tree doesn't mandate eventId presence on all events | ⚠️ Documented gap; not a blocker but worth acknowledging |
Tree nodes referenced - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — plugin SDK and runtime architecture - [`plugins/runtime/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — event bus, lifecycle, worker isolation - [`engineering/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/NODE.md) — backend architecture and service patterns
### Summary This PR fixes two real bugs that prevent the Slack plugin from working correctly: 1. **Slack HMAC verification** — `express.json()` was the only body parser, so form-encoded Slack slash commands never had `rawBody` populated. HMAC verification always failed. The fix adds `express.urlencoded()` with a `verify` callback alongside the JSON parser. 2. **Plugin event duplication** — When a plugin registers multiple `ctx.events.on()` subscriptions for the same event pattern (e.g., two `issue.created` handlers), the event bus delivered N copies of the same event to the worker. The fix deduplicates via an `eventId`-based Map with a 10-second sliding window, collapsing N deliveries into 1. Both changes are **pragmatic and localized**: - They don't affect plugin process isolation, capability enforcement, or data persistence (which the tree marks as core). - The in-memory dedup cache is acceptable for transient collision prevention. - The documented gap (eventId-less events can still duplicate) is acknowledged and not a blocker. ✅ **Fit:** This PR aligns with the plugin architecture as described in the tree. It fixes bugs at the boundary between the backend webhook handler and the plugin event bus without violating the architectural constraints (process isolation, capability gating, plugin-worker contract). --- Reviewed commit: 85d8a3a · Tree snapshot: 6e68dbe · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3714.body:
Greptile Summary

Adds `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` to the fallback anchor renderer in `MarkdownBody` so that plain external links open in a new tab, and adds two regression tests plus a `vi.mock` factory to isolate the `@paperclipai/shared` module in the test environment. The implementation is correct for `https?://` URLs, but the fallback also catches relative/internal paths (e.g. `/page` or bare identifiers when `linkIssueReferences={false}`), which will silently inherit `target="_blank"` despite the PR stating only external links are affected.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; remaining findings are P2 improvements and do not block functionality. The core fix is a one-line change with correct semantics for external URLs. Both new tests pass and the overall risk is low. The two P2 findings (fallback scope and mock fidelity) are worth addressing but are not blocking bugs. ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.tsx (fallback URL scope), ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.test.tsx (mock fidelity)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.tsx | Single-line fix adding `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` to the fallback anchor renderer; the change applies to all non-issue, non-mention links (not just `https://` URLs). | | ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.test.tsx | Adds two new tests and a full `vi.mock("@paperclipai/shared")` factory that re-implements several parse helpers inline, creating a maintenance risk if the real implementations drift. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.tsx Line: 187-189 Comment: **`target="_blank"` applies to relative/internal URLs too** The fallback fires for *every* href that is not an issue reference or mention chip — including relative paths (e.g. `[see guide](/help/guide)` or `[link](PAP-1271)` when `linkIssueReferences={false}`). Those will now open in a new tab, contrary to the PR description that says "only external links are affected." Consider scoping the `target`/`rel` attributes to actual external URLs: ```suggestion {linkChildren} ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/MarkdownBody.test.tsx Line: 20-51 Comment: **Inline mock duplicates real parse-function logic** The factory reimplements `parseAgentMentionHref`, `parseProjectMentionHref`, and `parseSkillMentionHref` from scratch. The real implementations apply additional validation (`normalizeAgentIcon`, `normalizeHexColor`, empty-id guards) that these copies omit, so tests can pass here while hiding breakage against the real code. Additionally, `AGENT_ICON_NAMES` is set to `["bot", "code"]` rather than the full list, so tests using any other icon name would silently get a default icon instead of the expected one. `parseMentionChipHref` (in `mention-chips.ts`) ultimately re-exports these three functions, so an alternative is to keep the mock at the `../lib/mention-chips` boundary and let `@paperclipai/shared` run as-is, avoiding the drift risk. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3716.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes Windows path resolution in `resolveXdgConfigHome` so that the source OpenCode config is correctly located at `%APPDATA%\opencode` instead of the non-existent `~/.config/opencode`, resolving false-positive "Model is unavailable" errors on Windows during Test Environment probes. - **Incomplete fix**: The source-config copy is now correct, but the return env still sets `XDG_CONFIG_HOME: runtimeConfigHome`. OpenCode on Windows ignores `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and always uses `%APPDATA%` — so the `permission.external_directory=allow` injection written to the temp dir is silently discarded at runtime. The returned env should set `APPDATA: runtimeConfigHome` on Windows to complete the isolation.

Confidence Score: 4/5

The PR fixes model discovery on Windows but leaves the runtime permission isolation incomplete — headless approval prompts may still appear on Windows. One P1 finding: the returned env injects XDG_CONFIG_HOME which OpenCode on Windows ignores, so the permission.external_directory=allow modification never takes effect at runtime. The source-copy half of the fix is correct, but the isolation guarantee that prepareOpenCodeRuntimeConfig is supposed to provide remains broken on Windows. packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts — specifically the return value's env block (lines 89–93)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts | Adds Windows APPDATA path resolution for source config discovery (correct), but the return env still injects XDG_CONFIG_HOME instead of APPDATA, so the runtime permission-injection isolation won't work on Windows since OpenCode ignores XDG_CONFIG_HOME on that platform. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts`, line 89-93 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/e24031df8b8f70c2556cfd1b486d31bfdb3afac2/packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts#L89-L93)) **Runtime isolation still broken on Windows** The fix correctly copies the source config from `APPDATA\opencode`, but the returned env still injects `XDG_CONFIG_HOME: runtimeConfigHome`. According to OpenCode's own documentation and issue tracker, OpenCode on Windows always uses `%APPDATA%\opencode` for its config directory and does **not** honor `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`. This means the `permission.external_directory=allow` modification written to the temp dir will be silently ignored at runtime, leaving OpenCode pointing to the original `APPDATA` location — without the permission injection that prevents headless approval prompts. The return env should inject `APPDATA: runtimeConfigHome` on Windows instead of `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`: ```ts return { env: { ...input.env, ...(path.sep === "\\" ? { APPDATA: runtimeConfigHome } : { XDG_CONFIG_HOME: runtimeConfigHome }), }, notes: [ "Injected runtime OpenCode config with permission.external_directory=allow to avoid headless approval prompts.", ], cleanup: async () => { await fs.rm(runtimeConfigHome, { recursive: true, force: true }); }, }; ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts Line: 89-93 Comment: **Runtime isolation still broken on Windows** The fix correctly copies the source config from `APPDATA\opencode`, but the returned env still injects `XDG_CONFIG_HOME: runtimeConfigHome`. According to OpenCode's own documentation and issue tracker, OpenCode on Windows always uses `%APPDATA%\opencode` for its config directory and does **not** honor `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`. This means the `permission.external_directory=allow` modification written to the temp dir will be silently ignored at runtime, leaving OpenCode pointing to the original `APPDATA` location — without the permission injection that prevents headless approval prompts. The return env should inject `APPDATA: runtimeConfigHome` on Windows instead of `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`: ```ts return { env: { ...input.env, ...(path.sep === "\\" ? { APPDATA: runtimeConfigHome } : { XDG_CONFIG_HOME: runtimeConfigHome }), }, notes: [ "Injected runtime OpenCode config with permission.external_directory=allow to avoid headless approval prompts.", ], cleanup: async () => { await fs.rm(runtimeConfigHome, { recursive: true, force: true }); }, }; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts Line: 89-93 Comment: **Runtime isolation still broken on Windows** The fix correctly copies the source config from `APPDATA\opencode`, but the returned env still injects `XDG_CONFIG_HOME: runtimeConfigHome`. According to OpenCode's own documentation and issue tracker, OpenCode on Windows always uses `%APPDATA%\opencode` for its config directory and does **not** honor `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`. This means the `permission.external_directory=allow` modification written to the temp dir will be silently ignored at runtime, leaving OpenCode pointing to the original `APPDATA` location — without the permission injection that prevents headless approval prompts. The return env should inject `APPDATA: runtimeConfigHome` on Windows instead of `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`: ```ts return { env: { ...input.env, ...(path.sep === "\\" ? { APPDATA: runtimeConfigHome } : { XDG_CONFIG_HOME: runtimeConfigHome }), }, notes: [ "Injected runtime OpenCode config with permission.external_directory=allow to avoid headless approval prompts.", ], cleanup: async () => { await fs.rm(runtimeConfigHome, { recursive: true, force: true }); }, }; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts Line: 13 Comment: **Non-idiomatic Windows detection** `path.sep === "\\"` works but is unconventional. The standard Node.js idiom for platform detection is `process.platform === "win32"`, which is more explicit and self-documenting. ```suggestion if (process.platform === "win32") { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts Line: 16-18 Comment: **Inaccurate comment — `APPDATA` already includes `Roaming`** The Windows `APPDATA` environment variable already expands to `C:\Users\\AppData\Roaming`, so "APPDATA/opencode or APPDATA/Roaming/opencode" describes the same path twice. The comment should simply say `%APPDATA%\opencode`. ```suggestion // OpenCode stores config in %APPDATA%\opencode (i.e. AppData\Roaming\opencode). // prepareOpenCodeRuntimeConfig expects the PARENT of the "opencode" subdirectory, // so returning %APPDATA% is correct here. ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3718.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR replaces the fixed 30-day monthly retention cutoff in `pruneOldBackups` with a calendar-month boundary computed by `monthlyRetentionCutoff`, correctly anchoring to the first day of the earliest retained month. The regression test freezes `Date.now` to March 31 and verifies that the newest January backup survives when `monthlyMonths=2`, which the old 60-day approximation would have incorrectly pruned.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; the core fix is correct for all common server configurations and the two remaining findings are timezone edge-case P2s. All remaining findings are P2. The calendar-month arithmetic is correct, JavaScript's Date constructor handles negative month indices properly, and the change is strictly additive. No data loss or incorrect pruning is possible on UTC servers, which covers the vast majority of real deployments. Both changed files share the same local-time vs UTC inconsistency; `backup-lib.ts` is the source of truth and `backup-lib.test.ts` inherits the timezone sensitivity from it.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/db/src/backup-lib.ts | Adds `monthlyRetentionCutoff` using calendar-month arithmetic to replace the 30-day approximation; core fix is correct for UTC but uses local-time `Date` constructor inconsistently with `isoWeekKey`'s UTC approach. | | packages/db/src/backup-lib.test.ts | Adds a regression test that freezes `Date.now` to March 31 UTC and verifies January backup retention with `monthlyMonths=2`; test logic is sound for UTC but the frozen timestamp resolves to April 1 local time in UTC+13/UTC+14 timezones, which would break the assertions. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/db/src/backup-lib.ts Line: 101-105 Comment: **Local-time `Date` constructor inconsistent with UTC in `isoWeekKey`** `new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth() - months, 1)` constructs a local-time midnight boundary. `isoWeekKey` explicitly normalises to UTC (`Date.UTC(...)`). On a server whose local timezone is ahead of UTC by a large offset (e.g. UTC+13/UTC+14, where March 31 12:00 UTC becomes April 1 locally), `now.getMonth()` returns April, so `monthlyCutoff` shifts one month forward and valid January backups fall below it and are deleted. `monthKey` also uses local time, so both functions are internally consistent, but matching UTC throughout would make the boundary predictable regardless of server timezone. ```suggestion function monthlyRetentionCutoff(nowMs: number, monthlyMonths: number): number { const months = Math.max(1, monthlyMonths); const now = new Date(nowMs); return Date.UTC(now.getUTCFullYear(), now.getUTCMonth() - months, 1); } ``` If this is changed, `monthKey` should also be updated to use UTC methods (`getUTCFullYear`, `getUTCMonth`) so bucket assignment and cutoff remain in sync. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/db/src/backup-lib.test.ts Line: 83 Comment: **Frozen UTC timestamp resolves to April locally in UTC+13/UTC+14** `Date.UTC(2026, 2, 31, 12, 0, 0)` (March 31 12:00 UTC) is April 1 local time in UTC+13 and UTC+14 timezones. `monthlyRetentionCutoff` calls `now.getMonth()` (local), which would then return 3 (April), computing a February 1 cutoff instead of January 1. In that case `janNewest` falls below the cutoff and is pruned, making `prunedCount` equal 3 and the `existsSync(janNewest)` assertion fail. Using a UTC-noon timestamp that is unambiguously March in all real-world timezones, or simply pinning the frozen time to a mid-month date (e.g. March 15), would make the test reliable in all CI environments. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR deduplicates four separate hardcoded status arrays into a single import of `ISSUE_STATUSES` from `@paperclipai/shared`, which is a sound change. The `NewIssueDialog` will now correctly expose `blocked` and `cancelled` as options when creating a new issue (previously omitted). - The PR description does not follow the required [PR template](/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md): it's missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Per [CONTRIBUTING.md](/CONTRIBUTING.md), all sections are required — including a **Model Used** entry specifying the AI provider, exact model ID/version, and any relevant capability details. The footer says \"Generated with Claude Code\" but no model specifics are given. - The claim \"No behaviour change for the existing status set\" is not fully accurate: the kanban column order changes for `blocked` (see inline comment on `KanbanBoard.tsx`), and the `StatusIcon` picker order also shifts slightly.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge after addressing the PR template and confirming the KanbanBoard column reorder is intentional. All code findings are P2 — a subtle column-order shift in KanbanBoard and a minor helper duplication. No correctness, data-integrity, or security issues were found. The deduplication itself is clean and the server-side logic is unchanged. ui/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx — verify that moving the `blocked` column to after `done` is intentional.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Replaces local `ALL_ISSUE_STATUSES` literal with `ISSUE_STATUSES` from shared; logic is unchanged since the set is the same 7 values. | | ui/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx | Replaces local `boardStatuses` with `ISSUE_STATUSES`; kanban column order changes silently — `blocked` moves from position 5 (before `done`) to position 6 (after `done`). | | ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx | Expands new-issue status dropdown from 5 to 7 statuses (adds `blocked` and `cancelled`); introduces a new `statusLabel` helper that duplicates the same function in KanbanBoard.tsx and StatusIcon.tsx. | | ui/src/components/StatusIcon.tsx | Replaces local `allStatuses` with `ISSUE_STATUSES`; minor picker reorder — `blocked` and `cancelled` swap positions (was `done, cancelled, blocked`, now `done, blocked, cancelled`). |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx Line: 26 Comment: **Kanban column order silently changed** The previous `boardStatuses` placed `blocked` at position 5 — between `in_review` and `done`. `ISSUE_STATUSES` puts it at position 6 — after `done`. The kanban board will now render the `blocked` column to the right of `done`, which reverses the expected workflow progression (blocked issues are still in-flight, so they typically appear before terminal states). The PR description says "No behaviour change for the existing status set", but this is a visible UI layout change. If this reordering is intentional, it's worth calling out explicitly. If not, consider keeping `done` and `blocked` in the original relative order, or fixing the canonical order in `ISSUE_STATUSES` itself. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx Line: 229-231 Comment: **`statusLabel` duplicated in three files** An identical `statusLabel` helper (replace underscores, title-case each word) is now defined independently in `NewIssueDialog.tsx`, `KanbanBoard.tsx`, and `StatusIcon.tsx`. Since this PR already threads `ISSUE_STATUSES` through from `@paperclipai/shared`, it's a good opportunity to export `statusLabel` (or a similar `issueStatusLabel` utility) from the shared package or a UI utility module and import it in all three places. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR correctly relaxes the permission gate on read-only agent config/skill endpoints from `agents:create` down to plain company membership, while keeping the environment-probe endpoint (`POST .../test-environment`) at the stricter `agents:create` level. The `hasCompanyAccess` undefined-reference flagged in a prior review has been resolved; the implementation now uses the properly-imported `assertCompanyAccess`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the permission relaxation is intentional, correctly scoped, and the previous undefined-reference bug is resolved. All remaining findings are P2 style/cleanup suggestions. The logic change is correct: read gates lowered to company membership, mutation gates unchanged, and secret-bearing endpoints retain the stricter assertCanCreateAgentsForCompany check. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/agents.ts | Permission relaxation for read-only config/skill endpoints: assertCanReadConfigurations now requires only company membership, and actorCanReadConfigurationsForCompany simplifies to a try/catch around assertCompanyAccess. Mutation-adjacent endpoint (test-environment) correctly moved to assertCanCreateAgentsForCompany directly. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/agents.ts Line: 233-240 Comment: **`actorCanReadConfigurationsForCompany` is always `true` at its call sites** Both callers (`GET /companies/:companyId/agents` at line 975 and `GET /agents/:id` at line 1159) already call `assertCompanyAccess` before reaching this function. If that throws, execution never arrives here, so the try/catch can never return `false`. Greptile Summary

This PR adds an opt-in OpenTelemetry bootstrap (`server/src/instrumentation.ts`) that activates only when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set, with no behavior change or dependency graph impact otherwise. The implementation is clean: `sdk.start()` is guarded in a try/catch, shutdown uses `process.once`, and missing packages produce a clear diagnostic warning.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — no behavior change when the env var is unset, and the only finding is a minor observability caveat for the opt-in path. Both prior review concerns (sdk.start() error handling and process.once signal deduplication) are fully addressed. The one remaining finding is a P2 note about a sub-second window where very early requests may be untraced, which is an inherent trade-off of the dynamic-import design and does not block merge. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/instrumentation.ts | New opt-in OTel bootstrap; well-structured with try/catch around sdk.start(), process.once for shutdown, and informative warn on missing packages. Minor: fire-and-forget async init creates a narrow window where very early requests are untraced. | | server/src/index.ts | Adds instrumentation import as the first statement; no other logic changes. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/instrumentation.ts Line: 17-18 Comment: **Async bootstrap may miss early-startup requests** `bootstrapOtel` is fired with `void` and resolves after all five dynamic `import()` calls complete. Meanwhile, `startServer()` in `index.ts` proceeds synchronously — the server can start listening (and accepting requests) before `sdk.start()` runs. Any spans generated during that window are silently dropped. For operators who need guaranteed trace coverage from the first request, the idiomatic fix is the Node.js `--import` flag: ``` node --import ./dist/instrumentation.js server.js ``` which runs the bootstrap before the module graph is even built. The current fire-and-forget approach is safe and unlikely to miss spans in practice (DB init takes far longer than dynamic-import resolution), but it's worth a note in the inline docs so operators know this is best-effort, not guaranteed. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3735.body: @greptileai review — new commits address the prior feedback and update the PR description to match the template.

Noting the P2 about the async-bootstrap early-request window: agreed on the description, but leaving it as-is. The alternative — `await import("./instrumentation.js")` — couples server startup to dynamic-import availability of the OTel packages. If the packages aren't installed or fail to load, the server wouldn't start at all, which is strictly worse than dropping the first ~50ms of spans. The current behavior matches how other opt-in OTel integrations ship.

3736.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds an optional Redis pub/sub transport to `live-events.ts` so WebSocket clients on any replica see all `LiveEvent`s, not just those originating on their own pod. The feature is fully opt-in (gated on `PAPERCLIP_LIVE_EVENTS_REDIS_URL` / `PAPERCLIP_REDIS_URL`) and falls back to local-only delivery on any failure. All three concerns flagged in the previous review round (double-emission routing, silent init failure, missing ioredis error handlers) have been resolved in this revision.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all P0/P1 concerns from prior rounds are resolved; one P2 remains about event ID uniqueness across replicas that should be confirmed but does not block merge The only remaining finding is P2: per-process event IDs are not globally unique once cross-replica delivery is active, which matters only if the client or reconnect logic uses `id` as a cursor or dedup key. Since no confirmed client dependency on ID uniqueness was found, this does not block merge per confidence guidance (default 5/5 when all findings are P2 or lower). No files require special attention beyond the P2 event-ID note in server/src/services/live-events.ts

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/live-events.ts | Adds optional Redis pub/sub transport for cross-replica live event delivery; previous review concerns (double-emission, silent failure, missing error handlers) are all addressed; one remaining P2 concern about per-process event IDs becoming non-unique across replicas |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/live-events.ts Line: 130-143 Comment: **Per-process event IDs are not globally unique in multi-replica mode** `nextEventId` starts at `0` independently in every process. After this PR, a WebSocket client on replica A receives events from *both* replica A (local emit) and replica B (via Redis). Both replicas generate IDs `1, 2, 3, …` independently, so the client can see two distinct events that both carry `id: 5`. If the WebSocket reconnect path or any client-side logic uses `id` as a deduplication key or an "events since X" cursor, the collision would silently suppress legitimate events from one replica. A simple fix would be to prefix the ID with the origin, or switch to a UUID for cross-replica events: ```ts // option A – keep the cheap counter but namespace it id: `${originId}-${nextEventId}`, // option B – UUID always (safe, slightly heavier) id: randomUUID(), ``` If `id` is client-side display-only and never used as a cursor or dedup key, this can stay as-is — but worth confirming before shipping multi-replica. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

`queryKeys.issues.list(companyId)` is used in `LiveUpdatesProvider` as an *exact-key* `getQueryData` lookup to resolve issue refs and build toast labels. Because this query now uses `["issues", companyId, "participant-agent", "__all__", "with-routine-executions"]`, that call returns `undefined` whenever no other consumer has loaded the standard list. The result is that live-update toasts fall back to truncated IDs (e.g. `Issue abc12345`) rather than the full identifier+title.

`invalidateQueries` still works correctly (prefix match), so cache refresh is unaffected — this is only a quality-of-life regression for toast labels. Worth considering whether `resolveIssueQueryRefs` should also try the extended key, or whether the Issues page should additionally populate the canonical `queryKeys.issues.list` cache entry.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

---

This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: packages/shared/src/types/workspace-runtime.ts
Line: 163-169

Comment:
**`mode` union includes values absent from `ExecutionWorkspaceMode`**

`adapter_managed` and `cloud_sandbox` are not members of `ExecutionWorkspaceMode` (the type that governs the `mode` column in the DB). They are values from `ExecutionWorkspaceStrategyType` / `ExecutionWorkspaceProviderType`, so they can never appear in a real workspace's `mode` field. Adding them here creates a misleading type that may confuse callers or future type narrowing.

If these modes are genuinely expected to appear in the `mode` column going forward, the right fix is to add them to `ExecutionWorkspaceMode` itself. If they are not, the union addition should be dropped to keep the type accurate.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

---

This PR touches several visible UI surfaces — the issues filter label ("Hide routine runs"), the properties pane workspace/branch/folder section, the parent arrow link, and the run dialog routine name header. 3750.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds server-side infrastructure for an observability plugin: lifecycle events (`agent.run.started/finished/failed`) emitted via `logActivity` from the heartbeat service, a `agent.delegation.created` event for agent-to-agent handoffs, relocation of `cost_event.created` emission from the costs route into the service layer, and a stability fix setting explicit stale thresholds for the orphaned run reaper. The PR description does not follow the required PR template from `CONTRIBUTING.md` — it's missing the **Thinking Path** (in blockquote format), **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used** sections, and the **Checklist**. Please update the description to include all required sections before merging.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge after addressing the PR template — all code-level findings are P2 style suggestions All remaining findings are P2 (redundant field in delegation payload, potential activity log growth from cost events). The core logic for lifecycle events, delegation tracking, and the reaper threshold is correct. No P0/P1 issues identified. server/src/routes/issues.ts (redundant subtaskId field) and server/src/services/costs.ts (activity log volume from cost events)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Added `agent.delegation.created` to `PLUGIN_EVENT_TYPES` — minimal, correct change | | server/src/index.ts | Added explicit `staleThresholdMs` (2 min startup, 5 min periodic) to `reapOrphanedRuns` calls instead of relying on implicit defaults | | server/src/routes/costs.ts | Removed plugin event emission from cost route; logic now lives in the service layer | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Added `agent.delegation.created` events for reassignment and subtask creation; subtask delegation event has a redundant duplicate field (`issueId` === `subtaskId`) | | server/src/services/costs.ts | Added `void logActivity` for `cost_event.created`; this now also inserts a row into the `activityLog` table on every cost event, which wasn't the case before the move from the route | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Added `agent.run.started` via `logActivity` on run claim, and `agent.run.finished/failed/cancelled` on status transitions — fire-and-forget pattern consistent with the rest of the service |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 1377-1385 Comment: **Redundant `issueId` and `subtaskId` fields in delegation payload** Both `issueId` and `subtaskId` resolve to `issue.id`, so plugin consumers see the same UUID under two different keys. This is likely to confuse observability plugin authors who would naturally expect `subtaskId` to differ from `issueId`. Either drop `issueId` (since the entity is already `issue`) or remove `subtaskId` and only keep `issueId`. Note also that the sibling reassignment event (around line 1738) only uses `issueId`, so the payload shapes are inconsistent across the two `agent.delegation.created` emission sites. ```suggestion details: { delegatingAgentId: actor.agentId, delegatingRunId: actor.runId, delegatedAgentId: issue.assigneeAgentId, issueId: issue.id, reason: "subtask_creation", identifier: issue.identifier, }, ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/costs.ts Line: 97-119 Comment: **Every cost event now writes a row to the `activityLog` table** Previously the plugin event was emitted directly from the costs route without going through `logActivity`, so no row was written to `activityLog`. Routing through `logActivity` adds a DB insert on every cost event (one per LLM API call). On a busy instance this table will grow at the same rate as token usage. If this is intentional, a follow-up retention/pruning policy for `cost_event.created` activity rows would be worth planning. If activity log persistence isn't needed here, the plugin bus could be notified without the DB write. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3751.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds an Ollama adapter that wires any tool-calling Ollama model into the Paperclip agent loop. There are three P1 issues that should be resolved before merge: the default `baseUrl` is a personal LAN IP (`10.0.0.185`) rather than `localhost`, the `read_file`/`write_file` tools expose the server's filesystem to LLM-driven input with no path sandboxing, and `"ollama_agent"` is absent from `BUILTIN_ADAPTER_TYPES` leaving the built-in unprotected from external unregistration. Additionally, the PR description does not follow the required template from `CONTRIBUTING.md` — it is missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used** sections, and the **Checklist**. Please fill out the PR template fully before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 2/5

Not safe to merge — hardcoded personal IP breaks the adapter for all users, unrestricted filesystem access is a security risk, and the PR template is not filled out. Three P1 findings: (1) default URL targets a private machine's IP, silently breaking the adapter for everyone else; (2) LLM-driven read_file/write_file with no path sandboxing is a direct security hole; (3) 'ollama_agent' missing from BUILTIN_ADAPTER_TYPES means plugin machinery can silently delete the built-in. PR description also lacks all required template sections per CONTRIBUTING.md. server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts (IP default + filesystem security) and server/src/adapters/builtin-adapter-types.ts (needs 'ollama_agent' added).

Security Review

 - **Unrestricted path traversal (`read_file` / `write_file`)**: Tool arguments are LLM-generated; no sandboxing prevents reading `/etc/passwd`, private keys, `.env`, the PGlite database, or writing over server files. Affects `ollama-adapter.ts` lines 298–309.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts | Full Ollama adapter implementation — has a hardcoded personal LAN IP as the default base URL and exposes unrestricted filesystem read/write to LLM-driven tool calls, both of which are P1 issues requiring fixes before merge. | | server/src/adapters/registry.ts | Registers `ollamaAdapter` as a built-in adapter; `"ollama_agent"` is missing from `BUILTIN_ADAPTER_TYPES`, leaving the adapter unprotected against external unregistration. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts Line: 23 Comment: **Hardcoded personal LAN IP as default URL** `DEFAULT_BASE_URL` is set to `"http://10.0.0.185:11434"`, which is a private LAN IP that belongs to the author's machine. Every user who deploys this adapter without explicitly configuring `baseUrl` will silently target a host they cannot reach, making the adapter non-functional out of the box. The standard Ollama default is `localhost:11434` (or `127.0.0.1:11434`). ```suggestion const DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:11434"; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts Line: 298-309 Comment: **Unrestricted filesystem access via `read_file` and `write_file` tools** Both `read_file` and `write_file` accept arbitrary paths with no sandboxing or validation. Because the path comes from LLM-generated tool calls (which are themselves driven by potentially adversarial task descriptions), any file readable or writable by the server process is exposed — including credentials, private keys, `.env` files, the database, and system files like `/etc/passwd`. `write_file` is especially dangerous: it creates parent directories and can overwrite any file the server process owns, including server binaries or config files. At minimum, paths should be restricted to a designated working directory. Example guard: ```ts function safePath(rawPath: string, workDir: string): string { const resolved = path.resolve(workDir, rawPath); if (!resolved.startsWith(workDir + path.sep) && resolved !== workDir) { throw new Error(`Path traversal denied: ${rawPath}`); } return resolved; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/adapters/registry.ts Line: 88 Comment: **`ollama_agent` not added to `BUILTIN_ADAPTER_TYPES`** `ollamaAdapter` is registered in `registerBuiltInAdapters()` alongside all other built-in adapters, but `"ollama_agent"` was not added to `BUILTIN_ADAPTER_TYPES` in `builtin-adapter-types.ts`. This has two concrete effects: 1. `unregisterServerAdapter("ollama_agent")` will call `adaptersByType.delete("ollama_agent")` rather than returning early — the adapter can be silently removed by plugin machinery. 2. External plugins can override `ollama_agent` and the overriding will not be logged with the `"overrides built-in adapter"` message. `"ollama_agent"` should be added to the set in `server/src/adapters/builtin-adapter-types.ts` alongside the other built-in types. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts Line: 421 Comment: **System prompt sent as `user` message instead of `system` role** The prompt is placed in the first `user` message. The `OllamaMessage` type already includes `"system"` as a valid role and Ollama models honour a `system` message. Using the correct role improves instruction-following on most models and avoids conflating the system prompt with the task conversation. ```suggestion const messages: OllamaMessage[] = [ { role: "system", content: cfgStr(c.systemPrompt) ?? buildPrompt(ctx, paperclipApiUrl) }, { role: "user", content: "Begin." }, ]; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

🌱 **gardener** · 🆕 `NEW_TERRITORY` · severity: `medium` · commit: `3178aee` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Adapter packaging | Adds `server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts` (647 lines) + registry entry; no dedicated package | `adapters/NODE.md`: "One package per adapter rather than a monolithic adapter module" — each adapter lives under `packages/adapters//` with `server/cli/ui` layers | ⚠️ Conflict | | `ServerAdapterModule` contract | Implements execute loop; unclear whether `testEnvironment`, `sessionCodec`, `getConfigSchema`, `listSkills/syncSkills`, `getQuotaWindows` are wired | Shared contract across all adapters; UI renders settings from declarative `getConfigSchema` so no frontend change is needed per adapter | ❓ Partial | | Parallel adapter work | Overlaps with #3748 (OpenRouter) adding a new local adapter via the correct package layout | Tree expects consistency across adapters so CLI + UI layers pick up new runtimes automatically | ❓ Partial | | Local runtime fit | Ollama as a locally-hosted model runtime fits the existing `*-local` pattern conceptually | Local adapters spawn the agent CLI / client via `adapter-utils` helpers | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — One package per adapter; three-layer (server/cli/ui) structure; declarative config schema. - [`adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md) — All runtimes plug into one server adapter contract.
### Recommendation **Why:** Ollama is a reasonable new runtime to support, but the adapter currently lives as a single file under `server/src/adapters/` rather than a `packages/adapters/ollama-local/` package with `server/cli/ui` layers. Recent commits (`fix(security): replace hardcoded LAN IP with localhost`, `fix(ollama-adapter): address Greptile review — sandbox filesystem too…`) address code-review concerns but don't change the structural fit. The one-package-per-adapter decision is explicit on the tree and every existing adapter follows it; diverging here creates drift (billing inference, CLI formatting, UI transcript rendering, quota windows) and makes future cross-adapter refactors harder. **Suggested path forward:** 1. Restructure as `packages/adapters/ollama-local/` with `server/cli/ui` subpackages following `claude-local`, `codex-local`, `gemini-local` as references. 2. Compose via `@paperclipai/adapter-utils` (`runChildProcess`/HTTP client, `buildPaperclipEnv`, session codec normalization) rather than bespoke plumbing. 3. Wire `testEnvironment`, `getConfigSchema`, and `detectModel` so the frontend renders Ollama settings and health status without custom UI code. 4. Coordinate with #3748 (OpenRouter) so both new adapters land with the same package layout. --- Reviewed commit: 3178aee · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

@serenakeyitan Acknowledged,  the one-package-per-adapter pattern documented under adapters/NODE.md is the right long-term shape. For this PR i intentionally kept the adapter as a single file to unblock local Ollama experimentation; restructuring into packages/adapters/ollama-local/ with server/cli/ui layers (and the missing testEnvironment() pre-flight) will land as a follow-up once PR [#3748](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/3748) (OpenRouter) settles the template. Noted separately so it is not lost.

3752.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a full-featured OpenTelemetry observability plugin (`plugins/paperclip-observability`) covering distributed tracing, metrics, structured logs, session streaming, health scoring, and budget governance. The architecture is well-structured with a clean event router, focused handler modules, and good test coverage. Two blocking issues need to be resolved before merge: - A hardcoded development company ID in both `activity.log()` calls in `worker.ts` will route plugin activity messages to the wrong company in any non-development environment. - `onConfigChanged` reinitializes the OTel SDK but `telemetryCtx` (created once in `setup()`) retains references to the old, shutdown `meter`/`tracer`/`otelLogger` — all subsequent events silently use the defunct SDK handles until the plugin restarts. Also, the PR description is missing the \"thinking path\" format required by CONTRIBUTING.md — please add a top-down reasoning block explaining the problem and approach before the change summary.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not safe to merge — two P1 defects in worker.ts will cause wrong-company activity logging and silent telemetry loss after config changes. Two confirmed P1 bugs: hardcoded company ID sends activity logs to the wrong company in all non-dev deployments, and the stale telemetryCtx after onConfigChanged means all events use a shutdown OTel provider. Both are straightforward to fix but need to be addressed before merging. plugins/paperclip-observability/src/worker.ts (hardcoded company ID on lines 768 and 775; stale telemetryCtx in onConfigChanged)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/worker.ts | Core plugin worker with two P1 bugs: hardcoded dev company ID in activity.log() calls, and telemetryCtx not updated in onConfigChanged leaving stale meter/tracer after config reload. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/otel-setup.ts | OTel SDK v2 initialization; uses SimpleLogRecordProcessor which exports synchronously — BatchLogRecordProcessor is preferred for production. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/telemetry/metrics-handlers.ts | Metrics event handlers; instruments recreated via createCounter/createHistogram on every event invocation rather than being cached at setup time. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/telemetry/trace-handlers.ts | Comprehensive span lifecycle management for run, cost, issue, session, and cross-agent delegation; endedRunSpanContexts pruning is correctly implemented. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/telemetry/router.ts | Clean event-to-handler routing using Promise.allSettled with proper error logging per handler; no issues found. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/manifest.ts | Plugin manifest with instanceConfigSchema; all config fields including enableLogs are present and match config.ts defaults. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/config.ts | Config types and resolveConfig with sensible defaults; no issues. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/health-score.ts | Composite health scoring algorithm (0-100) across heartbeat age, error state, budget utilization, and run success rate; algorithm is correct. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/telemetry/session-handlers.ts | Session lifecycle spans and metrics for streaming events; sessionMeta cleanup is handled correctly on done/error paths. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/telemetry/log-handlers.ts | Structured OTel log emission with trace context correlation and plugin logger fallback; no issues found. | | plugins/paperclip-observability/src/telemetry/activity-handlers.ts | Activity event handlers emitting child spans and metrics for tool invocations; well-structured with proper attribute extraction. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: plugins/paperclip-observability/src/worker.ts Line: 768-770 Comment: **Hardcoded company ID will log to wrong company** Both `ctx.activity.log()` calls (lines 768 and 775) use a hardcoded company ID that appears to be a development-environment artifact. In any other deployment this logs to the wrong company's activity feed. The reference example plugin in `packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability` passes `companyId: ""` instead. ```suggestion await ctx.activity.log({ companyId: "", message: `Metrics collection — ${snapshots.length} agents, ${issueSnapshots.length} issue buckets, ${govSnapshots.length} governance snapshots, ${healthSnapshots.length} health scores, ${eventsProcessed} events processed since startup`, }); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: plugins/paperclip-observability/src/worker.ts Line: 774-778 Comment: **Same hardcoded company ID in setup activity log** Same hardcoded company ID appears in the startup `activity.log()` call. ```suggestion await ctx.activity.log({ companyId: "", message: "Observability plugin initialised and subscribed to domain events", }); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: plugins/paperclip-observability/src/worker.ts Line: 842-868 Comment: **`onConfigChanged` leaves `telemetryCtx` with stale meter/tracer** `telemetryCtx` is created once in `setup()` capturing `otel.meter`, `otel.tracer`, and `otel.otelLogger` by value. When `onConfigChanged` shuts down the old SDK and assigns a new `initOTel()` result to the `otel` variable, `telemetryCtx` still holds references to the shut-down SDK's meter and tracer. All subsequent events will attempt to record to a shutdown OTel provider and silently produce no telemetry. The fix is to make `telemetryCtx` resolve `meter`, `tracer`, and `otelLogger` dynamically from the current `otel` handle at dispatch time (e.g. via getters), or recreate and re-register `telemetryCtx` inside `onConfigChanged`. The example plugin avoids this by accessing the module-level `otel` directly from each handler closure rather than via a frozen context object. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: plugins/paperclip-observability/src/otel-setup.ts Line: 129-135 Comment: **`SimpleLogRecordProcessor` adds synchronous per-log latency** `SimpleLogRecordProcessor` exports each log record synchronously before returning, which adds OTLP round-trip latency to every event handler call. `BatchLogRecordProcessor` (from `@opentelemetry/sdk-logs`) queues records in memory and flushes on a configurable interval, matching the behaviour already used for metrics and traces. ```suggestion loggerProvider = new LoggerProvider({ resource, processors: [ new BatchLogRecordProcessor( new OTLPLogExporter({ url: `${config.otlpEndpoint}/v1/logs` }), ), ], }); ``` You'll also need to update the import: `import { LoggerProvider, BatchLogRecordProcessor } from "@opentelemetry/sdk-logs";` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: plugins/paperclip-observability/src/telemetry/metrics-handlers.ts Line: 44-52 Comment: **Instruments re-created on every event call** Every metrics handler calls `ctx.meter.createCounter()`, `createHistogram()`, etc. on each event invocation. The OTel SDK deduplicates by name so it won't produce duplicate instruments, but it does execute a map lookup + validation on every single event. The idiomatic OTel pattern is to create instruments once (e.g. in a factory function called during router setup) and pass them into handlers. Consider extracting instrument creation into a separate initialisation step. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Plugin extensibility | Full OpenTelemetry plugin: traces, metrics, logs, health scoring, governance via the plugin event bus | Plugin SDK + runtime is the declared extension surface for cross-cutting concerns like observability | 🆕 New | | Plugin placement | Adds both `packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability/` and a top-level `plugins/paperclip-observability/` dir | `plugins/examples/NODE.md`: "Examples live in-tree, not in a separate repo." Canonical location is `packages/plugins/examples/` via pnpm workspace | ⚠️ Conflict (dual location) | | Capability manifest / JSON-RPC | Plugin worker + manifest declare events/jobs; host-worker split | `plugins/runtime/NODE.md`: process isolation via JSON-RPC; capability-gated RPC; manifest-driven contract | ✅ Aligned | | Cross-PR coupling | Adds `server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts` (581 lines) that belongs to #3751, and depends on #3750's new plugin event types | Tree expects per-package isolation; mixing unrelated adapter code into a plugin PR muddies review & bisect | ⚠️ Conflict | | New plugin event types | Relies on `agent.run.started/finished/failed/cancelled`, `agent.delegation.created`, `cost_event.created` | Not yet documented on the tree as a stable event contract | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — SDK / runtime / examples split; JSON-RPC host-worker protocol. - [`plugins/runtime/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — `PluginEventBus` routes domain events to subscribed plugins; capability enforcement at host boundary. - [`plugins/examples/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/examples/NODE.md) — Examples live under `packages/plugins/examples/` via pnpm workspace. - [`plugins/examples/examples-live-in-tree-as-sdk-contract-tests.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/examples/examples-live-in-tree-as-sdk-contract-tests.md) — Example plugins double as SDK contract tests. - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — One package per adapter; ollama-adapter.ts belongs in its own package, not bundled with a plugin PR.
### Recommendation **Why:** The observability plugin itself is a strong fit for Paperclip's plugin extensibility principle — exactly the kind of cross-cutting concern plugins are meant to absorb. Two process concerns hold up a clean ALIGNED: (1) the plugin is mirrored under two directories (`packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability` and top-level `plugins/paperclip-observability`), which contradicts the "examples live under `packages/plugins/examples/`" decision and risks divergence; (2) the PR also contains the Ollama adapter file from #3751, coupling two unrelated changes. Latest commit (`fix(observability): address Greptile review — drop hardcoded companyI…`) addresses code-review feedback but not the structural concerns. **Suggested path forward:** 1. Pick one canonical location — keep the full implementation under `packages/plugins/paperclip-observability/` (not an "example") since it's a first-party production plugin, with a minimal `packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability-example/` if you also want a tutorial version. Delete the top-level `plugins/paperclip-observability/` tree. 2. Split out `server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts` into its own PR (or rebase onto #3751 once that lands). 3. Consider splitting the 9k-line plugin into a core tracing plugin plus opt-in metric/health-score/governance extensions so the review and failure surface is smaller. 4. Once the plugin event types from #3750 stabilize, propose a tree node under `plugins/runtime/` documenting the `agent.*` and `cost_event.*` event contract. --- Reviewed commit: 3406382 · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

On the dual directories: packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability/ is the minimal reference example kept in the monorepo as the documented "hello world" shape; plugins/paperclip-observability/ is the full-featured plugin we actually run. We can call this out explicitly in plugins/NODE.md as part of this PR so future contributors know which one is canonical. On scope: agreed it is large ,  splitting now would create a hard dependency graph (metrics and health scoring share the same event router and span map), so the plan is to land the core router + traces + logs here and peel the health-score module into its own package in a follow-up once the plugin APIs settle.

The `verify` and `e2e` jobs are failing at `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` because `pnpm-lock.yaml` is out of sync with the new dependencies introduced in `packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability/package.json.`
This is a structural CI tension, not a defect in this PR. The repo has two conflicting constraints:

The policy job blocks any PR that modifies `pnpm-lock.yaml` (unless it targets chore/refresh-lockfile)
`verify` and `e2e` run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, which fails when a package.json declares deps not yet in the lockfile

Any PR that introduces new workspace dependencies cannot satisfy both rules on a single feature branch.
Upstream precedent (e.g. [#3109](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/3109)) resolves this in two steps:
* Merge the feature PR with the lockfile step red
* The scheduled [refresh-lockfile.yml](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/master/.github/workflows/refresh-lockfile.yml) workflow opens a follow-up PR on `chore/refresh-lockfile that regenerates pnpm-lock.yaml` and merges clean under the policy exemption

If you'd prefer to front-run this, i  can open a companion c`hore/refresh-lockfile` PR today containing only the regenerated lockfile entries for the two new packages (plugins/paperclip-observability, packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability) and their `@opentelemetry/*` deps .  happy to go whichever route works for you.
