3758.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a 500 error on `PATCH /api/issues/:id` by validating the `x-paperclip-run-id` header against a UUID regex in `actorMiddleware`, dropping invalid values before they reach the `activity_log.run_id` FK column. The fix is minimal, scoped to one file, and the behavior change (500 → 200 with `run_id = null`) is strictly an improvement. The PR description is detailed and covers root cause, verification steps, and scope well, but is missing the **Thinking Path** section required by `CONTRIBUTING.md` — a short bullet chain from Paperclip's high-level purpose down to this specific fix.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all findings are P2 style suggestions that don't affect correctness. The fix is minimal, targeted, and strictly improves behavior (500 → 200). Both remaining comments are P2: one is a regex hoisting style nit, the other is a defensive consistency suggestion for the JWT path which is already safe in practice. Neither blocks merge. No files require special attention beyond the inline P2 notes in server/src/middleware/auth.ts.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/middleware/auth.ts | Adds UUID validation for `x-paperclip-run-id` header before storing in `req.actor.runId`; minor style issue with regex placement inside the request handler. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

3761.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR refines the issue-assignment wake path by distinguishing known 409 "Agent is not invokable" conflicts from unexpected errors, downgrading the former from `warn` to structured `info` telemetry and adding two targeted unit tests. The implementation is clean and focused. The PR description is missing required sections from the CONTRIBUTING.md template: a **Thinking Path** (in blockquote format tracing from project context to this change), a **Risks** section, a **Model Used** section, and the **Checklist**. Please fill out the full PR template before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all findings are P2 style/improvement suggestions with no blocking correctness issues. The core logic is correct — known 409s are now handled separately from unexpected errors with richer structured logging. The one edge case (matching 409 with missing status detail) is a minor gap that doesn't affect the happy path. No security or data-integrity concerns. No files require special attention beyond the P2 suggestions noted inline.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issue-assignment-wakeup.ts | Adds `readInvokableConflictStatus` helper and splits the catch handler into info (known 409) vs. warn (unexpected); edge case where a matching 409 lacks a `status` detail still reaches the warn path. | | server/src/__tests__/issue-assignment-wakeup.test.ts | New unit tests cover the happy-path (info log on known 409) and unexpected-error (warn log) cases; `rethrowOnError` behavior is untested. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issue-assignment-wakeup.ts Line: 22-28 Comment: **Known 409 without `status` detail silently degrades to `warn`** `readInvokableConflictStatus` returns `null` when the 409 carries the right message but `details.status` is missing, empty, or not a string. Because the caller checks `if (assigneeStatus)` before deciding which log level to use, that case falls through to the `warn` path — contradicting the stated goal of downgrading all `Agent is not invokable` conflicts to `info`. Consider returning a sentinel like `"unknown"` when the conflict matches but the status field is absent: ```suggestion function readInvokableConflictStatus(err: unknown): string | null { if (!(err instanceof HttpError)) return null; if (err.status !== 409 || err.message !== "Agent is not invokable in its current state") return null; const details = err.details; if (!details || typeof details !== "object") return "unknown"; const status = (details as { status?: unknown }).status; return typeof status === "string" && status.trim().length > 0 ? status : "unknown"; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/issue-assignment-wakeup.test.ts Line: 39-55 Comment: **Missing coverage for `rethrowOnError: true`** Both the non-invokable and unexpected-error branches re-throw when `rethrowOnError` is set, but neither path has a test for that. A small additional case for each would ensure the flag isn't accidentally dropped in a future refactor. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a hang in the OpenClaw gateway by splitting the final PATCH (which previously combined `status` and `comment`) into two sequential calls: a POST to record the completion comment, followed by a status-only PATCH. The change is a focused two-line edit to the skill template string in `buildWakeText` and is backward-compatible with older servers.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the change is a minimal, correct prompt-template edit with no logic or API contract risk. Only one file changed, the edit is two lines of instruction text, no runtime logic is affected, and the two-step POST → PATCH flow is strictly safer than the previous single PATCH. The sole open finding is a P2 PR-template compliance request that does not affect correctness. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Two-line change to `buildWakeText` splits a single PATCH (status + comment) into a POST comment followed by a status-only PATCH; logic is correct and minimal. |

3763.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR eliminates a redundant `UPDATE issues SET updatedAt = now()` that fired on the same row inside `addComment` when the PATCH handler had already called `svc.update()` moments earlier — a pattern that caused lock contention reported in production. The fix adds an optional `{ skipUpdatedAt?: boolean }` parameter to `addComment` and passes `{ skipUpdatedAt: true }` from the PATCH handler. The logic is sound: `svc.update()` unconditionally writes `updatedAt: new Date()` to the patch object (line 1594 of `issues.ts`) and always runs before `addComment` is reached in this handler, so skipping the second UPDATE is safe in all PATCH code paths — not just the "status+comment" case described in the PR title. **The PR description does not follow the required template from CONTRIBUTING.md.** It is missing the Thinking Path, What Changed, Verification, Risks, Model Used, and Checklist sections. Please fill out the template before requesting merge (see CONTRIBUTING.md and `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Code change is correct and safe, but the PR description is missing all required template sections (Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) per CONTRIBUTING.md. The fix itself is logically correct — svc.update() always sets updatedAt before addComment is reached, so skipUpdatedAt: true is always valid in this handler. The remaining inline findings are P2 (comment clarity and a missing test assertion). The score is 4 rather than 5 solely because the PR description does not follow the required template, which CONTRIBUTING.md explicitly requires before merge. No files have correctness issues. The PR description (not a file) needs the required template sections filled in.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds optional `{ skipUpdatedAt?: boolean }` param to `addComment`; guarded correctly with `if (!options?.skipUpdatedAt)` before the secondary UPDATE | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | PATCH handler calls `addComment` with `{ skipUpdatedAt: true }` unconditionally whenever `commentBody` is present — applies more broadly than the "status+comment" framing in the PR title, but is correct since `svc.update()` always runs before this point |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/routes/issues.ts`, line 1730-1736 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/d490a6bb9714fbb875f3b44b9e409a8d80d76386/server/src/routes/issues.ts#L1730-L1736)) **No test asserts `skipUpdatedAt: true` is passed** The existing test suite in `issue-update-comment-wakeup-routes.test.ts` covers the comment-only and assignee+comment PATCH paths but mocks `addComment` without checking its options argument. A regression — e.g., accidentally dropping the fourth argument — would silently reintroduce the double-UPDATE. Consider adding an assertion: ```ts expect(mockIssueService.addComment).toHaveBeenCalledWith( expect.any(String), expect.any(String), expect.any(Object), { skipUpdatedAt: true }, ); ```

3764.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds an early-return guard in `releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote` that marks all pending `deferred_issue_execution` wakeups as `skipped` when an issue is already `done` or `cancelled` at end-of-run, preventing agents from being re-woken on completed work. The PR description doesn't follow the required template from `CONTRIBUTING.md`. It is missing a **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Please fill out the [PR template](.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) before merging.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated and the dead-code concern is confirmed or cleaned up. The fix is focused, additive, and runs inside an existing row-locked transaction. All findings are P2: the dead `shouldReopenDeferredCommentWake` block is a cleanup/confirmation issue, and the missing PR template sections are a process concern. Neither blocks correctness. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — confirm whether the `shouldReopenDeferredCommentWake` block (lines 4321–4363) should be removed now that it is unreachable.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds an early-return guard before the deferred-wakeup promotion loop to skip all pending `deferred_issue_execution` wakeups when the issue is already `done` or `cancelled`; the guard renders the existing `shouldReopenDeferredCommentWake` block dead code. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts`, line 4321-4363 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/468b997dcc59f3525d81561b76c0969468382372/server/src/services/heartbeat.ts#L4321-L4363)) **`shouldReopenDeferredCommentWake` is now unreachable dead code** The early-return guard added at line 4256 returns `null` for every terminal-state issue before this `while` loop is entered. Because the issue row is locked with `FOR UPDATE` and the whole block runs in one transaction, `issue.status` cannot change to `"done"` or `"cancelled"` between the guard and this check — making `shouldReopenDeferredCommentWake` permanently `false` in this code path. This silently removes the previous behaviour where a comment-triggered deferred wakeup would reopen a done/cancelled issue and re-run the agent. If that behaviour was intentional (e.g. a human comments on a closed issue while a run is still in flight), those wakeups are now silently skipped instead. Please confirm this is intentional and, if so, remove the dead block to avoid misleading future readers.

Greptile Summary

This PR adds an **Instance Settings → Backups** page that exposes `GET/PATCH /api/instance/settings/backup` endpoints backed by `config-store.ts`, which reads/writes `config.json` with an atomic backup-before-overwrite. The shared Zod schema, API client, sidebar entry, and React route are all wired up correctly. - **P1 — Broken navigation for non-admin board users:** The `GET /instance/settings/backup` handler calls `assertCanManageInstanceSettings` (instance-admin required), but the comparable GET endpoints for general and experimental settings allow any board user. Because the sidebar renders the \"Backups\" link unconditionally and there is no admin guard on the `/instance/settings/*` routes in `App.tsx`, a non-admin board user who clicks the link will land on a 403 error page. - **Missing PR template sections:** The description does not include a Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, or Model Used section, all of which are required by CONTRIBUTING.md and the PR template. Please also include before/after screenshots — this is a visual change.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge until the GET authorization inconsistency is resolved; non-admin board users hit a 403 when navigating to the new Backups page. One P1 finding remains: the GET endpoint requires instance-admin while the sidebar link is visible to all board users, creating a broken navigation path. All other pieces (config-store, shared schema, API client, routing) are correct. Fixing the auth check or adding a sidebar guard resolves the blocker. server/src/routes/instance-settings.ts (GET auth guard) and ui/src/components/InstanceSidebar.tsx (unconditional Backups link)

Looks correct. | | packages/shared/src/validators/instance.ts | Adds instanceBackupSettingsSchema and patchInstanceBackupSettingsSchema; validation ranges match databaseBackupConfigSchema in config-schema.ts. | | ui/src/components/InstanceSidebar.tsx | Adds Backups nav item; link is always visible regardless of admin status, which becomes a problem given the admin-only GET endpoint. | | ui/src/App.tsx | Adds /instance/settings/backups route pointing to InstanceBackupSettings; wired correctly under the Layout wrapper. | | ui/src/api/instanceSettings.ts | Extends API client with getBackup/updateBackup methods using correct shared types. | | ui/src/lib/queryKeys.ts | Adds backupSettings key under instance namespace; straightforward addition. | | packages/shared/src/index.ts | Re-exports new instanceBackupSettingsSchema and related types from validators/index.ts. | | packages/shared/src/validators/index.ts | Adds exports for instanceBackupSettingsSchema, patchInstanceBackupSettingsSchema and their inferred types. |

### Recommendation **Why:** The tree documents explicit delegation as the task assignment model ("Delegation = creating a task and assigning it"). Proactive self-assignment introduces a new pattern where agents autonomously claim work from backlog/todo queues. While the atomic checkout mechanism supports safe claiming, this changes the coordination model — an agent scanning and grabbing work is fundamentally different from being assigned work by a manager or human. The opt-in toggle (default: off) mitigates risk, but the pattern itself is undocumented. **Suggested path forward:** This is a reasonable feature that the opt-in default handles well. Consider adding a brief section under `product/agent-model/NODE.md` documenting proactive self-assignment as a new capability pattern — when it fires, what scope it considers, and how it interacts with the delegation model. This helps future contributors understand the two task acquisition paths (explicit delegation vs. proactive claiming). --- Reviewed commit: eb3647e · Tree snapshot: fff6709 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3768.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two related heartbeat bugs: defaulting `intervalSec` to 300 in `parseHeartbeatPolicy` (aligning the server with the client's `?? 300` default), and shallow-merging `runtimeConfig` on PATCH so partial UI submissions no longer clobber sibling config keys. - **`parseSchedulerHeartbeatPolicy` in `agents.ts` still defaults `intervalSec` to `0`**, so the admin `/instance/scheduler-heartbeats` endpoint reports `schedulerActive: false` for agents the scheduler is actually firing on — the two parse functions are now inconsistent. - The `runtimeConfig` merge mutates `patchData` before `summarizeAgentUpdateDetails` runs, causing the audit log to over-report `changedRuntimeConfigKeys` (all merged keys, not just the patched ones). - The PR description does not follow the required PR template (missing Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, and Checklist sections as specified in CONTRIBUTING.md).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the parseSchedulerHeartbeatPolicy default inconsistency; the other finding is a minor audit-log quality issue. One P1 finding remains: the admin scheduler UI incorrectly shows schedulerActive: false for agents the scheduler actually fires on, because parseSchedulerHeartbeatPolicy was not updated alongside parseHeartbeatPolicy. The audit-log over-reporting is P2 and non-blocking. The core fixes (intervalSec default and runtimeConfig shallow merge) are correct. server/src/routes/agents.ts — parseSchedulerHeartbeatPolicy default and post-merge audit log capture

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Updates parseHeartbeatPolicy default for intervalSec from 0 to 300, aligning server default with the client UI default — straightforward and correct. | | server/src/routes/agents.ts | Adds shallow merge for runtimeConfig on PATCH (correct fix), but parseSchedulerHeartbeatPolicy still defaults intervalSec to 0, so the admin /instance/scheduler-heartbeats endpoint will incorrectly report schedulerActive: false for agents the scheduler actually fires on. |

3769.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds opt-in push notifications (Discord webhook and Telegram bot) that fire when an agent marks an issue as `blocked` or posts a comment containing help-seeking keywords. The config plumbing and trigger logic are clean, but two P1 issues need to be addressed before merging: - **Sensitive data leak**: `dispatchHumanEscalation` is called with the raw, unredacted `input` — bypassing the `sanitizeRecord` and `redactCurrentUserValue` transforms applied for DB writes. Sensitive fields (e.g. redacted usernames) are forwarded verbatim to external webhooks. - **Silent notification failures**: Neither `postDiscord` nor `postTelegram` checks `response.ok`; HTTP 4xx/5xx responses (bad token, rate limit, invalid URL) resolve without error and no warning is logged. Additionally, the PR description is missing the required PR template sections (Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) per [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md).

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not safe to merge — two P1 issues: potential sensitive data exfiltration to external webhooks and silent notification failures masking misconfigurations. Two genuine P1 defects exist on the changed code path: raw unredacted details are forwarded to external services (security/data-integrity), and HTTP errors from the webhook/API are silently swallowed rather than logged. Both will cause incorrect behavior in production today, not hypothetically. server/src/services/activity-log.ts and server/src/services/notification-dispatcher.ts both need fixes before merge.

Security Review

 - **Credential/data leak via external webhook** (`activity-log.ts` line 97): raw `input.details` — including fields that `sanitizeRecord`/`redactCurrentUserValue` would strip — are forwarded to the Discord or Telegram webhook before any redaction is applied. If `censorUsernameInLogs` is enabled, usernames and other sensitive values are exfiltrated externally. - **Agent-generated text injected into Telegram Markdown** (`notification-dispatcher.ts` line 49): `payload.body` is derived from untrusted agent comment text and placed verbatim into a `parse_mode: "Markdown"` message, which can corrupt formatting or cause Telegram to error.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/notification-dispatcher.ts | New service for Discord/Telegram notifications; HTTP errors silently ignored (fetch doesn't throw on 4xx/5xx), issueTitle field never used in messages, and loadConfig() called on every dispatch. | | server/src/services/activity-log.ts | Adds escalation dispatch on agent-blocked and help-keyword events; raw unredacted input.details are forwarded to external webhooks, bypassing sanitizeRecord/redactCurrentUserValue. | | server/src/config.ts | Adds three optional notification env vars to Config interface and loadConfig(); straightforward and correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/activity-log.ts Line: 97 Comment: **Unredacted details sent to external webhooks** `dispatchHumanEscalation` receives the raw `input` object, so `input.details` is the unredacted payload. The `sanitizeRecord` and `redactCurrentUserValue` transforms are applied only to `redactedDetails` used for the DB insert — they are never applied before the notification fires. If `censorUsernameInLogs` is enabled, usernames and other redacted values will still be forwarded to the Discord/Telegram webhook. Pass `redactedDetails` explicitly instead of the raw input: ```typescript // After computing redactedDetails (line 43-45): void dispatchHumanEscalation({ ...input, details: redactedDetails }).catch(() => {}); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/notification-dispatcher.ts Line: 29-33 Comment: **HTTP errors silently ignored — notifications appear to succeed on 4xx/5xx** `fetch` only rejects (throws) on network-level failures; a 401 bad token, 404 invalid webhook URL, or 429 rate limit returns a resolved `Promise` with a non-OK `Response`, so the `.catch()` wrapper in `dispatchNotification` never fires and no warning is logged. The same applies to `postTelegram` (line 46). ```suggestion const response = await fetch(webhookUrl, { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ embeds: [embed] }), }); if (!response.ok) { throw new Error(`Discord webhook returned ${response.status}`); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/notification-dispatcher.ts Line: 46-50 Comment: **HTTP errors silently ignored in Telegram path** Same as the Discord `postDiscord` call — `fetch` resolves without throwing on 4xx/5xx, so a bad bot token or invalid chat ID produces no warning. Additionally, `parse_mode: "Markdown"` is used but `payload.body` comes from unsanitized agent comment text; characters like `*`, `_`, and `` ` `` will break the Markdown parse and cause Telegram to return a 400, which would also be silently swallowed. ```suggestion const response = await fetch(`https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`, { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ chat_id: chatId, text, parse_mode: "Markdown" }), }); if (!response.ok) { throw new Error(`Telegram API returned ${response.status}`); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/notification-dispatcher.ts Line: 13-33 Comment: **`issueTitle` populated but never surfaced in messages** The `NotificationPayload.issueTitle` field is set by both call sites in `activity-log.ts`, but neither `postDiscord` nor `postTelegram` includes it in the output. Operators see only an issue identifier (e.g. `PROJ-42`) with no title, which reduces the at-a-glance usefulness of the notification. Consider adding it as an embed field in Discord and a line in the Telegram message. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/notification-dispatcher.ts Line: 53-54 Comment: **`loadConfig()` called on every notification dispatch** `loadConfig()` re-reads environment variables (and potentially file-based config) on every call. Since notifications can fire frequently if agents are blocked often, consider accepting a pre-resolved `config` object as a parameter (or reading it once at module level) rather than re-computing it inside `dispatchNotification`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

### Recommendation **Why:** The "thin core, rich edges" principle (decision #9) explicitly lists "special surfaces" as plugin territory, and external messaging integrations (Discord, Telegram) are a textbook example. The plugin system already supports event subscriptions and webhook endpoints — a notification plugin could subscribe to `issue.updated` and `issue.comment_added` events and dispatch to any channel. Baking specific third-party integrations into the core server couples the control plane to external services it should not know about. **Suggested path forward:** Implement this as a plugin using the plugin SDK's event subscription system. The plugin would listen for `issue.updated` (status=blocked) and `issue.comment_added` events, apply the keyword matching logic, and dispatch to Discord/Telegram. This keeps the core thin while delivering the same user value. If the plugin SDK lacks something needed (e.g., access to config env vars), that gap is worth fixing in the SDK rather than bypassing it. Greptile Summary

This PR extends the `issue_relations` table to support four relation types (`blocks`, `blocked_by`, `related`, `duplicate`) and adds `GET`/`POST`/`DELETE` REST endpoints under `/api/issues/:id/relations`. The inverse-edge logic in `create` and `deleteById` is well-structured and the `onConflictDoNothing` idempotency approach is sound. - The PR description is missing all required template sections (Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) per CONTRIBUTING.md — please fill these in before requesting merge. - In the idempotent `create` path, if `onConflictDoNothing` skips the insert and the fallback `SELECT` returns `null` (race condition), the route returns `201 null`; adding a guard would harden this. - The `POST` response returns a raw schema row without the `relatedIssue` nested object included in the `GET` response, creating an inconsistent API shape.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the PR template compliance and the null-return edge case in the idempotent create path. All findings are P2; no P0/P1 bugs found. However, the CONTRIBUTING.md template requirement (Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) is explicitly mandatory per the repo's contribution policy and not yet fulfilled, which warrants a 4 rather than 5. server/src/services/issue-relations.ts — null-return in idempotent create path and response shape inconsistency with listForIssue

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issue-relations.ts | New service implementing listForIssue, create (with inverse edge management), and deleteById; create can return null in the idempotent conflict path, and POST response shape differs from GET | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds GET/POST/DELETE REST endpoints for issue relations; follows existing route patterns; error handling delegates to global errorHandler middleware | | packages/db/src/schema/issue_relations.ts | Widens the type column's TypeScript assertion from "blocks" to IssueRelationType; no DB migration needed since it's a TS-only change on a text column | | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Extends ISSUE_RELATION_TYPES from ["blocks"] to four types; straightforward additive change | | packages/shared/src/validators/issue.ts | Adds createIssueRelationSchema (relatedIssueId: uuid, type: enum) and its inferred type; clean and correct | | packages/shared/src/index.ts | Re-exports createIssueRelationSchema and CreateIssueRelation type; straightforward barrel update | | packages/shared/src/validators/index.ts | Adds createIssueRelationSchema and CreateIssueRelation type to re-exports; clean barrel update | | server/src/services/index.ts | Exports issueRelationService from the new module; single-line addition, correct |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issue-relations.ts Line: 137-153 Comment: **`create` can return `null` in the idempotent path** When `onConflictDoNothing` skips the forward insert (relation already exists), the fallback `SELECT` could theoretically return `null` under a concurrent delete — causing `res.status(201).json(null)` in the route. In addition, on the happy-path the returned row is the raw schema row without the `relatedIssue` nested object that `listForIssue` includes, so callers get inconsistent shapes from `POST` vs `GET`. Consider guarding against the null case: ```typescript if (!created) { const existing = await tx .select() .from(issueRelations) .where( and( eq(issueRelations.companyId, issue.companyId), eq(issueRelations.issueId, issueId), eq(issueRelations.relatedIssueId, input.relatedIssueId), eq(issueRelations.type, input.type), ), ) .then((rows) => rows[0] ?? null); if (!existing) throw conflict("Relation already existed but could not be retrieved"); return existing; } ``` How can I resolve this? The service-level guard would only matter if `listForIssue` were called outside the route — consider removing it or relying on the companyId from the existing route-level check. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. 3774.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a deterministic `blocked → todo` auto-transition for dependent issues when their last blocker reaches `done`, complementing the existing wakeup path. It also ships the full CRUD API (`GET/POST/DELETE /issues/:id/relations`) and extends `ISSUE_RELATION_TYPES` with `blocked_by`, `related`, and `duplicate` (from the stacked #3772). The PR description is missing the required sections from the `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` — specifically the **Thinking Path**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** blocks. Per CONTRIBUTING.md, all PRs must follow this template.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the missing PR template and reviewing the two P2 logic notes; no data-loss or breaking-change risk. All remaining findings are P2, but two of them (the UPDATE race condition that can emit duplicate activity log entries, and the wakeup gap for cancelled-blocker scenarios) describe correctness gaps in the new code path rather than pure style issues, warranting a 4 rather than 5. The PR template omission is a process requirement that must be met before merge. server/src/services/issue-relations.ts (UPDATE guard + cancelled-blocker asymmetry) and server/src/routes/issues.ts (201 on idempotent create)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issue-relations.ts | New service with CRUD for issue relations and `autoTransitionBlockedDependents`; minor issues: UPDATE should add a status guard to prevent duplicate activity log entries under concurrent resolution, and the `cancelled`-blocker handling is asymmetric with `listWakeableBlockedDependents`. | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds GET/POST/DELETE relation endpoints and integrates `autoTransitionBlockedDependents` into the `becameDone` block; POST always returns 201 even for idempotent (already-existing) relation creates. | | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Extends `ISSUE_RELATION_TYPES` from `["blocks"]` to include `"blocked_by"`, `"related"`, and `"duplicate"` — clean, additive change. | | packages/db/src/schema/issue_relations.ts | Replaces the inline `"blocks"` type literal with the shared `IssueRelationType` — straightforward schema improvement. | | packages/shared/src/validators/issue.ts | Adds `createIssueRelationSchema` and its type — correct use of `z.enum` over the shared constants array. | | packages/shared/src/index.ts | Re-exports new schema and type — no issues. | | packages/shared/src/validators/index.ts | Re-exports new validator and type — no issues. | | server/src/services/index.ts | Adds `issueRelationService` export — no issues. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issue-relations.ts Line: 262-271 Comment: **UPDATE lacks a `status = 'blocked'` guard — duplicate activity log entries possible** If two different blockers of the same dependent both resolve concurrently, both `autoTransitionBlockedDependents` calls can snapshot `C` as `blocked`, compute it eligible, and successfully run `UPDATE ... SET status = 'todo'` — Postgres row-locking lets both proceed (the second is a silent no-op), but the caller still returns all items in `toTransition`, so two `issue.auto_unblocked` log entries are emitted for the same issue. Switching to a `.returning()` + status guard in the WHERE clause means only the winning transaction yields the row, and the caller only logs entries that actually changed: ```typescript const updated = await tx .update(issues) .set({ status: "todo", updatedAt: new Date() }) .where( and( inArray(issues.id, toTransition.map((c) => c.id)), eq(issues.status, "blocked"), ), ) .returning({ id: issues.id, companyId: issues.companyId, assigneeAgentId: issues.assigneeAgentId, }); return updated; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 1358-1359 Comment: **`201 Created` returned for an already-existing relation** When `onConflictDoNothing` skips the insert, the service fetches and returns the pre-existing row. The caller always responds with `201`, which is semantically incorrect for an idempotent request that found an existing resource — clients expecting `201` to mean "just created" will be misled. Consider returning `200` when `created` was `undefined` in the service (i.e. the edge already existed), or have the service signal the distinction to the route. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issue-relations.ts Line: 252-257 Comment: **`cancelled` blocker treated as resolved here but not in `listWakeableBlockedDependents`** `autoTransitionBlockedDependents` correctly counts a `cancelled` blocker as resolved (it doesn't add to `openBlockersByIssue`), so a dependent whose only remaining non-cancelled blocker just finished will be auto-transitioned to `todo`. However, `listWakeableBlockedDependents` (in `issues.ts`) requires `allBlockersDone` — where `done` is checked strictly and `cancelled` fails the test — so no wakeup fires for that agent. The issue ends up in the right state (`todo`) but the assignee agent is never notified. Worth aligning the two definitions of "resolved" so the wakeup fires in the cancelled-blocker case as well. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

### Recommendation **Why:** The "No Automatic Recovery" decision in the tree is scoped to agent crashes (stale `in_progress` tasks), not to dependency resolution. Auto-transitioning a blocked issue to `todo` when its sole blocker completes is arguably a different pattern — it is deterministic (all blockers resolved = no longer blocked) rather than speculative (agent crashed = maybe retry?). However, the tree does not explicitly distinguish these cases, so the fit is ambiguous. The activity logging (`issue.auto_unblocked`) and wakeup behavior are well-aligned. **Suggested path forward:** This is a reasonable extension that the tree does not clearly prohibit or endorse. If the maintainers agree this is the right behavior, update `product/task-system/NODE.md` to clarify that "No Automatic Recovery" applies to agent crash recovery (status reset), not to deterministic dependency resolution (blocked-to-todo when blockers complete). This would resolve the ambiguity for future contributors. Alternatively, make the auto-transition opt-in per company/routine if the team wants conservative defaults. --- Reviewed commit: f52df35 · Tree snapshot: fff6709 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3775.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a full-stack `IssueRelations` panel that lets users view, add, and remove four relation types (`blocks`, `blocked_by`, `related`, `duplicate`) on issue detail pages, backed by three new REST endpoints and a service that automatically maintains inverse edges. The PR description does not follow the required template from `CONTRIBUTING.md`. The following required sections are missing: **Thinking Path**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and the **Checklist**. Because this is a visible UI change, before/after screenshots are also required per `CONTRIBUTING.md`. Please fill out the full PR template before requesting merge.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to match the required CONTRIBUTING.md template (Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, screenshots); the code itself is correct. All remaining inline findings are P2 (UX polish, query-key convention, a theoretical null-return edge case). The one blocker is the missing PR template sections and screenshots required by CONTRIBUTING.md — which is a hard requirement for merge per the repo's contribution guidelines. No files have correctness issues; `ui/src/components/IssueRelations.tsx` has the most P2 polish items (error handling, loading state, query key).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/db/src/schema/issue_relations.ts | Upgrades the `type` column's TS type annotation from the literal `"blocks"` to the `IssueRelationType` union — safe TypeScript-only change, no DB migration needed. | | packages/shared/src/constants.ts | Extends `ISSUE_RELATION_TYPES` from `["blocks"]` to include `blocked_by`, `related`, and `duplicate`; clean, additive change. | | packages/shared/src/validators/issue.ts | Adds `createIssueRelationSchema` (uuid `relatedIssueId` + enum `type`) and its inferred type; straightforward Zod schema addition. | | server/src/services/issue-relations.ts | New service with `listForIssue`, `create` (with inverse-edge maintenance), and `deleteById` (cascade-deletes inverse). Logic is correct; minor: `create` can return `null` in an edge-case race, and the service re-queries the issue that the route already fetched. | | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds three REST endpoints (GET/POST/DELETE) for issue relations; auth, validation, and activity logging are all wired correctly. | | ui/src/api/issue-relations.ts | Typed API client wrapping the three new REST endpoints; clean and consistent with the rest of `ui/src/api`. | | ui/src/components/IssueRelations.tsx | New panel component for viewing/adding/removing relations; works correctly but has a few P2 UX gaps: no error handling on mutations, "No matches" flashes during loading, and the search query key is not using the centralized `queryKeys` factory. | | ui/src/lib/queryKeys.ts | Adds `relations(issueId)` key to the issues namespace; consistent with existing patterns. | | ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx | Mounts `IssueRelations` below `IssueProperties` in both the desktop side panel and mobile bottom sheet; straightforward integration. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/IssueRelations.tsx Line: 72-89 Comment: **No error feedback for failed mutations** Both `createRelation` and `deleteRelation` lack `onError` handlers. If the server returns an error (e.g. self-relation check, cross-company issue, relation already removed), the popover stays open or the row stays visible with no indication anything went wrong. Users are left wondering why their action had no effect. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issue-relations.ts Line: 137-152 Comment: **`create` can return `null` in a race condition** When the forward edge already existed (`!created`), the fallback `select` uses `rows[0] ?? null`. In the (extremely unlikely) window between the conflict and the `select`, another transaction could delete that row, making `existing` null. The route then calls `res.status(201).json(null)`, which won't crash the UI (the `onSuccess` handler ignores the returned value), but the TypeScript return type implies a non-null row. Consider throwing if `existing` is null, or returning a sentinel so the caller knows the relation already existed. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/IssueRelations.tsx Line: 63-70 Comment: **Ad-hoc query key bypasses centralized `queryKeys`** The search query uses a hand-rolled key array `["issues", selectedCompanyId, "search", search]` instead of the `queryKeys` factory used throughout the codebase. If the `issues` namespace is ever reorganized or globally invalidated via `queryKeys`, this query won't be affected and stale search data will linger in the cache. Consider adding a `queryKeys.issues.search(companyId, term)` entry and using it here. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/IssueRelations.tsx Line: 142-164 Comment: **"No matches" flashes during the initial fetch** `searchResults` defaults to `[]`, so while the query is still fetching, the "No matches" message renders immediately — users see a false negative for a moment before results arrive. Destructuring `isLoading` (or `isPending`) from the `useQuery` call and gating the empty-state message on `!isLoading` prevents the flicker. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3776.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a periodic `agent-stale-cleanup` service that auto-recovers agents stuck in `error` or stale `running` status back to `idle`, and hardens the opencode adapter with a 15-minute default timeout and `doom_loop`/`question` deny permissions. - **P1 — dead threshold in `resetStaleRunningAgents`**: The DB query pre-filters with `runningCutoff` (10 min) for *both* statuses, making the subsequent `errorCutoff` (5 min) guard on line 77 permanently false. Error agents will only be recovered after 10 minutes, not the documented 5 — the `ERROR_RECOVERY_THRESHOLD_MS` constant has no effect.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the threshold bug in resetStaleRunningAgents — the opencode adapter changes are clean. One P1 logic bug: the 5-minute error-recovery threshold is effectively dead code because the query uses the 10-minute running threshold for both statuses. Agents in error will recover, just 2x slower than intended. Remaining findings are P2 style/consistency issues that do not block correctness. server/src/services/agent-stale-cleanup.ts — the threshold query logic needs correction before the documented 5-minute error recovery works as intended.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/agent-stale-cleanup.ts | New periodic cleanup service — contains a logic bug where the 5-minute error-recovery threshold is unreachable because the query pre-filters with the 10-minute running threshold, causing error agents to also wait 10 minutes instead of 5. | | server/src/index.ts | Wires in the new cleanup service at startup; the returned cleanup function is discarded and the service is not gated on heartbeatSchedulerEnabled (minor consistency issues). | | server/src/services/index.ts | Adds barrel exports for the two new cleanup functions; straightforward change. | | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/execute.ts | Changes default timeout from 0 (infinite) to 900 s via asNumber(config.timeoutSec, 900); no issues. | | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.ts | Adds doom_loop: deny and question: deny to the injected runtime config to prevent interactive prompts in headless runs; logic is correct. | | packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/runtime-config.test.ts | Test expectations updated to match the two new permission fields; correctly covers the opt-out path as well. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/index.ts`, line 576-620 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/395442470deaaf1fbda6a8a5bec8a5015761b250/server/src/index.ts#L576-L620)) **Cleanup service not gated on `heartbeatSchedulerEnabled`** All heartbeat-related services (`heartbeatService`, `routineService`, and the `reapOrphanedRuns` / `tickTimers` loops) are started only when `config.heartbeatSchedulerEnabled` is true, but `startStaleAgentCleanup` is called unconditionally. On deployments where the heartbeat scheduler is disabled, agents never transition through `running`/`error` states via the normal scheduler path, so the periodic sweep is unnecessary churn. Consider wrapping it in the same `if (config.heartbeatSchedulerEnabled)` block for consistency.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/index.ts Line: 576-620 Comment: **Cleanup service not gated on `heartbeatSchedulerEnabled`** All heartbeat-related services (`heartbeatService`, `routineService`, and the `reapOrphanedRuns` / `tickTimers` loops) are started only when `config.heartbeatSchedulerEnabled` is true, but `startStaleAgentCleanup` is called unconditionally. On deployments where the heartbeat scheduler is disabled, agents never transition through `running`/`error` states via the normal scheduler path, so the periodic sweep is unnecessary churn. Consider wrapping it in the same `if (config.heartbeatSchedulerEnabled)` block for consistency. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/agent-stale-cleanup.ts Line: 40-45 Comment: **5-minute error-recovery threshold is dead code** The initial DB query filters agents with `lt(agents.updatedAt, runningCutoff)` — meaning only agents whose `updatedAt` is older than **10 minutes** are returned. Because `errorCutoff` is only 5 minutes in the past and `runningCutoff` is 10 minutes in the past, any agent returned by the query is already older than 5 minutes, making the subsequent guard on line 77 (`agent.lastUpdated > errorCutoff`) permanently `false`. Error agents are therefore only recovered after 10 minutes, not the documented 5. Fix: use per-status cutoffs in the query so the intent is reflected in the actual filtering: ```ts .where( or( and(eq(agents.status, "running"), lt(agents.updatedAt, runningCutoff)), and(eq(agents.status, "error"), lt(agents.updatedAt, errorCutoff)), ), ) ``` With this change the guard on line 77-79 becomes redundant and can be removed. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/index.ts Line: 672-674 Comment: **Cleanup function discarded; log message cites only one threshold** `startStaleAgentCleanup` returns a `() => void` that calls `clearInterval`, but the return value is never captured. If anything in the future needs a graceful drain before `process.exit`, the interval won't be stopped. Storing it alongside the other service teardown paths would be safer: ```ts const stopStaleAgentCleanup = startStaleAgentCleanup(db); // then in shutdown(): stopStaleAgentCleanup(); ``` Additionally, the log message says "10-minute threshold" but there are actually two thresholds (5 min for `error`, 10 min for `running`); updating the message would make it easier to correlate with the constants at the top of the file. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/index.ts Line: 576-620 Comment: **Cleanup service not gated on `heartbeatSchedulerEnabled`** All heartbeat-related services (`heartbeatService`, `routineService`, and the `reapOrphanedRuns` / `tickTimers` loops) are started only when `config.heartbeatSchedulerEnabled` is true, but `startStaleAgentCleanup` is called unconditionally. On deployments where the heartbeat scheduler is disabled, agents never transition through `running`/`error` states via the normal scheduler path, so the periodic sweep is unnecessary churn. Consider wrapping it in the same `if (config.heartbeatSchedulerEnabled)` block for consistency. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/agent-stale-cleanup.ts Line: 113 Comment: **Indentation off on the return statement** `return resetCount;` is at column 0 instead of being indented inside the function body, unlike every other statement in the function. ```suggestion return resetCount; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

🌱 **gardener** · ⚠️ `CONFLICT` · severity: `high` · commit: `3954424` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Agent error recovery | Auto-resets agents from `error` to `idle` after 5 min, from `running` to `idle` after 10 min with no active runs | "When an agent crashes mid-task, Paperclip does **not** auto-reassign or auto-release the task. Stale tasks are surfaced through dashboards. Recovery is manual/explicit." (task-system/NODE.md) | ⚠️ Conflict | | Problem surfacing | Silent auto-recovery on a 2-min sweep interval; errors cleared before operator may see them | "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery, no automatic reassignment." (product/NODE.md decision #6) | ⚠️ Conflict | | Adapter hardening | opencode-local: default timeout 900s, deny doom_loop and question permissions | Adapter config is adapter-owned (adapters/NODE.md); sensible defaults are within adapter scope | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "No Automatic Recovery" decision: stale/error state is surfaced, not auto-fixed - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #6: "Surface problems, don't hide them" - [`product/agent-model/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Agent statuses: `active`, `paused`, `idle`, `running`, `error`, `terminated` - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter-defined config and runtime behavior
### Recommendation **Why:** The tree explicitly documents "No Automatic Recovery" as a load-bearing design decision. The rationale states: "Automatic recovery hides failures. Good visibility lets the right entity decide what to do." Auto-resetting agents from `error` to `idle` on a silent 2-minute sweep contradicts this directly — an operator may never see that an agent errored, because by the time they check the dashboard it has been quietly reset. The opencode adapter hardening portion is fine (adapter-owned config). **Suggested path forward:** Split this PR: land the opencode adapter hardening (timeout + permission defaults) separately since it aligns with adapter-owned config. For the auto-recovery service, consider making it opt-in via an instance setting (e.g. `autoRecoverStalledAgents: true`) that defaults to **off**, so the default behavior remains "surface problems, don't hide them." Alternatively, surface a high-priority activity event or board notification before resetting — that way the operator sees the error even if it auto-recovers. If the team decides auto-recovery should be default-on, update `product/task-system/NODE.md` to reflect that decision. --- Reviewed commit: 3954424 · Tree snapshot: fff6709 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3779.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR addresses several PAP-1497 follow-up gaps: stale active-run display on completed issues, missing creator filters in the Issues page and Inbox, oversized issue list payloads (`executionPolicy`, `executionState`, `executionWorkspaceSettings` nulled out; `description` capped at 1200 chars), `::int` overflow in all cost/finance aggregate queries (replaced with `::double precision`), markdown field leakage from the company-skills list, and route-placeholder strings becoming spurious issue links. Targeted regression tests cover every changed path. All remaining findings are P2 style suggestions.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all remaining comments are P2 style/cleanup suggestions with no blocking correctness issues. The overflow fix, payload trimming, skill list projection, and filter additions are well-tested and straightforward. The one logic observation (stale `activeRun` in `timelineRuns`) is a pre-existing transient display edge case that does not cause data loss, and all other findings are style nits. CI passes and targeted regression tests are included. `ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx` — the `timelineRuns` memo still references raw `activeRun` rather than `resolvedActiveRun`, which can transiently hide a completed run from the historical timeline.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx | Fixes stale active-run display using `resolveIssueActiveRun`, but `timelineRuns` still references raw `activeRun` which can be stale after issue completion, transiently hiding the completed run from the timeline. | | server/src/services/costs.ts | All aggregate casts changed from `::int` to `::double precision` via the new `sumAsNumber` helper, eliminating Postgres int32 overflow on large cost/token sums. | | server/src/services/finance.ts | Finance debit/credit/estimated expressions migrated from `::int` to `::double precision`; `netCents` ordering expression updated consistently. | | server/src/services/issues.ts | Introduces `issueListSelect` that nulls out `executionPolicy`, `executionState`, `executionWorkspaceSettings` and truncates `description` to 1200 chars; adds optional `includeCommentBodies` to `findMentionedProjectIds`. | | server/src/services/company-skills.ts | Refactors `list()` to use a narrow DB projection excluding the `markdown` column; adds `CompanySkillListDbRow`/`CompanySkillListRow` types and corresponding `toCompanySkillListRow` mapper. | | server/src/services/companies.ts | Cast updated to `::double precision`; 3781.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR extends the existing requester-wakeup pattern to the `reject` and `request-revision` approval routes by extracting the logic into a shared `queueRequesterWakeup` helper, fixing agents that were left waiting for a timer heartbeat after a rejection or revision request. - **P1 — `request-revision` wakeup fires on every call, not just newly-applied transitions**: `reject` and `approve` guard `queueRequesterWakeup` behind `if (applied)` to prevent duplicate wakeups on idempotent retries, but `requestRevision` has no equivalent guard — if the endpoint is called more than once on the same approval, the agent is woken up each time. - **P2 — unused `primaryIssueId` variable** in the `approve` handler after the refactor moved the computation into `queueRequesterWakeup`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after addressing the missing idempotency guard on the request-revision wakeup path. One P1 finding: the request-revision route unconditionally wakes the agent on every call, unlike reject and approve which gate the wakeup on `applied === true`. This is a real behavioral inconsistency that can cause duplicate agent runs on retry. The rest of the change is a clean refactor with good test coverage. server/src/routes/approvals.ts — the request-revision handler and the lingering `primaryIssueId` variable in the approve handler.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/approvals.ts | Introduces `queueRequesterWakeup` helper and wires reject/request-revision wakeups; request-revision is missing the `applied` guard that reject and approve use, leaving it vulnerable to duplicate agent wakeups on retry. | | server/src/__tests__/approval-routes-idempotency.test.ts | Adds regression assertions for reject and request-revision wakeups; tests cover the happy path but no test guards against duplicate wakeups when request-revision is called more than once. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `server/src/routes/approvals.ts`, line 226 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/2540e2dab58c76c7b85d33ea31f9c9e76377e238/server/src/routes/approvals.ts#L226)) **Unused variable after refactor** `primaryIssueId` was needed by the old inline wakeup block but is no longer referenced after it was moved into `queueRequesterWakeup`. This will produce a TypeScript/linter unused-variable warning.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/approvals.ts Line: 226 Comment: **Unused variable after refactor** `primaryIssueId` was needed by the old inline wakeup block but is no longer referenced after it was moved into `queueRequesterWakeup`. This will produce a TypeScript/linter unused-variable warning. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/approvals.ts Line: 305-308 Comment: **Wakeup fires on every call, not just newly-applied revisions** `reject` and `approve` both return `{ approval, applied }` and only call `queueRequesterWakeup` when `applied === true`, preventing duplicate wakeups on idempotent retries. `requestRevision` returns the approval directly (no `applied` flag) and calls `queueRequesterWakeup` unconditionally. If a board user (or an automated retry) calls this endpoint again on the same approval, the requesting agent is woken up a second time — a duplicate run that the other two decision paths actively prevent. If `svc.requestRevision` is idempotent at the DB level (i.e. it can be called multiple times successfully), the wakeup will fire on every call. The fix is either to have `requestRevision` return `{ approval, applied }` like `reject`/`approve`, or to check the pre-call status against the post-call status before calling `queueRequesterWakeup`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/approvals.ts Line: 226 Comment: **Unused variable after refactor** `primaryIssueId` was needed by the old inline wakeup block but is no longer referenced after it was moved into `queueRequesterWakeup`. This will produce a TypeScript/linter unused-variable warning. ```suggestion const linkedIssueIds = linkedIssues.map((issue) => issue.id); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3783.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a thin `detectAvailablePort` helper that forwards a configured `hostname` to `detect-port`, so Paperclip's startup probe matches the actual bind address instead of probing all interfaces. Both the server listen-port probe and the embedded-Postgres fallback are updated to use the helper, and a focused vitest suite validates the two code paths. The logic is correct and the change is appropriately scoped. The only gap is a missing test case for the `undefined`-hostname path (i.e., calling `detectAvailablePort(port)` with no second argument), which is exercised in production by call sites that always supply a value but is not covered by the test suite.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the only finding is a minor test-coverage gap; all production paths are correct. All three changed files are correct. The helper logic correctly handles blank/undefined hostnames, the two call sites in index.ts are updated consistently, and the TypeScript types pass. The single P2 comment is about a missing test for an edge case that does not affect runtime behavior. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/port-detection.ts | New helper that wraps `detect-port` with optional hostname — logic is correct, handles blank/undefined hostname gracefully by falling back to the old behavior. | | server/src/__tests__/port-detection.test.ts | Two focused tests covering the hostname-aware and blank-hostname paths; missing explicit coverage for the `undefined` (no second arg) case and for non-loopback hosts like `"0.0.0.0"`. | | server/src/index.ts | Swaps bare `detect-port` calls for the new helper at both the server listen probe and the embedded-Postgres fallback probe; changes are targeted and consistent. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/port-detection.test.ts Line: 29-35 Comment: **Missing test for `undefined` hostname** The helper accepts `hostname?: string`, so callers can omit the second argument. The current tests cover a whitespace string `" "` but not the `undefined` / no-arg path. While the code is obviously correct (optional chaining returns `undefined` → falsy branch), a test makes the contract explicit and protects against accidental future refactors. ```ts it("falls back to default detect-port probing when host is undefined", async () => { detectPortMock.mockResolvedValue(3100); await expect(detectAvailablePort(3100)).resolves.toBe(3100); expect(detectPortMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith(3100); }); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

### Recommendation **Why:** Same structural concern as the previous review still stands on the re-review commit (`a362f6bf`, "fix(pr): restore upstream dev and skill behavior"). Recent commits on this branch (`Prevent members from consuming invite links`, `feat: paginate company invite history`, `Improve invite auth clarity`, `Deduplicate human join requests`, `Fix inbox approvals for new users`) are excellent invite-flow hardening, but they operate inside the new multi-operator shape that the tree does not yet describe. The single-tenant decision in `product/company-model/NODE.md` is unchanged on the tree, so merging this would land a direction change as implementation detail. **Suggested path forward:** Before merge, open a tree PR that either (a) amends `product/company-model/NODE.md` and `product/NODE.md` #8 to describe the move from single-tenant to collaborative multi-operator (with explicit scope: still "one org's operators", not generic SaaS multi-tenancy), or (b) keeps the feature behind a deployment-mode flag so `local_trusted` and the existing single-operator mental model stay unchanged. Greptile Summary

This is a large branch-consolidation PR implementing multi-user access for Paperclip: authenticated board sessions, invite flows, company membership management, profile settings, join-request deduplication, and tightened authorization across plugins, adapters, and workspace routes. All critical issues from previous review rounds appear addressed — the profile-image silent-clear bug, non-atomic role+grants update, last-owner race condition, `data:` URI validation bypass, DB-level join-request uniqueness, and plugin lifecycle authorization are all correctly fixed. Remaining findings are P2: the owner-guard transaction logic is duplicated across four places (two route handlers and two service functions that are never called from routes), the avatar upload in `ProfileSettings` silently requires a selected company context, and a minor React `useMemo` deps gap in `Auth.tsx`. Per CONTRIBUTING.md, this PR has UI changes across invite landing, company access, profile settings, sidebar, and account menu — **please provide before/after screenshots** before merging.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once screenshots are provided; all previous P0/P1 issues are resolved and only P2 style/maintenance findings remain. Every critical concern from prior review threads (atomic member updates, last-owner protection, data: URI validation, DB-level join-request uniqueness, plugin admin authz) is correctly addressed. The three new findings are P2: code duplication that does not introduce a current bug, a UX limitation in profile avatar upload, and a minor hooks lint issue. No data-loss or security regressions were found. server/src/services/access.ts and server/src/routes/access.ts — the new service functions `updateMember`/`updateMemberAndPermissions` should replace the duplicated inline transaction logic in the two PATCH route handlers.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/authz.ts | Adds `hasBoardOrgAccess`/`assertBoardOrgAccess` and extends `assertCompanyAccess` with viewer/status enforcement; instance-admin bypass on the outer membership check is intentionally removed, consistent with the multi-user design. | | server/src/services/access.ts | Adds `updateMember` and `updateMemberAndPermissions` with correct `FOR UPDATE` lock and last-owner guard; both functions are exported and tested but not called from any route handler — route handlers re-implement the same inline transaction logic, creating four copies of the guard. | | server/src/routes/access.ts | Large addition of invite listing, company-member CRUD, user-directory, and admin-user endpoints; `PATCH /members/:memberId` and `PATCH /…/role-and-grants` both duplicate the owner-guard transaction inline rather than calling the service-layer functions. | | server/src/middleware/auth.ts | Extends board actor with `userName`, `userEmail`, and full `memberships` array (role + status) for all auth paths; well-structured and consistent across session, API-key, and local-implicit sources. | | packages/shared/src/validators/access.ts | Adds profile-image validation that correctly restricts to `http:`/`https:` and asset paths (fixes prior `data:` URI issue); `updateCurrentUserProfileSchema` correctly uses optional transform to preserve image on name-only updates. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0057_tidy_join_requests.sql | Cleans up duplicate pending-human join requests via ranked CTEs and adds partial unique indexes on `(company_id, requesting_user_id)` and `(company_id, lower(request_email_snapshot))`, providing DB-level uniqueness enforcement. | | server/src/routes/auth.ts | Profile PATCH now uses conditional spread to avoid clearing image on name-only updates; GET-session and GET/PATCH profile are clean. | | server/src/routes/plugins.ts | Destructive lifecycle mutations (delete, enable, disable, config) now require `assertInstanceAdmin`; read/data endpoints use `assertBoardOrgAccess` — addresses prior concern about under-restricted plugin management. | | server/src/lib/join-request-dedupe.ts | In-memory dedup helpers are now backed by DB-level partial unique indexes from the migration; correctly handles user-ID and email identity matching. | | ui/src/pages/CompanyAccess.tsx | New company-member management page using single `updateMemberAccess` call to atomic `/role-and-grants` endpoint; correctly handles pending join requests and propagates the `access` capability flags. | | ui/src/pages/InviteLanding.tsx | Adds human sign-in/sign-up flow on the invite landing page with branded company header, error-code mapping for common auth failures, and `invite-memory` persistence for post-login redirect. | | ui/src/pages/ProfileSettings.tsx | New profile settings page; avatar upload correctly ties to company file storage but silently requires a selected company context, which may surprise users without an active membership. | | tests/e2e/multi-user-authenticated.spec.ts | New E2E suite covering multi-user authenticated flows; paired with separate playwright config for the authenticated mode. | | packages/db/src/schema/join_requests.ts | Adds Drizzle-ORM partial unique indexes matching the SQL migration; correctly uses `sql` template for `lower()` expression index. |

Path: ui/src/pages/ProfileSettings.tsx Line: 91-100 Comment: **Avatar upload silently requires a selected company** `uploadAvatarMutation` gates on `!!selectedCompanyId`, throwing "Select a company before uploading a profile avatar." if none is selected. 3784.body:
This is a large feature PR with a lot of surface area. Here are a few focused notes on bugs, security, and coverage gaps:

**1. Invite landing auto-accept race condition (`InviteLanding.tsx`)**
The `useEffect` that triggers `acceptMutation.mutate()` depends on `acceptMutation` itself (an unstable object). This can cause the effect to re-fire more often than intended. Consider tracking a stable `autoAcceptStarted` ref or adding a stricter guard so the mutation fires exactly once per token.

**2. Missing input validation on invite form**
In `CompanyInvites.tsx`, the role radio buttons are client-side only. Make sure the backend `createCompanyInvite` endpoint validates `humanRole` against the allowed set and rejects unknown roles, otherwise a crafted request could create an invite with an invalid/missing role.

**3. `useMemo` dependency on `companyMembers?.users` in `IssueDetail.tsx`**
`buildCompanyUserProfileMap` and `buildMarkdownMentionOptions` are called inside `useMemo` with `companyMembers?.users` as the only dependency. If those utility functions are ever changed to be impure or to default something globally, this could leak. Not a bug now, but worth a quick audit.

**4. Test coverage gaps**
- `JoinRequestQueue.tsx` and `InstanceAccess.tsx` do not appear to have corresponding test files in this diff. Given they handle approve/reject mutations and admin promotion/demotion, unit tests for the error states (403, empty lists, mutation failures) would add good hardening.
- `CompanyAccess.tsx` is similarly untested in this changeset.

**5. Clipboard fallback**
In `CompanyInvites.tsx`, `copyInviteUrl` falls back to a toast when `navigator.clipboard` is unavailable. That is good UX, but verify that the invite URL is also visually selectable in the UI so the user can still copy it manually.

Overall this is a well-structured changeset with good fixture/test coverage for the landing flows. The main follow-ups are backend validation and tests for the queue/admin pages.

3785.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a blocker-dependency check to the `issue_status_changed` wakeup path: before enqueuing the wakeup for an assignee, it calls `getRelationSummaries` and suppresses the wakeup while any `blockedBy` issue has a status other than `done`. The production logic is straightforward and the fail-open error handling (warning + proceed) is appropriate. - **P1 – ineffective negative test**: `vi.waitFor(() => expect(mockWakeup).not.toHaveBeenCalled())` resolves on its first poll (the mock is uncalled at that moment), before the async IIFE in the route has had a chance to run. The suppression test would pass even if the wakeup were still being fired — it needs to drain async work first (e.g. `await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 0))`) before the synchronous assertion. - **P2 – `cancelled` blockers treated as unresolved**: `blocker.status !== \"done\"` leaves an issue permanently wakeup-suppressed when its only blocker is cancelled, since it will never reach `done`. Consider also releasing the block on `\"cancelled\"` status (consistent treatment exists in `listWakeableBlockedDependents`, so this would require a parallel change there too).

Confidence Score: 4/5

Production change is safe to merge; the test for the core fix is unreliable and should be corrected before merge. One P1 finding: the suppression regression test uses `vi.waitFor` with a negative assertion that passes immediately, giving no actual coverage for the fix. The production-side logic is correct and fail-open, but without a valid test the stated regression coverage does not exist. server/src/__tests__/issue-dependency-wakeups-routes.test.ts — the negative `vi.waitFor` assertion at lines 212–214 needs to be replaced with a flush-then-assert pattern.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds blocker-check guard before `issue_status_changed` wakeup; logic is correct but `cancelled` blockers are not treated as resolved, which may cause permanent suppression. | | server/src/__tests__/issue-dependency-wakeups-routes.test.ts | New regression tests added; the negative assertion uses `vi.waitFor` incorrectly and passes immediately, providing no real coverage for the suppression behavior. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/issue-dependency-wakeups-routes.test.ts Line: 212-214 Comment: **Negative `vi.waitFor` passes immediately, not actually verifying suppression** `vi.waitFor(() => expect(mockWakeup).not.toHaveBeenCalled())` resolves on its very first poll because `mockWakeup` starts uncalled. The `void (async () => { ... })()` IIFE in the route handler is still queued when this assertion is evaluated, so the test exits before the async block has a chance to actually fire (or not fire) the wakeup. If a bug caused the wakeup to be sent anyway, this test would still pass. The positive tests work correctly because `vi.waitFor` retries until the mock *is* called. For the negative case you need to drain pending async work first, then assert synchronously: ```suggestion await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 0)); expect(mockWakeup).not.toHaveBeenCalled(); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 1826 Comment: **`cancelled` blocker treated as unresolved** `blocker.status !== "done"` means a blocker in `"cancelled"` state is considered unresolved and will suppress the wakeup indefinitely. If the blocking issue is cancelled (i.e. it will never be completed), the dependent issue can never be woken via this path without manual intervention. `listWakeableBlockedDependents` has the same condition (`blockerStatus === "done"`), so this is consistent, but it may be worth consciously choosing whether `cancelled` should also release the block. ```suggestion hasUnresolvedBlockers = relations.blockedBy.some( (blocker) => blocker.status !== "done" && blocker.status !== "cancelled", ); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3786.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a bug in `EnvVarEditor` where switching a row to "Secret" mode without selecting a secret would silently fall back to emitting a plain binding. The fix skips unresolved secret-mode rows in `emit` and renames `sealRow` to `createSecretForRow`, adding a `window.prompt` to collect the secret value when no plain value is pre-filled. Three regression tests are added for the main secret-mode flows. Screenshots for the enabled-state change on the "New" button were noted as included in the checklist — please confirm they are attached to the PR, as screenshots are required for visual changes.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is narrowly scoped, intentional, and well-tested for the changed paths. All findings are P2 (style and test quality suggestions). No logic bugs, data integrity issues, or security vulnerabilities were found. The behavioral change is correct and explicitly tested. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/EnvVarEditor.tsx | Core bug fix: `emit` now skips secret-mode rows without a `secretId` instead of falling back to plain, and `createSecretForRow` (renamed from `sealRow`) prompts for the secret value when `plainValue` is empty. Logic is clean and correct. | | ui/src/components/EnvVarEditor.test.tsx | New regression tests cover existing-secret selection, no-fallback-to-plain, and new-secret creation from secret mode. The Seal button flow (plain mode to create-from-value) is not tested, and one assertion is ambiguous. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/EnvVarEditor.tsx Line: 148 Comment: **`window.prompt` collects sensitive value in plain text** `window.prompt` renders an unmasked browser dialog — the secret value is visible on screen and cannot use `type="password"`. For a credential that will be stored as a secret, this is a meaningful UX regression from the previous "Seal" flow where the value was already in a form field. Consider a small modal/dialog with a masked input, or at minimum document this as a known limitation. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/EnvVarEditor.test.tsx Line: 126 Comment: **Ambiguous assertion — can't distinguish "not called" from "called with `undefined`"** `latestEnvCall` returns `undefined` for both `onChange` never being called and `onChange(undefined)` being called. The assertion doesn't verify that `onChange` was actually invoked. A stricter pair of assertions avoids this ambiguity: ```suggestion expect(onChange).toHaveBeenCalledWith(undefined); expect(latestEnvCall(onChange)).toBeUndefined(); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/EnvVarEditor.test.tsx Line: 59-101 Comment: **"Seal" button (plain mode) path is untested** The three tests cover the secret-mode flows well, but the plain-mode "Seal" button — which now also calls `createSecretForRow` — has no coverage. In plain mode with a non-empty value, `createSecretForRow` uses `row.plainValue` directly (no second `window.prompt`) and then clears it after creating the secret. A fourth test would lock in that behaviour and catch regressions in the `sealRow → createSecretForRow` rename. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
