3787.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes #3661 by calling `.toISOString()` on the Drizzle-returned `Date` anchor before interpolating it into the keyset-pagination SQL predicate in `listComments`. Without the conversion, the `Date` object was passed raw into the `sql` template tag followed by `::timestamptz`, causing a runtime `TypeError` whenever the `after` cursor path was exercised.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — single-line serialization fix with no API, schema, or ordering behavior changes. The only remaining finding is a pre-existing P2 design issue (invalid cursor returning `[]` instead of a 400) that this PR neither introduces nor worsens. The fix itself is correct: `toISOString()` produces a string PostgreSQL can cast via `$1::timestamptz`, and the keyset logic is unchanged. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds `.toISOString()` on the Drizzle-returned `Date` before interpolating it into the keyset-pagination SQL predicate, fixing the TypeError on `GET /api/issues/:id/comments?after=...`. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issues.ts Line: 2094 Comment: **Invalid cursor silently returns empty array** When `afterCommentId` is provided but doesn't match any comment on the issue, the function returns `[]`, which is indistinguishable from a legitimate end-of-results response. A client iterating pages could silently swallow a bad cursor and halt pagination prematurely. Returning `null` or throwing/propagating a typed error to the route layer (so it can send a 400) would make the contract clearer, though this is pre-existing behavior unrelated to the timestamp fix. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**Root cause** — Passing a JS Date object directly into a Drizzle/sql template literal can cause driver-specific serialization issues (e.g., inconsistent timezone handling). Serializing to an ISO string and explicitly casting with `::timestamptz` is a clean, safe fix.

No blockers. LGTM.

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a session isolation bug in the openclaw-gateway adapter where `sessionKeyStrategy: \"issue\"` heartbeat wakes (no `issueId`) silently fell through to the shared fixed-session fallback. The fix is minimal and correct: the `issue` guard is widened to always match, returning a per-run session key when `issueId` is absent. All existing strategy paths (`run`, `fixed`, issue-with-id) are unaffected.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the change is a one-line guard expansion with a clear behavioral intent, no unintended side effects on other strategy paths, and a passing regression test. Both remaining findings are P2 (test clarity and vitest workspace exclusion). The core logic change is correct and low-risk. No files require special attention beyond the minor test-clarity note in execute.test.ts.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Expands the `issue` strategy guard to always match, using `paperclip:run:{runId}` as the fallback when `issueId` is absent — correctly fixes the silent shared-session accumulation. | | packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.test.ts | Adds a regression test for the heartbeat path with no issueId; the `configuredSessionKey` value passed in this test case is never consulted by the issue branch, which slightly weakens the test's clarity. |

3790.body:
Greptile Summary

Wraps `svc.checkout()` in `POST /issues/:id/checkout` with a narrow try/catch that converts a Postgres 23505 unique-constraint violation on `issues_open_routine_execution_uq` into a 409 Conflict, letting agents handle routine execution contention through the normal conflict path instead of crashing on an unexpected 500. The approach and type-assertion pattern are consistent with how similar constraint errors are handled elsewhere in the codebase (`plugin-registry.ts`, `companies.ts`).

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the change is narrow, correct, and follows established codebase patterns. Only one changed file with a single, well-scoped try/catch. The constraint name, error code, and re-throw behavior are all correct. The sole remaining finding (no test for the new 409 path) is a P2 quality concern that doesn't affect correctness or safety. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/routes/issues.ts | Adds a narrow try/catch around svc.checkout() to convert Postgres 23505/issues_open_routine_execution_uq into a 409; all other errors are re-thrown; pattern is consistent with plugin-registry.ts and companies.ts. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/routes/issues.ts Line: 2043-2055 Comment: **No test coverage for the new 409 path** The PR checklist acknowledges tests aren't passing locally, but there's no indication that a test will be added later. The 409 mapping for `issues_open_routine_execution_uq` is simple enough to unit-test with a mock that throws a `{ code: "23505", constraint: "issues_open_routine_execution_uq" }` error from `svc.checkout()`. Without a test, a future refactor that changes the constraint name or restructures the catch block could silently regress this back to a 500 — which is exactly the bug this PR is fixing. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

🌱 **gardener** · 🆕 `NEW_TERRITORY` · severity: `medium` · commit: `issue` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|------------|---------------|-----| | Bundled skill `required: true` override | Issue asks for per-agent exclusion of bundled Paperclip skills (to reduce heartbeat context size for ICs) | Adapters tree describes skill injection via symlinks + `ensureCodexSkillsInjected` / `ensureGeminiSkillsInjected`, but no decision on per-role skill scoping or a `required → optional` override policy | 🆕 New | | Config surface (`desiredSkills` / `excludeSkills`) | Proposes extending `desiredSkills` semantics or adding `excludeSkills` to adapter config | Tree says adapter config is declarative via `getConfigSchema`, but does not document a skill-scoping contract | 🆕 New | | Context-budget rationale | ~12KB/heartbeat wasted for Codex, ~1KB for Gemini; follow-up to shipped caveman-extension removal (1a) | Tree is silent on context-budget targets or cross-adapter skill policy | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Skill injection via symlinks into temp `.claude/skills/`; declarative config schema over custom UI. - [`adapters/codex-local/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/codex-local/NODE.md) — Pre-run `ensureCodexSkillsInjected` for skill freshness. - [`adapters/gemini-local/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/gemini-local/NODE.md) — Persistent skill sync via `listGeminiSkills` / `syncGeminiSkills`. - [`product/agent-model/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Adapter-defined agent internals (agent config blobs are adapter-specific, not part of Paperclip protocol).
### Recommendation **Why:** The tree treats adapter-internal skill/config as adapter-defined (see agent-model's "Adapter-Defined Identity"), and describes skill injection mechanics but never decides whether `paperclip-*` skills bundled by `adapter-utils` are per-role or universal. That decision is load-bearing for the "Context Budget" optimization thread (#3794 telemetry, #3793 task-aware injection, this issue) and determines whether `desiredSkills` is additive or authoritative. **Suggested path forward:** Before implementing a config option, capture the decision in the tree — most naturally a new leaf under `adapters/` (or a shared `adapters/skill-injection-contract.md`) answering: 1. Is `desiredSkills` additive or authoritative? 2. When is a bundled skill `required: true` justified (e.g., the `paperclip` protocol skill itself) vs. role-scoped? 3. What is the precedence order between bundled-defaults, agent-config, and company-level overrides? Option 3 in the issue (make bundles `required: false` by default and let `desiredSkills` own the set) is the cleanest fit with "Adapter-Defined Identity" — the adapter ships defaults, but the agent config is the source of truth. Worth confirming with `adapters` owners before coding, since this also interacts with #3793's task-aware injection proposal. --- Reviewed commit: issue · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3796.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR wires the heartbeat run lifecycle into the plugin event bus by adding a reusable `emitPluginEvent()` helper in `activity-log.ts` and emitting `agent.run.started`, `agent.run.finished`, `agent.run.failed`, and `agent.run.cancelled` from `claimQueuedRun` and `setRunStatus` in `heartbeat.ts`. The change is narrow and additive, but there is one contract issue worth fixing before merge: - `emitAgentRunPluginEvent` forwards `run.status` verbatim into the payload. For `timed_out` runs (mapped to `agent.run.failed`), `payload.status` is the internal DB value `\"timed_out\"` rather than the documented `\"failed\"`, creating a mismatch that could silently break plugins checking `payload.status`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after normalising the `timed_out` status in the `agent.run.failed` payload; all other changes are additive and low-risk. One P1 finding: `payload.status: "timed_out"` is emitted inside an `agent.run.failed` event, leaking an undocumented internal status and breaking plugins that check `payload.status === "failed"`. The fix is a one-liner. Everything else (helper extraction, event wiring, tests) is correct and well-structured. `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` line 125 — status normalisation in `emitAgentRunPluginEvent`

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/activity-log.ts | Adds `emitPluginEvent()` helper extracted from existing inline code; refactors `logActivity` to call it — logic is equivalent to the original | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds `emitAgentRunPluginEvent` helper and wires run lifecycle events at claim and terminal-status points; `timed_out` runs emit `agent.run.failed` but expose the raw internal `"timed_out"` status in the payload, creating a contract mismatch for plugins | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-plugin-events.test.ts | New integration tests covering succeeded, failed (exit-code), and cancelled paths; missing coverage for the `timed_out` → `agent.run.failed` mapping |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 125 Comment: **`timed_out` internal status leaks into `agent.run.failed` payload** `run.status` is passed directly, so when a run times out, the emitted event has `eventType: "agent.run.failed"` but `payload.status: "timed_out"`. `"timed_out"` is an internal DB enum value not exposed in the plugin API contract, so any plugin that checks `payload.status === "failed"` to confirm the event type will silently miss timeout failures. The payload should normalise the status to the documented value: ```suggestion status: run.status === "timed_out" ? "failed" : run.status, ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-plugin-events.test.ts Line: 155-185 Comment: **Missing test for `timed_out` → `agent.run.failed` mapping** The test suite covers `exitCode: 1` failures and cancelled runs, but not the `timedOut: true` adapter result. That path is the only one that sets `run.status = "timed_out"`, which is then mapped to `agent.run.failed` — a non-trivial special case worth explicit coverage to guard against regressions and to verify the payload normalisation suggested above. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Addressed the actionable review items in the latest push:
- normalized `timed_out` to `payload.status: "failed"` for `agent.run.failed`
- added explicit timeout coverage
- bumped the test wait window to reduce CI flakiness

I also checked the cancellation-order note. In the current code path `cancelRunInternal()` is not wrapped in a single `db.transaction`, and `setRunStatus()` persists the run update before emitting the plugin event, so there is not a transaction-rollback mismatch in the current implementation. I left that part unchanged to keep this PR narrowly scoped, but I can follow up separately if the team wants stricter post-update/event ordering semantics.

3798.body:
Greptile Summary

Replaces the fixed `monthlyMonths * 30 days` cutoff with a calendar-month–anchored helper (`monthlyRetentionCutoff`) that sets the cutoff to the first day of the month `monthlyMonths` calendar months before the current month. The implementation is correct — `setDate(1)` is applied before `setMonth` to avoid day-overflow on end-of-month dates, and the local-time arithmetic is consistent with the existing `monthKey` bucketing used during pruning.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the cutoff math is correct, the only finding is a suggestion to add more test scenarios. No P0 or P1 issues found. The core arithmetic (setDate(1) before setMonth, Math.max guard, negative-month rollover) all behave correctly for the cases verified. The single P2 comment is a test-coverage suggestion that does not affect production behavior. packages/db/src/backup-lib.test.ts — could benefit from year-rollover and monthlyMonths=1 test cases.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/db/src/backup-lib.ts | Extracts monthlyRetentionCutoff into a calendar-month–aware helper; logic is correct — setDate(1) before setMonth avoids day-overflow; consistent with local-time monthKey bucketing. | | packages/db/src/backup-lib.test.ts | Adds a focused unit test for monthlyRetentionCutoff covering a 31-day month; correct assertions, but only a single scenario (March 31, monthlyMonths=2) — year-rollover and monthlyMonths=1 cases are untested. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: packages/db/src/backup-lib.test.ts Line: 79-87 Comment: **Single test scenario limits confidence** The test only exercises `monthlyMonths=2` on March 31 (mid-year, no year boundary). Two additional cases are worth checking: - **Year rollover** — `now` in January with `monthlyMonths=2` should yield November of the prior year. The `setMonth(-1)` path is untested. - **`monthlyMonths=1`** — with today=March 31, the expected cutoff is February 1. This is the minimal retention case and the one most likely to be mis-set by callers. ```ts it("handles year-boundary rollover", () => { const now = new Date(2026, 0, 15, 12, 0, 0).getTime(); // Jan 15, 2026 expect(monthlyRetentionCutoff(now, 2)).toBe(new Date(2025, 10, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0).getTime()); // Nov 1, 2025 }); it("monthlyMonths=1 anchors to start of previous calendar month", () => { const now = new Date(2026, 2, 31, 12, 0, 0).getTime(); // March 31, 2026 expect(monthlyRetentionCutoff(now, 1)).toBe(new Date(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0).getTime()); // Feb 1, 2026 }); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

Switches the two Claude 4.5 direct model IDs in the `claude_local` adapter from version-pinned strings (`claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`) to stable short aliases (`claude-sonnet-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4-5`), matching the pattern already used by the 4.6 entries and fixing enterprise-scoped key access issues reported in #3777. Both aliases are confirmed valid by Anthropic's API docs and model announcement posts. Bedrock-specific IDs in `src/server/models.ts` are untouched.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — minimal two-line change that aligns model aliases with confirmed Anthropic API stable identifiers. The change is a two-line string substitution with no logic changes, no Bedrock impact, and the new aliases (`claude-sonnet-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4-5`) are confirmed valid by Anthropic's official API docs and release announcements. All new behavior is covered by tests. No files require special attention.

3808.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two real issues — Claude Code silently using its 50-turn default when `maxTurnsPerRun` was unset, and expensive Haiku compaction calls on long sessions for `claude_local`/`codex_local` — by always passing `--max-turns` (falling back to 1000 for existing agents) and enabling proactive Paperclip-managed session rotation (8 runs / 400k tokens / 24h). The core adapter and compaction changes are clean and well-tested. The PR description does not follow the required template from `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md` — it is missing a **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Please copy the template and fill in all sections before merging.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge once the PR description is updated to follow the required template; all code changes are correct and tested. All remaining findings are P2: a process violation (missing PR template), a governance suggestion for the auto-merge workflow, and two minor style issues in the deploy workflow. No correctness or data-integrity bugs were found in the adapter or session-compaction logic. .github/workflows/ai-auto-merge.yml — auto-merge without review check may surprise human contributors

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/adapter-utils/src/session-compaction.ts | Adds ADAPTER_MANAGED_SESSION_POLICY (8 runs / 400k tokens / 24h) and applies it to claude_local, codex_local, and hermes_local; logic is correct and tests verify the new thresholds | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts | Adds || 1000 fallback so --max-turns is always passed; intentional at 1000 for backward compat with existing agents while new agents default to 200 via UI | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/test.ts | Applies the same || 1000 fallback in the hello probe, consistent with execute.ts | | ui/src/components/agent-config-defaults.ts | Lowers UI default maxTurnsPerRun from 1000 to 200 for new agents; straightforward config change | | server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-workspace-session.test.ts | Adds parseSessionCompactionPolicy tests; 3 new test cases correctly cover adapter defaults, conservative fallback, and explicit override precedence | | .github/workflows/ai-auto-merge.yml | New auto-merge workflow: merges any open, non-draft PR to master when the "PR" CI run passes — does not verify required approvals before attempting merge | | .github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml | New deploy workflow for self-hosted runner; hardcodes a user-specific PATH that will silently break if the runner environment changes |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: .github/workflows/ai-auto-merge.yml Line: 57-70 Comment: **Auto-merge fires for all open PRs without a review check** The workflow merges any open, non-draft PR targeting `master` once the `PR` workflow succeeds — it never verifies that required reviews are present. For purely AI-authored PRs this is intentional, but human contributors will get a confusing experience: CI turns green, the auto-merge workflow runs, and then GitHub's branch protection rejects the merge with a 405 if approvals are required, silently failing the workflow step. Consider gating the merge on an AI-authored label (e.g. `ai-generated`) or adding an explicit `mergeable_state === 'clean'` check before calling `pulls.merge`, so the intent is clear and non-AI PRs degrade gracefully. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: .github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml Line: 17 Comment: **Hardcoded user-specific PATH** The `PATH` is pinned to `/home/rk9admin/...`, so if the self-hosted runner is reprovisioned under a different OS user or the pnpm store is relocated, every deploy will silently fail with `pnpm: command not found`. Consider sourcing the pnpm environment via `~/.bashrc` or exporting `PATH` in the runner's global environment configuration instead. ```suggestion PATH: $HOME/.local/share/pnpm:$HOME/.local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: .github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml Line: 35-38 Comment: **Fixed sleep before health check** `sleep 5` is a best-guess delay; a slow machine or a large restart could still leave the service not yet accepting connections, causing the health check to fail on a healthy deploy. A retry loop is more robust: ```suggestion for i in $(seq 1 10); do curl -sf http://localhost:3100/api/health && echo "Paperclip OK" && exit 0 sleep 3 done echo "Health check failed after 30s" && exit 1 ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3809.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes observer churn in the `MarkdownEditor` mention-decoration effect by removing `value` from the `useEffect` dependency array, batching decoration work into `requestAnimationFrame`, and disconnecting the observer during DOM writes to avoid self-triggered mutation loops. The logic is sound: `decorateProjectMentions` already closes over the DOM reference and `mentionOptionByKey`, so external value changes propagate correctly through Lexical's own DOM updates which the persistent observer will catch. A companion regression test verifies only one `MutationObserver` instance is created across value changes.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all findings are minor style suggestions with no correctness or reliability impact. The core logic is correct: removing `value` from the dependency array is safe because decoration is driven by DOM mutations (caught by the persistent observer) rather than the raw prop value. The disconnect-decorate-reconnect pattern correctly prevents the observer from reacting to its own writes. Both findings are P2 style/clarity suggestions. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/MarkdownEditor.tsx | Removes `value` from the observer effect dependency array, batches decoration into `requestAnimationFrame`, and disconnects the observer during DOM writes to prevent self-triggering feedback loops — correct and well-scoped. | | ui/src/components/MarkdownEditor.test.tsx | Adds a regression test for the observer lifecycle fix; correctly stubs `MutationObserver`, verifies only one instance is created across value changes, and restores the global in a `finally` block. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/MarkdownEditor.tsx Line: 821-824 Comment: **Redundant `disposed` guard at end of `flushDecorations`** `decorateProjectMentions()` is synchronous, so `disposed` cannot change between the early-return check and the final `if (!disposed) observe()` call. The second guard is dead code in practice — `observe()` will always run if the function gets past the first check. Consider simplifying to a direct `observe()` call, which makes the control flow easier to follow. ```suggestion const flushDecorations = () => { frameId = null; if (disposed) return; observer.disconnect(); decorateProjectMentions(); observe(); }; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/MarkdownEditor.test.tsx Line: 282 Comment: **`observe` call-count assertion may mislead future readers** `toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)` is correct for this test scenario — the mock observer's callback never fires, so the only `observe()` call is from the initial synchronous `flushDecorations()` during effect setup. In production, however, `observe()` is called once per rAF decoration cycle (disconnect → decorate → re-observe), so the count grows with each mutation batch. A brief comment explaining that "in the mock environment no mutation callbacks fire, so observe is called exactly once on setup" would prevent future readers from misreading this assertion as a production-level invariant. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR adds `pnpm hermes:verify` / `pnpm hermes:demo` scripts and supporting documentation to give operators a one-command path for verifying and regression-testing the local Paperclip + Hermes workflow. - The shared utility module (`hermes-local-common.ts`) hard-codes `/Users/neo/projects/paperclip` inside the error-message startup command shown to any developer who can't reach the API — this will mislead everyone who isn't the PR author. - `hermes-local-verify.ts` does not implement a `--help` handler, despite the PR's Verification section claiming `pnpm hermes:verify --help` was tested; passing that flag silently runs a full live-API verification instead. - `doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_LOCAL_WORKFLOW.md` embeds the same machine-specific absolute paths across multiple runnable code blocks, which contradicts the stated goal of making this workflow reusable for the team.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the hardcoded machine path in the error message and the missing --help handler in the verify script. One P1 finding (misleading error guidance pointing to a non-existent path for other developers) and two P2 findings (missing --help, per-phase timeout semantics) prevent a 5/5. No security or data-integrity concerns. scripts/hermes-local-common.ts (hardcoded path in error message), scripts/hermes-local-verify.ts (missing --help), doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_LOCAL_WORKFLOW.md (machine-specific paths throughout)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | scripts/hermes-local-common.ts | Shared utility module for Hermes local scripts; hardcodes `/Users/neo/projects/paperclip` in the error-message startup command, which will mislead any developer who isn't the PR author. | | scripts/hermes-local-demo.ts | One-click demo/regression script; `--timeout-sec` is applied independently to each wait phase so actual max wait is 2× the flag value, which may surprise operators. | | scripts/hermes-local-verify.ts | Environment-verification script; no `--help` argument handler despite the PR's verification steps claiming `pnpm hermes:verify --help` works. | | doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_LOCAL_WORKFLOW.md | Operator workflow doc; numerous machine-specific absolute paths (`/Users/neo/projects/…`) are embedded as runnable commands, making the "productized" doc feel like personal notes and unusable as-is for other contributors. | | package.json | Adds `hermes:verify` and `hermes:demo` scripts using the existing `tsx` bootstrap pattern already used in `preflight:workspace-links`. |

Comments Outside Diff (2)

 1. `scripts/hermes-local-common.ts`, line 513-518 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/45aee5225b2adce470c36a2e6512c586b51658b6/scripts/hermes-local-common.ts#L513-L518)) **Hardcoded machine-specific path in error guidance** `DEFAULT_PAPERCLIP_START_COMMAND` embeds `/Users/neo/projects/paperclip`, which is then interpolated verbatim into the error message every developer sees when the API is unreachable. Anyone running this script from a different checkout location will be told to `cd` to a path that doesn't exist on their machine. Or dynamically use `process.cwd()` so the message is accurate regardless of checkout location.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/hermes-local-common.ts Line: 513-518 Comment: **Hardcoded machine-specific path in error guidance** `DEFAULT_PAPERCLIP_START_COMMAND` embeds `/Users/neo/projects/paperclip`, which is then interpolated verbatim into the error message every developer sees when the API is unreachable. Anyone running this script from a different checkout location will be told to `cd` to a path that doesn't exist on their machine. Or dynamically use `process.cwd()` so the message is accurate regardless of checkout location. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
2. `scripts/hermes-local-demo.ts`, line 1082-1086 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/45aee5225b2adce470c36a2e6512c586b51658b6/scripts/hermes-local-demo.ts#L1082-L1086)) **`--timeout-sec` applies per phase, not as a total timeout** `timeoutMs` is passed independently to both `waitForExecutionRun` and `waitForRunCompletion`, so the maximum actual wait is `2 × --timeout-sec`. The help text and docs describe it as a single ceiling ("默认 180"), which will surprise operators whose demo appears to hang for up to 6 minutes when they specified 3.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/hermes-local-demo.ts Line: 1082-1086 Comment: **`--timeout-sec` applies per phase, not as a total timeout** `timeoutMs` is passed independently to both `waitForExecutionRun` and `waitForRunCompletion`, so the maximum actual wait is `2 × --timeout-sec`. The help text and docs describe it as a single ceiling ("默认 180"), which will surprise operators whose demo appears to hang for up to 6 minutes when they specified 3. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/hermes-local-common.ts Line: 513-518 Comment: **Hardcoded machine-specific path in error guidance** `DEFAULT_PAPERCLIP_START_COMMAND` embeds `/Users/neo/projects/paperclip`, which is then interpolated verbatim into the error message every developer sees when the API is unreachable. Anyone running this script from a different checkout location will be told to `cd` to a path that doesn't exist on their machine. ```suggestion const DEFAULT_PAPERCLIP_START_COMMAND = [ `cd `, `PAPERCLIP_CONFIG=${process.env.PAPERCLIP_CONFIG ?? DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH} \\`, `PAPERCLIP_INSTANCE_ID=${DEFAULT_INSTANCE_ID} \\`, "node cli/node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs cli/src/index.ts run", ].join("\n"); ``` Or dynamically use `process.cwd()` so the message is accurate regardless of checkout location. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/hermes-local-verify.ts Line: 1-14 Comment: **`--help` flag silently ignored** The PR's Verification section explicitly states `pnpm hermes:verify --help` was tested, but this script performs no argument parsing. Passing `--help` causes the full verification to run (requiring a live API), not a help printout — the opposite of what a user expects. `hermes-local-demo.ts` has a proper `--help` / `-h` handler; the same pattern should be added here for consistency. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: scripts/hermes-local-demo.ts Line: 1082-1086 Comment: **`--timeout-sec` applies per phase, not as a total timeout** `timeoutMs` is passed independently to both `waitForExecutionRun` and `waitForRunCompletion`, so the maximum actual wait is `2 × --timeout-sec`. The help text and docs describe it as a single ceiling ("默认 180"), which will surprise operators whose demo appears to hang for up to 6 minutes when they specified 3. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_LOCAL_WORKFLOW.md Line: 154-162 Comment: **Machine-specific absolute paths throughout the workflow doc** The document is titled as a "productized" workflow for the team, but it embeds `/Users/neo/projects/paperclip` and `/Users/neo/projects/hermes-paperclip-adapter` as runnable commands in numerous places (Section 1 prerequisites, Section 2 startup command, Section 3.1 build step, Section 6 Python snippet, Section 7 issue payload). Any other contributor who follows these steps verbatim will get `cd: no such file or directory`. Replace these with generic placeholders (e.g., `$PAPERCLIP_ROOT`, ``) or drop the hardcoded paths in favour of the repo-relative `pnpm hermes:…` entry points already described in Section 0. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Hermes adapter as a first-class local runtime | Adds two long workflow docs, `pnpm hermes:verify` / `pnpm hermes:demo` scripts, and JSON templates for a `hermes_local` adapter + demo issue | Adapters NODE lists sub-domains: `claude-local`, `codex-local`, `cursor-local`, `gemini-local`, `openclaw-gateway`, `opencode-local`, `pi-local`. **No `hermes-local` node exists.** | 🆕 New | | Workflow documentation surface | `doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_LOCAL_WORKFLOW.md` (378 lines, zh-CN), `doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_CHECKLIST.md` (56 lines, zh-CN), both embedding a single operator's absolute paths (`/Users/neo/projects/paperclip`) | Tree does not document `doc/` as the canonical home for operator workflows; deployment/CI docs live as tree decision records, not per-adapter checklists in the source repo | 🆕 New | | Plugin-only integration path | Docs state "当前分支的 Hermes 接入以 **plugin-only** 模式为准" and reference `~/.paperclip/adapter-plugins.json` `file:` entries | Plugins NODE (not read inline here) owns plugin-SDK decisions; whether a new runtime ships as an adapter package vs. a plugin is a decision the tree does not capture for Hermes | 🆕 New | | Root `package.json` script surface | Adds `hermes:verify` + `hermes:demo` scripts pointing at `cli/node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs` | Engineering/backend dev-runner decisions are tree-documented; one-off adapter demo scripts at the monorepo root don't have tree coverage | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Lists the seven adapter sub-domains; Hermes is not among them, and the "one package per adapter" decision plus declarative config schema model are the canonical path for new runtimes. - [`adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md) — All runtimes plug into one server adapter contract rather than living alongside it as docs + scripts.
### Recommendation **Why:** Introducing a new adapter runtime — even "plugin-only" — is a tree-level decision: it changes the set of supported agents, the set of adapter sub-domains, and the integration mode (adapter package vs. plugin). 1,177 lines of documentation + demo scripts is non-trivial surface area, and it bakes in a specific machine's paths (`/Users/neo/projects/paperclip`), a specific port (3100), and Chinese-only narrative — none of which line up with how the existing adapter and deployment nodes are written. **Suggested path forward:** 1. Draft an `adapters/hermes-local/NODE.md` (or `plugins//NODE.md` if Hermes is deliberately plugin-only) that states: what Hermes is, why it's integrated as a plugin vs. a first-class `packages/adapters/` entry, which config keys are canonical, and how it relates to the shared adapter contract. 2. Decide whether the workflow checklist belongs in the source repo at all, or on the tree as a decision record linking to a minimal `scripts/hermes-local-*` reference implementation. If it stays in-repo, strip machine-specific paths, English-translate the core reference flow, and keep the zh-CN copy as a translation rather than the canonical text. 3. Confirm with an adapters/plugins owner before merging — the `hermes_local` adapter type key shows up in the JSON template and the scripts assume it exists in `/api/adapters`, so there's an implicit dependency on code that is not in this PR. Scoped as `medium` because this only lands docs + demo scripts (no runtime code), but it sets expectations about a runtime the tree hasn't adopted. --- Reviewed commit: 45aee522 · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3814.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `getMessageCustomMetadata` helper that safely normalizes missing or null `message.metadata.custom` to `{}`, and updates all four previously unguarded read sites in `IssueChatThread.tsx`. The implementation correctly handles all null/undefined variants and is backed by regression tests. - The PR checklist item for before/after screenshots is unchecked. Since this fix changes visible rendering (rich renderer instead of ErrorBoundary fallback for sparse payloads), please add screenshots showing the before (error fallback) and after (normal thread view) states, per the [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) guidelines.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 style suggestions with no correctness impact. The guard logic is correct, all four unguarded call sites were updated, and there are no remaining unguarded reads of `metadata.custom`. The only open item is a minor test gap and a missing screenshot, neither of which affects runtime behavior. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.tsx | Adds `getMessageCustomMetadata` helper that safely normalizes missing/null metadata to `{}`, and replaces all four unguarded `message.metadata.custom` reads with it. Implementation is correct and handles all null/undefined variants via optional chaining and a `null` check. | | ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.test.tsx | Adds regression tests for the new helper covering undefined/null metadata and the happy path; minor gap: `{ custom: undefined }` is not explicitly tested (but is covered by the implementation logic). |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.test.tsx Line: 670-674 Comment: **Missing test case for `custom: undefined`** The three guarded cases tested are `metadata: undefined`, `metadata: null`, and `metadata: { custom: null }`. There is no case for `{ metadata: { custom: undefined } }`. While the function handles it correctly (`typeof undefined === "object"` is `false`), adding it would make the test suite exhaustively document all falsy-custom paths. ```suggestion it("returns empty custom metadata when message metadata is missing", () => { expect(getMessageCustomMetadata({ metadata: undefined })).toEqual({}); expect(getMessageCustomMetadata({ metadata: null })).toEqual({}); expect(getMessageCustomMetadata({ metadata: { custom: null } })).toEqual({}); expect(getMessageCustomMetadata({ metadata: { custom: undefined } })).toEqual({}); }); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

3817.body:
## Code Investigation Findings

I traced this through the full authentication → comment creation → wakeup pipeline. The bug is real and reproducible under a specific but common setup condition. Here's the breakdown:

---

### Root Cause

Two separate defects combine to create the loop.

#### Defect 1 — Agent runs without `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` when JWT secret is missing

In `server/src/agent-auth-jwt.ts`, `createLocalAgentJwt()` reads its signing secret from `PAPERCLIP_AGENT_JWT_SECRET` or `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`:

```ts
// server/src/agent-auth-jwt.ts
const secret = process.env.PAPERCLIP_AGENT_JWT_SECRET?.trim() || process.env.BETTER_AUTH_SECRET?.trim();
if (!secret) return null;
```

If neither env var is set (common when running `pnpm dev` without first running `paperclipai onboard`), `createLocalAgentJwt()` returns `null`. The heartbeat logs a warning and proceeds:

```ts
// server/src/services/heartbeat.ts
const authToken = adapter.supportsLocalAgentJwt
  ? createLocalAgentJwt(agent.id, agent.companyId, agent.adapterType, run.id)
  : null;
if (adapter.supportsLocalAgentJwt && !authToken) {
  logger.warn({ ... }, "local agent jwt secret missing or invalid; running without injected PAPERCLIP_API_KEY");
}
```

In `packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts`, the API key is only injected when `authToken` is truthy:

```ts
if (!hasExplicitApiKey && authToken) {
  env.PAPERCLIP_API_KEY = authToken;  // ← never reached when authToken is null
}
```

The MCP server crashes on startup (it requires `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY`), so the agent falls back to direct `curl`/HTTP calls using the `paperclip-issue-update.sh` script or similar, which run **without an Authorization header**.

#### Defect 2 — `local_trusted` mode gives unauthenticated requests full board identity

In `server/src/middleware/auth.ts`, when no `Authorization` header is present in `local_trusted` mode:

```ts
req.actor =
  opts.deploymentMode === "local_trusted"
    ? { type: "board", userId: "local-board", isInstanceAdmin: true, source: "local_implicit" }
    : { type: "none", source: "none" };
// ...
// No bearer header → hits next() with the default actor intact
next();
```

So the agent's `PATCH /api/issues/:id` call — authenticated as board — creates the comment with:
- `authorAgentId: null`
- `authorUserId: "local-board"` (or the actual logged-in user's ID if a session cookie is present)

UI renders this as `"You"` because `authorAgentId` is null.

#### Defect 3 — `selfComment` guard only checks actor type, not run identity

In both the `PATCH /issues/:id` and `POST /issues/:id/comments` handlers, the guard that prevents re-waking the assignee after their own comment is:

```ts
const actorIsAgent = actor.actorType === "agent";
const selfComment = actorIsAgent && actor.actorId === assigneeId;
const skipWake = selfComment || isClosed;
```

Because the actor is `"user"` (not `"agent"`), `selfComment` is always `false`. A wakeup is enqueued for the assignee even though the comment came from that assignee's own active run.

---

### The Loop (step by step)

1. Agent run A starts on issue X (status `in_progress`)
2. Agent does work, posts `PATCH /api/issues/X` `{ status: "done", comment: "..." }` — but unauthenticated → treated as board
3. Comment created: `authorAgentId = null`, `authorUserId = "local-board"` → UI shows "You"
4. `selfComment = false` → wakeup for assignee is queued (but deferred because run A holds the execution lock)
5. `findRunIssueComment(run.id)` finds **no** comment with `createdByRunId = run.id` (because the comment was attributed to board, not the run) → `finalizeIssueCommentPolicy` may queue a **missing-comment retry**
6. Run A finishes → `releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote` promotes the deferred wakeup
7. Because the issue is now `done` and the deferred wake has a `wakeCommentId`, the promotion logic **reopens the issue to `todo`** (`releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote` → `shouldReopenDeferredCommentWake`)
8. Run B starts → agent sees `todo` issue with a comment from "You" → repeats

---

### Suggested Fixes

Three independent layers, any one of which would break the loop:

**Fix A (preferred) — Auto-generate JWT secret on server startup in `local_trusted` mode**

In `server/src/config.ts` (or `server/src/index.ts`), after loading the env file, if `PAPERCLIP_AGENT_JWT_SECRET` and `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` are both absent and the deployment mode is `local_trusted`, generate and persist a random secret. This is the same logic `ensureAgentJwtSecret()` uses in the CLI (`cli/src/config/env.ts`). This eliminates the root auth gap without requiring the user to run `onboard` first.

**Fix B — Resolve agent identity from run ID in auth middleware**

In `server/src/middleware/auth.ts`, when `local_trusted` mode receives a request with `X-Paperclip-Run-Id` but no Authorization header, look up the run in the DB and use its `agentId`/`companyId` to set an agent actor. This would correctly attribute the comment even without a JWT.

```ts
// pseudocode in actorMiddleware
if (opts.deploymentMode === "local_trusted" && !authHeader && runIdHeader) {
  const run = await db.select().from(heartbeatRuns)
    .where(eq(heartbeatRuns.id, runIdHeader))
    .then(rows => rows[0] ?? null);
  if (run && (run.status === "running" || run.status === "queued")) {
    req.actor = { type: "agent", agentId: run.agentId, companyId: run.companyId,
                  runId: runIdHeader, source: "local_implicit_run", keyId: undefined };
    return next();
  }
}
```

**Fix C — Guard wakeup using `createdByRunId` / `executionRunId`**

In the PATCH and POST comment route handlers, augment `selfComment` to also suppress wakeup when the comment's `createdByRunId` matches the issue's current `executionRunId`:

```ts
const commentRunId = comment.createdByRunId;
const activeRunId = currentIssue.executionRunId;
const selfComment =
  (actorIsAgent && actor.actorId === assigneeId) ||
  (commentRunId != null && commentRunId === activeRunId);
```

This catches the case where a comment was posted by the execution run (regardless of auth attribution) and prevents the spurious wakeup.

---

### Recommended approach

Fix A is the cleanest prevention — it closes the auth gap that causes downstream misattribution in everything (comments, activity log, `findRunIssueComment`). Fix B is a good defence-in-depth layer. Fix C is a targeted safety net that makes the wakeup logic robust against future attribution gaps.

Fix A alone also resolves the `findRunIssueComment` miss (step 5 above), which prevents the missing-comment retry entirely.

**Relevant files:**
- `server/src/agent-auth-jwt.ts` — `jwtConfig()` / `createLocalAgentJwt()`
- `server/src/config.ts` — env loading at module load time
- `server/src/middleware/auth.ts` — `actorMiddleware`
- `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` — `releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote`, `finalizeIssueCommentPolicy`, `findRunIssueComment`
- `server/src/routes/issues.ts` — `selfComment` guard in both PATCH and POST comment handlers
- `packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts` — `authToken` injection
- `cli/src/config/env.ts` — `ensureAgentJwtSecret()` (reuse this logic server-side)

3823.body:
Greptile Summary

Fixes a real bug where `deriveAuthTrustedOrigins` omitted `config.port` from derived origins, causing Better Auth's strict origin check to reject all sign-ins on non-standard ports with a 403. The logic correctly skips port appending for standard ports (80/443) and for hostnames that already carry an explicit port, and a companion test suite covers the main scenarios well. One edge-case gap: the bare-IPv6 regex `/^[0-9a-f:]+$/` also matches all-hex `hostname:port` strings (e.g., `cafe:3000`), causing `hasExplicitPort` to remain `false` and the config port to be double-appended. Adding a second condition requiring more than one colon would close the gap.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; fixes a legitimate auth regression with well-covered tests, and the one remaining edge case is extremely unlikely in practice. All remaining findings are P2: the hex-hostname false-positive requires an all-hex hostname with an explicit port, which is functionally impossible for real-world hostnames like `localhost`, IP literals, or `example.com`. No P0/P1 issues exist. No files require special attention; the IPv6 detection in `better-auth.ts` line 65 could be hardened but does not block merge.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/auth/better-auth.ts | Adds port appending to derived trusted origins; IPv6 detection regex has an edge-case false-positive for all-hex hostnames with an explicit port. | | server/src/__tests__/derive-auth-trusted-origins.test.ts | New test file with 10 cases covering standard/non-standard ports, explicit ports, IPv6 variants, explicit baseUrl, local_trusted mode, and empty hostnames; no test covers the all-hex hostname:port edge case. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/auth/better-auth.ts Line: 65-68 Comment: **IPv6 regex can false-positive on hex hostname:port pairs** The regex `/^[0-9a-f:]+$/` matches any string of hex digits and colons — including ordinary `hostname:port` entries where the hostname happens to be all-hex (e.g., `cafe:3000`, `dead:8080`, `ab:1234`). Those strings contain exactly one colon, so `hasExplicitPort` is `false` (no `]:`), and the config port gets appended a second time: `http://cafe:3000:3100`. A more precise bare-IPv6 detector also requires at least two colons (every real IPv6 address has `::` or multiple segment separators): ```suggestion const isIPv6 = trimmed.includes("[") || (/^[0-9a-f:]+$/.test(trimmed) && (trimmed.match(/:/g) ?? []).length > 1); const hasExplicitPort = isIPv6 ? trimmed.includes("]:") // e.g. "[::1]:3100" : trimmed.includes(":"); // e.g. "localhost:3100" ``` This correctly classifies `::1` (two colons → IPv6) while treating `cafe:3000` (one colon) as a hostname with an explicit port. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix: include server port in derived auth..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/ba78087d9143a4c1c4865595d3d584202876cfe5) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=28649946)

3825.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a recurring `duplicate key value violates unique constraint \"issues_open_routine_execution_uq\"` failure during startup/periodic recovery by detecting stale heartbeat execution locks and clearing them before retrying issue creation and wakeup queuing. The logic is sound and the new test confirms the happy-path repair scenario. The PR description is missing required sections from the PR template (Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks, Checklist) — all of which are required per CONTRIBUTING.md before a PR can be merged. Please fill out the full template.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge once the update rows-affected check is fixed and the PR template sections are filled in. One P1 finding: `clearStaleExecutionLockForRoutineIssue` unconditionally returns `true` regardless of whether the optimistic update actually modified any rows, which can produce spurious failed runs under concurrent startup recovery. The missing PR template sections are also a merge blocker per CONTRIBUTING.md. server/src/services/routines.ts — the clearStaleExecutionLockForRoutineIssue function should verify rows were actually updated via .returning().

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/routines.ts | Adds stale execution lock repair and retry logic for `issues_open_routine_execution_uq` conflicts; `clearStaleExecutionLockForRoutineIssue` may return `true` even when no rows were updated (concurrent race). | | server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts | Adds test for stale-lock repair during issue creation; missing coverage for the parallel `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup` conflict repair path. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/routines.ts Line: 698-708 Comment: **Update returns `true` regardless of rows affected** The optimistic WHERE clause (`eq(issues.executionRunId, candidate.executionRunId)`) correctly guards against concurrent clearing, but the function unconditionally returns `true` even if zero rows were modified. If two processes race to clear the same stale lock, the loser's update silently matches no rows and then proceeds to retry `issueSvc.create` — which will conflict again and bubble up to the outer catch, marking the run as `failed`. Under concurrent startup/recovery, this can produce extra failed runs that feed back into recovery. Use `.returning()` so the return value reflects whether the lock was actually cleared: ```suggestion const updated = await executor .update(issues) .set({ executionRunId: null, executionAgentNameKey: null, executionLockedAt: null, updatedAt: new Date(), }) .where(and(eq(issues.id, candidate.id), eq(issues.executionRunId, candidate.executionRunId))) .returning({ id: issues.id }); return updated.length > 0; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts Line: 352-412 Comment: **No test coverage for `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup` conflict path** The new test exercises the stale-lock repair during `issueSvc.create`, but the PR also adds an identical repair path inside the `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup` catch block (lines 918–933 of `routines.ts`). That second path has no test. A test that lets issue creation succeed but forces a conflict at the wakeup-queue step would cover both branches and guard against regressions there. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

🌱 **gardener** · 🔍 `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `b131b0ff` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Stale routine execution-lock repair (original scope) | On `23505 / issues_open_routine_execution_uq` during routine dispatch, clear `executionRunId` when the referenced heartbeat run is terminal and retry `issueSvc.create` | Task-system NODE: *"No Automatic Recovery... Stale tasks are surfaced through dashboards. Recovery is manual/explicit."* Tree does not yet distinguish lock bookkeeping from task reassignment. | ❓ Partial | | New partial unique index `issues_open_routine_origin_uq` (migration 0057) | Adds a second conflict surface: `(company_id, origin_kind, origin_id)` for open routine-execution issues where `hidden_at IS NULL`. Complements the existing `issues_open_routine_execution_uq`. | Database NODE / Routines NODE: duplicate-open-routine-issue prevention. Decision to prevent duplicates via DB uniqueness is consistent with existing patterns. | ✅ Aligned | | New `assertCanCreateIssues` route guard + legacy `canCreateIssues`/`canCreateAgents` permission read | Blocks non-CEO, non-board agents from creating issues unless `agent.permissions.canCreateIssues \|\| canCreateAgents` is set. Board actors pass through unchanged. | Governance NODE: *"Unified API, Different Authorization... agent API keys have scoped access."* Shared API + actor-scoped authorization decision record. The new gate aligns with this model. | ✅ Aligned | | Permission-shape — `Record` field named `permissions` on agents | Inline permission key enumeration (`canCreateIssues`, `canCreateAgents`) lives only in a route-local helper. Tree does not yet document the agent-permissions schema or how it interacts with role-based access (`role === "ceo"` bypass). | Agent-model / Governance: role-based agent permissions are not described in the tree beyond CEO/role primitives. | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Atomic Task Checkout, No Automatic Recovery, Single-Assignee Model. - [`product/routines/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/routines/NODE.md) — Routine concurrency policies; coalesce/skip/enqueue semantics. - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Unified API, Different Authorization; Governance Enforced at Server Layer. - [`engineering/backend/shared-api-and-actor-scoped-authorization.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/shared-api-and-actor-scoped-authorization.md) — Route-level actor checks over separate namespaces. - [`engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Failure hardening: duplicate runs, race conditions, adapter timeout outliving.
### Recommendation **Why:** The prior review's core concern — the tree doesn't distinguish "clear a stale `executionRunId` lock when its run is terminal" (lock bookkeeping) from "auto-reassign a crashed task to a new agent" (task recovery) — is still open. Since the last pass, two new changes landed: (1) a second partial unique index that further constrains open routine-execution issues, which is sound and aligned with the existing uniqueness pattern; and (2) a route-level permission gate that blocks non-CEO agents from creating issues unless their `permissions.canCreateIssues` or `permissions.canCreateAgents` is set. The gate itself is consistent with the Unified API + Actor-Scoped Authorization decision, but the **shape of the `agent.permissions` record** (which keys exist, how they compose with `role`) is not documented in the tree — and reviewers touching other agent-write routes will need that shape to apply the same pattern consistently. **Suggested path forward:** 1. Still outstanding from the first review: add a note to [`product/routines/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/routines/NODE.md) and [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) clarifying that auto-clearing a terminal-run `executionRunId` lock is lock bookkeeping, not task reassignment. 2. New this pass: add a short decision to [`product/agent-model/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) (or a new sibling node) enumerating the `agent.permissions` keys Paperclip reads at the route layer (today: `canCreateIssues`, `canCreateAgents`; plus the implicit `role === "ceo"` bypass). Otherwise future PRs will either duplicate the enumeration ad hoc or miss gates. 3. Consider whether the two uniqueness indices (`issues_open_routine_execution_uq` on `executionRunId` vs the new `issues_open_routine_origin_uq` on `(companyId, originKind, originId)`) should be consolidated or documented as complementary guards in the database NODE. 4. Once (1) and (2) are captured in the tree, this moves to ALIGNED. Severity stays medium because the lock-bookkeeping vs task-recovery distinction is still unwritten, and the new agent-permissions key set is also unwritten — both will be cited as precedent by future PRs. --- Reviewed commit: b131b0ff · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

Follow-up push after live incident audit (duplicate routine issue storm across companies):

### What was missing in the previous fix
The prior stale-lock recovery reduced failures, but open routine uniqueness was still gated by `execution_run_id IS NOT NULL` (`issues_open_routine_execution_uq`).
When `execution_run_id` had already been cleared/null, duplicate open issues could still be created for the same routine origin.

### Additional changes now pushed to this PR
- `server/src/services/routines.ts`
  - `findLiveExecutionIssue` now has a final fallback to any open issue with matching `(company_id, origin_kind='routine_execution', origin_id)` even when not bound to a live heartbeat run.
  - This ensures conflict handling coalesces to existing open routine-origin issues instead of re-attempting create paths.
- `packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts`
  - Added `issues_open_routine_origin_uq` partial unique index definition.
- `packages/db/src/migrations/0057_open_routine_origin_uniqueness.sql`
  - Adds DB guardrail unique index for open routine-origin uniqueness independent of `execution_run_id`.
- `packages/db/src/migrations/meta/_journal.json`
  - Added migration entry for `0057_open_routine_origin_uniqueness`.

### Validation
- `pnpm --filter @paperclipai/db run check:migrations`
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p server/tsconfig.json`
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p packages/db/tsconfig.json`

Commit: `5a2412e3f51807524004f8d56d4be96c20dbbd37`

Follow-up noise-control patch pushed to the PR branch:

- Commit: `b131b0ff`
- File: `server/src/routes/issues.ts`
- Change: add `assertCanCreateIssues(...)` on `POST /companies/:companyId/issues`.
- Behavior: agent-authenticated issue creation now requires legacy creation privilege (`role=ceo` or `permissions.canCreateAgents/canCreateIssues`). Non-privileged agents now get `403 Missing permission: issues:create`.

This prevents routine/agent self-spawn issue noise from standard worker agents while preserving existing assignment permission semantics (`tasks:assign`) and workspace-command mutation guards.

3834.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR adds a stale-queued execution lock coalescing path to `issueService`: a new `cancelStaleQueuedExecutionRun` helper clears `executionRunId` fields on issues whose queued heartbeat run is older than 15 minutes, and both `checkout` and `assertCheckoutOwner` call it before enforcing ownership checks, letting the assignee's active heartbeat recover without board intervention. - **P1** — Inside `cancelStaleQueuedExecutionRun` the heartbeat-run `UPDATE` is not guarded by `.returning()`, so under PostgreSQL READ COMMITTED a concurrent session can transition the run from `queued` → `running` between the two statements; the issue lock is then cleared while the run stays `running`, leaving an orphaned heartbeat process.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge once the heartbeat update is guarded with .returning() to prevent the issue lock from being cleared without confirming the heartbeat row was actually cancelled. One P1 data-consistency gap: the transaction can succeed with the issue lock cleared but the heartbeat run left running if a concurrent session picks it up between the two UPDATE statements. The race window is narrow and practically unlikely (a 15+ minute queued run suddenly starting), but the resulting state — orphaned running heartbeat with no execution-lock claim — is incorrect. Fixing it requires a one-line .returning() check. All other findings are P2. server/src/services/issues.ts — cancelStaleQueuedExecutionRun transaction (lines 992–1006)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/issues.ts | Adds cancelStaleQueuedExecutionRun helper and hooks it into checkout/assertCheckoutOwner; has a data-consistency gap where the heartbeat update is not verified inside the transaction, leaving an orphaned running process when the update matches 0 rows. | | server/src/__tests__/issues-service.test.ts | Adds a new describe block with two embedded-Postgres regression tests for stale queued lock coalescing; both happy-path cases are well-structured, but the threshold boundary (non-stale run) is not exercised. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/issues.ts Line: 992-1006 Comment: **Heartbeat cancellation not verified — issue lock cleared without confirming heartbeat update** The two updates inside the transaction are not guarded against partial failure. Under PostgreSQL's READ COMMITTED isolation, a concurrent session can commit a status change on the heartbeat run between the `UPDATE issues` statement and the `UPDATE heartbeatRuns` statement. If the heartbeat run transitions from `"queued"` to `"running"` in that window, the issue's `executionRunId` is nulled out (lock cleared), but the heartbeat row stays in `"running"` — an orphaned running process that no longer has an execution-lock claim on the issue. Add `.returning()` to the heartbeat update and return `null` inside the transaction if no rows matched, so the transaction rolls back and the issue lock is left intact: ```ts const cancelledRuns = await tx .update(heartbeatRuns) .set({ status: "cancelled", finishedAt: now, error: `superseded by later assignee heartbeat run ${input.actorRunId} after stale queued lock`, errorCode: "stale_queued_lock", updatedAt: now, }) .where(and(eq(heartbeatRuns.id, input.expectedExecutionRunId), eq(heartbeatRuns.status, "queued"))) .returning({ id: heartbeatRuns.id }); if (cancelledRuns.length === 0) return null; // concurrent transition; don't clear the issue lock return { executionRunId: input.expectedExecutionRunId }; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/__tests__/issues-service.test.ts Line: 1317-1385 Comment: **Missing negative test for the 15-minute staleness threshold** The two new tests verify that a 16-minute-old queued lock is coalesced, but there is no counterpart test confirming that a run younger than 15 minutes is left untouched. Without this, a threshold regression (e.g., the constant is accidentally changed or the comparison flipped) would go undetected. Consider adding a test case that seeds a stale timestamp of only 10 minutes and asserts the `executionRunId` remains unchanged after a `checkout` or `assertCheckoutOwner` call. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Stale queued execution-run lock cancellation during checkout / ownership assertion | `cancelStaleQueuedExecutionRun` auto-cancels a `queued` heartbeat run older than 15 min (never started, never finished) when the original assignee presents a newer `actorRunId`, then clears the issue's execution lock fields so the same assignee can proceed | Task-system NODE: *"No Automatic Recovery... Stale tasks are surfaced through dashboards. Recovery is manual/explicit."* Heartbeat-run-orchestration NODE: failure hardening covers duplicate runs, adapter timeout outliving, race conditions — but not stale-queued-run cancellation thresholds. | ❓ Partial | | Single-assignee invariant preservation | Repair path fires only when `current.assigneeAgentId === actorAgentId`; no cross-agent reassignment | Task-system NODE: Single-Assignee Model + Atomic Task Checkout | ✅ Aligned | | 15-minute staleness threshold (`STALE_QUEUED_EXECUTION_RUN_MS`) | Fresh constant in the issues service | Tree does not document the threshold for a queued heartbeat run being safe to auto-cancel | 🆕 New | | `assertCheckoutOwner` accepts `checkoutRunId == null` | After stale-lock clearing, an assignee with a valid `actorRunId` asserts ownership even though `checkoutRunId` is null | Task-system: 409-conflict semantics target **other** agent holding the lock; allowing a null-lock ownership assertion by the assignee is a refinement | ❓ Partial | | New: reap runs whose tracked child process has already exited (`reapOrphanedRuns`) | Previously skipped any run id in `runningProcesses`; now detects `exitCode !== null` or dead PID and deletes the stale tracking map entry so reaper logic can proceed | Heartbeat-run-orchestration NODE: explicit failure hardening against "adapter processes that outlive their timeout window" — this is the symmetric case (tracking outlives the process) | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Atomic Task Checkout, Single-Assignee Model, No Automatic Recovery. - [`engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Run lifecycle, failure hardening separation between adapter failures and agent failures. - [`product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md) — Scoped-wake auto-checkout; same 409 lock semantics.
### Recommendation **Why:** The original concern remains: the tree documents the *intent* (no auto-reassignment of tasks) but not the *boundary* (auto-clearing of orphaned heartbeat-run locks when the assignee tries to advance their own work is allowed and bounded). The new third commit — detecting when a tracked child process has already exited and removing it from `runningProcesses` so `reapOrphanedRuns` can then process it — directly strengthens the "failure hardening" section of the heartbeat-run-orchestration node. It's a sound extension of the documented pattern: the server is explicitly permitted to reconcile its runtime-process map against reality, and that's what this commit does. The stale-queued-run cancellation (original scope) is still the piece that should be written down because it introduces a new numeric threshold (15 min) and a new trigger condition (assignee presents a fresher `actorRunId`). **Suggested path forward:** 1. Add a decision to [`engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) describing **stale queued-run coalescing**: run is `queued`, never-started, unchanged for ≥15 min; trigger is the original assignee presenting a newer `actorRunId`; outcome is `status=cancelled`, `errorCode=stale_queued_lock` with an audit-trail `error` field citing the superseding run. 2. While there, add a short note on **orphaned-tracking recovery**: when `runningProcesses.get(runId).child.exitCode !== null` (or PID is dead), the map entry is removed so the standard reaper path can re-drive it. This is the non-controversial mirror of "process outlives its tracking" and fits the existing failure-hardening language. 3. Cross-reference from [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md)'s "No Automatic Recovery" clause: the invariant is about task assignment, not heartbeat-run lock bookkeeping. 4. Consider whether the 15-minute threshold belongs alongside (or should be derived from) the heartbeat timeout — today it's an independent constant that could drift. 5. Once (1)–(3) are captured, this moves to ALIGNED. Severity stays medium: the repaired paths are user-visible (an assignee who previously saw a stuck issue now silently advances), the thresholds are not documented, and the new reaper-map commit introduces another spot where the server silently reconciles state. --- Reviewed commit: 480d75df · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Greptile Summary

This PR adds bulk Pause All / Resume All agent actions to the Companies page (3-dot menu) and CompanySwitcher (inline icon buttons) via a new `useBulkCompanyAgentMutations` hook. Two P1 issues need fixing before merge: - **Stale UI on partial failure**: when any agent fails to pause/resume, `mutationFn` throws and `onSuccess` (with its `invalidate()` call) is skipped. Agents that did get paused/resumed are invisible in the UI until a manual refresh. Moving `invalidate()` to `onSettled` fixes this. - **Dropdown doesn't close on company selection**: `CompanyRow` renders a plain `
` instead of `DropdownMenuItem`, removing Radix's auto-close behaviour — the switcher stays open after clicking a company. Additionally, the PR description does not follow the required PR template from `CONTRIBUTING.md` — it is missing the **Thinking Path**, **What Changed**, **Verification**, **Risks**, **Model Used**, and **Checklist** sections. Please fill these in before requesting a merge review. As this is a UI change, before/after screenshots are also required per `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Not safe to merge as-is — two P1 regressions need fixing first. Two P1 bugs: (1) the CompanySwitcher dropdown no longer auto-closes when a company is selected because DropdownMenuItem was replaced with a plain div, and (2) a partial-failure scenario in the hook silently skips cache invalidation, leaving stale agent status in the UI. Both are straightforward to fix; the Companies.tsx integration is clean and correct. ui/src/components/CompanySwitcher.tsx (dropdown close regression) and ui/src/hooks/useBulkCompanyAgentMutations.ts (onSettled invalidation).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/hooks/useBulkCompanyAgentMutations.ts | New shared hook for bulk pause/resume with fan-out and toast feedback; partial-failure path skips cache invalidation, leaving stale agent state in the UI. | | ui/src/components/CompanySwitcher.tsx | Replaces DropdownMenuItem with a plain div for company rows; this breaks the Radix auto-close behavior so the dropdown stays open after selecting a company. | | ui/src/pages/Companies.tsx | Adds CompanyBulkMenuItems sub-component into the existing three-dot dropdown; wiring and separator placement look correct. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/components/CompanySwitcher.tsx Line: 39-65 Comment: **Dropdown no longer closes when selecting a company** `CompanyRow` renders a plain `
` instead of `DropdownMenuItem`. Radix `DropdownMenu` only auto-closes the popover when a `DropdownMenuPrimitive.Item` fires its `onSelect` event — a plain `
` click does not trigger this. The result is that clicking a company row to select it leaves the dropdown open, forcing the user to press Escape or click elsewhere to dismiss it, which is a regression from the previous behavior. To fix while still keeping the inline buttons functional, you can keep `DropdownMenuItem` as the wrapper and call `e.preventDefault()` inside the pause/resume button handlers — Radix respects `preventDefault` on `onSelect` to block the close: ```tsx function CompanyRow({ company, isSelected, onSelect }: { ... }) { const { bulkPause, bulkResume } = useBulkCompanyAgentMutations(company.id); const busy = bulkPause.isPending || bulkResume.isPending; return ( {company.name} ); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/hooks/useBulkCompanyAgentMutations.ts Line: 37-46 Comment: **Cache not invalidated after partial pause/resume failure** When `failed > 0` the `mutationFn` throws, which routes to `onError` and skips `onSuccess` entirely — meaning `invalidate()` is never called. But at that point some agents have already been paused (the successful ones from `Promise.allSettled`). The query cache stays stale, so the agent list and org tree in the UI won't reflect the partial changes until the user manually navigates away or refreshes. The simplest fix is to move `invalidate()` into `onSettled`, which fires regardless of success or failure: ```ts const bulkPause = useMutation({ mutationFn: async () => { ... }, onSuccess: (count) => { pushToast({ title: `Paused ${count} agent${count === 1 ? "" : "s"}`, tone: "success" }); }, onError: (err: Error) => pushToast({ title: err.message, tone: "error" }), onSettled: () => invalidate(), }); ``` Apply the same pattern to `bulkResume`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
