Greptile Summary

This PR adds a collapsible sidebar for desktop with icon-only mode (48 px), tooltips for all nav items, localStorage persistence, and a \"zen\" centered reading mode for issue detail pages when the sidebar is collapsed. - **`InstanceSidebar` broken when collapsed** — `InstanceSidebar.tsx` still has `w-60` hardcoded on its `
`. The Layout wraps it in `overflow-hidden w-12`, so icons rendered by `SidebarNavItem` (which centers content within the 240 px element) land at ~112 px — outside the 48 px clip — making the entire instance-settings sidebar appear blank. This path (any `/instance/settings/…` route) is unaffected by the collapse mechanism and needs the same `collapsed ? \"w-12\" : \"w-60\"` treatment applied to `Sidebar.tsx`. - The PR description is missing the required **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, and before/after screenshots sections mandated by [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for all PRs with visual changes.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the InstanceSidebar blank-sidebar bug on instance settings routes. One clear P1 visual defect: InstanceSidebar is not updated to handle collapsed mode, causing a completely blank icon rail on all /instance/settings/… routes when the sidebar is collapsed. All other findings are P2. The core collapsed-sidebar feature is well-implemented across the remaining components. ui/src/components/InstanceSidebar.tsx — needs the same collapsed-width treatment applied to Sidebar.tsx

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/components/InstanceSidebar.tsx | Not updated for collapsed mode — hardcoded `w-60` on the aside causes all nav icons to be rendered outside the 48 px overflow clip, making the sidebar blank when collapsed on instance settings routes. | | ui/src/components/Layout.tsx | Adds collapse toggle (ChevronsLeft/Right), switches sidebar wrapper from `w-0` to `w-12` when collapsed, and renders icon-only footer. `border-r` is missing on the desktop footer row, leaving a visual gap at the sidebar's bottom-right corner. | | ui/src/context/SidebarContext.tsx | Adds `collapsed` derived state (`!isMobile && !sidebarOpen`), localStorage persistence for sidebar state, and updates `setSidebarOpen` to also accept a function updater. Logic is clean and correct. | | ui/src/components/Sidebar.tsx | Main sidebar correctly adapts to collapsed mode: shows company dot/initial, icon-only nav with tooltips, and hides search and plugin slots. Implementation is thorough. | | ui/src/components/SidebarNavItem.tsx | Correctly handles icon-only mode with tooltip wrapper and badge dot overlay when collapsed. Clean conditional rendering. | | ui/src/components/SidebarAgents.tsx | Skips Collapsible when collapsed and renders agent icons with tooltips. Budget pause and live-run indicators are silently hidden in collapsed mode with no alternative visual signal. | | ui/src/components/SidebarProjects.tsx | Shows project color dots with tooltips when collapsed; DnD is preserved but sub-slots are hidden. Mobile `setSidebarOpen(false)` handler is correctly omitted in collapsed path (desktop-only state). | | ui/src/components/SidebarSection.tsx | Replaces section label with a thin divider when collapsed. Simple and correct. | | ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx | Adds `mx-auto` centering when sidebar is collapsed for zen reading mode. Applied in two places in the file consistently. |

`. When the sidebar is collapsed the Layout wraps it in `overflow-hidden w-12`, so the 240 px-wide aside is clipped to 48 px. The `SidebarNavItem` children do read `collapsed` and switch to `justify-center`, but they center relative to the 240 px aside — placing the icon at ~112 px, well outside the 48 px clip. The result is a completely blank icon rail whenever a user collapses the sidebar while on any `/instance/settings/…` route. Fix: apply the same `collapsed ? "w-12" : "w-60"` pattern used in `Sidebar.tsx`: You'll also need to import `useSidebar` and `cn` and hide the `"Instance Settings"` text label (same as the company name in `Sidebar.tsx`) when collapsed.

Greptile Summary

Adds a template gallery — a curated registry of pre-built agent companies that users can install in one click. The implementation covers shared Zod types, a file-backed registry service with in-memory caching, three new API endpoints (`GET /templates/companies`, `POST /templates/companies/install`, `POST /templates/refresh`), and a React frontend with a `TemplatesPage`, `CompanyCard`, sidebar nav link, and React Query mutations. - **P1 — `TemplatesPage.tsx` line 43:** `installing` is only `true` for the card matching `install.variables`, so every other card's Install button stays enabled during an active install. TanStack Query v5 fires `onSuccess` for each completed `mutate()` call, meaning rapid multi-card clicks can produce duplicate company creations and multiple `navigate()` calls.

Confidence Score: 4/5

One P1 bug (concurrent installs can create duplicate companies) should be fixed before merging; the two P2 findings are non-blocking. The concurrent-install defect in TemplatesPage is a present correctness issue — other cards stay clickable during an install and TanStack Query v5 calls onSuccess for each completing mutation, making duplicate company creation a realistic outcome. The remaining two findings (CWD-based path and error detail leakage) are P2 quality/hardening items that don't block the primary flow. ui/src/pages/TemplatesPage.tsx (P1 concurrent-install bug), server/src/app.ts (fragile CWD path), server/src/routes/templates.ts (error detail field)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | ui/src/pages/TemplatesPage.tsx | New template gallery page — P1: only the active card's Install button is disabled during an ongoing install, leaving other cards clickable and allowing duplicate company creation. | | server/src/routes/templates.ts | New Express routes for listing, installing, and refreshing templates — auth gating is correct; 503 detail fields expose absolute server file paths to authenticated clients. | | server/src/app.ts | Mounts templateRoutes and shares portabilityService — registry path resolved via process.cwd(), which breaks if the server starts from a non-root directory. | | server/src/services/template-registry.ts | File-backed registry with in-memory cache and invalidation — clean implementation; concurrent cold-start reads are harmless. | | packages/shared/src/template-types.ts | Adds Zod schemas and TypeScript types for template registry — well-structured and validates slug format, URL, and counts correctly. | | ui/src/components/templates/CompanyCard.tsx | Simple presentational card component — renders correctly; the disabled prop is driven by the installing flag passed from TemplatesPage. | | server/src/routes/companies.ts | Accepts optional portabilityOverride to share the service instance with the templates router — minimal, non-breaking change. | | ui/src/components/Sidebar.tsx | Adds Templates nav link to the Company section using LayoutGrid icon — consistent with existing sidebar items. | | ui/src/api/templates.ts | Thin API client wrapping list, install, and refresh endpoints with correct shared types. | | tests/e2e/templates-gallery.spec.ts | Playwright smoke test for list + install flow with appropriate 120s timeout for the real GitHub fetch. | | docs/templates/registry.json | Seed registry with four curated companies — all entries conform to the templateRegistrySchema. | | ui/src/App.tsx | Registers /templates route inside a Layout wrapper — consistent with existing route patterns. | | ui/vitest.config.ts | Adds globals and test-setup file to wire up jest-dom matchers for the UI package. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/pages/TemplatesPage.tsx Line: 42-43 Comment: **Concurrent installs not guarded — duplicate company creation possible** `installing` is `false` for every card whose slug doesn't match the active mutation, so their buttons stay enabled while an install is in flight. In TanStack Query v5, calling `mutate()` while a prior mutation is pending fires both requests and calls `onSuccess` for each one that completes — which would create two companies and trigger two `navigate()` calls in quick succession. Fix: gate the disabled state on `install.isPending` globally, while keeping the per-slug check only for the label: ```suggestion onInstall={(slug) => install.mutate(slug)} installing={install.isPending && install.variables === company.slug} disabled={install.isPending} ``` Then update `CompanyCard` to accept and forward a separate `disabled` prop to `

3841.body: Just noticed that this is a duplicate of #3829 . Maybe my fix and workaround can be useful - so I don't close it yet myself.

## Follow-up: second affected row found

A second issue was discovered with the same root cause. Another Issue also had an em dash (`—`, bytes `e2 80 94`) starting at byte 1200 of its description, causing the same `invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0xe2` error.

The data was patched the same way (`replace(description, '—', ' - ')`), but this confirms the issue is not isolated to a single row — any issue whose description happens to have a multi-byte UTF-8 character beginning at byte 1200 will trigger it.

The server-side code fix (changing `substring(description FROM 1 FOR 1200)` to `left(convert_from(convert_to(description, 'SQL_ASCII'), 'UTF8'), 1200)`) is the proper structural fix and prevents this for all future rows once deployed. Until then, any other issue description that grows to exactly the wrong byte length with a multi-byte character at the boundary would cause the same 500.

3842.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR implements the full L1–L3 cost-monitoring reconciliation stack, fixing `spentMonthlyCents = 0` for Claude subscription users by computing equivalent API costs from token counts, adding `cache_creation_input_tokens` throughout the event pipeline, and introducing a new `billing_reconciliation` table for daily Anthropic Admin API drift detection. - **L3 reconciliation produces false drift alerts for subscription agents**: `fetchAnthropicUsageForDay` skips all subscription runs (no `metadata.user_id` via CLI), so `anthropicCents` is always `0` for subscription agents. When `ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_API_KEY` is set, every active subscription agent fires a 100% drift warning daily, making the alert signal useless. - **Cross-company contamination**: In multi-company instances the Anthropic org-level report can include agent IDs from other companies; these get added to `allAgentIds` and are written with the wrong `companyId`. - The PR description does not follow the required template (missing Thinking Path, What Changed, Risks, Model Used, and Checklist sections per `CONTRIBUTING.md`).

Confidence Score: 3/5

The L1 subscription cost fix and DB migration are safe, but L3 reconciliation will produce constant false-positive drift alerts for subscription deployments when the admin key is present. The P1 finding (subscription agents always showing 100% drift) is a present defect in the new billing-reconciliation service that would make the feature actively misleading in common configurations. The L1 fix (normalizeBilledCostCents + token cost computation) is correct and the migration is safe. server/src/services/billing-reconciliation.ts — drift calculation and allAgentIds union logic need attention before this is deployed with ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_API_KEY.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/billing-reconciliation.ts | New L3 reconciliation service; has a design defect where subscription agents always produce 100% drift alerts when admin key is present, plus a cross-company data contamination risk in multi-company setups. | | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds ANTHROPIC_MODEL_PRICING table and computeEquivalentCostCents; normalizeBilledCostCents correctly computes token-based cost for subscription_included billing type. | | server/src/services/costs.ts | Adds cacheCreationInputTokens support to createEvent and all query aggregations; defaults to 0 safely. | | packages/db/src/migrations/0057_shiny_ghost_rider.sql | Adds cache_creation_input_tokens column to cost_events (with safe default) and creates billing_reconciliation table with appropriate FK constraints and indexes. | | server/src/index.ts | Wires daily billing reconciliation into the heartbeat scheduler with a 5-minute poll interval checking the 03:05–03:09 UTC window; in-memory dedup guard is safe for idempotency. | | packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts | Adds cacheCreationInputTokens field to UsageSummary construction from cache_creation_input_tokens in the result event. | | packages/db/src/schema/billing_reconciliation.ts | New Drizzle schema for billing_reconciliation table; well-typed with correct FK references and index definitions matching the migration. | | packages/db/src/schema/cost_events.ts | Adds cacheCreationInputTokens column to cost_events schema with NOT NULL default 0, consistent with migration. | | packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts | Makes cacheCreationInputTokens optional in UsageSummary interface, consistent with backward compatibility. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/billing-reconciliation.ts Line: 196-206 Comment: **Subscription agents always trigger 100% drift alerts** For Claude subscription billing, `fetchAnthropicUsageForDay` returns no records (the CLI can't tag `metadata.user_id`), so `anthropicCents` is always `0` for every subscription agent. The drift calculation then falls into the middle branch and returns `100`, which exceeds `DRIFT_ALERT_THRESHOLD_PCT = 10`. Every subscription agent with any activity will fire a `WARN` log daily, making the L3 alert signal useless when `ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_API_KEY` is configured alongside subscription billing. Consider skipping the drift alert (or recording drift as `null`) for agents whose Paperclip cost came entirely from subscription-billing events, since Anthropic's org-level API can never provide matching data for those runs. ```typescript const driftPct = anthropicCents > 0 ? ((Math.abs(paperclipCents - anthropicCents) / anthropicCents) * 100) : paperclipCents > 0 ? 100 : 0; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/billing-reconciliation.ts Line: 183-191 Comment: **Cross-company agent contamination in multi-company setups** `fetchAnthropicUsageForDay` returns org-level data for all agents across the entire Anthropic org. In a multi-company Paperclip instance, `anthropicByAgent.keys()` can include agent IDs that belong to a *different* company. Those IDs get added to `allAgentIds` for the current company's reconciliation run, causing `billing_reconciliation` rows to be inserted with `agentId` from company B but `companyId` set to company A. Both FK constraints pass (the agent and company both exist), but the association is incorrect. Filtering `allAgentIds` to only the current company's known agents would isolate each company's reconciliation: ```typescript // Instead of unioning in anthropicByAgent.keys(), // restrict to agents that actually belong to this company. const allAgentIds = new Set(companyAgents.map((a) => a.id)); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/src/services/billing-reconciliation.ts Line: 21-28 Comment: **Duplicated pricing table requires manual synchronisation** `MODEL_PRICING` here and `ANTHROPIC_MODEL_PRICING` in `heartbeat.ts` are identical and the comment says they are "kept in sync manually." This is fragile — a price update in one place won't automatically propagate. Consider exporting a single pricing table from a shared module (e.g. `@paperclipai/adapter-utils` or a new `packages/billing` package) and importing it in both services. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `skipIfNoActionableAssignments` boolean to the heartbeat policy that suppresses timer-triggered wakeups when an agent has no assigned issues in `todo` or `backlog` status, preventing wasted token spend on blocked/stalled work. A matching UI toggle is added to the Advanced Run Policy section. The implementation is backwards compatible (default `false`) and event-triggered wakes are unaffected. The PR description is missing several required sections from the PR template (`.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`): there is no **Thinking Path** in the required blockquote format, no **Risks** section, no **Model Used** section, and no **Checklist**. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, every PR must follow the template. Additionally, since a UI toggle was added, before/after screenshots are required per `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge after addressing the PR template gaps and adding screenshots; code logic is correct and backwards compatible. All findings are P2: the non-atomic writes are a low-probability edge case with benign worst-case behavior (extra skip records, no data loss), and the template/screenshot gaps are process concerns, not correctness issues. The core skip logic, status filter, companyId/agentId scoping, and UI default values are all correct. server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — the two non-atomic writes in the skip path (lines 4547–4551).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/services/heartbeat.ts | Adds skipIfNoActionableAssignments to parseHeartbeatPolicy and enqueueWakeup; logic and scoping look correct but the two skip-path DB writes (writeSkippedRequest + lastHeartbeatAt update) are not atomic. | | ui/src/components/AgentConfigForm.tsx | Adds ToggleField for skipIfNoActionableAssignments in the Advanced Run Policy section; default value and onChange handler follow existing patterns correctly. |

Path: server/src/services/heartbeat.ts Line: 4547-4551 Comment: **Non-atomic skip + timer-reset writes** `writeSkippedRequest` and the `lastHeartbeatAt` update are two independent `await`s with no transaction. If the second write fails (e.g. transient DB error), the skipped-request row is committed but `lastHeartbeatAt` is not advanced. On the next scheduler tick the agent's interval appears to have elapsed again, the same check runs, writes another skip row, and the loop repeats — producing a burst of skip records until the update eventually succeeds. Consider wrapping both writes in a single `db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })` so they succeed or fail together. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

🌱 **gardener** · ✅ `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `6258b477` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Per-agent heartbeat option to skip timer wakes when no actionable assignments | New `skipIfNoActionableAssignments` flag on heartbeat policy; on `source === "timer"`, count assignee's issues in `todo`/`backlog`; if 0, log `heartbeat.skipIfNoActionableAssignments`, bump `lastHeartbeatAt`, return null — event-triggered wakes unaffected | Agent Model NODE — *Heartbeat Protocol*: "Paperclip controls **when** to fire (schedule/frequency), **how** to fire, and **what context** to include." This PR adds a per-agent skip predicate that sits in the "when" layer without changing the protocol shape. | ✅ Aligned | | Budget / token-burn intent | Hint text: *"Prevents token burn on blocked or stalled work."* Only applies to timer wakes; mentions, assignments, on-demand wakes all bypass it. | Governance NODE — *Budget Hard-Stops*: "hidden token burn is not [allowed]." Skipping idle timer wakes when there's nothing to do aligns with the safe-autonomy principle. | ✅ Aligned | | UI surface | New `ToggleField` on `AgentConfigForm` with descriptive hint covering which statuses count and which wake types are unaffected | Frontend NODE — agent config form lives in `AgentConfigForm`; React Query + centralized API patterns unchanged | ✅ Aligned | | Single-assignee scoping | Count query filters by `eq(issues.assigneeAgentId, agentId)` + in `["todo", "backlog"]` | Task-system NODE — Single-Assignee Model; Issue Status Workflow fixed enum | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/agent-model/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Heartbeat Protocol; Context Delivery Spectrum. - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Budget Hard-Stops; "hidden token burn is not allowed." - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Issue Status Workflow (`backlog`, `todo`, `in_progress`, `in_review`, `done`, `blocked`, `cancelled`); Single-Assignee Model. - [`engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Run lifecycle state machine; scheduling vs execution boundary.
No concerns. This is a narrow, per-agent skip predicate on timer-triggered heartbeats that fits the "Paperclip controls when to fire" clause of the Heartbeat Protocol and the "no hidden token burn" side of Budget Hard-Stops. Event-driven wakes (assignment, on-demand, mention) remain unaffected, so the agent still responds immediately to new work — the skip only collapses idle polling. --- Reviewed commit: 6258b477 · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 3850.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR wraps the upstream `hermesExecute` function to unconditionally prepend `--yolo` to `extraArgs`, fixing Hermes agents running as non-interactive subprocesses where approval prompts would always time out. The wrapper is idempotent (won't double-add the flag) and passes `tsc --noEmit`. The PR description is missing the **Thinking Path**, **Model Used**, and **Risks** sections required by CONTRIBUTING.md. Please add these before merging — the Thinking Path in particular (a top-down narrative from product context to what was changed) and the Model Used section (provider, exact model ID/version) are hard requirements in the PR template.

Confidence Score: 5/5

The code change is correct and safe to merge; the only outstanding item is the missing PR template sections (Thinking Path, Model Used, Risks). No code defects found — the wrapper is idempotent, type-safe, and TypeScript-verified. All remaining feedback is P2 (process/documentation). Per scoring guidance, P2-only findings default to 5/5. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | server/src/adapters/registry.ts | Adds hermesExecuteWithYolo wrapper that prepends --yolo to extraArgs before delegating to upstream hermesExecute; idempotent and type-safe, replaces the direct hermesExecute reference in hermesLocalAdapter. | Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(hermes): spawn Hermes with --yolo by..."](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/2f4faf6b71df6c8a63da277d9a91c17a8fc77f4b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=28716285)

🌱 **gardener** · 🆕 `NEW_TERRITORY` · severity: `low` · commit: `2f4faf6` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Hermes-local adapter | Wraps `hermesExecute` in `registry.ts` to always prepend `--yolo` to extraArgs; idempotent | `adapters/NODE.md` lists sub-domains (claude-local, codex-local, cursor-local, gemini-local, openclaw-gateway, opencode-local, pi-local) but **not hermes-local** — tree is entirely silent on this adapter | 🆕 New | | Agent-side dangerous-command bypass | `--yolo` is Hermes's own interactive safety prompt; skipping it when running as non-interactive subprocess | `product/governance/NODE.md`: "Governance rules are control-plane invariants enforced at the server, not at the adapter or agent level." Hermes's `--yolo` is agent-internal, not a Paperclip approval gate — server-enforced board/issue approvals are untouched | ✅ Aligned | | Opinionated adapter wiring | Paperclip owns the `--yolo` policy rather than delegating to config | `product/NODE.md` decision #5: "Unopinionated about agent implementation" but also "the adapter defines the agent's inner workings" — policy lives in the adapter module, consistent with decision #5 | ✅ Aligned |
Tree nodes referenced - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter sub-domain list (hermes-local absent) - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — governance rules enforced server-side, not at adapter layer - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #5 (unopinionated about agent implementation)
### Recommendation **Why:** The behavior itself is fine — Hermes runs as a non-interactive subprocess so approval prompts would always time out. `--yolo` bypasses *Hermes's internal* safety prompt, not Paperclip's server-enforced approvals. But the tree doesn't currently document hermes-local as a sub-domain at all (sibling issue paperclipai/paperclip#3833 noticed the same gap), so there's no place to record this policy decision or future hermes-specific conventions. **Suggested path forward:** Land the fix — it's a blocker for Hermes being usable at all and aligns with how sibling adapters handle their own interactive-prompt equivalents. Separately, add a node at `adapters/hermes-local/NODE.md` on the tree that captures: (1) Paperclip integrates with Hermes via the upstream `hermes-paperclip-adapter` package, (2) non-interactive execution means Paperclip prepends `--yolo` to every run, (3) any other hermes-specific conventions (session management capability, skill sync support). This gives future changes a documented home and closes the gap #3833 flagged. --- Reviewed commit: 2f4faf6 · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.
